Session Notes

Source: SESSION_NOTES.md

Purpose: Continuity between sessions. Each session reads this first and writes to it before closing out.

ACTIVE TASK

What Session 141 Did

Deliverable: Lint cleanup -> green CI (owner pick) ‚Äî clear every lintr warning + the cyclocomp config wart so the lint GitHub Actions check passes; the exact RED-on-master state Learning 133 flagged. (DONE.) A firsthand lint_package() found 57 lints across 33 files (the CI log‚Äôs ‚Äú~30‚Äù undercounted). Final: 0 lints, full suite 0/0, R CMD check Status OK. Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. Strict-TDD REFACTOR (declared every response; behavior-neutral). Phase gates: one scope AskUserQuestion (3 owner decisions) + one REFACTOR phase-gate AskUserQuestion before any edit. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized fan-out under ultracode ‚Äî the 46 per-file fixes ran through a fix->adversarial-review workflow (56 agents), but the load-bearing step was my central re-verification (re-lint + full suite + R CMD check + reading the entire diff), which caught a cascade the per-file fixers were blind to. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Two commits planned: the content commit (config + 28 source + man/) + this close-out commit (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES). - REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî established the true baseline + the one real risk before editing: local lint_package() = 57 (= CI exactly), lintr/cyclocomp installed locally so verification is firsthand. The ‚Äúdragon‚Äù: coalesce_linter (11) wants %||%, but it needs R >= 4.4 (pkg Depends: R (>= 4.1.0)) and shiny 1.13 no longer exports %||% (verified "%||%" %in% getNamespaceExports("shiny") = FALSE) ‚Äî so adopting it would silently break R 4.1-4.3. Surfaced this; owner chose to disable coalesce_linter in .lintr (consistent with the 7 linters it already NULLs). - REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî fixed 46 code lints across 28 files via a fix->adversarial-review workflow: 31 return_linter (drop terminal return(); restructure terminal if/else chains ‚Äî chooseDate/removeDuplicates/convertDate/dataframe2string/qcStudbook), 6 seq_along, 5 file.path (1 R/ + 4 data-raw), 2 line wraps, 2 !any. 27 files clean by both fixer + independent reviewer. - REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî central re-lint caught a CASCADE the fixers couldn‚Äôt see (-> Learning 134): after the workflow, re-lint read 5, not 0 ‚Äî all in removeDuplicates.R: the boolean_arithmetic autofix sum(duplicated(x))==0L -> !any(duplicated(x)) cleared its target but TRIGGERED if_not_else + any_duplicated + unnecessary_nesting. Rewrote to if (anyDuplicated(x) > 0L) <dups> else NULL + a guard clause for the stop() branch -> all four cleared, behavior preserved (incl.¬Ýempty-vector edge). Re-lint -> 0. - REAL WORK #4 ‚Äî context-blind-linter judgments: (a) nonportable_path_linter flagged a MIME-type string in modPedigree‚Äôs fileInput(accept=...); the author had already # nolint-ed the adjacent "text/csv" line (intent = false positive) but missed the second string ‚Äî the fixer wrapped the MIME type in file.path() (output identical, semantically wrong), so I restored the readable string + extended the # nolint. (b) cyclocomp wart: added any::cyclocomp to lint.yaml (.lintr already NULLs the linter; linters_with_tags() warns at config-load when absent ‚Äî non-fatal). (c) the one roxygen line wrap (R/data.R:358) made man/ stale, so regenerated (roxygen2 == pinned 8.0.0; diff confined to man/rhesusPedigree.Rd).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED by R CMD check. The change is behavior-neutral R code + lint/CI config; R CMD check (--no-manual --ignore-vignettes) loaded the namespace, ran all examples (+ donttest) and the full testthat + spelling suites -> Status: OK (0/0/0), which exercises the real runtime paths. No separate Shiny-app launch needed: no startup/dispatch/config-resolution behavior changed (the only mod*.R edits were a # nolint comment + a restored-identical MIME string + implicit-return/seq_along/%||%-free rewrites). The .lintr/lint.yaml edits are CI-only, not runtime.

Session 140 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 141): Score 9/10. S140‚Äôs handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was an excellent executor‚Äôs map for this task. It listed ‚Äú(Lint cleanup ‚Üí green CI)‚Äù as the FIRST suggested-next with the exact linter names (return_linter/seq_linter/coalesce_linter/nonportable_path_linter/boolean_arithmetic_linter) and the cyclocomp wart, correctly classified it as a behavior-neutral REFACTOR, and Learning 133 even pre-stated ‚Äúa future REFACTOR session could clear it to green‚Äù ‚Äî so I started with the scope already framed. Every carried fact held: lint RED on master, the branch-divergence (add-methodology 1 ahead / behind the merge), the standing keeps, CRAN gating. ROI clearly positive. The ‚àí1: the count was ‚Äú~30‚Äù but the real figure is 57 (S140 read it off the truncated CI log, reasonably) ‚Äî and the handoff didn‚Äôt flag the two non-mechanical traps a fixer hits (%||% unavailable below R 4.4 / not in shiny 1.13; the nonportable_path MIME-type false positive). Minor, since both were discoverable firsthand; naming them would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 141): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, ghost-check clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) established the true baseline before editing ‚Äî corrected ‚Äú~30‚Äù->57, installed lintr/cyclocomp for firsthand verification, and pinned tests green as a baseline; (2) caught the one real correctness risk up front (%||% below the R floor / dropped by shiny 1.13) and surfaced it as an owner decision rather than silently narrowing R support; (3) the central re-lint caught the cascade the per-file fixers were blind to (boolean_arithmetic -> 3 new linters) ‚Äî and I rewrote it behavior-preserving rather than whack-a-mole; (4) treated linter messages as suggestions, not mandates ‚Äî kept the readable MIME string + # nolint instead of file.path()-wrapping a MIME type; (5) regenerated man/ for the roxygen wrap (version-matched, diff confined); (6) read the entire git diff firsthand + the full triple (lint 0, suite 0/0, R CMD check OK); 0 corrections, ASCII-only, plain language. Weaknesses (honest): (a) my first workflow invocation failed (pipeline() got a non-array ‚Äî args didn‚Äôt arrive as JSON; relaunched with the list embedded) ‚Äî no harm, 0 agents ran, but a sloppy first call; (b) the workflow‚Äôs per-file fixers produced a residual (the cascade + the MIME file.path) ‚Äî caught entirely by my central verify (no shipped defect), but it means the fan-out‚Äôs value was bounded and the verification did the real work; (c) moderate difficulty ‚Äî a careful, well-bounded refactor. I reserve 10 for a first pass with no failed tool call and no fixer residue.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. Lint cleanup is DONE (0 lints locally; R CMD check OK). Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (See lint go green on a PR) the lint workflow triggers on pull_request and on push to main/master ‚Äî NOT on push to add-methodology. To watch it pass, it rides the next add-methodology -> master PR (or any PR). Nothing for a Claude session unless you want a PR opened. - (Branch strategy) still your open decision (S140): continue on add-methodology (now further ahead of master) vs move to master + feature branches. Untouched. - (CRAN Phase 5, owner-run) win-builder x3 + R-hub v2 + submit_cran() ‚Äî owner PAT + email; HARD STOP (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md). - A GitHub issue ‚Äî #46 (species first-class), #45/#28/#9 (parent ID via conception date), #2 (GVA iteration-count), #37 (unused exports), #36 (chimp pyramid), #29 (makeGrpNum->makeGroupNum), or older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1. Do NOT bundle options (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED ‚Äî content commit (add-methodology): .lintr (coalesce_linter = NULL), .github/workflows/lint.yaml (any::cyclocomp), 26 R/*.R + 2 data-raw/*.R source files (lint fixes ‚Äî see CHANGELOG for the per-linter breakdown), man/rhesusPedigree.Rd (regenerated, one reflowed line). CHANGED ‚Äî close-out commit: CHANGELOG.md (S141 [Unreleased] entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 134), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). NOT committed (standing keeps): .DS_Store (modified), PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked). Read firsthand: SESSION_RUNNER + SAFEGUARDS (full), the failed CI lint log (gh run view 27843775241 --log-failed), .lintr, lint.yaml, DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE, and every changed source diff. Transients (in /tmp, NOT repo): background job outputs; the devtools::check build dir (auto-cleaned ‚Äî no .Rcheck/tarball left in the repo).

Gotchas: (1) lint is now GREEN locally (0 lints) but the GitHub check is still RED on master ‚Äî the fix is on add-methodology, not yet merged; the lint workflow does NOT run on push to add-methodology (only pull_request + push to main/master), so it goes green when the next PR runs. (2) coalesce_linter is permanently DISABLED in .lintr by design ‚Äî do NOT ‚Äúre-enable + adopt %||%‚Äù without first bumping Depends: R (>= 4.4.0) or adding a dependency that provides %||% (shiny 1.13 does not export it). (3) A lint sweep is iterative ‚Äî an autofix can clear its target and trigger other linters (Learning 134); always re-run lint_package() over the whole tree until 0, never trust per-file results. (4) modPedigree‚Äôs fileInput(accept=) MIME strings carry # nolint: nonportable_path_linter ‚Äî they are MIME types, not paths; do not ‚Äúfix‚Äù them. (5) man/rhesusPedigree.Rd is generated ‚Äî the wrap came from R/data.R‚Äôs roxygen; edit the source + roxygenise(), never hand-edit the .Rd. (6) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call; never commit. (7) Branch: add-methodology is further ahead of master after this; per Learning 129, git fetch + git rev-list --left-right --count origin/master...add-methodology before acting on any branch-sync/merge prerequisite. (8) Carried CRAN gotchas: package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29 (Learning 95); CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated.

Session 139 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 140): Score 8/10. S139‚Äôs handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was directly actionable for this task: it listed ‚ÄúReview / merge PR #54‚Äù as the top SUGGESTED-NEXT with the PR URL, explicitly said ‚Äúmerge timing is the owner‚Äôs call,‚Äù and the gotchas held (PR open-not-merged, CRAN gated, generated files). ROI clearly positive ‚Äî I executed straight off it. The ‚àí2: it did not flag the PR‚Äôs CI status ‚Äî lint was red on #54, and the handoff‚Äôs ‚ÄúR CMD check OK, tests 0/0‚Äù was a local claim that didn‚Äôt mention the GitHub Actions checks, so the lint-red was a surprise I had to discover and triage in S140. Mitigating: at S139 close-out the CI was almost certainly still running (the PR had just been opened; the Ubuntu devel job alone takes ~16 min), so the status wasn‚Äôt knowable yet ‚Äî but a one-line ‚ÄúCI is running on #54; run gh pr checks 54 before merging‚Äù would have pre-armed S140 and made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 140): 8/10. Claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) did not merge blind ‚Äî verified mergeable + ran gh pr checks + read the failing job log before touching the merge button; (2) classified the red check correctly (non-required lint, pre-existing style debt, not introduced by S139, all correctness checks green) and surfaced it rather than merging silently over a red check OR refusing an explicit directive over a non-blocking style check; (3) used a merge commit (history-preserving) and did not delete the branch; (4) verified the merge landed firsthand (git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION = 2.0.0; merge-base --is-ancestor) instead of trusting the gh exit ‚Äî which mattered, because my own malformed --json merged query had briefly masked the success; (5) captured Learning 133 and recorded the new known state (lint red on master) + the branch-divergence decision for the owner. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low difficulty ‚Äî a triage + one command; (b) I malformed the first gh pr view --json merged query, which made a successful merge momentarily look failed ‚Äî caught instantly by re-checking with valid fields, no harm, but a sloppy first call; (c) proceeded with the merge over a red (non-required) check without a separate confirmation round ‚Äî defensible given the explicit ‚Äúmerge #54‚Äù + the non-blocking nature + all correctness checks green, but a more conservative read would have paused to confirm ‚Äúmerge despite the non-required lint?‚Äù; I judged the explicit directive sufficient. I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty, end-to-end deliverable.

What Session 139 Did

Deliverable: Clear S138‚Äôs punch-list (owner directive ‚Äúperform S138‚Äôs next steps‚Äù) ‚Äî (DONE). Four items, in a safe order: (1) NEWS names' spell flag ‚Äî fixed at source (backtick the literal base-R message in NEWS.Rmd; the curl was a pandoc smart-quote render artifact, not a source typo), re-rendered NEWS.md, spell_check_package() 1‚Üí0 flags; (2) README ‚ÄúFor more information‚Äù de-dup ‚Äî removed README.Rmd‚Äôs own hardcoded Vinson/Raboin citation block (the duplicate; the surviving copy renders from the shared _introduction.Rmd child), re-rendered README.md via build_readme(); (3) CRAN plan ¬ß9 Phase-3 reconcile ‚Äî marked ¬ß9 row 3 ‚úÖ + added a Phase-3 STATUS block (DESCRIPTION/README/CITATION.cff all verified at 2.0.0); (4) opened PR #54 add-methodology ‚Üí master (open, NOT merged). Issue #53 was already CLOSED by the owner earlier today ‚Äî no action. Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. VERIFICATION/docs phase (declared every response ‚Äî no production logic changed; no RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR transition, so no phase-gate AskUserQuestion). 0 stakeholder corrections. Owner explicitly overrode ‚Äú1 and done‚Äù to batch S138‚Äôs menu; executed in a safe order with a commit + a confined-diff check between the generated-file re-renders. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode ‚Äî surgical doc edits + an outward-facing PR; a fan-out would risk conflicting edits to the same tree and adds no confidence over a firsthand confined-diff read. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Two commits: 6be6a8aa (the 3 content fixes) + the close-out commit (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES); both pushed. - REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî diagnosed the names' flag as a RENDER artifact, fixed at source (‚Üí Learning 132): S138 said ‚Äúfix the quote (don‚Äôt whitelist)‚Äù at NEWS.md:51. But NEWS.Rmd:53 already used straight ASCII quotes ‚Äî pandoc‚Äôs smart extension curls 'row.names' ‚Üí ‚Äòrow.names‚Äô at render, and the closing ' against names makes the bogus token names‚Äô. So there was no source quote to ‚Äústraighten.‚Äù Wrapped the literal message in backticks in NEWS.Rmd (`duplicate 'row.names' are not allowed` ‚Äî code spans are smart-quote-exempt and read as the verbatim message), re-rendered NEWS.md (github_document, html_preview=FALSE) ‚Üí exactly a one-line diff; spelling::spell_check_package() ‚Üí 0 flags (was 1). Did NOT hand-edit NEWS.md (generated) and did NOT disable smart globally (would curl-strip every other quote in the 500-line file). - REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî mapped the README duplication before cutting: the citation rendered twice ‚Äî first from the shared child vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd (also consumed by a3manual.Rmd + ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd), second from README.Rmd‚Äôs own hardcoded block (lines 46-51). Removed only the README.Rmd block ‚Üí README-only blast radius (manual/tutorial vignettes untouched), citation survives once in the Introduction. Re-rendered via devtools::build_readme(); git diff README.md = removed block + an incidental, correct getVersion() date refresh (2026-06-17 ‚Üí 2026-06-19). Confirmed grep "For more information" README.md = 1 hit. - REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî plan ¬ß9 reconcile, evidence-based: verified firsthand DESCRIPTION 2.0.0, README.md ‚ÄúVersion 2.0.0‚Äù, CITATION.cff version: 2.0.0; NEWS re-renders; version-tests green (S138 0/0). Marked docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md ¬ß9 row 3 ‚úÖ + added a Phase-3 STATUS block matching the Phase-4/5 pattern. - REAL WORK #4 ‚Äî PR #54 opened (open, not merged): git fetch + rev-list confirmed origin/add-methodology == HEAD (0/0, no push-before-PR needed ‚Äî Learning 129); git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION = 1.1.0.9000 (PR genuinely needed). Scoped the real diff (22 commits, 50 files, +1814/‚àí577; merge-base after PR #52/S117) and wrote a comprehensive, accurate PR body (2.0.0 highlights from NEWS, CRAN-status note, verification, ‚Äúmerge timing is your call‚Äù). https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/pull/54

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî no runtime behavior changed (no R//startup/dispatch/data/config path touched). The proportionate doc build-equivalents all passed: devtools::build_readme() rendered README cleanly, rmarkdown::render(NEWS.Rmd, github_document) rendered NEWS cleanly, spell_check_package() ‚Üí 0 flags, and each generated-file git diff was confined to the intended change. No full R CMD check re-run ‚Äî out of scope: the R//data//tests//man//DESCRIPTION tree is unchanged since S138‚Äôs Status: OK gate; the only shipped change is NEWS.md prose (the check does not validate NEWS content beyond presence). Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24).

Session 138 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 139): Score 8/10. S138‚Äôs handoff was a strong executor‚Äôs map: it listed the exact punch-list as a SUGGESTED-NEXT menu (the four items I cleared), kept the standing-keep gotchas (.DS_Store, PED_GV_AUDIT) accurate, and correctly said the names' fix should be ‚Äúfix the quote, don‚Äôt whitelist.‚Äù Its firsthand facts held (versions at 2.0.0, branch sync, the README de-dup carried as FM #8). ROI clearly positive ‚Äî I executed straight off the menu. The ‚àí2: (a) the names' item pointed at NEWS.md:51 and called it a ‚Äúcurly apostrophe in a quoted base-R error‚Äù ‚Äî accurate symptom, but it did not flag that this is a render artifact (NEWS.Rmd already has straight quotes), so ‚Äúfix the quote‚Äù could mislead a literal reader toward a source quote-swap that doesn‚Äôt exist, or toward editing the generated NEWS.md directly; I had to diagnose the smart-quote root cause myself (now Learning 132). (b) ‚ÄúClose #53‚Äù rode the menu as an open item, but the owner had already closed it before this session ‚Äî a world-changed staleness (not S138‚Äôs fault, but the kind of thing a ‚Äúverify state before acting‚Äù note guards against ‚Äî cf.¬ÝLearning 129). (c) No note that README.md/NEWS.md are generated and the README dup spans a shared child ‚Äî I mapped that firsthand. A one-line ‚Äúboth are generated; README dup = shared _introduction.Rmd + README.Rmd‚Äôs own block‚Äù would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 139): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top via offset reads, dashboard 98/100, git + gh, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD db100064=S138, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs direction; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) diagnosed the names' flag as a pandoc smart-quote render artifact rather than chasing a non-existent source typo or hand-editing the generated NEWS.md ‚Äî and fixed it the durable way (code span at source), verifying the re-render diff was one line + spell check 0; (2) mapped the README duplication before cutting and removed the LOW-blast-radius copy (README.Rmd‚Äôs own block, not the shared child) ‚Äî manual/tutorial vignettes untouched; (3) verified every claim firsthand (versions, 0/0 branch sync, #53 already closed, the real PR diff scope) instead of trusting the handoff; (4) caught that #53 was already closed ‚Üí did not redundantly act; (5) opened the PR but did NOT merge ‚Äî respected the outward-facing/owner decision (merge timing + CRAN gating), with an accurate, comprehensive body that scopes the real diff and doesn‚Äôt overclaim; (6) confined every commit (excluded the standing keeps), captured Learning 132; ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) batched a 6-item menu in one session ‚Äî a deliberate, owner-authorized override of ‚Äú1 and done‚Äù, but worth naming as an exception, not a precedent; (b) low-to-moderate difficulty ‚Äî docs/admin + two re-renders; the value is the render-artifact diagnosis + verification discipline, not depth; (c) no full R CMD check this session (proportionate ‚Äî tree unchanged since S138‚Äôs OK gate, doc build-equivalents passed ‚Äî but named, not assumed); (d) the README diff carries an incidental version-date refresh beyond the pure de-dup (correct, since the README was genuinely regenerated today, but it‚Äôs a second change in the same file). I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty, single-deliverable session.

(3) README.md AND NEWS.md are GENERATED ‚Äî README.md from README.Rmd (via devtools::build_readme()), NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd (via rmarkdown::render(..., github_document, html_preview=FALSE)). Never hand-edit the .md ‚Äî edit the .Rmd source and re-render (plan gotcha #3; Learning 132). The vignettes/manual_components/_*.Rmd children are SHARED with a3manual.Rmd + ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd ‚Äî editing a child changes those vignettes too; to de-dup README-only, remove README.Rmd‚Äôs own block, not the child. (4) A spell/lint flag on a generated file can be a pandoc smart-quote artifact ‚Äî the source may already be ‚Äúcorrect‚Äù; wrap the literal in a code span rather than disabling smart (Learning 132). (5) README.md version-date is now 2026-06-19 (re-render refreshed getVersion()) ‚Äî expected, not a defect. (6) spell_check_package() is now 0 flags. (7) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call; never commit. (8) Branch: both S139 commits are pushed to origin/add-methodology; per Learning 129, git fetch + git rev-list --left-right --count origin/add-methodology...HEAD before acting on any push/merge prerequisite. (9) Carried CRAN gotchas: package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29 (Learning 95); the 2 false-positive --as-cran NOTEs (incoming-feasibility CRAN-persistent; HTML-manual local-only).

What Session 138 Did

Deliverable: owner pick (option 2 ‚Äî the real fix for issue #53) ‚Äî rename the malformed sire column from si.re (period) / si re (space) to sire.id across the 6 example pedigree datasets + sweep dependent source/docs/tests, and remove the now-unneeded si inst/WORDLIST whitelist (S137‚Äôs tentative resolution, superseded). (DONE.) Full strict TDD RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR‚ÜíVERIFY with a phase-gate AskUserQuestion at every transition. Target name sire.id (owner-chosen): a period in a column name is a realistic ‚Äúinexperienced data provider‚Äù messy header, so the fix KEEPS a period-bearing header under test (rather than removing periods) ‚Äî but in a sensible place that tokenizes to real words (sire+id) so it no longer spell-flags. Verified: fixColumnNames("sire.id") ‚Üí sire, exercising MORE normalization than si.re (periodRemoved + sireIdToSire vs periodRemoved only). Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. Strict TDD (declared every response). Gated AskUserQuestions (4): PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR (all ‚Äúfull scope‚Äù), plus the pre-RED owner confirmation of the target name. 0 stakeholder corrections. Builds directly on S137‚Äôs audit (issue #53) ‚Äî this is the deferred real fix the owner chose to do now instead of S137‚Äôs whitelist. New session within the conversation (claimed it, 1B stub BEFORE technical work). - RED: new tests/testthat/test_exampleData_columnNames.R ‚Äî (1) data contract pinning all 6 datasets‚Äô exact names to include sire.id; (2) malformed-name-absent check; (3) a normalization-invariant guard (fixColumnNames ‚Üí canonical). Confirmed RED: tests 1+2 = 12 assertions FAIL (names held si.re/si re), guard = 6 PASS, 0 errors. - GREEN (minimum to pass): regenerated the 6 .RData ‚Äî load ‚Üí rename the si.re/si re column to sire.id ‚Üí save(compress="gzip"), with a stopifnot(identical(new, old)) proving values byte-identical (only the name moved); nrow preserved. Re-ran ‚Üí 18/18 green, 0 error. (createPedOne.R deliberately left for REFACTOR ‚Äî the test reads the shipped .RData, not the generator.) - REFACTOR (behavior-neutral): R/createPedOne.R:17 `si re` ‚Üí sire.id; si.re ‚Üí sire.id in the 5 R/data.R roxygen blocks (replace_all, exactly 5 hits) ‚Üí roxygen2::roxygenise() regenerated exactly the 5 man/*.Rd (NAMESPACE untouched, si.re gone from man/); removed the si line from inst/WORDLIST; updated the website-only vignette prose vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91. - VERIFY: full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (866 blocks incl.¬Ýapp/e2e). One necessitated test fix surfaced by the suite (NOT by the pre-flight grep): test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R:23 asserted the summary change-log has 9 newlines; sire.id adds the sireIdToSire line ‚Üí 10 (9L‚Üí10L, with a comment). Verified the new output is correct (the extra line is a real normalization step), then re-ran ‚Üí 0/0. R CMD check (--no-manual --ignore-vignettes) ‚Üí Status: OK (0/0/0). spell_check_package() ‚Üí only the unrelated names‚Äô (NEWS.md:51). Installed the dev package + ran the vignette‚Äôs qcStudbook(pedGood, reportChanges=TRUE) chunk ‚Üí correct richer change-log (periodRemoved: "sire.id to sireid" + sireIdToSire: "sireid to sire"). ‚Üí Learning 131.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED. This changes runtime-consumed data, so I exercised the real path: R CMD INSTALL the dev package with the regenerated data (loads OK), then ran qcStudbook() on the new pedGood (the vignette‚Äôs actual chunk) ‚Üí correct output; plus the full testthat suite (which runs qcStudbook on every renamed dataset) is 0/0, and R CMD check is Status: OK. The quarto freeze cache is git-ignored (.gitignore:40) ‚Äî the vignette‚Äôs executed output regenerates at the next site build, so there is no committed artifact to refresh. Build artifacts (nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz, .Rcheck/ under /tmp) removed.

Session 137 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 138): Score 9/10. S137 (my immediately-prior session, same conversation) set this up well: issue #53 carried the full blast-radius inventory (the 6 datasets, the R/data.R/man/ doc sites, the website-only vignette flagged .Rbuildignore‚Äôd, the verify-only tests) and the target-name framing that the owner then resolved ‚Äî so this fix executed straight off #53‚Äôs map with no re-discovery. The firsthand facts all held (fixColumnNames mapping, canonical schema, the 6th pedOne dataset, the three-convention picture). The ‚àí1: #53‚Äôs blast radius listed the tests as ‚Äúverify-only, auto-follow / likely no edit‚Äù ‚Äî accurate for the literal-name dependents it found, but it did not flag test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R‚Äôs output-shape assertion (the newline count), which DID need an edit. A note that ‚Äúoutput-shape/count assertions over these datasets may shift ‚Äî run the full suite, don‚Äôt trust the name-grep‚Äù would have made it a 10 (now captured as Learning 131).

Self-assessment (Session 138): 9/10. Headline strengths: (1) clean strict-TDD execution ‚Äî RED proven failing for the right reason, GREEN minimal (data only) with a byte-identical-values proof, REFACTOR behavior-neutral, all four phase gates posed as AskUserQuestion; (2) honored the owner‚Äôs design insight ‚Äî sire.id keeps the realistic period case under test (and actually exercises more normalization than si.re), rather than my earlier sire_id which would have lost period coverage; (3) values provably preserved (identical() check in the regen, not just trusted); (4) VERIFY caught the output-shape test the pre-flight grep missed ‚Äî and I verified the new value was correct before bumping it, not just made it pass; (5) real build-equivalent (R CMD check Status: OK, not just the test read) + runtime smoke (install + run the vignette chunk); (6) confined the diff, regenerated man/ cleanly, removed the now-dead whitelist, left the unrelated names‚Äô flag alone; (7) captured Learning 131. Weaknesses (honest): (a) my pre-flight breakage scan under-predicted ‚Äî it said ‚Äúno test breaks‚Äù but one did (output-shape); caught in VERIFY (no shipped defect), but a cleaner first pass would have run the suite before claiming no breakage (the lesson of 131); (b) did not re-render the quarto vignette ‚Äî correct call (the freeze cache is git-ignored and auto-regenerates; re-rendering produces no committed change), but it means the local freeze cache is momentarily stale until the next site build; (c) moderate, not high, difficulty ‚Äî a careful rename + sweep. I reserve 10 for a first pass whose own breakage prediction is complete.

(DONE.) (2) Ran a 4-reader ‚Üí synthesize ‚Üí adversarial-verify workflow (wscue59am) and cross-checked the adversary‚Äôs own corrections firsthand (it cited R/fixColumnNames.R:16‚Äôs @example as space-form evidence; the example actually uses Sire_ID ‚Äî the opposite). (3) Filed issue #53 (bug+low priority): https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/issues/53 ‚Äî affected-dataset table, the ‚Äúnot-broken‚Äù evidence, full blast-radius, and the target-name decision framed for the owner (the evidence genuinely splits 3 ways, so I did not assert one). (4) Owner chose the least-disruptive resolution ‚Üí added si to inst/WORDLIST; spell check went 2 flags ‚Üí 1 (si cleared; the unrelated names‚Äô curly-apostrophe at NEWS.md:51 left untouched). Scoped to inst/WORDLIST (+1 line) + CHANGELOG/learnings/notes; no R//man//NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/tests//data//NEWS change. Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. AUDIT/VERIFICATION phase (declared every response; no RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR ‚Äî the only shipped-content change is the gate-invariant inst/WORDLIST, consumed only by the error=FALSE+skip_on_cran tests/spelling.R; R CMD check never reads it, so it is the same class S134 made without a TDD cycle). No gated AskUserQuestion (no RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR transition occurred). 0 stakeholder corrections ‚Äî the owner‚Äôs WORDLIST direction was a scope decision, not a correction of an error; I surfaced the honest trade-off before executing. Right-sized fan-out under ultracode: the ‚Äúis it a bug / what‚Äôs the right name‚Äù question genuinely benefited from independent perspectives + an adversarial pass (and the adversary caught a real synthesis overreach), but the highest-value step was cross-checking the adversary firsthand ‚Äî neither agent‚Äôs single ‚Äúcorrect name‚Äù survived the source read. Reclaimed the S137 number from an abandoned same-numbered owner-run-Phase5 stub (CRAN is owner-blocked; that stub had no committed work). - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî VERIFIED THE DEFECT FIRSTHAND + CORRECTED THE INHERITED COUNT: a names() scan of every data/*.RData found the malformed sire column in 6 datasets, not 5 ‚Äî the five si.re (period) datasets plus pedOne‚Äôs si re (space, generated by R/createPedOne.R:17). Grep confirmed the malformed token lives ONLY in R/data.R roxygen + generated man/*.Rd + one website-only vignette ‚Äî nowhere in R/ logic or tests/. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî PROVED IT IS NOT FUNCTIONALLY BROKEN (empirically): pkgload::load_all + fixColumnNames() ‚Üí si.re, si re, sire_id, Sire_ID, Sire Id, sire all normalize to sire; ego_id,si.re,dam_id,sex,birth_date ‚Üí id,sire,dam,sex,birth. So qcStudbook() (R/qcStudbook.R:174) accepts every form; canonical schema is c("id","sire","dam","sex","birth") (R/getRequiredCols.R:24). The defect is internal inconsistency, NOT a break ‚Äî not a CRAN blocker. .Rbuildignore:60 (^vignettes/articles$) confirms the studbook-quality-control.qmd reference is website-only, not check-gated. - üö® REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî CROSS-CHECKED THE ADVERSARIAL PASS (‚Üí Learning 130): the synthesis recommended target sire_id; the adversary correctly struck the synthesis‚Äôs false ‚Äúperiod-header contradicts the value-level period rule‚Äù argument (headers/values are separate code paths) BUT argued the target should be the space form si re, citing the fixColumnNames @example ‚Äî which I read firsthand and found uses Sire_ID (underscore), undercutting the adversary‚Äôs own evidence. The repo ships three messy-sire conventions (si re space in createPedOne+~8 local fixtures; Sire_ID underscore in the @example; si.re period in the 5 .RData) and the period form matches none. So the issue frames the choice (si re / sire_id / sire, with trade-offs) for the owner rather than asserting one. - üö® REAL WORK #4 ‚Äî OWNER‚ÄôS LEAST-DISRUPTIVE RESOLUTION, EXECUTED WITH BEFORE/AFTER VERIFICATION: spelling::spell_check_package() before = 2 flags (si in 5 man/*.Rd; names‚Äô in NEWS.md:51). Added si to inst/WORDLIST in correct C-locale position (between sexRatioWithAddions and simParent). After = si cleared; only the unrelated names‚Äô remains ‚Äî left untouched (separate item; its real fix is the NEWS curly-quote, not a whitelist). This silences the symptom; the malformed names stay in the shipped data, now tracked by #53 (low priority). Deliberately reverses S134‚Äôs ‚Äúleave si flagged to keep the defect visible‚Äù ‚Äî correct now that #53 captures it. Surfaced the trade-off to the owner before acting (silences vs fixes; reverses S134‚Äôs choice; minor: whitelisting a short token weakens future typo-catching).

Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). No R CMD check re-run ‚Äî out of scope; the WORDLIST change is gate-invariant and the code tree is unchanged since the S134 gate.

Self-assessment (Session 137): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top via offset reads, dashboard 98/100, git + fetch/rev-list, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí last committed HEAD 2397506a=S136 + flagged the abandoned S137 stub), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Weaknesses (honest): (a) low technical difficulty ‚Äî an audit + a one-line WORDLIST add; the value is verification discipline, not depth; (b) the deliverable shifted mid-session (issue ‚Üí issue + WORDLIST) ‚Äî fine, but it means the issue‚Äôs ‚Äúfix is a separate session‚Äù framing is now partly moot (the owner chose to defer the big fix); (c) I could not record the decision on #53 itself ‚Äî the auto-permission classifier (correctly) denied an unrequested public issue comment, so #53‚Äôs own thread doesn‚Äôt yet note the resolution (owner can comment/close). I reserve 10 for a higher-difficulty, end-to-end-completable deliverable.

Session 135 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 136): Score 9/10. S135‚Äôs handoff was an executor‚Äôs dream: it named Phase5-finish as the next step, wrote a complete runbook with the exact win-builder/R-hub commands + the submission HARD STOP, kept cran-comments.md CRAN-facing-only, and ‚Äî notably ‚Äî had itself corrected the long-standing false ‚ÄúPR #53‚Äù assumption with firsthand gh/git checks (Learning 128). Every fact I re-verified held: Version 2.0.0, the 0/0/2 gate, the maintainer address, empty revdeps, the ‚Äútree unchanged since the gate‚Äù implication. Gotchas were accurate and load-bearing (the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keeps; ‚ÄúPhase 4 COMPLETE ‚Äî don‚Äôt re-run‚Äù; the 2 NOTEs; si.re defect; tooling absent/installable). ROI very high; no false credit; ghost-check anchor exact (24175785=S135). The ‚àí1: the handoff and the runbook stated ‚Äúorigin is 2 commits behind ‚Äî push add-methodology first‚Äù as a standing instruction with no ‚Äúre-fetch to confirm before acting ‚Äî the remote may have moved‚Äù caveat. It was true when S135 verified it, but the branch was pushed before I ran, so the instruction had silently gone stale ‚Äî exactly the durability gap Learning 129 now closes. A one-line ‚Äúverify with git fetch + rev-list at the moment you depend on this‚Äù would have made it a 10. (Not a fault of S135‚Äôs diligence ‚Äî the world changed after the handoff ‚Äî but the handoff‚Äôs job is to arm the successor against precisely that.)

Self-assessment (Session 136): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top via offset reads since the file is large, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD 24175785=S135, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) caught the branch-state contradiction and chased it to ground with fetch+rev-list instead of trusting either git status or the handoff ‚Äî and the answer (prerequisite already done) materially simplifies the owner‚Äôs remaining path; (2) surfaced the outward-facing scope fork to the owner (didn‚Äôt unilaterally trigger external submissions, didn‚Äôt silently narrow) ‚Äî the SAFEGUARDS-correct move; (3) re-verified every external-doc fact firsthand, including the easily-skipped ‚Äúnothing changed since the gate‚Äù claim via git diff --name-only; (4) ran the real build-equivalent (R CMD build) rather than asserting the tarball builds; (5) marked the superseded caveat explicitly with the retiring command+result (auditable) rather than deleting it silently; (6) captured Learning 129 ‚Äî the time-axis sibling of Learning 128; (7) kept scope tight (no cran-comments.md change because it was already correct; no code touched), removed the build artifact, ASCII-only options, plain language, 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low technical difficulty ‚Äî verification + doc prep; the value is discipline (the stale-prerequisite catch, the firsthand re-verification), not depth; (b) the deliverable is inherently a half-step ‚Äî ‚ÄúPhase5-finish‚Äù cannot be finished by me (the PAT, the email inbox, and submit_cran() are the owner‚Äôs), so this session delivers prep, not the finish ‚Äî correct division, modest output; (c) build-not-full-check ‚Äî I verified assembly via R CMD build, not a fresh --as-cran (the right call given Phase 4 is done + tree unchanged, but worth naming). I reserve 10 for a session whose deliverable is itself end-to-end completable rather than gated on owner secrets.

Deliverable: owner‚Äôs pick (A) ‚Äî CRAN 2.0.0 submission plan Phase 4 (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md ¬ß4) ‚Äî the full local R CMD check --as-cran gate. (DONE.) Ran a true gate (4 missing Suggests installed ‚Üí no _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false): Status: 2 NOTEs = 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING, both NOTEs false-positive. Pre-gate hygiene all clean (roxygenise zero-diff, URL 17/17, regression 0/0). Reconciled inst/WORDLIST (+35 legit terms; owner-approved). Surfaced a real data defect (si.re column in 5 of 6 example pedigrees) ‚Äî left out of scope. Scoped to inst/WORDLIST + plan/CHANGELOG/learnings docs; no R//man//NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/tests//data//NEWS change. Started / Completed: 2026-06-18 Status: DONE. VERIFICATION phase (Phase 4 classified N/A for new code; declared at the top of every response). Gated AskUserQuestion: one ‚Äî the inst/WORDLIST reconciliation (an author‚Äôs-scope decision over shipped content that diverged from the plan‚Äôs ‚Äúnearly clean‚Äù expectation), owner chose Reconcile now. No RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR transition (no code/behavior change; the spell test is error=FALSE+skip_on_cran). 1 transparent scope refinement (excluded si after tracing it to the si.re defect ‚Äî more conservative than the literal approval, flagged to the owner). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode ‚Äî the ground-truth verifier is CRAN‚Äôs own R CMD check; this is a sequential gate, not a fan-out, so parallel agents add no confidence (they‚Äôd only re-read one deterministic log). File-based R scripts throughout (no shell-backslash misfire); ASCII-only question options; plain language. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî RAN A TRUE GATE, NOT A FORCED CHECK (‚Üí Learning 127): installed the 4 Suggests S133‚Äôs handoff named (covr, shinytest2, shinyWidgets, spelling) + urlchecker into the renv library, so R CMD check --as-cran ran with no _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false. First confirmed it was safe: installing the package shinytest2 does not install Chrome (chromote fetches lazily) and all e2e/app tests skip_on_cran (verified ‚Äî only 2 unguarded files, both deliberately browser-free fake-stub unit tests), so the check never launches a browser. Result Status: 2 NOTEs = 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî VERIFIED EACH SPELL FLAG BEFORE WORDLIST‚ÄôING; CAUGHT A DATA DEFECT (‚Üí Learning 127): the plan said ‚Äúconfirm only EHR/Raboin/kinships remain‚Äù; reality was 37 flagged words (dictionary drifted ~35 legit identifiers behind S100‚ÄìS133). Did NOT run spelling::update_wordlist() (which adds all flagged) ‚Äî read each word‚Äôs source first. That isolated names' (a curly-quote tokenizer artifact of the quoted base-R error 'row.names' in NEWS) and, crucially, traced si to a column literally named si.re in 5 of the 6 example pedigrees (the 6th, pedInvalidDates, uses sire). Added the 35 clean terms by hand (C-locale/radix sort ‚Üí pure +35 insertions, 0 reorderings, 310‚Üí345 lines); left names' and si flagged on purpose (adding si would mask the defect). - üö® NEW FINDING (out of scope ‚Äî flagged for its own session): si.re is almost certainly a data defect (should be sire/sire_id to pair with ego_id/dam_id). Not a CRAN blocker (odd column names are legal). Candidate GitHub issue + RED-first fix session: regenerate the 5 .rda datasets with the corrected column + sweep dependent code/tests/docs. Owner has NOT yet been asked to file an issue ‚Äî offer it next. - Pre-gate hygiene (captured evidence): roxygenise() ‚Üí zero diff (man/+NAMESPACE in sync); urlchecker::url_check() ‚Üí 17/17 correct; clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (863 rows; 167 skipped; 5 warnings, all in one passing test test_modPyramid.R ‚ÄúmodPyramidServer handles input changes‚Äù ‚Äî pre-existing baseline, modPyramid uses none of the installed Suggests, below the failed+error gate threshold and not surfaced by R CMD check). - The 2 NOTEs (both false-positive): (1) CRAN incoming feasibility ‚Äî ‚ÄúNew submission / Package was archived on CRAN‚Äù ‚Äî expected, CRAN-persistent, pre-explain in Phase-5 cran-comments.md; (2) ‚Äúchecking HTML version of manual‚Äù ‚Äî 'tidy' not recent enough + package 'V8' unavailable ‚Üí both sub-checks skipped ‚Äî a local-toolchain NOTE, CRAN-absent (CRAN has recent HTML Tidy + V8). Verified twice (initial build + a rebuild after the WORDLIST change ‚Äî identical; WORDLIST is gate-invariant, consumed only by the skip_on_cran spell test).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED by the --as-cran run. The check installed the package and ran the full runtime ‚Äî examples, the entire testthat suite, the vignette rebuild ‚Äî all green. The only shipped-content change (inst/WORDLIST) alters no runtime behavior and is gate-invariant. Build artifacts (nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz, nprcgenekeepr.Rcheck/) removed before commit; not gitignored (tiny optional follow-up ‚Äî see gotcha 5).

Archival reason confirmed firsthand in the CRAN db override: ‚ÄúArchived on 2025-07-29 as issues were not corrected in time. / Tested elapsed times.‚Äù This is the dragon-#1 ‚Äúmeasure first, let the profile name the offender‚Äù outcome ‚Äî surfaced the changed premise to the owner rather than inventing a timing fix. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî DISPROVED THE INHERITED ‚ÄúPHASE 2 BLOCKED‚Äù PREMISE (‚Üí Learning 126): S131/S132 handoffs (and plan ¬ß3.4) said Phase 2 was blocked here ‚Äî ‚Äúrenv NOT materialized / devtools absent.‚Äù A 20-line env probe disproved it: R 4.6.0 with the renv library already materialized (.libPaths() ‚Üí renv/library/nprcgenekeepr-229b003c/...), all 18 Imports present, pandoc present ‚Äî and base-R R CMD build/R CMD check --as-cran need no devtools. Installed one missing build-only Suggest, markdown (+ litedown), into the renv library so a3manual.Rmd builds. Phase 2 ‚Äî and the local Phase-4 check ‚Äî ARE runnable here. - üö® REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî FOUND + FIXED THE REAL CRAN-BLOCKER (strict TDD): the --as-cran profile reported Status: 1 WARNING ‚Äî checking for unstated dependencies in 'tests' ... '::' import not declared from: 'withr'. tests/testthat/test_loadSiteConfig.R uses withr::local_tempdir()/local_envvar() (6 uses); withr was in neither Imports nor Suggests. Added withr to Suggests (alphabetical, after testthat). CRAN rejects on any WARNING ‚Äî this was real. - üö® REAL WORK #4 ‚Äî ENCODED THE PROBE WITH R‚Äôs PARSER, NOT A REGEX (‚Üí Learning 126): the RED probe had to mirror R CMD check‚Äôs ‚Äúunstated test deps‚Äù judgment. A first-draft text regex false-flagged devtools (only in #' comments) and shinytest2.R (a filename in a comment). Rewrote it to use getParseData(parse(f)) SYMBOL_PACKAGE tokens ‚Äî comment/string/filename-immune, exactly what the parser resolves pkg:: to ‚Äî yielding precisely the one real offender (withr), matching the check. - RED: _s133_red.R (file-based; parser-based) ‚Äî 2 assertions (withr declared; no undeclared pkg:: in tests/) ‚Üí 2 failures naming only withr. GREEN: one Edit (DESCRIPTION Suggests += withr) ‚Üí probe 0 (both PASS); rebuilt tarball + re-ran R CMD check --as-cran (_R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false) ‚Üí Status: 2 NOTEs = 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING (withr WARNING gone, timing still clean); full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (699 test blocks excl. test-app-/test-e2e-; 0/0 across all 863). REFACTOR: N/A. No permanent testthat guard (owner chose the temp-probe path; matches the S131/S132 metadata-fix class). - Used file-based R probes/scripts from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109/121); no misfires. Single-quoted grep throughout. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. The withr-in-Suggests change is a CRAN-hygiene metadata fix, not a user-facing behavior change ‚Üí no NEWS line warranted (NEWS untouched). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]); the relevant standing memory ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]) already exists and its sibling-lesson is now Learning 126. [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the 5 repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); removed transients _s133_red.R/_s133_check3.sh/_s133_check3.log/_s133_suite.R + the build artifacts (nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz, nprcgenekeepr.Rcheck/); no Rplots.pdf.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED by the --as-cran run, then N/A for the change itself. The change is one metadata line (no runtime behavior altered), but the verification was the strongest available: R CMD check --as-cran installed the package and ran the full runtime ‚Äî 136 examples, the entire testthat suite, and the vignette rebuild ‚Äî all green. Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). The proportionate build-equivalent = the RED probe (green) + --as-cran 0 ERROR/0 WARNING + the full clean-regression read (0/0) + a git diff confined to the intended files. A complete Phase-4-grade check (with covr/shinytest2/shinyWidgets/spelling installed, no _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_) was NOT run ‚Äî see gotcha (2).

S132‚Äôs handoff was strong on process reflexes ‚Äî every one fired correctly this session: the shell-backslash‚Üífile-probe trap (all scripts file-based, no misfire), the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call (honored at commit), the branch state / PR #53 note, the news-vs-changelog policy, and the process-history reflex ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]). The ‚àí2: the handoff‚Äôs central environment claim about Phase 2 ‚Äî ‚Äúthis may be blocked here ‚Ä¶ Needs renv::restore() + devtools (absent in this env)‚Äù (and gotcha (6) ‚Äúfull R CMD check/--as-cran (Phase 2/4) NOT runnable here‚Äù) ‚Äî was stale and wrong. The renv library is in fact materialized, and base-R R CMD build/check --as-cran need no devtools, so Phase 2 was fully runnable here. The inaccuracy originated in the plan‚Äôs ¬ß3.4 ‚Äúrenv NOT materialized (Learning 92)‚Äù baseline and rode the S102‚ÜíS131‚ÜíS132 handoff chain unverified ‚Äî the same ‚Äúinherited claim treated as fact‚Äù pattern S132 itself flagged about S131 (and exactly what its own Learning 125 warns against, here applied to a phase‚Äôs premise rather than its status). A quick env probe would have caught it. Captured as Learning 126.

Self-assessment (Session 133): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD aff73b19=S132, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) measured before fixing ‚Äî ran the gold-standard --as-cran --timings rather than trusting the plan‚Äôs ‚Äútiming‚Äù hypothesis, and found it does not reproduce (dragon #1 honored); (2) verified the inherited ‚Äúblocked‚Äù premise firsthand and disproved it (env probe) ‚Äî the single most valuable correction this session, and it unblocks Phase 4 too; (3) honored the STOP-and-re-scope boundary ‚Äî surfaced the changed premise to the owner with evidence + a grounded 3-option re-scope question instead of inventing a timing fix OR silently narrowing; (4) found + fixed the real CRAN-blocker (undeclared withr) under full strict TDD with all gates; (5) encoded the probe with R‚Äôs parser after the regex draft false-flagged comments ‚Äî invariant matches the tool‚Äôs own mechanism (Learning 126); (6) verified three independent ways (probe green, --as-cran 0/0, regression 0/0); (7) confined the change to one line, left the pre-existing shinytest2 mis-ordering alone (FM #8); (8) ran profiling serially on purpose (honest timing) ‚Äî the rare correct SOLO under ultracode; (9) file-based scripts (no shell-backslash misfire), ASCII-only options, plain language, removed all transients, reconciled the plan/¬ß3.4 stale baseline; 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) very low technical difficulty ‚Äî a one-line fix; the value is the discipline, not depth; (b) I installed markdown into the renv library without asking ‚Äî judged routine build infrastructure (and noted it), but it is an env change a stricter reading would have gated; (c) the first RED probe draft used a regex that false-flagged comments ‚Äî caught and fixed within RED (no shipped defect), but a cleaner first pass would have parsed immediately; (d) the --as-cran check ran with _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false (4 Suggests absent) ‚Äî the 0/0 is real but not a complete Phase-4 gate; I reserve 10 for a delivery that is full-Suggests --as-cran-clean.

Read firsthand: the CRAN plan (¬ß3.1/¬ß3.2/¬ß3.3 + Phase 1 + ¬ß9), DESCRIPTION, .Rbuildignore, man/appServer.Rd/man/appUI.Rd, LICENSE/LICENSE.md, CITATION.cff, README.Rmd (+ render mechanism), _introduction.Rmd, CHANGELOG S102 entry, git log on the Phase-1 files. Gotchas: (1) CRAN Phase 1 is DONE (S102 + S132) ‚Äî do NOT re-flag the .Rbuildignore cruft, DESCRIPTION typo, \value docs, VignetteBuilder, Config order, or LICENSE year as open; all verified in place. The plan + CHANGELOG now say so. (2) The species typo is gone from all TRACKED content ‚Äî but the untracked, git-ignored vignettes/a3manual.html/a3manual.md still carry the old mulatto (stale local renders); they are NOT committed and NOT in a clean CRAN tarball ‚Äî they regenerate clean on the next vignette build. Do not ‚Äúfix‚Äù them by hand; do not commit them. (3) README.md is generated from README.Rmd which child-includes vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd (README.Rmd:32) ‚Äî NEVER hand-edit README.md; edit the sources + re-render with pkgload::load_all + rmarkdown::render(output_format = github_document(html_preview = FALSE)) (avoids README.html litter). The reference block renders TWICE (direct + child) ‚Äî see option (D). (4) CITATION.cff is build-ignored (.Rbuildignore:67) so it does NOT ship ‚Äî its abstract mirrors DESCRIPTION and would be regenerated by cffr::cff_write() (cffr ABSENT ‚Üí hand-edited here, as S131 did for version/date). (5) Optional DOI (DESCRIPTION:20 PMC <https:>) is the ONLY remaining Phase-1 item; plan marks it low / <https:> acceptable ‚Äî leave unless a future session converts it. (6) Tooling gaps: devtools/usethis/cffr ABSENT; rmarkdown/knitr/roxygen2/pkgdown/pkgload/quarto PRESENT ‚Äî full R CMD check/--as-cran (Phase 2/4) NOT runnable here; run those where devtools is available. (7) Shell backslash trap (recurring) ‚Äî file-based R probes; single-quoted grep -E (Learnings 108/109/121). (8) Process-history reflex ‚Äî before resuming any plan phase, git log/grep CHANGELOG to confirm it isn‚Äôt already done (Learning 125; memory [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]). (9) Standing uncommitted keeps: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (10) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 13 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS132) and carries 15 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS132) ‚Äî rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (11) Carried CRAN gotchas: the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; Phase 2 (archival timing) is the here-be-dragons critical path.

The honest RED surface is the invariant the change must establish: _s131_red.R parsed DESCRIPTION (read.dcf), CITATION.cff (version: line), and README.md (rendered version line) and asserted all three == 2.0.0, the stale 1.1.0.9000 / 1.0.7 are gone, and CITATION has a date-released ‚Äî 7 assertions, 7 failures on the current tree, 0 after the edits + re-render. (Metadata-bump cousin of RED-binding to a rendered NEWS.md / man/.Rd.) - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî VERIFIED THE PLAN‚ÄôS ¬ß3.1 INVENTORY AGAINST THE LIVE TREE before editing: a git grep confirmed exactly three bumpable hits (DESCRIPTION:4, README.md:8, CITATION.cff:11) and nothing hidden in R//man//tests//data-raw//inst/ (no inst/CITATION). README.Rmd:10 + the appUI About panel are on the ¬ß3.1 ‚ÄúNO edit ‚Äî auto-tracks getVersion()‚Äù list and were correctly left. - üö® REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî LITTER-FREE RENDER + CONFINED DIFF: re-rendered via rmarkdown::render(output_format = github_document(html_preview = FALSE)) (suppresses the NEWS.html-style preview litter at the source, improving on Learning 123‚Äôs clean-up-after). A retitle is best-placed to catch its own dangling pointers; that they‚Äôre .Rbuildignored dev docs makes it minor, not a gap. (Now captured as Learning 124‚Äôs ‚Äúa version retitle leaves dangling pointers‚Äù reflex.)

Oriented fully (read the plan‚Äôs Phase 3 + ¬ß6 spec, the current doubled NEWS, the S112‚ÄìS129 CHANGELOG, tooling, version-test surface), reported the scope honestly, gated the split decision; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) read the implementations before scoping (Learning 6) ‚Äî found the doubled section (dragon #4), the tooling gaps, and the version-test dynamism before proposing scope; (2) deterministic splice rather than a fragile 178-line Edit (Learning 123); (3) bound RED to the rendered NEWS.md so the probe also proves the re-render ran; (4) folded the delta correctly, classifying ship-vs-internal per each handoff‚Äôs news-vs-changelog note and collapsing 12 doc-fix sessions into one omnibus bullet; (5) adversarially verified the user-facing artifact (3 lenses: completeness vs git show HEAD: + plan + CHANGELOG, accuracy vs NAMESPACE/R/, dedup/style) BEFORE tightening, then token-swept to prove the tighten dropped no fact; (6) surfaced the genuine classification fork (data-type Major vs Minor) to the owner rather than deciding silently; (7) confined the diff to the two NEWS files; cleaned the NEWS.html render litter; 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII question options. Weaknesses (honest): (a) my first draft was too verbose (the adversarial verify‚Äôs 6 style nits) ‚Äî I should have written to the ¬ß6.2 terse spec on the first pass rather than tightening in REFACTOR; (b) Phase 3 is incomplete by design (split) ‚Äî the version bump + README/CITATION + version tests remain (Phase 3b), so 2.0.0 is not yet self-consistent across files (NEWS says 2.0.0, DESCRIPTION still 1.1.0.9000 ‚Äî intended, both land before Phase 4); (c) full R CMD check not run (devtools absent). I reserve 10 for a delivery that lands the terse spec first-pass and is full-check-verified.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. Phase 3a is DONE ‚Äî NEWS is a clean terse 2.0.0 Major/Minor entry, re-rendered, adversarially verified. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (B-cont) CRAN Phase 3b ‚Äî bump DESCRIPTION:4 1.1.0.9000 ‚Üí 2.0.0; regenerate README.md (rmarkdown::render + load_all; devtools::build_readme absent); set CITATION.cff version (stale at 1.0.7) ‚Üí 2.0.0 (hand-edit; cffr absent); run test_getVersion.R + test_appUI_version.R (both dynamic ‚Üí green). Small/mechanical; finishes the version-consistency half of Phase 3. Maintainer judgment for the owner: promote the summarizeKinshipValues() secondQuartile change and/or the rhesus data-type change from Minor ‚Üí Major? - (B-then) CRAN Phase 2 ‚Äî the archival ROOT CAUSE (example/test/vignette timing under --as-cran); ‚öÝ here-be-dragons / critical path; profile first. Needs renv::restore() + devtools (absent in this env). - (C) Merge add-methodology ‚Üí master ‚Äî after this commit add-methodology is 11 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS130) and carries 13 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS130); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). - A GitHub issue ‚Äî #46 (species first-class), #45/#28/#9 (parent ID via conception date), #2 (GVA iteration-count guidance), #37 (unused exports). Do NOT bundle options together (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any of them without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): NEWS.Rmd (1.1.0.9000 section ‚Üí terse 2.0.0 Major/Minor); NEWS.md (re-rendered from NEWS.Rmd); CHANGELOG.md (S130 [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 123); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No DESCRIPTION, R/, man/, data/, test, README, or CITATION change. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s130_red.R, _s130_splice.R, _s130_resplice.R, _s130_new_section.md, NEWS.html (github_document preview litter). Workflow: s130-news-verify (3 lenses) ‚Üí result at the wfizblaz3 task output.

Gotchas: (1) Phase 3 is HALF DONE (3a only) ‚Äî NEWS is rewritten + at 2.0.0, but DESCRIPTION is still 1.1.0.9000. This NEWS-says-2.0.0 / DESCRIPTION-says-1.1.0.9000 mismatch is INTENTIONAL within the split; both must land before Phase 4. Phase 3b finishes it. (2) NEWS.md is generated from NEWS.Rmd ‚Äî never hand-edit NEWS.md; edit the .Rmd and rmarkdown::render(output_format="github_document") (pandoc 3.1.1 present). (3) rmarkdown::render(github_document) drops an untracked NEWS.html preview (Learning 123) ‚Äî clean it before commit (or render with html_preview=FALSE). (4) The 2.0.0 heading date (20260618) is provisional ‚Äî reconfirm at submission (Phase 5). (5) CITATION.cff version is stale at 1.0.7 (not even 1.1.0.9000) ‚Äî fix to 2.0.0 in Phase 3b (hand-edit; cffr absent). (6) Tooling gaps in this env: usethis, cffr, devtools ABSENT; rmarkdown/knitr/roxygen2/desc PRESENT ‚Äî Phase 3b uses rmarkdown/hand-edit substitutes for build_readme/use_version/cff_write. (7) Both version-dependent tests are dynamic (test_getVersion.R checks structure; test_appUI_version.R reads packageVersion() and asserts ‚Äú1.0.8‚Äù absent) ‚Äî the bump won‚Äôt break them; run them in 3b to confirm. (8) Maintainer-judgment items flagged for the owner: the secondQuartile change and the rhesus data-type change are under Minor ‚Äî owner may promote to Major. (9) Shell backslash trap (recurring) ‚Äî file-based R probes/splices; single-quoted grep -E (Learnings 108/109/121). (10) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (11) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 11 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS130) and carries 13 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS130) ‚Äî rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (12) Carried CRAN gotchas: renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run (devtools absent).

Deliverable: owner‚Äôs pick (A3) ‚Äî corrected the Studbook Quality Control vignette‚Äôs pedGood column description at vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:90-91 (carried from S116). (DONE ‚Äî reworded the lead-in from ‚ÄúThe shipped pedGood data set has deliberately messy headers (ego_id, si.re, dam_id, birth_date):‚Äù to ‚Äú‚Ä¶has five columns ‚Äì four with deliberately messy headers (ego_id, si.re, dam_id, birth_date) and an already-canonical sex that QC leaves untouched:‚Äù. A single 3-line prose edit.) Website-only article (vignettes/articles/ is .Rbuildignored) ‚Äî no R//man//NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/data//test/CRAN-vignette change. Started / Completed: 2026-06-18 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Two gated AskUserQuestions for the genuine fork + the mandatory transitions: (1) pre-RED approach ‚Äî rewrite the lead-in vs append a clause (owner chose rewrite the lead-in); (2) PRE-RED‚ÜíRED; (3) RED‚ÜíGREEN; (4) GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR declared N/A (prose content, structure preserved). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: SOLO ‚Äî a 3-line prose fix; the value is catching that the carried ‚Äúomits sex‚Äù framing was imprecise, binding the RED probe to the live object, and rendering to verify the prose now matches the chunk. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî THE CARRIED ONE-LINER WOULD HAVE PRODUCED A WRONG FIX (‚Üí Learning 122): the carried nit said ‚Äúomits the sex column,‚Äù which reads as ‚Äúadd sex to the parenthetical.‚Äù A probe on the live object refuted that literal fix: names(pedGood) = ego_id, si.re, dam_id, sex, birth_date, and the article‚Äôs own changes chunk renames exactly the FOUR non-sex columns ‚Äî sex is absent from changedCols because it already uses the canonical name. Adding sex to the ‚Äúdeliberately messy headers‚Äù list would have mislabeled it AND contradicted the chunk‚Äôs rendered output two lines below. The accurate fix conveys ‚Äúfive columns: four messy + an already-clean sex.‚Äù (S116‚Äôs richer framing ‚Äúthe dataset is 5 columns‚Äù was buried ~350 lines down in the notes; the compressed carried one-liner lost it.) - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî BOUND THE RED PROBE TO THE LIVE OBJECT + SOURCE .qmd (Learning 122): a vignette has no generated man/.Rd to bind to (unlike Learnings 119/120). The probe parses the lead-in paragraph from the .qmd and asserts 3 ground-truth preconditions (names(pedGood) has 5 cols incl.¬Ýsex; the 4 listed headers == pedGood‚Äôs non-sex columns) + 2 RED assertions (lead-in mentions sex; states ‚Äúfive‚Äù) ‚Üí 2 failures / 3 passing on current prose; 0 failures (all 5 pass) after the edit. Binding to names(pedGood) means the test fails if the data ever diverges from the prose. - üö® REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî RAN THE REAL BUILD-EQUIVALENT (render) AND CLEANED ITS LITTER: pkgdown::build_article("articles/studbook-quality-control") rendered clean; verified in the HTML that (a) the new ‚Äúhas five columns ‚Äì four with deliberately messy headers‚Äù sentence is present, and (b) changedCols still shows exactly the 4 renames with no sex to X (prose now matches what the chunk demonstrates). The render writes HTML to the gitignored pkgdown_site/ but LITTERED the tracked tree with pkgdown/ (favicons), vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.rmarkdown (Quarto intermediate), and an auto vignettes/articles/.gitignore ‚Äî snapshotted git status before, removed all three after, re-checked the tree is back to the intended set. - RED: _s129_red.R (parse the .qmd lead-in, 5 assertions) ‚Üí 2 failures on current prose. GREEN: the 3-line rewrite at lines 90-92; probe ‚Üí 0 failures; pkgdown render clean + content verified. REFACTOR: N/A (prose, structure preserved). No permanent testthat test added (matches the S120‚ÄìS127 doc-fix class; testthat does not execute this website-only article). - Used file-based R probes from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109/121); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. Website-only article ‚Äî does NOT ship to CRAN, so NO NEWS.md line warranted (unlike S123‚ÄìS125‚Äôs user-facing changes). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the two _s129_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit); cleaned the render litter (pkgdown/, *.rmarkdown, the auto .gitignore); pkgdown_site/ left in place (gitignored); no Rplots.pdf.

Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). The proportionate build-equivalent = the pkgdown render (SAFEGUARDS ‚ÄúVerify the Build Equivalent‚Äù for doc projects) + the live-object-bound source probe + a git diff confined to the one .qmd. Full testthat suite NOT run ‚Äî no R//test/data/ change, and testthat does not execute the website-only article. One identity check licenses deleting every block drawn from that object (both pedOne and pedDF). - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî THE HANDOFF‚ÄôS LINE-RANGES WERE A LEAD, NOT A CENSUS: a robust ERE sweep (grep -rEn) found a THIRD identical-class block the S127 handoff never named ‚Äî test_getPotentialParents.R:116-119 (pedDF, NEW-53 mutation test), same file as the named pedOne block. (2) Two look-alikes are NOT the class ‚Äî test_fillBins.R:22 (as.Date inside age arithmetic) and test_modGeneticValue.R:1274 (as.character inside expect_setequal) are coercions inside expressions, not redundant self-assignments ‚Äî leave them. (3) The no-op holds only because rhesusPedigree ships canonical types (character id/sire/dam, Date birth; S123/Learning 116). The fixtures now rely on the object‚Äôs types directly ‚Äî if those ever regress, the fixtures inherit the regression. (4) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) ‚Äî do NOT ‚Äúclean‚Äù them. (5) Shell backslash trap extends to grep (Learning 121) ‚Äî use single-quoted grep -rEn (ERE), NOT double-quoted BRE \|/\$ (macOS BSD grep silently matches nothing); and write R probes to a FILE + Rscript. (6) A REFACTOR-only deliverable has NO RED phase (Learning 121) ‚Äî the existing green suite is the safety net; run the pre-REFACTOR scope gate + GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR gate only. (7) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (8) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 9 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS128) and carries 11 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS128) ‚Äî rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (9) Carried CRAN gotchas: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run this session (devtools absent).
