(5) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) ‚Äî do NOT ‚Äúclean‚Äù them. (6) Shell backslash trap (recurring) ‚Äî write R probes to a FILE and Rscript it. (7) Markdown IS enabled in roxygen ‚Äî keep prose free of stray markdown. (8) rd-roclet-only regen (roxygen2::roxygenise(roclets="rd")) keeps NAMESPACE byte-identical. (9) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (10) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 7 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS126) and carries 9 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS126) ‚Äî rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (11) Carried CRAN gotchas: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run this session (devtools absent).

What Session 125 Did

What Session 125 Did

Deliverable: owner‚Äôs pick (A9) ‚Äî hardened addGenotype() so its integer allele encoding is consistent regardless of whether the two allele columns arrive as character or factor. (DONE ‚Äî coerced both allele columns to character at the top of the function; factor input now produces output identical to character input, verified on the real bundled rhesusGenotypes: 35-allele dictionary codes 10001‚Äì10035, max distinct codes per allele = 1.) This realizes the latent code fragility S124 surfaced and flagged (Learning 117): genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] / [, 3L] indexes a name-keyed integer vector by a factor ‚Üí R uses the factor‚Äôs INTEGER CODES not its labels ‚Üí encoding inconsistent between the two columns (and between callers). First CODE fix in the S118‚ÄìS125 run (S118‚ÄìS122 were doc/prose; S123‚ÄìS124 were DATA). Scoped to R/addGenotype.R + tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R + man/addGenotype.Rd; no NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/data//checkGenotypeFile.R change (NAMESPACE proven byte-identical). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Two gated AskUserQuestions for the genuine forks + the 3 mandatory transitions: (1) pre-RED scope ‚Äî addGenotype-only vs also-harden-checkGenotypeFile (owner chose addGenotype-only / point-of-use); (2) PRE-RED‚ÜíRED; (3) RED‚ÜíGREEN; (4) GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR. The coerce-at-top-vs-coerce-at-index implementation detail was ground-truth-determined (coerce-at-top is strictly more robust ‚Äî it also fixes the version-fragile c()-on-factors dict build) ‚Üí presented as the gate baseline, NOT re-gated (Learning 113). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only Explore breadth scan to confirm scope (callers / sibling-pattern / checkGenotypeFile behavior) with all load-bearing claims verified firsthand, then SOLO TDD (the fix is a deterministic 2-line coercion ‚Äî a fan-out for the edit would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî THE SCOPE DECISION TURNED ON THE CALL GRAPH, NOT THE DOC (‚Üí Learning 118): addGenotype‚Äôs roxygen says genotype ‚Äúis to be provided by checkGenotypeFile so it is not checked‚Äù ‚Äî which reads as ‚Äúharden the gate.‚Äù The breadth scan + firsthand reads refuted that as the complete answer: addGenotype has DIRECT callers that bypass checkGenotypeFile (the roxygen example passing rhesusGenotypes, test_addGenotype.R:17, test_geneDrop.R:28), so a gate-only fix leaves them exposed. The only fix that closes the bug for ALL callers is at the point of use. Also confirmed: checkGenotypeFile is a pure structural validator (no type coercion), and no sibling genotype function shares the dict[factorColumn] pattern (getGVGenotype/getGenotypes/getGenoDefinedParentGenotypes/hasGenotype are clean) ‚Üí scope stayed narrow. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî THE RED FIXTURE HAD TO MAKE PER-COLUMN CODES DIVERGE FROM THE GLOBAL SORT: the naive fixture (both columns the same allele set in sorted order) does NOT reproduce the bug ‚Äî per-column factor codes coincidentally align with the global sort(unique()) positions. The minimal trigger is first_name = c("a","b"), second_name = c("b","c") as factors: globally a‚Üí10001, b‚Üí10002, c‚Üí10003, but the per-column codes (a,b‚Üí1,2 and b,c‚Üí1,2) make allele ‚Äúb‚Äù encode as 10002 in first but 10001 in second. Designing it required tracing the exact index arithmetic, not just ‚Äúmake it a factor.‚Äù Character input is the oracle. - üö® REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî COERCE AT THE TOP, NOT ONLY AT THE INDEX: the breadth agent suggested wrapping only the two lookups (genoDict[as.character(genotype[, 2L])]). That fixes the lookup but leaves the dict BUILD (sort(unique(c(col1, col2)))) relying on c()-on-factors (pre-R-4.1 dropped factor attrs ‚Üí mis-named dict). Coercing both columns to character once at the top fixes build AND lookup in one place, version-robustly. - RED: two new tests in test_addGenotype.R ‚Äî (1) factor-input output identical to character-input output (expect_identical on $first and $second); (2) allele ‚Äúb‚Äù gets the same code as first and as second ‚Äî confirmed 2 failed / 0 error on the unhardened function; the existing character-input test stayed green (4/0). GREEN: the 2-line coercion at the top of addGenotype; all 3 tests pass; full suite 0/0. REFACTOR: added a one-line @details roxygen note documenting the internal coercion; regenerated man/addGenotype.Rd (rd roclet only ‚Üí NAMESPACE untouched). - Used file-based R probes from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. This change SHIPS and is user-facing (addGenotype output changes for any factor-columned input) ‚Üí fold into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the four _s125_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no Rplots.pdf).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE ‚Äî this IS a runtime-behavior change. ‚ÄúSuite passes‚Äù was necessary but insufficient (the fix changes addGenotype output for factor input). Ran addGenotype(rhesusPedigree, rhesusGenotypes) on a factor-coerced copy of the real bundled object (375√ó12, 31 genotype rows): first/second came out identical to the character path; the combined dictionary is the full 35 alleles (codes 10001‚Äì10035); max distinct codes per allele = 1 (consistent encoding). Computed the consistency property at the genotype-row level (gr <- !is.na(first_name), aligned al/co vectors) to avoid the S124 short-logical-recycling trap (gotcha #11). Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent) ‚Äî proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the factor-vs-character smoke on the real object.

Session 124 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 125): Score 9/10. S124 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as (A9) and made it trivial to execute: it gave the exact bug mechanism (genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] indexes by integer codes not labels), the exact fix location (‚Äúcoerce the allele columns to character inside addGenotype‚Äù), the exact test design hint (‚Äúfeed a factor-columned genotype, assert consistent name-based codes‚Äù), and the exact numbers to expect (combined dict 35 alleles, codes 10001‚Äì10035 vs the buggy per-column 18/23). Every carried gotcha held: the shell-backslash‚Üífile-probe trap (I used file probes from command #1, no misfires), the consistency-check trap gotcha #11 (verify at the genotype-row level, NOT by indexing the 375-row output with a 31-row logical ‚Äî S124 hit this itself and documented it; I followed the warning and avoided the round-trip), the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call, and the branch state (5 ahead of origin/add-methodology). ROI very high; no false credit; the bug characterization was accurate firsthand. The ‚àí1: S124‚Äôs A9 wording offered the fix location as ‚Äúinside addGenotype (or in checkGenotypeFile)‚Äù without noting that direct callers bypass checkGenotypeFile, so the ‚Äúor checkGenotypeFile‚Äù branch ‚Äî taken literally ‚Äî would NOT have fully closed the bug. It listed addGenotype first (correct lead), but I had to run the breadth scan to confirm the gate-only path was insufficient. A perfect handoff would have flagged the direct-caller asymmetry.

Self-assessment (Session 125): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD 9b713206 = S124, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) the scope decision turned on the call graph, not the doc contract ‚Äî proved (via breadth scan + firsthand reads) that direct callers bypass checkGenotypeFile, so the point-of-use fix is the only complete one, and that the bug is isolated (no sibling pattern) so scope stayed narrow; (2) designed a RED fixture that actually reproduces the bug ‚Äî traced the index arithmetic to make per-column codes diverge from the global sort (the naive fixture would have passed even unfixed); (3) coerced at the source, not just the buggy line ‚Äî also neutralized the version-fragile c()-on-factors dict build the breadth agent‚Äôs patch would have left; (4) verified everything firsthand (bug mechanism, caller types, checkGenotypeFile behavior) ‚Äî used the Explore scan for completeness but re-derived its load-bearing claims; (5) real Phase-3E smoke on the actual bundled object (factor-coerced) with the consistency property computed correctly at the genotype-row level ‚Äî sidestepped the S124 gotcha #11; (6) proved zero regressions (3/3 tests, full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, diff confined to 3 files); (7) correct TDD discipline (declared every response; gated the genuine scope fork + the 3 transitions; did not re-gate the ground-truth-determined coerce-at-top detail ‚Äî Learning 113); (8) file-based probes from command #1; 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) ‚Äî the checkRd + load_all + suite + NAMESPACE-diff + smoke is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check; (b) low technical difficulty ‚Äî the fix is 2 lines; the value is the scope analysis, the reproducing fixture, and the realistic smoke, not depth; (c) I did not add an explicit NA-with-factor RED case (the coercion is NA-safe ‚Äî as.character(factor-with-NA) preserves NA, and the character NA path is already covered by test_geneDrop.R ‚Äî but a dedicated assertion would have made it airtight; owner-approved RED scope was the two tests). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. (A9) is DONE ‚Äî addGenotype() is now robust to factor allele columns; the function-level fragility S124 flagged is CLOSED. Nothing mid-flight. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (A10) Data-doc nit (ships, small): R/data.R:345 cites a nonexistent source rhesusGenotypes.csv; the real value-identical export is obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv. Roxygen-source fix ‚Üí man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd regen (same shape as the S120‚ÄìS122 data-doc class). - (A8) Adjacent REFACTOR cleanup (carried from S123): remove the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-op conversions in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125. Behavior-preserving; re-run suite. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan ‚Äî Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump ‚Üí 2.0.0; fold in S112‚ÄìS125, incl.¬ÝS123/S124‚Äôs user-facing data-type changes and S125‚Äôs user-facing addGenotype behavior change). - (C) Merge add-methodology ‚Üí master ‚Äî after this commit add-methodology is 6 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS125) and carries 8 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS125); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 ‚Äî fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A10)/(A8)/(A3)/(B) together (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any of them without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): R/addGenotype.R (2-line character coercion at top + @details roxygen note); tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R (2 new tests: factor==character output, cross-column consistency); man/addGenotype.Rd (regenerated ‚Äî \details block, 1 hunk); CHANGELOG.md (S125 [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 118); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, data/, checkGenotypeFile.R, or other-function change. Read firsthand: R/addGenotype.R, R/checkGenotypeFile.R, R/fixGenotypeCols.R, R/modInput.R:375-414 (the Shiny call path), tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R. Breadth scan (Explore): mapped all 3 callers, confirmed checkGenotypeFile does not coerce, confirmed no sibling dict[factorColumn] pattern. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s125_red.R, _s125_green.R, _s125_refactor.R, _s125_phase3e.R.

Gotchas: (1) The addGenotype factor fragility is now FIXED ‚Äî do not re-flag it; the function coerces allele columns to character at the top, so factor input is encoded consistently (and identically to character input). (2) checkGenotypeFile was intentionally NOT changed (owner chose addGenotype-only) ‚Äî it remains a pure structural validator that does NOT coerce types; if a future task wants the documented ‚Äúopener‚Äù contract enforced at the gate, that is a separate deliverable. (3) addGenotype has DIRECT callers that bypass checkGenotypeFile (roxygen example, test_addGenotype.R, test_geneDrop.R) ‚Äî any future change to genotype handling must account for them, not just the Shiny gate path. (4) Consistency-check trap (carried, gotcha #11 from S124): verify addGenotype‚Äôs allele encoding at the genotype-row (31) level, NOT by indexing the 375-row merged output with a 31-row logical (recycles ‚Üí spurious result). (5) rhesusGenotypes doc cites a nonexistent rhesusGenotypes.csv (R/data.R:345); real source is obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv (option A10). (6) Redundant no-op conversions remain in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125 ‚Äî carried deferred REFACTOR (A8). (7) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) ‚Äî do NOT ‚Äúclean‚Äù them. (8) Shell backslash trap (recurring) ‚Äî write R probes to a FILE and Rscript it. (9) Markdown IS enabled in roxygen ‚Äî keep prose free of stray markdown. (10) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (DONE ‚Äî id/first_name/second_name all factor‚Üícharacter; dim 31√ó3, unique counts 31/18/23 and 0 NA preserved; values cross-checked identical to the shipping obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv.) Same DATA + strict-TDD shape as S123‚Äôs rhesusPedigree re-export (Learning 116). Scoped to data/rhesusGenotypes.RData + new data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R + new tests/testthat/test_rhesusGenotypes.R + the rhesusGenotypes id doc line in R/data.R + man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd; no NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/.Rbuildignore/other-data-object/addGenotype.R change (^data-raw$ already build-ignored from S123; NAMESPACE proven byte-identical). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Four gated AskUserQuestions: (1) pre-RED scope ‚Äî id-only vs all-three-columns (owner chose all three ‚Äî full type-correctness); (2) PRE-RED‚ÜíRED; (3) RED‚ÜíGREEN; (4) GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR. One genuine fork (scope) + the 3 mandatory transitions; coerce-in-place and the data-raw/ convention were ground-truth/established-pattern-determined ‚Üí not re-gated (Learnings 113/116). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only 3-prong investigation workflow (provenance / dependents / test-patterns) to frame scope, with all key claims verified firsthand before authoring; then SOLO coercion + oracle verification (the work is a deterministic coercion ‚Äî a fan-out for the edit would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî THE FIRSTHAND INVESTIGATION REFRAMED A7 (‚Üí Learning 117): S123 flagged A7 as ‚Äúde-factor id‚Äù. Probing showed three factor columns, and that id is the column that does NOT matter ‚Äî merge(ped, genotype, by="id") coerces it either way and every consumer is id-agnostic (independently confirmed by the dependents scan). The columns that matter are the two allele columns: addGenotype()‚Äôs genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] indexes a name-keyed vector by a factor ‚Üí R uses the factor‚Äôs INTEGER CODES not its labels ‚Üí an inconsistent encoding (same allele ‚Üí different code in first vs second). Character columns index by name ‚Üí consistent (verified: same allele‚Üísame code = TRUE for character / FALSE for factor; combined dict 35 alleles, codes 10001‚Äì10035 vs the buggy per-column 10001‚Äì10018 / 10001‚Äì10023). The package‚Äôs own test_addGenotype.R already feeds stringsAsFactors=FALSE ‚Äî the shipped factor object was the anomaly. So scope was a correctness decision, not cosmetic; gated it; owner chose all three. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî PROVENANCE confirmed COERCE-not-REGENERATE: data/rhesusGenotypes.RData has a single 2020 commit (31c4679d), no scripted generator, shares all 31 obfuscated ids with rhesusPedigree (re-deriving via non-deterministic obfuscatePed() would change ids + desync the sibling). New idempotent data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R coerces the three columns and re-saves in place (compress="xz", 734‚Üí608 B). The value-identical inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv is an independent cross-check (all 3 columns identical). - üö® REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî BUNDLED doc + man with the data change: reverted R/data.R:347 id doc factor‚Üícharacter (first_name/second_name make no type claim, untouched) and regenerated man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd (1-line diff) in the SAME deliverable so the data-doc-mismatch class (S114‚ÄìS122) stays closed. - RED: test_rhesusGenotypes.R (22 assertions pinning types AND value-preservation ‚Äî dim 31√ó3, names, unique 31/18/23, NA 0, membership spot-checks 0BNY0G/I67LRJ/K0M2RD, A004_B002/A008_B015b, A004_B048a/A002a_B069a), confirmed failing on the factor object (the 6 expect_type("character")/expect_false(is.factor()) assertions; value assertions already passed). GREEN: ran the coercion, the 1-line doc revert, regenerated man/. REFACTOR: N/A (own code clean; no redundant conversions introduced ‚Äî no test references the bundled genotype object). - Used file-based R probes from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. This change SHIPS and is user-facing (column types changed; the addGenotype example output changes) ‚Üí fold into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE ‚Äî this IS a runtime-behavior change. ‚ÄúSuite passes‚Äù was necessary but insufficient (the data change alters addGenotype output). Ran addGenotype() on the shipped corrected object directly, no conversions ‚Üí 375√ó12, 31 genotype rows, codes spanning the combined 35-allele dictionary, encoding consistent (same allele‚Üísame code = TRUE); coercion idempotent (re-run is a no-op); CSV cross-check id/first_name/second_name all identical. (Note: my first consistency check printed a spurious FALSE ‚Äî it indexed the 375-row merged result with a 31-row logical; recomputed correctly at the genotype-row level ‚Üí TRUE. Caught and reconciled, not a defect.) Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent) ‚Äî proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the consumer-on-shipped-object smoke test + the CSV cross-check.

Session 123 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 124): Score 8/10. Every carried gotcha held exactly ‚Äî the data-raw/ pattern (.RData not .rda, save(..., compress="xz"), do NOT usethis::use_data, ^data-raw$ build-ignored), the shell-backslash‚Üífile-probe trap, the roxygenise(roclets="rd") regen method, the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call, and the branch state (4 ahead of origin/add-methodology; 6 not on origin/master). The ‚Äúbut verify‚Äù steer is exactly what surfaced the fuller picture. ROI high; no false credit. The ‚àí2: A7 was characterized as id-only / ‚Äúmatch the convention‚Äù ‚Äî cosmetic framing that missed that (a) the object has three factor columns and (b) the column it named (id) is the one that doesn‚Äôt matter, while the two it didn‚Äôt mention (first_name/second_name) carry a latent addGenotype correctness issue. S123 wasn‚Äôt tasked to deep-investigate rhesusGenotypes, and it did say ‚Äúverify‚Äù ‚Äî but the scope label under-sold the deliverable.

Self-assessment (Session 124): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD 19a47fab=S123, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) the firsthand investigation REFRAMED the task ‚Äî found the 3-column degradation and the addGenotype factor-indexing correctness issue, so the literal ‚Äúde-factor id‚Äù (which would have shipped a half-degraded object still producing inconsistent codes) was avoided; (2) verified everything firsthand (structure, consistency property, no type-asserting tests, CSV value-identity) ‚Äî never relied on the workflow‚Äôs relayed claims; the workflow independently CONFIRMED the allele-column finding; (3) gated the genuine fork (scope) as a correctness decision and did not re-gate the established coerce-in-place/data-raw pattern (Learnings 113/116); (4) value-preservation in the RED tests + a real Phase-3E smoke on the shipped object + an independent CSV cross-check; (5) bundled doc+man+test atomically; (6) proved zero regressions (new test 22/22, full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, diff confined to intended files); (7) flagged (not fixed) the addGenotype factor-fragility and the doc-source nit (FM #8); (8) file-based probes from command #1; 0 corrections; plain language; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent); (b) my first Phase-3E consistency check had an indexing bug (375-row result indexed by a 31-row logical) that printed a misleading FALSE ‚Äî caught and corrected, but a closer first read would have avoided the round-trip; (c) the investigation workflow‚Äôs dependents agent overlapped my firsthand id-agnosticism check (it was still additive ‚Äî it independently confirmed the allele-column issue). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified and free of a self-inflicted round-trip.

Roxygen-source fix ‚Üí man/ regen (same shape as the S120‚ÄìS122 data-doc class). - (A8) Adjacent REFACTOR cleanup (carried from S123): remove the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-op conversions in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125. Behavior-preserving; re-run suite. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan ‚Äî Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump ‚Üí 2.0.0; fold in S112‚ÄìS124, incl.¬ÝS123/S124‚Äôs user-facing data-type changes). - (C) Merge add-methodology ‚Üí master ‚Äî after this commit add-methodology is 5 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS124) and carries 7 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS124); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 ‚Äî fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A9) with (A8)/(A10)/(B) (FM #18/#25); do NOT start the addGenotype hardening without the owner picking (A9) (a code + test change).

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): data/rhesusGenotypes.RData (re-exported ‚Äî 3 columns coerced to character, values preserved, 734‚Üí608 B); data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R (NEW ‚Äî idempotent coercion script); tests/testthat/test_rhesusGenotypes.R (NEW ‚Äî 22 type+value assertions); R/data.R (1 #' edit, id doc line ~347 factor‚Üícharacter); man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd (regenerated, 1-line diff); CHANGELOG.md (S124 [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 117); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, .Rbuildignore, addGenotype.R, or other-data-object change. Read firsthand: the live rhesusGenotypes structure (probe), R/addGenotype.R, R/data.R:338-354, tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R, tests/testthat/test_rhesusPedigree.R (mirror), data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R (mirror), inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s124_probe.R, _s124_smoke.R, _s124_consistency.R, _s124_groundtruth.R, _s124_phase3e.R.

Gotchas: (1) data-raw/ pattern (carried): data objects are .RData (not .rda); re-export by overwriting in place ‚Äî save(obj, file="data/X.RData", compress="xz") ‚Äî and do NOT use usethis::use_data (writes a lowercase .rda duplicate). ^data-raw$ is already build-ignored. The new data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R is idempotent (re-run is a no-op). (2) NEW ‚Äî addGenotype() is FRAGILE to factor inputs (option A9): genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] indexes a name-keyed vector by a factor ‚Üí uses INTEGER CODES not labels ‚Üí inconsistent allele encoding. The shipped rhesusGenotypes is now character so the EXAMPLE is correct, but the FUNCTION is unhardened ‚Äî do NOT assume it‚Äôs fixed. (3) addGenotype() emits first/second integer codes via a name-keyed dict (c() of the two allele columns ‚Üí sort(unique()) ‚Üí +10000L); character allele columns are REQUIRED for a consistent encoding. (4) rhesusGenotypes doc cites a nonexistent rhesusGenotypes.csv (R/data.R:345); real source is obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv (option A10). (5) Redundant no-op conversions remain in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125 ‚Äî carried deferred REFACTOR (A8). (6) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) ‚Äî do NOT ‚Äúclean‚Äù them. (7) Shell backslash trap (recurring) ‚Äî write R probes to a FILE and Rscript it. (8) Markdown IS enabled in roxygen ‚Äî keep prose free of stray markdown. (9) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (10) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 5 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS124) and carries 7 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS124) ‚Äî rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (11) Consistency-check trap: verify addGenotype‚Äôs allele encoding at the genotype-row (31) level, NOT by indexing the 375-row merged output with a 31-row logical (that recycles and prints a spurious FALSE). (12) Carried CRAN gotchas: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run this session (devtools absent).

What Session 123 Did

Deliverable: owner‚Äôs pick (A6) ‚Äî re-exported the bundled rhesusPedigree object so its degraded columns carry canonical types matching examplePedigree, preserving every value. (DONE ‚Äî id/sire/dam factor‚Üícharacter, birth factor-of-date-strings‚ÜíDate, exit all-NA logical‚ÜíDate all-NA; sex/gen/age already correct, left unchanged. All values preserved: dim 375√ó8, 375 unique ids, NA patterns intact, BRI2MW birth 1998-12-06, range 1970-07-03..2013-12-21.) This is the first real DATA change in the S118‚ÄìS123 run ‚Äî touches data/, adds tests, runs the full RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR cycle. Scoped to data/rhesusPedigree.RData + new data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R + new tests/testthat/test_rhesusPedigree.R + the rhesusPedigree doc in R/data.R + man/rhesusPedigree.Rd + .Rbuildignore; no NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/other-data-object change (NAMESPACE proven byte-identical). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Five gated AskUserQuestions: (1) pre-RED scope ‚Äî Type-correctness vs full-canonical-match vs minimal (owner chose Type-correctness: fix the genuine type bugs, keep sex factor F,M and gen integer ‚Äî no gratuitous widening); (2) pre-RED reproducibility ‚Äî add a data-raw/ script vs in-place-only (owner chose add data-raw script); (3) PRE-RED‚ÜíRED; (4) RED‚ÜíGREEN; (5) GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR. The 3 transition gates are the project‚Äôs mandatory TDD contract; the 2 pre-RED questions were the genuine forks. The determined fixes (birth‚ÜíDate, exit‚ÜíDate, id/sire/dam‚Üícharacter ‚Äî all ground-truth-determined) were the presented baseline, not re-gated (Learning 113). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only 4-prong investigation workflow (provenance / target-structure / dependents / test-patterns) to frame the scope decision, then SOLO implementation + oracle verification (the work itself is a deterministic coercion ‚Äî a fan-out would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî PROVENANCE forced COERCE-not-REGENERATE (‚Üí Learning 116): the .rda has no reproducible generator ‚Äî obfuscated from inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv via obfuscatePed(), hand-saved 2020-02-02 (commit 31c4679d), never scripted/seeded. Obfuscation is non-deterministic, so re-deriving from the CSV would produce different obfuscated ids/dates than the shipped object (and desync sibling objects sharing those ids). The committed data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R therefore loads the existing object and coerces its TYPES while preserving all VALUES (idempotent), rather than regenerating. ‚ÄúRe-export‚Äù for an opaque artifact = value-preserving type coercion, not regeneration. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî INVESTIGATED BEFORE GATING to PRUNE UNSAFE OPTIONS (‚Üí Learning 116): two plausible-sounding options were eliminated before the owner ever saw them ‚Äî ‚Äúre-derive from CSV‚Äù (non-deterministic) and ‚Äúdrop exit‚Äù (getPotentialParents.R:68-76,114 reads ba$exit; dropping breaks the object‚Äôs main consumer). Verified firsthand that no test asserts the current factor/logical types (grep: none) so the fix breaks nothing. The scope gate offered only safe choices. - üö® REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî BUNDLED the doc + man with the data change: changing the object‚Äôs types without updating its R/data.R doc + regenerating man/ in the SAME session would have reopened the exact data-doc-mismatch class S114‚ÄìS122 spent eight sessions closing. So one coherent deliverable: .rda + data-raw/ + doc reversal (id factor‚Üícharacter; birth factor of birth-date strings‚ÜíDate vector; exit logical‚ÜíDate) + man/ regen + tests + .Rbuildignore. - RED: test_rhesusPedigree.R (31 assertions pinning types AND value-preservation ‚Äî dim, id-all-unique, sire/dam 124 NA, birth range + BRI2MW 1998-12-06, exit all-NA), confirmed failing on the degraded object (id/sire/dam integer-factors; birth factor ‚Äî min errored ‚Äúnot meaningful for factors‚Äù; exit not S3). GREEN: ran the coercion (file 7449‚Üí4340 B via xz), updated the 3 doc lines, regenerated man/rhesusPedigree.Rd (3-line diff). REFACTOR: N/A (own code clean) ‚Äî the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-ops in test_getPotentialParents.R/test_modPotentialParents.R were flagged, not removed (adjacent scope, FM #8; owner chose skip). - Used file-based R probes from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. This change SHIPS and is user-facing (column types changed) ‚Üí fold into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now; more NEWS-worthy than the S114‚ÄìS122 prose fixes). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the seven _s123_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no Rplots.pdf).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE ‚Äî this IS a runtime-behavior change. ‚ÄúSuite passes‚Äù was necessary but insufficient (the potential-parents tests do their own as.character/as.Date, now no-ops, which could mask a still-wrong type). The conclusive test ran getPotentialParents() on the SHIPPED corrected object directly, with no conversions (birth is Date, id is character) ‚Üí ran cleanly, 50-element result; the suite‚Äôs positional dam/sire assertions pass. (Note: getPotentialParents() returns an unnamed positional list ‚Äî the tests index by position, e.g.¬Ýdams_1=element 1, dams_4=element 4 ‚Äî so names(result) are NOT focal ids; an early naive spot-check on that was reconciled, not a defect.) Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent) ‚Äî proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all 162 + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the consumer smoke test.

Session 122 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 123): Score 9/10. S122 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as option (A6), characterized the degradation firsthand and accurately (birth factor-of-strings, exit all-NA logical, id/sire/dam/sex factors), and correctly flagged it as a DATA change touching data/ + tests under strict TDD ‚Äî a separate, larger deliverable than a doc fix (which is exactly how it played out). Every carried gotcha held: the near-identical examplePedigree/rhesusPedigree describe blocks (I anchored the id edit on the following sire line so I wouldn‚Äôt hit rhesusGenotypes‚Äôs identical id line at R/data.R:347), the roxygenise(roclets="rd") regen method, the shell-backslash‚Üífile-probe trap, the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call, and the branch state. ROI very high; no false credit. The ‚àí1: its suggested-next phrasing for A6 (‚Äúso birth is a real Date and exit holds real dates (or is dropped)‚Äù) contained one infeasible sub-option (there are NO exit dates in the source ‚Äî exit is all-NA there too) and one unsafe sub-option (dropping breaks getPotentialParents). Right about the bug; slightly off on the proposed remedies ‚Äî both resolved by this session‚Äôs investigation.

Self-assessment (Session 123): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD 17cd5922=S122, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) investigated provenance + dependents BEFORE gating and pruned unsafe options (re-derive-from-CSV, drop-exit) so the owner chose only among safe ones; (2) chose coerce-in-place over regenerate ‚Äî correct given the non-reproducible non-deterministic generator ‚Äî and made it reproducible+idempotent via committed data-raw/; (3) verified everything firsthand (structure, BRI2MW date, gen/age types, no type-asserting tests, exit required) ‚Äî never relied on the workflow‚Äôs relayed claims; (4) value-preservation in the RED tests (not just types) + a real Phase-3E smoke test on the shipped object (not the can-be-masked suite-passes signal); (5) bundled doc+man+tests with the data change so the data-doc-mismatch class stayed closed; (6) proved zero regressions (new tests pass, full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, 162 exports, diff confined to intended files); (7) correct TDD discipline (declared every response; gated the genuine forks + the 3 mandatory transitions; did not re-gate ground-truth-determined fixes); (8) used file-based probes from command #1; 0 corrections; plain language; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) ‚Äî so the new .rda‚Äôs compression NOTE and the ^data-raw$ build-ignore are unconfirmed by the actual build (xz is best-available, so a compression NOTE is unlikely, but unverified); (b) my first smoke-test spot-check (BRI2MW %in% names(pp)) was naive about the unnamed-positional-list result ‚Äî caught and reconciled, but cost a round-trip a closer read of the test would have avoided; (c) the 4-prong investigation workflow had mild overlap (provenance/target-structure both read data.R). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. (A6) is DONE ‚Äî rhesusPedigree now ships canonical types; the data-DOC and data-ARTIFACT classes for it are both closed. Nothing mid-flight. Same TDD shape as A6. - (A8) Adjacent REFACTOR cleanup (small): remove the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-op conversions in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 and test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125 (deferred from this session‚Äôs REFACTOR). Behavior-preserving; re-run suite. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan ‚Äî Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump ‚Üí 2.0.0; fold in S112‚ÄìS123, incl.¬ÝS123‚Äôs user-facing data-type change). - (C) Merge add-methodology ‚Üí master ‚Äî after this commit add-methodology is 4 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120‚ÄìS123) and carries 6 commits not on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS123); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 ‚Äî fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A7) with (A8)/(B) (FM #18/#25); do NOT start the rhesusGenotypes re-export without the owner picking (A7) (data + test change).

What Session 122 Did

Deliverable: owner‚Äôs pick (A5), promoted to the full sweep ‚Äî a one-pass factual-claim audit of all 24 data docs in R/data.R against their live objects (dims, column names/order, types, factor levels, counts, prose), fixing every confirmed discrepancy, then regenerating the 4 affected man/ pages. (DONE ‚Äî 9 fixes across 4 objects, all oracle-verified: checkRd 0 problems √ó4, NAMESPACE byte-identical, load_all 162 exports, full-suite regression 0 fail / 0 error, rendered .Rd ‚ÜîÔ∏é source in sync.) Scoped to R/data.R + 4 data man/ pages; NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/data/ unchanged. Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (roxygen prose ‚Äî only #' comment lines changed, no executable R, no package logic or test surface; RED vacuous; declared every response, as S108‚ÄìS121). Two gated AskUserQuestions ‚Äî (1) scope (narrow 2-doc fix vs.¬Ýfull 24-doc audit; owner chose full, the deliverable Learning 114 had proposed); (2) the 2 data-artifact findings (document actual types now vs.¬Ýleave docs + queue a data fix; owner chose document actual types now). The 7 unambiguous doc-bug fixes were ground-truth-determined ‚Üí not separately gated (Learning 113 ‚Äî over-gating avoided). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as oracle ground truth computed once by me + a workflow (5 independent lens scanners for completeness over all 24 docs, then an adversarial refuter per unique discrepancy) ‚Üí Learning 115. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - The audit (12 candidates ‚Üí 10 confirmed, 2 rejected): the adversarial pass earned its keep both ways ‚Äî it independently re-derived and confirmed all 10 real mismatches AND rejected flagging rhesusPedigree sire/dam (they make NO type claim ‚Äî ‚Äúthe male/female parent ‚Ä¶ NA for unknown‚Äù; ‚Äúcorrecting‚Äù them to ‚Äúfactor‚Äù would invent a fix). Bounding scope is the skeptic‚Äôs symmetric job in a fix-audit. - The 9 fixes (each verified firsthand against the live object before authoring ‚Äî Learning 109/111): examplePedigree \item{sex} "M","F","U" ‚Üí "F","M","H","U" (live factor: 4 levels incl.¬Ýzero-count H); \item{ancestry} ‚Äúcharacter vector or NA ‚Ä¶ free-form text‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúfactor with levels: INDIAN, CHINESE, HYBRID, JAPANESE, OTHER, UNKNOWN ‚Ä¶‚Äù (a closed 6-level factor, not free-form character). rhesusPedigree \item{id} ‚Äúcharacter‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúfactor‚Äù; \item{sex} "M","F","U" ‚Üí "F","M" (no U level); \item{birth} ‚ÄúDate vector‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúfactor of birth-date strings (282 levels)‚Äù; \item{exit} ‚ÄúDate vector‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúlogical vector, all NA (no exit dates recorded ‚Ä¶)‚Äù. rhesusGenotypes \item{id} ‚Äúcharacter‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúfactor‚Äù. qcBreeders @description + @source ‚ÄúA list of ‚Ä¶‚Äù ‚Üí ‚ÄúA character vector of ‚Ä¶‚Äù (counts 3 males/26 females confirmed correct). Edited rhesusPedigree‚Äôs whole \describe block as one chunk because it is near-identical to examplePedigree‚Äôs ‚Äî the shared lines (id -- character, Date vector of birth/exit) are correct in examplePedigree (id IS character; birth/exit ARE Date) and must NOT be touched; block-level disambiguation kept the edits surgical. - üö® 2nd-order finding ‚Äî DATA artifact (gated, doc fixed, DATA flagged for a future session): rhesusPedigree ships birth as a factor-of-strings (not Date) and exit as all-375-NA logical (no exit dates at all) ‚Äî the obfuscation degraded these columns. Owner chose to document the actual types now (docs are now accurate to the shipped object), but the underlying DATA oddity may warrant a re-export (birth ‚Üí real Date; exit ‚Üí real dates or drop the column). That is a DATA change (touches data/, tests, TDD) ‚Äî flagged as option (A6), NOT done this session (FM #8, ‚Äú1 and done‚Äù). - Used file-based R probes from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. Oracle ground truth derived once by me (deterministic); the fan-out reasoned over it; R execution kept with the orchestrator to avoid concurrent-temp-file races. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. These help pages SHIP ‚Üí fold S122‚Äôs 9 fixes into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). Self-assessment (Session 122): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD b117a9c8 = S121, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) derived authoritative ground truth for all 24 objects once and verified every fix against the live object before authoring ‚Äî never edited from a flag‚Äôs wording; (2) gated the two genuine forks (scope; data-artifact handling) and did NOT re-gate the 7 ground-truth-determined doc-bugs (Learning 113); (3) the completeness fan-out earned itself ‚Äî 5 independent lenses + adversarial verify turned the single flagged sex bug into the full class-complete set (10 confirmed) and the adversarial pass bounded scope by rejecting sire/dam non-claims (‚Üí Learning 115); (4) separated doc-bug from data-artifact ‚Äî surfaced that rhesusPedigree birth/exit are degraded DATA, fixed the doc per the owner‚Äôs call, and flagged the DATA for a future session rather than silently enshrining or silently ignoring it; (5) surgical block-level disambiguation of the near-identical examplePedigree/rhesusPedigree describe blocks ‚Äî left the genuinely-correct shared lines (id character, birth/exit Date in examplePedigree) untouched, verified by grep before AND after; (6) proved zero regressions (regen confined to 4 pages, NAMESPACE byte-identical, checkRd 0√ó4, load_all 162, suite 0/0); (7) used file-based probes from command #1; 0 corrections; plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) ‚Äî checkRd + load_all + regression + NAMESPACE diff is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check; (b) low technical difficulty ‚Äî the value is the audit rigor, the doc-bug/data-artifact split, and the scope-bounding, not depth; (c) the deliverable closes the data-DOC class but deliberately leaves the data-ARTIFACT (rhesusPedigree birth/exit) open ‚Äî correct scope discipline, but it means the rhesus example object still ships with degraded columns until a future (A6). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. (A5)/the full data-doc audit is done ‚Äî the 24 data docs are now factually accurate to their live objects (the recurring data-doc bug class S114‚ÄìS122 is CLOSED at the doc level). Nothing mid-flight. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (A6) The rhesusPedigree DATA artifact (NEW ‚Äî DATA change, not docs): birth ships as a factor-of-strings (not Date) and exit as all-NA logical (no exit dates). The docs now describe this accurately, but the owner may want to re-export rhesusPedigree so birth is a real Date and exit holds real dates (or is dropped). This touches data/ + tests under strict TDD ‚Äî a separate, larger deliverable than a doc fix. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116; separate artifact from man/). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan ‚Äî Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump ‚Üí 2.0.0; fold in S112‚ÄìS122 doc repairs). - (C) Merge add-methodology ‚Üí master ‚Äî after this commit add-methodology is 3 ahead of origin/add-methodology (unpushed S120 + S121 + S122) and carries 5 commits not yet on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS122); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 ‚Äî fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A6) with (A3)/(B) (FM #18/#25); do NOT start the rhesusPedigree data re-export without the owner picking (A6) (it is a data + test change, not a doc change).

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): R/data.R (9 #' edits across 4 data-doc blocks: examplePedigree sex+ancestry, rhesusPedigree id+sex+birth+exit, rhesusGenotypes id, qcBreeders description+source); man/examplePedigree.Rd, man/qcBreeders.Rd, man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd, man/rhesusPedigree.Rd (regenerated); CHANGELOG.md (S122 [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 115), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, test, CI-workflow, CRAN-vignette, or data/ change. Read firsthand: R/data.R (full file), every live data object‚Äôs structure (dims, column classes, factor levels), the 4 rendered .Rd diffs. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s122_sexprobe.R, _s122_groundtruth.R, _s122_compact.R, _s122_confirm.R, _s122_audit_wf.js, _s122_regen.R, _s122_checkrd.R, _s122_regress.R.

Gotchas: (1) NEW ‚Äî rhesusPedigree ships DEGRADED columns (option A6): birth is a factor-of-date-strings (282 levels), exit is logical with all 375 values NA, and id/sire/dam/sex are factors (stringsAsFactors-era load). The docs now describe this accurately; if the owner wants real Date birth/exit, that is a data/ re-export + test change, not a doc edit. (2) examplePedigree and rhesusPedigree have NEAR-IDENTICAL \describe blocks ‚Äî id/sire/dam/sex/gen/birth/exit/age items share text, but examplePedigree‚Äôs are CORRECT (id character; birth/exit Date) while rhesusPedigree‚Äôs were wrong. When editing either, anchor on a UNIQUE block-level chunk (e.g.¬Ýthe trailing } after age for rhesusPedigree; the ancestry/status/recordStatus tail for examplePedigree) ‚Äî a single-line \item match will hit the wrong block. Same trap for the \item{id}{-- character column of animal IDs} line (appears at examplePedigree:20 CORRECT, rhesusGenotypes:347 + rhesusPedigree:361 which were wrong). (3) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures, NOT corruption (carried) ‚Äî fixColumnNames normalizes them to sire; do NOT ‚Äúclean‚Äù the data columns. (4) Regenerate with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>, roclets="rd") (devtools absent); baseline 8.0.0 so regens are clean ‚Äî confirm via git diff --stat only intended pages changed and git diff --quiet -- NAMESPACE for import neutrality. (5) Shell backslash trap (recurring): write R probes to a FILE and run Rscript file.R. (6) Markdown IS enabled (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)) ‚Äî keep prose free of stray markdown syntax. (7) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (8) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 3 ahead of origin/add-methodology (unpushed S120 + S121 + S122) and carries 5 commits not yet on origin/master (S118‚ÄìS122) ‚Äî will ride a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (9) Carried CRAN gotchas remain valid: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

Unknown sires‚Äù). - üö® REAL WORK ‚Äî THE COMPLETENESS SCAN FOUND A 3RD SAME-CLASS BUG (‚Üí Learning 114; FLAGGED, NOT fixed): the \item{sex} of BOTH examplePedigree (R/data.R:25) and rhesusPedigree (R/data.R:366) claims factor with levels: "M", "F", "U", but the live factors are F,M,H,U (examplePedigree ‚Äî 4 levels, H count 0) and F,M (rhesusPedigree ‚Äî 2 levels, NO U). Neither matches the doc. The scanner only flagged examplePedigree; I verified firsthand and found rhesusPedigree (identical doc text) is ALSO wrong ‚Äî a per-group scanner is blind to a cross-group sibling (Learning 114). All other 24-doc factual claims (dims, column names, counts) verified correct. - Used file-based R probes from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work), did the full Phase 0 reads this same turn. - What changed (1 source file, 24 regenerated man/ pages, 0 new committed files): R/data.R ‚Äî 24 data-doc blocks restructured: short noun-phrase title, blank line, then the moved descriptive sentence as @description; \describe/@examples/@source/structure-dumps preserved byte-for-byte. man/ (24 regenerated): exampleNprcgenekeeprConfig, examplePedigree, finalRpt, focalAnimals, lacy1989Ped, lacy1989PedAlleles, ped1Alleles, pedDuplicateIds, pedFemaleSireMaleDam, pedGood, pedInvalidDates, pedMissingBirth, pedOne, pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam, pedSix, pedWithGenotype, pedWithGenotypeReport, qcBreeders, qcPed, qcPedGvReport, smallPed, smallPedTree, rhesusGenotypes, rhesusPedigree. Plus CHANGELOG.md (S117 [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 111), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, test, CI-workflow, CRAN-vignette, or data/ change. - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî VERIFIED EVERY COUNT BEFORE AUTHORING IT; FOUND qcPed SELF-CONTRADICTS (‚Üí Learning 111): before moving any ‚ÄúN rows and M columns‚Äù sentence into a description, I checked all 10 count-bearing docs against the loaded objects. Exactly one was wrong: qcPed title said ‚Äú277 rows and 6 columns‚Äù but the object is 280√ó8 ‚Äî and roxygen‚Äôs auto-\format already read ‚Äú280 rows and 8 columns‚Äù, so the page literally contradicted itself. Wrote the accurate 280√ó8 in the new description and flagged the correction (the S115/S116 verify-and-correct pattern). All 9 others (incl.¬Ýped1Alleles 554√ó6, the six error-set peds, pedWithGenotype 280√ó12) verified accurate, preserved verbatim. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî PAIRED SOLO+ORACLE WITH AN ADVERSARIAL PANEL FOR THE SUBJECTIVE DIMENSION (‚Üí Learning 111): checkRd/load_all/tests prove the rewrite RENDERS and is structurally clean, but cannot judge whether a title is APT/accurate/non-duplicative. So I ran a 3-lens critic workflow (accuracy / completeness / style+consistency), each lens grounding itself in git show HEAD:R/data.R + the loaded objects. Result 0 block / 0 should-fix / 3 nits: (a) examplePedigree‚Äôs \describe documents \item{recordStats} but the actual column is recordStatus ‚Äî PRE-EXISTING (identical in HEAD), in a \describe block I only touched at the title ‚Üí flagged, not fixed (FM #8), verified real against the data first; (b) smallPed ‚ÄúSmall hypothetical pedigree (17 animals)‚Äù was a confusable near-duplicate of lacy1989Ped ‚ÄúSmall hypothetical pedigree (Lacy 1989)‚Äù ‚Üí applied the disambiguation to ‚ÄúHypothetical 17-animal pedigree‚Äù (improving my own un-previewed draft); (c) ‚Äústudbook‚Äù vs ‚Äúpedigree‚Äù for the 6 QC fixtures ‚Üí kept studbook (owner approved it in the style preview; it‚Äôs defensible ‚Äî studbook input fixtures that represent pedigrees, feeding qcStudbook), noted for future. - ped1Alleles made well-formed: two ## Copyright comment lines interrupted its roxygen block (between title and @format); relocated them above the block so it is contiguous. Regenerated .Rd carries title + @description + @format cleanly. - Used file-based R probes from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109) that bit S112‚ÄìS114; no misfires this session. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. These help pages SHIP ‚Üí fold S117‚Äôs new titles into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). Session 116 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 117): Score 9/10. S116 pre-named THIS deliverable as suggested-next option (A2) (‚Äúgive the data docs proper short @titles with detail moved to @description ‚Äî e.g.¬ÝfinalRpt‚Äòs 4-sentence run-on; the error-set docs‚Äô titles are now their full first sentence‚Äù), so I located the scope instantly. Its gotchas held exactly and saved real time: the shell-backslash trap ‚Üí write file-based R probes (I used file-based probes from command #1, zero misfires); regen with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>) (devtools absent), baseline 8.0.0 so the regen confined to my intended pages; the NAMESPACE byte-identical check (git diff --quiet) as the import-neutrality oracle; and ‚Äî critically ‚Äî si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures, do NOT ‚Äúclean‚Äù them (I preserved them and explicitly told the critic panel not to flag them). The standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call held. ROI very high. The ‚àí1: S116‚Äôs A2 description referenced only finalRpt + the error-set docs as examples without noting the full set is 24 data docs sharing one consistent ‚ÄúX is a‚Ä¶‚Äù anti-pattern (I had to inventory the scope myself), and it could not foresee the latent qcPed count error (277√ó6 vs 280√ó8) or the examplePedigree recordStats‚ÜírecordStatus drift ‚Äî both discoverable only by probing/critic this session. Accurate within what a flag could know; no false credit.

Self-assessment (Session 117): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD 674b772d = S116, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) inventoried the true scope (24 data docs = 24 .rda = 24 \docType{data} pages, complete and self-contained) before posing the gated AskUserQuestion, which carried a concrete pedGood before/after preview + ASCII-only labels ([[ascii-only-in-question-options]]); (2) verified every count-bearing claim against the live object before authoring ‚Äî caught qcPed‚Äôs self-contradiction and corrected it (Learning 111, extends 109); (3) right-sized SOLO+oracle for structure but fanned out a 3-lens adversarial panel for the non-oracle quality dimension ‚Äî the legitimate, non-theater use of a critic workflow (it validated the rewrites AND surfaced a real near-duplicate + a pre-existing content bug) ‚Üí Learning 111; (4) respected the owner-decision boundary ‚Äî kept the owner-approved ‚Äústudbook‚Äù when the panel merely preferred ‚Äúpedigree‚Äù, but freely improved my own un-previewed smallPed draft; (5) held scope discipline ‚Äî FLAGGED (did not fix) the recordStats‚ÜírecordStatus drift, the garbled rhesusGenotypes fragment, and the created the SNPRC wording (FM #8), verifying the first against the data; (6) proved zero regressions (checkRd 0 √ó24, NAMESPACE byte-identical, load_all 162, suite 0/0); (7) used file-based probes from command #1 (no backslash misfires); 0 corrections; plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) ‚Äî checkRd + load_all + regression + NAMESPACE diff is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check; (b) the work is moderate-difficulty ‚Äî the value is the verify-before-authoring rigor + the solo/panel split, not deep technical complexity; (c) I left a within-doc term split (studbook title / ‚Äúpedigree‚Äù description) standing for the 6 fixtures ‚Äî defensible and owner-approved, but a reader could notice it. I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified with no residual known stylistic split in touched files.

Gotchas: (1) Adjacent content bugs FLAGGED, NOT fixed (FM #8) ‚Äî option (A): examplePedigree‚Äôs \describe item is recordStats but the actual column is recordStatus (verified 3694√ó12, last col recordStatus); rhesusGenotypes‚Äôs description still contains the garbled ‚ÄúThere are object.‚Äù (intentionally preserved this session); exampleNprcgenekeeprConfig says ‚Äúcreated the SNPRC‚Äù. (2) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures, NOT corruption (carried from S116) ‚Äî fixColumnNames normalizes them to sire; do NOT ‚Äúclean‚Äù the data columns. The data-doc column lists (set by S116) were preserved byte-for-byte. (3) qcPed is 280√ó8 ‚Äî its title formerly claimed 277√ó6 (now corrected in the description); roxygen‚Äôs auto-\format is a free oracle for shape claims (it already read 280√ó8). (4) studbook vs pedigree term split ‚Äî the 6 QC fixtures are titled ‚Äú‚Ä¶studbook‚Ä¶‚Äù (owner-approved preview) while their descriptions say ‚Äú‚Ä¶full pedigree‚Ä¶‚Äù; a future pass could unify to ‚Äúpedigree‚Äù if preferred (panel‚Äôs nit). (5) Shell backslash trap (recurring): write R probes to a FILE and run Rscript file.R; simple Rscript -e expressions (no regex/backslashes) are fine. (6) Regenerate with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>, roclets="rd") (devtools absent); baseline 8.0.0 so regens are clean ‚Äî confirm via git diff --stat that only intended pages changed, and git diff --quiet -- NAMESPACE for import neutrality. (7) Markdown IS enabled (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)) ‚Äî titles must avoid stray markdown syntax (mine are plain phrases + parens, safe); _id_/__examplePedigree__ in focalAnimals are intentional markdown emphasis, preserved. (8) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call; a stray Rplots.pdf can appear from Rscript plot calls ‚Äî none this session. (9) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 6 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S112‚ÄìS117) and ~183 ahead of master ‚Äî the pending action is the merge to master. (10) Carried CRAN gotchas remain valid: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL Suggests; pkgdown/quarto/devtools dev-lib-only; RSelenium undeclared; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

It‚Äôs cosmetic lint, not a regression. Reported the correction to the owner before touching anything (verify-the-claim applies to your OWN characterization). - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî THE FLAG UNDERCOUNTED SCOPE 3√ó (‚Üí Learning 110): S115 named 3 mod*.R files; a file-based detector scanning every @importFrom in R/ found 20 wrapped tags across 10 files, incl.¬Ýnon-mod appServer.R/appUI.R. Surfaced the 3-vs-10 boundary to the owner (a flag‚Äôs file list is a sample, not an inventory). - üö® REAL WORK #3 ‚Äî THE OBVIOUS FIX WAS INVERTED; si.re IS AN INTENTIONAL FIXTURE (‚Üí Learning 110): si.re (and pedOne‚Äôs si re) looked like a corrupted sire to rename in the data. Tracing it through the consumer: R/fixColumnNames.R:22,25 strips spaces then periods (si re -> si.re -> sire), and the end-to-end probe qcStudbook(pedGood) returns canonical columns id, sire, dam, .... So si.re is a deliberate raw studbook-input fixture (make.names("si re") at .rda creation) exercising the QC normalizer ‚Äî renaming would gut it. Correct fix = the docs, not the data. Posed the data-vs-doc fork; owner chose full-accuracy doc-fix. - Used file-based R probes from the first command ‚Äî dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109) that bit S112‚ÄìS114; no misfires this session. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified with no residual known issues in the touched files.

Deliverable: the owner‚Äôs pick (A2 ‚Äî full sweep) ‚Äî a roxygen-source doc-nit mop-up: removed the trailing period from every title flagged by checkRd (62 of 190 .Rd: 51 function docs + 11 data docs) and fixed S112‚Äôs three genetic-value @return nits (calcFE spurious r clause; calcFEFG/calcFG unbalanced fg-formula paren), then regenerated man/. (DONE ‚Äî verified by a per-page HEAD-vs-working checkRd diff proving 62 pages had ONLY the period NOTE removed and 0 pages gained any new problem; title-period NOTEs 62 ‚Üí 0; load_all 162 exports.) Scoped to 55 R/ files + 65 regenerated man/ pages; NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION unchanged. Did NOT fix flagged adjacent content bugs (FM #8). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (roxygen prose; no package logic or test surface, so RED is vacuous; declared every response, as S108‚ÄìS113). One gated AskUserQuestion (pre-RED scope decision ‚Äî full sweep vs.¬Ýfunctions-only vs.¬ÝGV-nits-only; owner chose full sweep). No TDD phase-transition gates (none apply). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as SOLO with a deterministic dry-run-first script + an oracle (checkRd) verification, not a fan-out ‚Äî when the check is a ground-truth oracle, parallel agents add variance not safety for ‚Äúremove one character‚Äù (extends S113‚Äôs deterministic-regen call) ‚Üí Learning 108. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work), did the full Phase 0 reads this same turn. - What changed (55 R/ edits + 65 man/ regenerated, 0 new files): R/ (54): 51 function files each lost one trailing title period; R/data.R lost it from 11 data-doc titles; R/calcFE.R @return dropped the spurious \code{r} clause (FE = 1/sum(p^2) uses only p); R/calcFEFG.R + R/calcFG.R @return closed the unbalanced paren (sum( (p ^ 2) / r} ‚Üí sum( (p ^ 2) / r)). man/ (65 regenerated): the 62 title pages + the 3 GV \value pages. Plus CHANGELOG.md (S114 [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 108), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, test, CI-workflow, or CRAN-vignette change. - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî DRY-RUN + AUDIT BEFORE WRITING, THEN AN ORACLE PROOF (‚Üí Learning 108): the 51 single-block function titles were de-periodized by a deterministic script run in dry-run first ‚Äî it printed every title paragraph + its before/after, all 51 audited before any write (each correctly found the title = first roxygen paragraph, incl.¬Ýthe 12 multi-line titles). Each function file then changed exactly one line (51 insertions / 51 deletions ‚Äî no encoding rewrite). R/data.R (11 titles) + the 3 @return blocks were hand-edited from a fresh read. Verification was an oracle: a per-page checkRd comparison of all 65 changed pages vs their HEAD versions (normalize line numbers, set-diff messages) proved 62 pages had ONLY \title should not end in a period removed and 0 pages gained any new problem; the 3 GV pages changed \value prose with no checkRd impact. Title-period NOTEs package-wide 62 ‚Üí 0. pkgload::load_all() OK (162 exports ‚Äî NAMESPACE consistent). No stray Rplots.pdf. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî RE-VERIFIED EVERY INHERITED FLAG (‚Üí Learning 105/106): the 62 count matched S113‚Äôs claim exactly; calcFE‚Äôs spurious r and the calcFEFG/calcFG unbalanced paren both confirmed real against source before editing. None actioned blindly. - The recurring shell backslash trap (bit me TWICE early): Rscript -e '... "\\.Rd$" ...' and grep("^\\\\title", ...) get mangled through the Bash layer (a backslash level stripped ‚Üí '\.' is an unrecognized escape / a \t tab matching nothing). Switched to file-based R probes (Write tool, run Rscript file.R) + backslash-free constructs (endsWith, [.], fixed=TRUE, [[:space:]]). S112/S113 hit variants ‚Äî reach for the file-based probe FIRST. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. These help pages SHIP ‚Üí fold the title/return corrections into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work), did the full Phase 0 reads this same turn. - What changed (27 files regenerated/edited, 0 new): DESCRIPTION: RoxygenNote: 7.3.2 -> Config/roxygen2/version: 8.0.0 (the 8.0.0 field rename) + the Suggests floor roxygen2 (>= 7.3.2) -> (>= 8.0.0). man/ (26 pages): 24 dataset docs adopt 8.0.0‚Äôs canonical usage form (\usage{ qcPed } -> \usage{ data(qcPed) }); man/appUI.Rd re-wraps its \value (cosmetic, identical content); man/nprcgenekeepr-package.Rd gets the two content repairs below. No R/, test, NAMESPACE, NEWS, ROADMAP, CI-workflow, or CRAN-vignette change. - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî THE REGEN REPAIRED STALE COMMITTED CONTENT (-> Learning 107): regenerating man/nprcgenekeepr-package.Rd corrected a shipped typo ('Macaca' 'mulatto' -> 'mulatta'; DESCRIPTION already read mulatta, so the committed 7.3.2 .Rd had drifted from its source) and added the maintainer R. Mark Sharp to the Authors list (he is aut in DESCRIPTION but was absent from the rendered page). The committed .Rd had drifted from source; the regen reconciled it. S112‚Äôs four hand-edited \value pages produced zero diff ‚Äî content already matched source (the clean reconciliation S112 predicted). The migration is the inverse of S112‚Äôs blast-radius trap: taken as its own deliverable it is small (26 of 190 .Rd), reviewable, and fixes drift. - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî PROVED ZERO REGRESSIONS, NOT ‚ÄúLOOKS FINE‚Äù (-> Learning 107): verification = ‚Äúis the WHOLE diff intended?‚Äù, not ‚Äúdid I break anything?‚Äù Read all 27 changed files line-by-line; tallied every added line (only the field rename, 24 data(...) forms, the appUI rewrap, the mulatta line, and the Sharp author line ‚Äî no surprises). tools::checkRd() on all 26 changed pages introduces zero new problems: the 11 reported \title-ends-in-period NOTEs are PRE-EXISTING ‚Äî confirmed identical on the committed HEAD versions, and 62 of 190 .Rd carry it package-wide (a separate A2 item). Did NOT assume a NOTE on a changed file was new. - The rejected alternative (pin 7.3.2): roxygen2 is not in renv.lock (dev-only dep, not snapshotted), so pinning needs a manual renv::install(roxygen2@7.3.2) that does NOT survive renv::restore ‚Äî more work, not durable, and it preserves the typo + missing author. Adopting needs no lock change. The owner chose adopt. - Verification (build-equivalent for a generated-doc change): complete-diff audit (27 files) + tools::checkRd() clean-of-new on all 26 pages + pkgload::load_all() OK (162 exports ‚Äî NAMESPACE consistent) + content checks (mulatta present, mulatto absent, Sharp in Authors, 32 data() usage forms) + no stray Rplots.pdf. renv.lock unchanged. devtools is NOT in the renv lib (roxygen2 8.0.0/pkgload ARE). - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history -> CHANGELOG here. The ‚àí1: S112 estimated ‚Äúall 30 .Rd‚Äù reformatted; the actual regen touches 26, and it framed the migration purely as a risk (‚Äúblast radius‚Äù) ‚Äî it could not foresee that the regen also repairs the mulatto typo + the missing author (drift between source and committed .Rd), nor that the diff is genuinely small when isolated. Self-assessment (Session 113): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check -> HEAD 9ae8a099 = S112, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick (A); claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) grounded the gated decision in a reverted dry-run before posing it ‚Äî measured the real blast radius (26 files, not ‚Äúall 190‚Äù), characterized all four change-types, and surfaced that roxygen2 isn‚Äôt in renv.lock (which decides adopt-vs-pin economics) ‚Äî so the AskUserQuestion was fully informed, with plain-ASCII labels + previews ([[ascii-only-in-question-options]]); (2) verified by reading the COMPLETE diff + checkRd on all 26 pages, and proved the 11 \title NOTEs PRE-EXIST on HEAD (62/190 package-wide) rather than assuming a NOTE on a changed file was a regression (-> Learning 107); (3) caught the beneficial side effect ‚Äî the regen repairs the shipped mulatto typo + adds the maintainer to Authors ‚Äî and disclosed it (it ships -> CRAN NEWS); (4) right-sized SOLO with a full-diff audit and said why (a deterministic inspectable regen has nothing to fan out ‚Äî a workflow would be theater); (5) held scope discipline ‚Äî bumped only the agreed Suggests floor, FLAGGED (did not fix) the 62 pre-existing \title NOTEs + S112‚Äôs A2 nits (FM #8); (6) 0 stakeholder corrections; plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; no stray artifacts. Weaknesses (honest): (a) two of my verification Rscripts hit self-inflicted bugs ‚Äî a grep no-match exit-2 and an R string-escape (\\.Rd) error ‚Äî caught and corrected immediately, no harm, but avoidable; (b) full R CMD check was NOT run (devtools absent; artifact-only change) ‚Äî checkRd + load_all + the complete-diff audit is the proportionate build-equivalent, but it is not the full check; (c) the migration is intrinsically low-difficulty ‚Äî the value is the decision grounding + the zero-regression proof, not technical depth. I reserve 10 for a delivery with no command misfires that is also full-check-verified.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. (A) is done; nothing mid-flight. The roxygen tooling baseline is now 8.0.0 ‚Äî future roxygenise() regens stay clean (no more version blast radius). Plus S112‚Äôs smaller flags: calcFE.R @return mentions r though FE = 1/sum(p^2) has none; the sum( (p^2) / r} fg formula has unbalanced parens. Low-risk roxygen-source fixes. - (B) Resume the CRAN plan ‚Äî Phase 2b (vignette rebuild timing) or Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump -> 2.0.0; fold in S112‚Äôs @return corrections AND S113‚Äôs typo/author repairs ‚Äî both ship). - (C) Push add-methodology ‚Äî now 2 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S112 + S113); it is 178 ahead of master, so the real pending action is the eventual merge to master. Do NOT bundle the A2 mop-up into the next content task; do NOT bundle phases (FM #18/#25).

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): DESCRIPTION; man/appUI.Rd, man/nprcgenekeepr-package.Rd, and 24 dataset .Rd (exampleNprcgenekeeprConfig, examplePedigree, finalRpt, focalAnimals, lacy1989Ped, lacy1989PedAlleles, ped1Alleles, pedDuplicateIds, pedFemaleSireMaleDam, pedGood, pedInvalidDates, pedMissingBirth, pedOne, pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam, pedSix, pedWithGenotype, pedWithGenotypeReport, qcBreeders, qcPed, qcPedGvReport, rhesusGenotypes, rhesusPedigree, smallPed, smallPedTree); CHANGELOG.md, PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 107), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Gotchas: (1) The roxygen baseline is now 8.0.0 (Config/roxygen2/version: 8.0.0, RoxygenNote retired, Suggests: roxygen2 (>= 8.0.0)) ‚Äî future regens are clean; the S112 version blast-radius trap is RESOLVED. Regenerate with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>) (NOT devtools::document() ‚Äî devtools is absent). (2) 62 of 190 .Rd have the pre-existing \title-ends-in-period checkRd NOTE (the largest A2 cluster) ‚Äî they pre-date this session (confirmed on HEAD); fix in the roxygen SOURCE (@title lines), not the .Rd, then regenerate. (3) These help pages SHIP ‚Äî S113‚Äôs mulatto->mulatta typo fix + the added maintainer, and S112‚Äôs @return corrections, all belong in the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS at 2.0.0 (flagged, not edited now). (4) man/ data docs now use \usage{ data(name) } (8.0.0 canonical form) ‚Äî expected, do not ‚Äúrevert‚Äù it. (5) roxygen2 is NOT in renv.lock (dev-only dep) ‚Äî a future renv::restore re-pulls whatever roxygen2 the dev installs; the committed man/ is the source of truth for the format, not the lock. (6) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call; a stray Rplots.pdf can appear from Rscript plot calls ‚Äî none this session. (7) Branch state: add-methodology is 2 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S112 + S113) and 178 ahead of master ‚Äî the pending action is the merge to master, not just a push. (8) Carried-forward CRAN gotchas remain valid: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL Suggests (which is why roxygen2 isn‚Äôt locked); pkgdown/quarto/devtools dev-lib-only; RSelenium undeclared; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

What Session 112 Did

(DONE ‚Äî re-verified via an 8-agent workflow + checkRd/load_all/render.) Scoped to 5 R/ + 4 man/ files; did NOT migrate roxygen versions, touch the CRAN plan, or fix flagged-but-out-of-scope items (FM #8/#18/#25). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (roxygen prose corrected to match already-correct behavior ‚Äî the code was right, only the docs were wrong, so RED is vacuous; declared every response). One AskUserQuestion (pre-implementation SCOPE decision, not a TDD gate): the calcFEFG p-defect also exists verbatim in calcFE.R/calcFG.R (shared calcFounderContributions() helper) ‚Äî owner chose ‚Äúfix siblings too.‚Äù No TDD phase-transition gates (none apply). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as an 8-agent verify + adversarial-cross-check Workflow (one verifier + one refuter per claim) feeding SOLO single-threaded edits + regen ‚Üí Learning 106. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub before technical work). - What changed (12 edits, 0 new): R/ (5): orderReport.R @return (both bullets: ‚Äúmean kinship < 0.25, ascending mk‚Äù ‚Üí z-score <= 0.25, ascending zScores), calcFEFG.R / calcFG.R / calcFE.R @return (p ‚Äúaverage number of descendants‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúthe vector of founder mean contributions to the current descendants‚Äù), reportGV.R @return (‚ÄúA dataframe‚Äù ‚Üí the nprcgenekeeprGV list + its 10 elements). man/ (4): matching \value edits in calcFE.Rd / calcFEFG.Rd / calcFG.Rd / reportGV.Rd (orderReport is @noRd ‚Äî no .Rd). Plus CHANGELOG.md (S112 [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 106), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, test, CI-workflow, or CRAN-vignette change. - üö® REAL WORK #1 ‚Äî RE-VERIFYING THE INHERITED FLAGS REFUTED ONE OF FOUR (‚Üí Learning 106, extends 105): the 4 drifts were code-read claims (3 from S108, 1 from S110) never confirmed against runtime. An 8-agent workflow (verifier + adversarial cross-checker per claim) confirmed 3 and refuted getPyramidPlot: its @return (‚Äúthe return value of par('mar')‚Äù) is correct ‚Äî the function returns plotrix::pyramid.plot()‚Äôs value, but pyramid.plot ends with return(oldmar) where oldmar <- par("mar"), so the returned value is a par("mar") vector (verified: returns c(5.1,4.1,4.1,2.1)). Trusting the flag would have replaced a correct @return with a wrong one. The cross-check also surfaced same-defect instances beyond scope (the sibling p descriptions; a 2nd orderReport bullet). - üö® REAL WORK #2 ‚Äî THE roxygen VERSION-MISMATCH BLAST-RADIUS TRAP (‚Üí Learning 106): roxygen2::roxygenise() rewrote ~30 .Rd files + changed DESCRIPTION ‚Äî far beyond my 4 functions ‚Äî because committed man/ is roxygen 7.3.2 (RoxygenNote: 7.3.2) but the dev lib is 8.0.0 (reformats every page; migrates the field to Config/roxygen2/version:). I git checkout -- man/ DESCRIPTION to drop the version artifacts, then hand-edited the 4 affected \value blocks to match source ‚Äî scoping the diff to 5 R/ + 4 man/. General rule: read git diff --stat after any regen; a diff beyond your edits = a tool/version mismatch reformatting files ‚Äî don‚Äôt commit it as part of a content fix. - Verification (build-equivalent for a doc change): tools::checkRd() clean on all 4 changed pages; pkgload::load_all() succeeds; rendered \value reads correctly (reportGV now lists the 10 elements, matching the list(...) constructor + the function‚Äôs own @examples); no leftover ‚Äúaverage number of descendants‚Äù / ‚Äúascending mk‚Äù anywhere. devtools is NOT in the renv lib (roxygen2/pkgload/pkgbuild ARE). - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG here. Unlike the website articles, these help pages SHIP ‚Äî so fold this correction into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now ‚Äî the CRAN plan owns the NEWS pipeline; [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the repo files; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); removed a stray Rplots.pdf (from load_all/probe) before committing.

Read git diff --stat after any regen ‚Äî a diff beyond your edits = the version mismatch; revert (git checkout -- man/ DESCRIPTION) and hand-edit the target \value, OR do the migration deliberately (option A). (2) devtools is NOT in the renv lib (roxygen2/pkgload/pkgbuild ARE) ‚Äî use roxygen2::roxygenise() not devtools::document(); pass an absolute package.dir to avoid the cwd-drift trap. (3) getPyramidPlot @return is CORRECT ‚Äî the S108-set ‚Äú4th drift‚Äù was refuted; do not ‚Äúfix‚Äù it (it returns pyramid.plot()‚Äòs oldmar = a par("mar") vector). (4) These help pages SHIP ‚Äî fold the corrections into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS at 2.0.0 (not edited now). (5) man/ is hand-edited (7.3.2 format) ‚Äî a later proper regen (after the version decision) reconciles cleanly since content matches source. (6) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call; a stray Rplots.pdf appears from load_all/Rscript plot calls ‚Äî remove before committing (done).
