What Session 111 Did

Deliverable: the owner‚Äôs pick (A‚Äô) ‚Äî one prose fix in vignettes/articles/breeding-group-formation.qmd: corrected the inverted focal-population description. The article said the focal set was ‚Äúthe founders still in the colony,‚Äù but the filter !(is.na(sire) & is.na(dam)) & is.na(exit) selects non-founders (animals with at least one known parent) still in the colony. Now reads ‚Äúthe non-founders still in the colony (those with at least one known parent)‚Äù ‚Äî the parenthetical defines the term inline so the fix also removes the ambiguity. (DONE ‚Äî verified two ways + re-rendered.) Resolves the S108-discovered inversion, open and carried forward as option (A‚Äô) through S109/S110. One-line fix only ‚Äî did NOT touch the roxygen drifts, the CRAN plan, or any other article (FM #18/#25/#8). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (documentation prose using an existing exported-function walkthrough; no R/ logic or test surface ‚Äî declared every response, same as S104‚ÄìS110). No AskUserQuestion ‚Äî the owner named (A‚Äô) directly; no scope sub-question remained. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as SOLO ‚Äî a one-line prose correction with a deterministic two-way verification has no breadth to fan out and no competing claims to adjudicate; a Workflow would be theater (the S106 / S100 call) ‚Üí Learning 105. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub before technical work) and confirmed fresh post-S110 state (the full Phase 0 reads were done this same turn). - What changed (4 edits, 0 new): EDIT vignettes/articles/breeding-group-formation.qmd (the one prose sentence, lines ~60‚Äì63). EDITS: CHANGELOG.md (S111 [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 105), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, test, NAMESPACE, man/, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, ROADMAP, CI-workflow, planning-doc, or CRAN-vignette change (the fix changes neither package contents nor roadmap state; ROADMAP needed no edit). - üö® THE REAL WORK WAS RE-VERIFYING AN INHERITED FLAG, NOT TRUSTING IT (‚Üí Learning 105): A flagged error from a prior session is a claim to re-check, not a fact to action ‚Äî the discoverer (S108) couldn‚Äôt fix it precisely because it wasn‚Äôt their deliverable, so the claim had never itself been re-verified against data. I confirmed it two ways before editing: (1) internal corroboration ‚Äî the same sentence trims the pedigree to the focal set ‚Äúplus the ancestors needed to compute their kinships,‚Äù and founders have no ancestors, so the focal set must logically be non-founders; (2) ground truth ‚Äî ran qcStudbook(examplePedigree) + the article‚Äôs exact filter: 327 focal animals, NONE founders, ALL with ‚â• 1 known parent (the studbook has 1,668 founders, zero in the focal set; the 327 matches the GVA article‚Äôs population-of-interest count). Both agreed ‚Üí fix safe. - Verification (build-equivalent): quarto render vignettes/articles/breeding-group-formation.qmd ‚Äî all 19 chunks executed on shipped data, clean HTML produced; prose-only change, computed output unchanged. Cleaned every render artifact before commit (.html, _files/, the quarto-generated .gitignore, .quarto/); no stray Rplots.pdf. - [news-vs-changelog]: website-only documentation = dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG only, no NEWS ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). Auto-memory: saved one NEW feedback memory ‚Äî [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] (owner: use plain ASCII in AskUserQuestion labels / presented option lists; non-ASCII punctuation slows selection) + its MEMORY.md index pointer. The doc work itself lives in the repo, not memory ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). The pyramid plots ONLY living animals (exit==NA) with a known age ‚Äî 46 of qcPed‚Äôs 89 living animals ‚Äî because 43 living animals (all male) lack a birth date ‚Üí no age ‚Üí cannot be placed. (2) That makes the example‚Äôs apparent ~3:1 female skew REVERSED from reality: the plot shows 35 F / 11 M, but the living colony is 35 F / 54 M (male-majority). A plot from incomplete data can show the OPPOSITE of reality, not just an attenuated version ‚Äî this became the article‚Äôs central honesty / QC-tie-in lesson. (3) Verified through 3 paths: quarto render (Quarto 1.7.33) ‚Üí clean HTML + both figures (deterministic shape ‚Äî no RNG/seed; qcPed ships a fixed age col; only the title date drifts via lubridate::now()); pkgdown::build_article("articles/age-sex-pyramid") (pkgdown 2.2.0) wraps it; an R CMD build tarball confirms vignettes/articles/ absent ‚Üí zero CRAN risk, 21 shipping vignette files intact. (4) Verified the verifier (2 lenses): code-correctness re-ran every number against qcPed (all 8 challenges held); pedagogy caught two honesty issues ‚Äî I confirmed both against the data/paper before FIX-FORWARD applying: strengthened the sex-skew caveat to say reversed (not merely ‚Äúunder-counts males‚Äù ‚Äî I had the 35F/54M numbers from my own probe and should have stated this first-draft), and reframed the Reference so V&R 2015 is the package‚Äôs origin, not the pyramid method‚Äôs source (the pyramid is general demography via plotrix::pyramid.plot()). Re-rendered (prose-only). - Discovered, NOT mine ‚Äî flagged: getPyramidPlot.R @return roxygen says ‚Äúthe return value of par('mar')‚Äù but the function returns plotrix::pyramid.plot()‚Äôs value ‚Äî a roxygen drift (joins the S108-discovered set ‚Üí now 4 drifts). Left untouched (FM #8). - [news-vs-changelog]: website-only documentation ‚Üí CHANGELOG only, no NEWS ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the 6 changed/new files; exclude .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT; removed all render/build artifacts + a stray Rplots.pdf (base-graphics default device from an Rscript plot call) before committing.

Gotchas: (1) getPyramidPlot(qcPed) plots ONLY living animals (exit==NA) with a KNOWN age ‚Äî fillBins drops exited and NA-age animals ‚Äî so for qcPed it plots 46 of 89 living, and the example‚Äôs apparent sex skew is reversed by 43 unaged (all-male) living animals. Any future demographic doc/feature must account for this input filter (a complete census needs birth dates). (2) Base graphics (plotrix): the plot call must be the chunk‚Äôs last expression; #| results: hide suppresses the (non-plot) return value. getPyramidPlot.R @return roxygen is WRONG (says par('mar'); returns pyramid.plot()‚Äôs value) ‚Äî joins the roxygen-repair set (A‚Ä≥). (3) The plot title embeds lubridate::now() ‚Üí the title shows the render date (cosmetic ‚Äúcensus as of‚Äù label); the pyramid shape is deterministic (no seed). (4) Ready-made article candidates are EXHAUSTED (4 done) ‚Äî a new article needs a fresh topic or new presentation material (slice 3). (6) breeding-group-formation.qmd ‚Äúfounders‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúnon-founders‚Äù inversion remains open (A‚Ä≤). (DONE ‚Äî verified via quarto render + pkgdown::build_article + an R CMD build tarball; two-lens adversarial review ‚Üí no must-fix, 2 grounded fixes applied + re-rendered.) EDITS: CHANGELOG.md (S109 [Unreleased]), ROADMAP.md (articles-so-far ‚Üí S107‚ÄìS109), docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md (¬ß7.1 slice-2 cell ‚Üí records the 3rd article), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 103), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, test, NAMESPACE, man/, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, CI-workflow, or CRAN-vignette change (the slice-2 infra already exists from S107; this article needed none of it touched). - üö® THE REAL WORK WAS GROUND-TRUTH-FIRST AUTHORING ‚Äî INCLUDING THE UNHAPPY PATHS (‚Üí Learning 103): (1) Ran qcStudbook() on real data in BOTH modes before writing prose. examplePedigree (3,694) ‚Üí standardized 3,694-row df; reportErrors=TRUE returns a diagnostic LIST (or NULL when clean), and each shipped error-demo set triggers exactly one finding (pedFemaleSireMaleDam‚ÜífemaleSires/maleDams; pedInvalidDates‚ÜíinvalidDateRows=3,4; pedDuplicateIds‚ÜíduplicateIds; pedMissingBirth‚ÜímissingColumns; pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam‚ÜísireAndDam); reportErrors=FALSE (production) auto-corrects female-sire/male-dam (s1‚ÜíM, d1‚ÜíF) and removes exact duplicates (9‚Üí8) but STOPS on missing column, invalid date, sire==dam, young parent, period-in-ID. (2) Running it REFUTED my own assumption ‚Äî I‚Äôd read the code and assumed invalid dates were coerced to NA; they actually stop the function. A behavior you only read in source is a hypothesis until you run it. (3) Verified through 3 paths: quarto render (Quarto 1.7.33) executes the chunks ‚Üí clean HTML (deterministic ‚Äî no RNG/seed; examplePedigree ships an age col so no Sys.Date() leaks; confirmed byte-identical across two renders); pkgdown::build_article("articles/studbook-quality-control") (pkgdown 2.2.0) wraps it in the site template; an R CMD build tarball confirms vignettes/articles/ is absent ‚Üí zero CRAN risk, the 21 shipping vignette files intact. (4) Verified the verifier (2 lenses): code-correctness (all 9 challenged claims confirmed vs qcStudbook.R + ~18 helpers) + pedagogy/render-determinism (byte-identical double render). No must-fix; confirmed the one real flag against the code (qcStudbook calls convertSexCodes(ignoreHerm=TRUE) ‚Üí H folds to U, never output) and FIX-FORWARD corrected the article‚Äôs ‚ÄúM/F/U/H‚Äù ‚Üí ‚ÄúM/F/U‚Äù; added a ## Setup {#sec-setup} section for house-style parallelism; re-rendered (prose-only). - [news-vs-changelog]: website-only documentation = dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S109 entry), no NEWS ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the 6 changed/new files; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); removed all render/build artifacts before committing.

Gotchas held firsthand and saved time: the articles/ prefix for pkgdown::build_article (verbatim, no rediscovery); ‚Äúdrop another .qmd, no further config‚Äù (held ‚Äî touched no config); the build-ignored-whole-dir proof by tarball; set_seed/determinism framing (relevant in reverse ‚Äî I confirmed QC needs NO seed, distinguishing it from the siblings); pkgdown 2.2.0 + quarto already installed (no reinstall); the artifact-cleanup list (pkgdown_site/, _files/, .quarto/, the quarto .gitignore) held exactly; standing-uncommitted-files + macos-dupe-scan disposition (applied verbatim). The ‚àí1: S108‚Äôs gotcha #1 named the symptom (‚Äúrender from the repo root‚Äù) but not the sharper trap mechanism ‚Äî a build command that does cd /tmp && R CMD build ‚Ä¶ strands the shell cwd, so the next git status/render silently runs in the wrong directory. I hit exactly that (caught immediately). Now captured as gotcha 1 below + Learning 103.

Self-assessment (Session 109): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD 7fedae91 = S108, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick (A ‚Üí studbook QC); claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) ground-truth-first authoring of BOTH happy and unhappy paths ‚Äî ran qcStudbook() in both modes on the real data + every shipped error-demo set, which refuted my own code-reading assumption (invalid dates stop, not NA-coerce) and produced the exact verified behavior table the article rests on; (2) grounded BOTH topic candidates via parallel recon before asking, so the AskUserQuestion was informed; (3) verified through 3 independent paths + proved zero-CRAN-risk authoritatively; (4) 2-lens adversarial review + verify-the-verifier held ‚Äî confirmed the real H-code flag against the code before FIX-FORWARD correcting the article (out-correcting an over-broad claim) and re-rendered; (5) held 1-and-done ‚Äî one article; did NOT author the pyramid, fix the sibling‚Äôs error, or touch the CRAN plan/roxygen; (6) 0 stakeholder corrections; plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; cleaned every artifact for a precise commit. Weaknesses (honest): (a) a self-inflicted process slip ‚Äî I used cd /tmp && R CMD build to keep the tarball out of the repo, which stranded the shell cwd in /tmp; the next git status failed (‚Äúnot a git repository‚Äù) ‚Äî exactly the trap S108 had flagged. Caught immediately and switched to absolute paths / git -C, no harm, but avoidable (‚àí1); (b) content authoring on now-well-trodden infra ‚Äî bounded difficulty; the value is QC-behavior accuracy + running the unhappy paths + verification rigor, not technical novelty; (c) the CI gh-pages site build is unverified locally (standing gap, S107/S108). I reserve 9‚Äì10 for a zero-snag delivery that is also CI-verified end-to-end.

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd. CHANGED (committed): CHANGELOG.md, ROADMAP.md, docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md (¬ß7.1 slice-2 cell), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 103), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Read firsthand (verification/authoring): the 2 recon Explore outputs, R/qcStudbook.R (full), the model article vignettes/articles/genetic-value-analysis.qmd, data/ listing, DESCRIPTION. Transient (NOT committed ‚Äî removed): /tmp/s109_qc.R, /tmp/s109_qc2.R, the /tmp tarball, and all render/build artifacts (pkgdown/, pkgdown_site/, vignettes/articles/.quarto/, *_files/, the .html, the quarto-generated vignettes/articles/.gitignore).

Gotchas: (1) cwd-drift trap (I hit it): a build command that does cd /tmp && R CMD build ‚Ä¶ strands the shell cwd in /tmp; the next git status/quarto render then runs in the wrong directory. Use absolute paths + git -C <repo>, or R CMD build <abspath> without cd. (2) qcStudbook(reportErrors=TRUE) returns a LIST or NULL (when clean), NOT a data frame ‚Äî you can‚Äôt get the cleaned df AND the error list in one call; production mode (FALSE) returns the df or STOPS. (3) Shipped error-demo data sets are the teaching hooks: pedGood / pedFemaleSireMaleDam / pedInvalidDates / pedDuplicateIds / pedMissingBirth / pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam ‚Äî each triggers exactly one finding. (4) Age‚Äìsex pyramid article (the next ready candidate) determinism caveat: getPyramidPlot() embeds lubridate::now() in the plot TITLE (date drifts) and getPyramidAgeDist() computes ages from now() ‚Äî but shipped qcPed carries a frozen age column, so getPyramidPlot(qcPed) only drifts the title date (cosmetic). It is base graphics (plotrix) ‚Üí the plot call must be the chunk‚Äôs last expression. (5) The 3 S108-discovered roxygen drifts (orderReport/calcFEFG/reportGV) remain unrepaired (A‚Ä≥, gated ‚Äî ships). (6) CI gh-pages build is NOT locally verified ‚Äî watch the next push to main/master (or a PR/release) for the pkgdown workflow result. (7) This close-out leaves add-methodology 9 ahead of origin (S101‚ÄìS109) ‚Äî push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (8) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (9) Discovered, NOT mine (still open): the breeding-group-formation.qmd ‚Äúfounders‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúnon-founders‚Äù prose inversion (A‚Ä≤). (10) Carried-forward CRAN gotchas remain valid: NEWS.md generated from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL Suggests; pkgdown/quarto are dev-lib-only (re-install after a fresh renv::restore); RSelenium undeclared; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

What Session 108 Did

Deliverable: ¬ß7.1 (adopted Hybrid doc policy) ‚Äî authored a second zero-CRAN-risk Quarto pkgdown article, vignettes/articles/genetic-value-analysis.qmd (a scripted, non-Shiny walkthrough of reportGV() on shipped examplePedigree: QC ‚Üí population of interest ‚Üí mean kinship + genome uniqueness ‚Üí the ranked report ‚Üí colony-level fe/fg diversity). (DONE ‚Äî verified via quarto render + pkgdown::build_article + an R CMD build tarball; adversarially reviewed ‚Üí no must-fix, 3 precision fixes applied + re-rendered.) Content production on the slice-2 mixed-mode infra S107 stood up ‚Äî not a new slice (drop-in .qmd, no new config). One article only ‚Äî did NOT start another article/slice, did NOT fix the sibling article‚Äôs discovered error (FM #18/#25/#8). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (documentation using existing exported functions + shipped data; no R/ logic or test surface ‚Äî declared every response, same as S104‚ÄìS107). One AskUserQuestion (pre-RED SCOPE decision, not a TDD gate): which topic ‚Äî owner picked genetic value analysis (from 3 grounded candidates: GVA / studbook QC / age‚Äìsex pyramid). No TDD phase-transition gates (none apply). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as 1 recon Explore agent + SOLO ground-truth-first authoring + 1 adversarial-review agent + direct multi-path verification (the S107 shape) ‚Üí Learning 102. - What changed (1 new + 5 edits): NEW vignettes/articles/genetic-value-analysis.qmd (the article). EDITS: CHANGELOG.md (S108 [Unreleased]), ROADMAP.md (slice-2 articles-so-far note), docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md (¬ß7.1 slice-2 cell ‚Üí records the 2nd article), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 102), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, test, NAMESPACE, man/, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, CI-workflow, or CRAN-vignette change (the slice-2 infra ‚Äî _quarto.yml, .Rbuildignore, DESCRIPTION CI dep, pkgdown.yaml ‚Äî already exists from S107; this article needed none of it touched). - üö® THE REAL WORK WAS GROUND-TRUTH-FIRST AUTHORING + VERIFICATION (‚Üí Learning 102): (1) Ran the real reportGV() pipeline before writing prose and let the printed output be the ground truth ‚Äî 327-animal population of interest (examplePedigree 3694 rows ‚Üí trimmed 704), 199 High Value / 128 Low Value, fe 109.67 / fg 47.62, kinship 327√ó327. This handed me the teaching example for free: rank-1 1SPLS8 has higher genome uniqueness but NOT the lowest mean kinship ‚Äî which is exactly why the ranking uses both metrics. (2) Source-checked the scoring scheme in the implementation, not the roxygen ‚Äî orderReport.R/rankSubjects.R (the High-Value mean-kinship tier gates on the z-score zScores <= 0.25, not raw mean kinship) and calcFEFG.R (fe = 1/Œ£p¬≤, fg = 1/Œ£(p¬≤/r), r = mean founder alleles retained ‚Üí fg ‚â§ fe; Lacy 1989). The article is MORE correct than the package‚Äôs own roxygen in 3 places (the orderReport @return ‚Äúmean kinship < 0.25‚Äù; calcFEFG‚Äôs description of p; reportGV @return ‚ÄúA dataframe‚Äù ‚Äî it returns a list). (3) Verified through 3 paths: quarto render (Quarto 1.7.33) executes the chunks on shipped data ‚Üí clean HTML (deterministic via set_seed(1L); the gene-drop behind gu/fe/fg is stochastic, mean kinship is not); pkgdown::build_article("articles/genetic-value-analysis") (pkgdown 2.2.0) wraps it in the site template; an R CMD build tarball confirms vignettes/articles/ is absent ‚Üí zero CRAN risk, shipped vignettes (a2interactive/a3manual/manual_components) intact. (4) Verified the verifier: the adversarial reviewer returned no must-fix; I confirmed each of its 3 low-severity flags against the code (meanKinship.R is colMeans incl.¬Ýthe diagonal ‚Üí ‚Äúall animals incl.¬Ýitself‚Äù; tier-2 sort ties break on ascending z-score; imports tier also requires id %in% founders), applied the fixes, and re-rendered (output unchanged ‚Äî prose-only). - Discovered, NOT mine ‚Äî flagged for a future session: the sibling S107 article breeding-group-formation.qmd comments its focal set is ‚Äúthe founders still in the colony,‚Äù but the filter !(is.na(sire) & is.na(dam)) selects non-founders (‚â• 1 known parent). One-line prose inversion; left untouched (FM #8 ‚Äî not this deliverable). - [news-vs-changelog]: website-only documentation = dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S108 entry), no NEWS (never ships; [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the work lives in the repo (article + analysis ¬ß7.1 + ROADMAP + CHANGELOG) ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the 6 changed/new files; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); removed all render artifacts (pkgdown/, pkgdown_site/, .quarto/, vignettes/articles/.gitignore, *_files/, the .html) before committing.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. A build-ignored, website-only pkgdown article changes NO package or app runtime behavior (no R//test/app-path change; the article doesn‚Äôt ship). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = the build-equivalent render (quarto render ‚Üí clean HTML, chunks execute on shipped data) and the real-consumer build (pkgdown::build_article on pkgdown 2.2.0) and the package-contents proof (R CMD build tarball). NOT FM #24 ‚Äî no build step mistaken for correctness; I verified the actual executed surface (the rendered report numbers) through independent paths. The one thing NOT locally verifiable: the GitHub Actions gh-pages site build ‚Äî confirm on next push (gotcha 7).

Every gotcha held firsthand and saved real time: the articles/ prefix for pkgdown::build_article (used verbatim ‚Äî no rediscovery); ‚Äúadding more = drop another .qmd in vignettes/articles/ (no further config needed)‚Äù (held exactly ‚Äî I touched no config); set_seed(1L) determinism; the whole dir is build-ignored (proven again by tarball); pkgdown 2.2.0 + quarto already installed (didn‚Äôt need reinstall); the standing-uncommitted-files + macos-dupe-scan disposition (applied verbatim). The ¬ß7.1 slices table was an accurate substrate. ROI very high. The ‚àí1: S107‚Äôs own article carries a factual prose inversion (its focal set labeled ‚Äúfounders‚Äù when the filter selects non-founders) ‚Äî a small quality miss in the predecessor‚Äôs deliverable that I had to notice and flag; its handoff was accurate (it just couldn‚Äôt flag an error it didn‚Äôt know it had). Also unflagged (general, fair): renders must run from the repo root for renv to activate rmarkdown ‚Äî I hit this only because my shell cwd drifted to /tmp.

Self-assessment (Session 108): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí HEAD 7f90e38c = S107, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick (A ‚Üí GVA); claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) ground-truth-first authoring ‚Äî ran the real reportGV() pipeline and built every claim on the actual output + the implementation, NOT the roxygen, which is how the article ended up more correct than the package‚Äôs own docs in 3 places; (2) the teaching example came from real data (rank-1 animal: high gu, not-lowest mk ‚Üí why both metrics); (3) verified through 3 independent paths (render + real pkgdown::build_article + R CMD build tarball) and proved zero-CRAN-risk authoritatively; (4) verify-the-verifier held ‚Äî confirmed all 3 adversarial flags against the code before applying, re-rendered; (5) held 1-and-done ‚Äî one article, did NOT fix the sibling‚Äôs discovered error or start another slice; flagged it instead (FM #8); (6) asked the one genuine scope decision (topic) up front, grounding all 3 candidates first; (7) 0 stakeholder corrections; plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; cleaned every build artifact for a precisely-scoped commit. Weaknesses (honest): (a) a self-inflicted process slip ‚Äî my shell cwd drifted to /tmp after an R CMD build ‚Ä¶ && cd, so one re-render failed with ‚Äúno package called ‚Äòrmarkdown‚Äô‚Äù; caught immediately and re-verified from the repo root, but avoidable (use absolute paths / don‚Äôt strand cwd) (‚àí1); (b) content authoring on now-well-trodden infra (S107 did the hard infra work) ‚Äî bounded difficulty; the value is genetics accuracy + verification rigor, not technical depth; (c) the CI gh-pages site build is unverified locally (same standing gap as S107). I reserve 9‚Äì10 for a zero-snag, zero-correction delivery that is also CI-verified end-to-end.

Gotchas: (1) Render from the REPO ROOT ‚Äî Quarto‚Äôs knitr engine relies on renv activating via the repo .Rprofile (cwd-based); rendering with cwd elsewhere (e.g.¬Ý/tmp) fails with ‚Äúthere is no package called ‚Äòrmarkdown‚Äô‚Äù. (2) pkgdown article name carries the articles/ prefix: pkgdown::build_article("articles/genetic-value-analysis") (bare stem errors ‚ÄúCan‚Äôt find article‚Äù). (3) Building generates artifacts to clean before commit: pkgdown_site/ (the site dest, NOT docs/), pkgdown/favicon/, vignettes/articles/.quarto/, a quarto-generated vignettes/articles/.gitignore (/.quarto/, redundant with the root .gitignore), a *_files/ support dir, and the .html ‚Äî the articles dir should end with only _quarto.yml + the .qmd sources. (4) set_seed(1L) makes the article deterministic; reportGV() does NOT seed internally (gene-drop is stochastic; mean kinship is not). (5) Discovered, NOT mine: breeding-group-formation.qmd mislabels its focal set ‚Äúfounders‚Äù (it‚Äôs non-founders) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs call to fix (A‚Ä≤). (6) 3 roxygen descriptions drifted from code (orderReport/calcFEFG/reportGV ‚Äî see A‚Ä≥); the new article is correct, but the package roxygen is stale. (7) CI gh-pages build is NOT locally verified ‚Äî watch the next push to main/master (or a PR/release) for the pkgdown workflow result (the S107 DESCRIPTION/pkgdown.yaml CI changes should install Quarto + render the .qmd). (8) This close-out leaves add-methodology 8 ahead of origin (S101‚ÄìS108) ‚Äî push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (9) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (10) Carried-forward CRAN gotchas remain valid: NEWS.md generated from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL Suggests; pkgdown/quarto are dev-lib-only (re-install after a fresh renv::restore); RSelenium undeclared; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

(3) Zero CRAN risk PROVEN by a real R CMD build tarball: vignettes/articles/ is absent; the shipping vignettes are unaffected. (4) Verified the verifier: a fresh adversarial reviewer flagged ‚Äú1/64 = second cousins‚Äù as wrong (claimed third cousins) ‚Äî but it confused coefficient of relationship (r; 2nd cousins = 1/32) with kinship coefficient (œÜ = r/2 = 1/64, which is what the package computes); the package‚Äôs own manual says ‚Äúsecond cousins‚Äù at this threshold. Original was correct, left unchanged. - [news-vs-changelog]: website-only doc/config = dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S107 entry), no NEWS (never ships; [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). Gotchas: (1) The _quarto.yml lives INSIDE vignettes/articles/, not the package root ‚Äî pkgdown turns that dir into a Quarto project; the single .Rbuildignore line ^vignettes/articles$ makes everything there website-only. Adding more Quarto articles = drop another .qmd in that dir (no further config needed). (2) pkgdown‚Äôs article name carries the articles/ prefix: pkgdown::build_article("articles/breeding-group-formation") (the bare stem errors ‚ÄúCan‚Äôt find article‚Äù). (3) CI gh-pages build is NOT locally verified ‚Äî DESCRIPTION Config/Needs/website: quarto + the pkgdown.yaml quarto-actions/setup step should make the GitHub Actions site build install Quarto and render the .qmd, but the actual Actions run is unconfirmed; watch the next push to main/master (or a PR/release) for the pkgdown workflow result. (4) Locally-installed pkgdown/quarto are dev-lib only, not in the lockfile ‚Äî re-install for local site builds after a fresh renv::restore. (5) Discovered, NOT mine ‚Äî flag for the CRAN plan: ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd is build-ignored (.Rbuildignore:32), so it does NOT ship to CRAN ‚Äî only 3 of the ‚Äú4 CRAN vignettes‚Äù (a2interactive, a3manual, simulatedKValues) actually ship. The analysis ¬ß1 marks ColonyManagerTutorial ‚Äúbuilt by CRAN: Yes‚Äù ‚Äî a discrepancy to reconcile during the resubmission (it‚Äôs effectively a website article, not a shipped vignette). (6) set_seed(1L) makes the article deterministic; groupAddAssign() does not seed internally. (7) This close-out leaves add-methodology 7 ahead of origin (S101‚ÄìS107) ‚Äî push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (8) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs keep-call. (9) Carried-forward CRAN gotchas remain valid: NEWS.md generated from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL Suggests; RSelenium undeclared; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

What Session 106 Did

Deliverable: ¬ß7.1 Slice 1 of the adopted Hybrid documentation policy ‚Äî convert the developer doc inst/extdata/meeting_notes.Rmd ‚Üí inst/extdata/meeting_notes.qmd (Quarto), making the three inst/extdata/ dev docs uniformly .qmd. (DONE ‚Äî git diff -M similarity 99%; Quarto renders to HTML; both filenames build-ignored ships=FALSE.) Implementation slice (one of the adopted Hybrid policy‚Äôs slices); the deliverable is the conversion. One slice only ‚Äî did NOT start slice 2/3/4 (FM #18/#25). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (format conversion of a build-ignored doc; no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR code or test surface; declared every response ‚Äî same as S104/S105). One AskUserQuestion (pre-RED SCOPE decision, not a TDD gate): slice 1 vs slice 2 ‚Äî owner picked slice 1. No TDD phase-transition gates (none apply). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode ‚Äî a single-file mechanical conversion (git mv + one YAML line) with a deterministic render + build-ignore check has no breadth to fan out and no independent claims to verify; a Workflow would be theater (the S101‚ÄìS105 call). ‚Üí Learning 100. - What changed (1 doc converted + 5 housekeeping): inst/extdata/meeting_notes.qmd ‚Äî git mv from .Rmd (preserves history) + the single YAML line output: html_document ‚Üí format: html (matching siblings claude_code.qmd / software_design_doc.qmd); body byte-for-byte (git diff -M HEAD = similarity index 99%, only the YAML line differs). Plus docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md (¬ß7.1 slice-1 row ‚Üí ‚úÖ Done), ROADMAP.md (slice-1-done note under the doc-engine policy), CHANGELOG.md (S106 [Unreleased] entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 100), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No .Rbuildignore edit needed (see below). No R/, test, NAMESPACE, man/, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, or vignette change. - üö® THE REAL WORK WAS THE VERIFICATION (‚Üí Learning 100) ‚Äî a rename-only ‚Äúconversion‚Äù would have hidden three things: (1) An Rmd‚Üíqmd SEMANTIC difference. The render warned that nprcgenekeepr::: (R‚Äôs internal-call operator, quoted from a 2020 CRAN-review reply, in prose) looked like a malformed ::: fenced div ‚Äî ::: is meaningful in Quarto, inert in R Markdown. Checked the rendered HTML: it renders correctly (mid-line ::: is not a div delimiter ‚Üí heuristic false-positive, no output defect). Left byte-faithful rather than rewrite the author‚Äôs historical prose for a cosmetic warning (FM #22). (2) The doc is NOT reproducibly renderable ‚Äî and that‚Äôs not mine to ‚Äúfix.‚Äù Its 5 R chunks hardcode 2020 absolute paths (/Users/rmsharp/.../20160816_GeneticManagementTools) and need packages absent from the default lib (standing renv-not-materialized); include=FALSE hides output but the chunk still executes, so a full render fails on the dead paths. It‚Äôs a historical meeting-notes log, not a live computational doc. Build-equivalent satisfied via quarto render --no-execute (YAML + structure ‚Üí 81 KB HTML, code shown not run); execution limitation stated, not silently skipped. Making it executable would be a behavior change (FM #8). (3) ‚ÄúIt was build-ignored‚Äù ‚âÝ ‚Äúthe new file is.‚Äù Confirmed by applying every .Rbuildignore regex to BOTH filenames in R: meeting_notes.Rmd and meeting_notes.qmd both ships=FALSE (covered by ^inst/extdata/meeting_notes\. ‚Äî extension-agnostic ‚Äî and ^inst/extdata/.*\.qmd$). Had the pattern been ...\.Rmd$, the conversion would have started SHIPPING the .qmd into the CRAN tarball ‚Äî a silent package-contents change for an archived package mid-resubmission. - [news-vs-changelog]: a build-ignored dev-doc format standardization = dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S106 entry), no NEWS (never user-facing). NO auto-memory ‚Äî the decision/slice now lives in the repo (analysis ¬ß7.1 + ROADMAP + CHANGELOG) ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the 6 changed files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. Converting a build-ignored developer doc changes NO package or app runtime behavior (no R//test/app-path change; the file doesn‚Äôt even ship). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = the build-equivalent render (quarto render --no-execute ‚Üí clean HTML) + the package-contents check (both filenames ships=FALSE) + the faithful-diff check (similarity 99%) ‚Äî all done. NOT FM #24 ‚Äî no build step mistaken for correctness; there is no runtime behavior to test.

Fair/light: S105 was a decision-recording session and S104‚Äôs inventory firsthand-read the four CRAN vignettes‚Äô engines, not this non-CRAN dev doc‚Äôs chunk internals (out of that scope) ‚Äî so the gap is reasonable, now captured in Learning 100.

I reserve 9‚Äì10 for a delivery that is both zero-correction AND lands a fully-executing verifiable artifact.

What Session 104 Did

Deliverable: Investigation + recommendation ‚Äî should the package‚Äôs documentation be migrated from R Markdown to Quarto to future-proof the documentation? (DONE ‚Äî docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md written.) Research/analysis workstream (SESSION_RUNNER planning rules). Research-only ‚Äî NO documents were converted (conversion is a separate, owner-approved implementation session; FM #18 held). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. Owner reframed mid-session: opened as ‚Äúis Rmd‚ÜíQuarto conversion contraindicated by build timing?‚Äù ‚Üí owner clarified ‚ÄúNOT overly concerned about timing‚Ä¶ more interested in future-proofing documentation.‚Äù Adapted: KEPT the in-flight timing/CRAN-viability research (still load-bearing as the guardrail) and ADDED a future-proofing pass. TDD phase = N/A (analysis doc, no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR code surface; declared every response). No AskUserQuestion gates (no TDD transitions; the deliverable is an analysis presenting the owner‚Äôs decision, not a code change). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as 2 sequential research Workflows + SOLO synthesis: pass 1 wwzzra7sf (9 agents ‚Äî CRAN-viability/sysreq/build-time/precompute/inventory + 3 adversarial verifiers), pass 2 w7tp1or3o (8 agents ‚Äî longevity/Posit-direction/realized-value/migration-cost/steelman + 3 adversarial verifiers); ~900k subagent tokens kept out of main context. The recommendation document is mine to author per the planning rules. ‚Üí Learning 98. - üö® RECOMMENDATION = HYBRID / partial adoption (owner‚Äôs decision; 3 options in the doc ¬ß7): KEEP the 4 CRAN vignettes on knitr/rmarkdown (zero CRAN risk, officially supported indefinitely, endorsed for this case by the Quarto maintainer); ADOPT Quarto on the non-CRAN surface where it carries no CRAN dependency and pays off ‚Äî pkgdown site, new long-form docs, slide decks, the inst/extdata/ dev docs (2 already .qmd). The long-form manual (a3manual.Rmd + 13 children) is the one deliberate fork (¬ß6.3): keep as a knitr vignette vs.¬Ýreposition onto the website as Quarto. - All 6 load-bearing claims survived adversarial refutation at HIGH confidence: (1) R Markdown is NOT deprecated ‚Äî rmarkdown 2.31 (2026-03)/knitr 1.51 (2025-12) actively maintained CRAN-critical infrastructure (Posit: ‚Äúnot going away, no plans for deprecation‚Äù; Xie laid off 2023-12-31 but Posit-funded contractor to keep maintaining knitr/rmarkdown); the only cost of staying is feature stagnation, not breakage. (2) Quarto CRAN vignette adds a SystemRequirements Quarto-CLI dep NOT guaranteed on CRAN machines (missing on macOS flavors 2025) + a documented transient ‚Äúno vignettes‚Äù NOTE ‚Äî bad for an archived package; maintainer cderv ‚Äúwould not advise‚Äù it for CRAN. (3) Realized Quarto benefit for simple single-language HTML vignettes is narrow (CRAN engine is theme:none/minimal:true ‚Üí callouts/tabsets/Bootstrap/multi-language disabled; only cross-references land). (4) Conversion can‚Äôt fix timing (same knitr R-chunk cost + fixed overhead ‚Üí slightly slower). (5) Precompute on the existing engine solves timing without Quarto. (6) Migration is mechanical/reversible; pkgdown supports mixed .qmd/.Rmd (2.1.0, 2024-07). - Firsthand inventory (not just the audit agent): all 4 CRAN vignettes use knitr engines (knitr::rmarkdown_notangle √ó3; a3manual = knitr::knitr); ColonyManagerTutorial is eval=FALSE (screenshots only); a2interactive runs real analysis; simulatedKValues has 3 sims n=10/100/1000 with set.seed(1L) before each (deterministic ‚Üí precompute-safe); a3manual is a 57-line wrapper over 13 manual_components/_*.Rmd. VignetteBuilder: knitr, no Quarto dep. The vignettes/*.html|.R|.md are git-ignored stale renders ‚Äî no precompute pattern exists. - [news-vs-changelog]: an analysis/planning doc = dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S104 entry), no NEWS ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], S101 plan precedent). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the new analysis doc + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (L98) + SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. An analysis document changes NO runtime/app behavior (no R//test change). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = two adversarial research Workflows (all 6 claims survived refutation) + firsthand vignette inventory (engine headers, child includes, gitignored renders). NOT FM #24 ‚Äî no build step mistaken for correctness; there is no runtime behavior to test.

What Session 103 Did

Deliverable: Phase 2 of the CRAN 2.0.0 submission plan (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md ¬ß4 Phase 2) ‚Äî archival ROOT CAUSE (CRAN ‚Äútested elapsed times‚Äù). Owner-scoped to Phase 2a (tests + native pipe + NEWS); Phase 2b (vignette rebuild timing) DEFERRED. (DONE ‚Äî Phase 2a; 2b remains.) Development workstream + strict TDD. Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE (Phase 2a). TDD phase = REFACTOR/mechanical ‚Äî every edit is no-numeric-change (skip guards change only WHAT runs on CRAN; native pipe is syntactic), so the plan‚Äôs RED-first simulation-number rule did NOT apply (avoided, not triggered; declared every response). Two AskUserQuestion gates: a pre-RED scope decision (defer vignettes 2b; convert ALL %>%) + the PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR gate spelling out every edit + verification. Owner steered live (build-vs-run deps; Config/Needs/build; native |> instead of declaring magrittr; NEWS entry; the harness-misattribution catch; the jargon flag). Right-sized SOLO ‚Äî measurement is CPU-sensitive (parallel checks would corrupt timing) and the fix is gated mechanical edits; a Workflow would corrupt the measurement and add no value. - Measure-first profile (the cause, named by data not assumption ‚Äî overturned the plan‚Äôs single-suspect framing): examples 6.6s total, slowest countLoops 1.28s ‚Äî none >5s, none flag (no action). Tests: the CRAN-running slow ones are the shiny module testServer tests (modGeneticValue 4.4s / modBreedingGroups 4.6s / modInput 2.7s / modBreedingGroups_groupAddAssign 2.2s / modPedigree_processing 1.2s; skip_if_not_installed("shiny") only ‚Äî shiny is an Import, so they RUN). The raw-slowest files (fillGroupMembers*/groupAddAssign, ~17s) are skip_if_not(user=="rmsharp") ‚Üí owner-machine-only, never on CRAN. Vignettes ~21s (ColonyManagerTutorial 7.8s / a2interactive 7.5s / simulatedKValues 5.4s) ‚Üí Phase 2b (the only iteration-reduction lever ‚Äî simulatedKValues n=1000 gene-drop, 3.68s ‚Äî would change displayed kinship numbers = a correctness regression, so it needs numeric-preserving precompute, a separate effort). - What changed (13 files): Tests ‚Äî file-level testthat::skip_on_cran() added to test_modGeneticValue.R, test_modBreedingGroups.R, test_modBreedingGroups_groupAddAssign.R, test_modInput.R, test_modPedigree_processing.R (removes ~15s CRAN time; CI/local unaffected). Native pipe %>%‚Üí|> ‚Äî vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd (5; dropped library(magrittr)), vignettes/ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd (3), tests/testthat/test_makeRelationsClasses.R (2), R/makeRelationClassesTable.R @examples (2) + hand-synced man/makeRelationClassesTable.Rd (\%>\%‚Üí|>). NEWS ‚Äî NEWS.Rmd Minor ‚ÄúCode modernization‚Äù bullet + re-rendered NEWS.md. Plus CHANGELOG.md (S103 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 97), this handoff. - Verification: full core suite via pkgload::load_all at NOT_CRAN=true ‚Üí 169 files, 0 failed, 0 errors (after installing the missing shinyBS Suggest in the dev lib ‚Äî the earlier ‚Äú52/69 errors‚Äù were 100% environment/harness, NOT regressions); at NOT_CRAN=false the 5 mod files skip (affected-file time 14.82s‚Üí0.01s). simulatedKValues.Rmd renders with native pipe (no magrittr); makeRelationClassesTable example runs; man/*.Rd parses; NEWS.md re-rendered with only the new bullet (no reformat); 0 %>% remain in any converted file.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not skipped. No R/ function BODY or app/runtime path changed (the makeRelationClassesTable edit is roxygen-comment-only; skip guards are test infra; NEWS is prose). The code that DID change is executed in vignettes/examples/tests and was run-verified (render + example + 0/0 suite). NOT FM #24 ‚Äî there is no untested runtime behavior masked by a passing build; I verified the actual executed surface.

Session 102 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 103): Score 9/10. S102 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT with the exact ¬ß4 Phase 2 pointer, flagged it as the critical path, named the prime suspect (simulatedKValues.Rmd), and carried the load-bearing rules: ‚Äúprofile first, let the data name the offender‚Äù (I did ‚Äî and it overturned the single-suspect framing: the cited ‚Äútested elapsed times‚Äù is the shiny-module tests, not the vignette) and RED-first any numeric-altering change (I respected ‚Äî deferred the iteration-reduction). ROI high. The ‚àí1: two inherited assumptions were stale/incomplete ‚Äî (a) the ‚Äúrenv NOT materialized, restore() required‚Äù gotcha: renv::restore() reported already-synchronized (the library lives at ~/Library/Caches/.../renv/library/nprcgenekeepr-229b003c/..., Imports present); the real gap is that the explicit snapshot omits ALL Suggests, so Suggests (testthat, shinyBS, ‚Ä¶) need separate installs ‚Äî a different problem than ‚Äúrestore first‚Äù; (b) neither the plan nor the handoff flagged the skip_if_not(user=="rmsharp") test guards (which make raw timing misleading) or that the package‚Äôs test harness needs load_all (not library()) to expose internals. All were caught by the measure-first discipline the plan itself prescribed.

Self-assessment (Session 103): 7/10. Oriented fully, claimed the session (1B stub before work), measured before concluding, and the measure-first paid off (corrected the single-suspect assumption; surfaced the rmsharp-only guards). Strengths: (1) profiled each surface honestly and re-measured when wrong ‚Äî when the owner asked ‚Äúwhy don‚Äôt you have shinyBS‚Äù, I found my real error (ran tests via library()+test_dir, which hides internals ‚Üí 52 false could not find function ‚Äúerrors‚Äù) and re-ran via load_all for trustworthy numbers; (2) respected the numeric-change rule ‚Äî deferred the only vignette speed-up that would alter displayed numbers, scoping to a no-numeric-change fix and gating it; (3) handled the owner‚Äôs live direction cleanly (native |> over declaring magrittr; Config/Needs/build for build-only tooling; per-surface dep precision); (4) verified thoroughly (0/0 regression after resolving the env gap, skip behavior both NOT_CRAN settings, render/example/.Rd/NEWS). Weaknesses (honest): (a) the harness misstep ‚Äî I should have used load_all from the start and not initially blamed missing Suggests for the 52 errors; the owner caught it (‚àí1+); (b) used jargon (‚Äúdragon-prone‚Äù) the owner objected to (saved as a memory) (‚àí1); (c) Phase 2 is partially delivered (2a; 2b deferred) ‚Äî owner-approved, but not the whole plan-Phase. Two genuine stakeholder corrections (vs the recent 0-correction streak). I reserve 9‚Äì10 for a clean-harness, zero-correction delivery.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. Phase 2a done + verified; nothing mid-flight. One AskUserQuestion ‚Äî the PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR permission gate spelling out every concrete edit + verification (RED/GREEN don‚Äôt apply with no test surface) + one scope question (delete ..Rcheck/ ‚Üí owner: delete). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (the 9-agent research already happened in S101; Phase 1 = a handful of deterministic mechanical edits with a deterministic build check ‚Äî a Workflow would be theater). ‚Üí Learning 96. - What changed (8 tracked files): (a) .Rbuildignore (+13 lines, end-anchored & paren-free per the perl-regex hazard): \.DS_Store$ + \.Rapp\.history$ (front-UNanchored so they ALSO catch man/.DS_Store + inst/extdata/.Rapp.history ‚Äî the plan‚Äôs root-anchored ^\.DS_Store$ would have missed man/.DS_Store), ^inst/extdata/.*\.qmd$, ^inst/extdata/README_modules\.md$, ^inst/extdata/example_usage\.R$, ^inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents\.R$, ^inst/extdata/submission\.txt$, ^inst/_pkgdown\.yml$. (c) R/appServer.R+R/appUI.R: added @return roxygen ‚Äî appServer = ‚ÄúNo return value, called for side effects‚Äù (Shiny server; confirmed it ends in module-server calls, no explicit return), appUI = ‚ÄúA shiny.tag.list‚Ä¶‚Äù. Do NOT bundle phases (FM #18/#25). Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): .Rbuildignore, DESCRIPTION, LICENSE, LICENSE.md, R/appServer.R, R/appUI.R, man/appServer.Rd, man/appUI.Rd (the Phase-1 deliverable); CHANGELOG.md (S102 [Unreleased] entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 96), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). DELETED: ..Rcheck/ (untracked; no git change). Read firsthand (verification): docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md (full), the 8 target files pre-edit, tests/testthat/test_appUI_version.R, tests/testthat/test_getVersion.R, R/appServer.R tail (return semantics). Transient (NOT committed, in /tmp): /tmp/nprcgenekeepr_1.1.0.9000.tar.gz (verification build), /tmp/s102_verify.R (Rd + ignore-sim script).

Gotchas: (1) Phase 1 is DONE + verified by a real tarball ‚Äî a future re-check is a delta, not a re-run. (2) renv library is NOT materialized (Learning 92) ‚Äî Phase 2+ REQUIRE renv::restore() first; until then only base-R checks work (Rscript --vanilla, R CMD build --no-build-vignettes). (3) .Rbuildignore is perl-regex ‚Äî an unbalanced paren even in a comment aborts R CMD build (keep lines anchored/paren-free). (4) NEWS.md is generated from NEWS.Rmd ‚Äî Phase 3 edits the .Rmd, never NEWS.md directly. (5) The hand-synced man/*.Rd \value may get a cosmetic whitespace diff when Phase 4 runs roxygen2::roxygenise() ‚Äî expected/benign. (6) This close-out leaves add-methodology 2 ahead of origin (S101 60b8c67c + this S102 commit) ‚Äî push when ready (accumulate-then-push; S96/S99 pattern). (7) No version bump happened (Version still 1.1.0.9000) ‚Äî Phase 3 bumps to 2.0.0. (8) The standing .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) remain uncommitted (owner‚Äôs keep-call).

Right-sized as a Workflow under ultracode (wy9xitgt6: 5 web-research + 4 read-only codebase-audit agents = genuine independent breadth; 396k subagent tokens kept out of main context) + SOLO synthesis (the plan is mine to author per the planning rules). - üö® HEADLINE (firsthand-verified, reshapes the whole task): the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN, not published. WebFetch of cran.r-project.org/web/packages/nprcgenekeepr/index.html ‚Üí ‚ÄúArchived on 2025-07-29 as issues were not corrected in time.‚Äù Last published = 1.0.8 (2025-07-26, = CRAN-SUBMISSION); prior 2022-11-03 archive ‚Üí 2025-04-24 unarchive (1.0.7) cycle; 1.0.8 was RE-archived ~3 days after publish. Most-cited trigger (R-pkg-devel thread) = CRAN test/example/vignette ELAPSED-TIME limits. ‚áí This is a resubmission of a (twice-)archived package: the root-cause timing fix is the critical path, a clean one-time local check is necessary-but-NOT-sufficient (CRAN‚Äôs PERIODIC re-checks archive established pkgs ‚Äî FM #24 at CRAN scale), and cran-comments.md must explicitly address the archival. - The plan = 6 phases, each a separate strict-TDD session with DONE-criteria + verification commands + STOP point + here-be-dragons: (1) static hygiene (.Rbuildignore cruft + DESCRIPTION defects + \value on appServer()/appUI()); (2) ‚öÝ archival root cause ‚Äî profile-then-fix example/test/vignette timing (the critical/dragons phase; vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd simulations the prime suspect); (3) the owner‚Äôs headline ‚Äî NEWS.Rmd rewrite into user-facing Major/Minor + bump DESCRIPTION ‚Üí 2.0.0 + regenerate README/CITATION.cff; (4) renv::restore() ‚Üí full local R CMD check --as-cran gate; (5) win-builder √ó3 + R-hub v2 + cran-comments.md ‚Üí owner submits; (6) post-acceptance tag + GH release + dev bump. - Evidence-based inventory (SESSION_RUNNER planning mandate) in the plan ¬ß3: every version-string location for the bump + historical markers NOT to bump (runGenekeepr.R:26 when="1.1.0", prior NEWS/inst strings, getVersion()-tracked files); DESCRIPTION/metadata defects ('mulatto'‚Üí'mulatta' typo DESCRIPTION:23+CITATION.cff:15; renv Config line before Package:; VignetteBuilder: knitr, rmarkdown‚Üíknitr; LICENSE year; missing \value); .Rbuildignore gaps (.DS_Store, .Rapp.history, inst/extdata/.Rapp.history, loose inst/extdata dev .qmd/.R/.md/submission.txt, inst/_pkgdown.yml); the 3 recurring false-positive NOTEs to pre-explain; no reverse dependencies (revdepcheck NOT required). Full NEWS Major/Minor/DROP classification in plan ¬ß6.3. - [news-vs-changelog]: a planning doc = dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S101 [Unreleased] entry), no NEWS (the actual NEWS rewrite is Phase 3) ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], S95/S97 audit precedent). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the new plan + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md + SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. Writing a plan changes NO runtime behavior (no R//test change). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = the firsthand CRAN-status WebFetch (the pivotal claim) + the evidence-based file:line inventory (9 read-only auditors). FM #24 N/A ‚Äî no build step this session (and the plan itself elevates FM #24 to a first-class risk for the executor: CRAN periodic re-checks, not the one-time check, archived this package).

Gotchas: (1) The package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (2025-07-29) ‚Äî any CRAN session is an archived-resubmission, not a routine update; the timing root cause must be MEASURED (profile examples/tests/vignettes under --as-cran), not assumed; cran-comments.md must address the archival (Learning 95). (2) NEWS.md is generated from NEWS.Rmd ‚Äî edit the .Rmd (plain ASCII) and re-render; never edit NEWS.md directly. (3) The 1.1.0.9000 NEWS section double-counts changes ‚Äî the rewrite must merge duplicates (plan ¬ß6.3 / dragon #4). (4) .Rbuildignore is perl-regex ‚Äî an unbalanced paren even in a comment aborts R CMD build (anchored, paren-free lines only). (5) renv library NOT materialized (Learning 92) ‚Äî renv::restore() is a prerequisite for the check phases. (6) A speed-up that changes simulation/sampling numbers is a correctness regression ‚Äî RED-first (dragon #2). (7) No reverse dependencies ‚Äî do NOT run revdepcheck (plan ¬ß3.5). (8) The owner performs the CRAN upload + email confirmation (Phase 5 STOP ‚Äî outward-facing). (9) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). What Session 100 Did

Deliverable: Open a pull request merging add-methodology ‚Üí master. (DONE ‚Äî gh pr create ‚Üí PR #51 OPEN, https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/pull/51. Base master ‚Üê head add-methodology; GitHub independently reports mergeable=MERGEABLE, commit_count=54. Left OPEN ‚Äî NOT merged (owner‚Äôs separate call). No R//test/NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION change; the PR publishes the already-committed S52‚ÄìS99 work for review.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Operational git/GitHub task (open a PR on already-committed work), not a code change ‚Üí TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared every response). One AskUserQuestion ‚Äî to confirm the exact public PR title/body before gh pr create (an outward-facing publish, SAFEGUARDS); this was a publish-confirmation, NOT a TDD phase gate. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (one gh pr create after deterministic git/gh pre-flight ‚Äî a Workflow would be theater; same call as the S96/S99 pushes). ‚Üí Learning 94. - Pre-flight (SAFEGUARDS) ‚Äî proved the merge is clean & purely additive BEFORE drafting: (1) gh pr list --head add-methodology --state all ‚Üí prior PRs #41 + #43 both MERGED (via merge commits), 0 open PRs. (2) git fetch origin master add-methodology. (3) The divergence looked alarming but was benign ‚Äî git rev-list --left-right --count origin/master...origin/add-methodology = 2 / 54, where the ‚Äú2 ahead‚Äù on master are exactly the prior Merge pull request #43/#41 nodes (git log origin/add-methodology..origin/master ‚Üí c6ad23dd, 0363ffe3), i.e.¬Ýadd-methodology‚Äôs own work merged back, NOT independent master work. (4) Three-part safety proof (‚Üí Learning 94): git merge-tree --write-tree origin/master origin/add-methodology ‚Üí exit 0, no conflicts; comm -23 of the two file trees ‚Üí no master-only files; the tip-to-tip diff is an exact mirror (master-vs-add-methodology = +406 / ‚àí6001, the inverse of add-methodology‚Äôs +6001 / ‚àí406 over 114 files) ‚Üí master is strictly behind, zero unique content. ‚áí merge gains exactly the 54 commits (S52‚ÄìS99), loses nothing. - PR body grounded in canonical sources, not commit-message memory: read NEWS.md (1.1.0.9000) + the CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] section for the user-facing summary; categorized the 54 commits (6 feat / 11 fix / 3 test / 33 docs). Drafted to /tmp/s100_pr_body.md, confirmed title+body via AskUserQuestion (‚ÄúCreate the PR as drafted‚Äù), then created. Verified post-create: base/head/state/mergeable/commit_count all correct. - [news-vs-changelog]: opening a PR publishes already-committed, already-logged work (the 54 commits each carry their own CHANGELOG/NEWS entries) ‚Üí not itself a new software change ‚Üí no new CHANGELOG/NEWS entry ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], the S96/S99 push precedent). Learnings (3C): Learning 94 added (the re-merge safety recipe + branch-model topology ‚Äî genuinely reusable for every future add-methodology ‚Üí master PR). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). - S100 CODA (post-close-out, owner-directed ‚Äî two follow-ons): (a) renv.lock ‚Äî an environment-generated update (R 4.5.2 ‚Üí 4.6.0 + 125 package-version lines; external renv snapshot, NOT produced by this session) appeared mid-session; flagged in the close-out report, owner said keep & commit ‚Üí committed lockfile-only as 638c17dd (chore(renv): refresh lockfile). (b) push ‚Äî owner then said push, so git push origin add-methodology published all S100 commits (ce255f99 handoff + 638c17dd renv.lock + this coda commit). ‚áí add-methodology ends FULLY synced with origin (0/0). PR #51‚Äôs head ref advanced to include these (57 commits total). (c) merge ‚Äî owner then said merge ‚Üí gh pr merge 51 --merge (merge commit, branch NOT deleted) ‚Üí PR #51 MERGED (7a8433b3 Merge pull request #51 from rmsharp/add-methodology, 2026-06-17T04:02Z); origin/master advanced c6ad23dd..7a8433b3. add-methodology survives (long-lived working branch), now has 0 commits not in master (all work merged). The git rev-list ‚Äúmaster 3 ahead of add-methodology‚Äù is benign ‚Äî the 3 accumulated Merge pull request #41/#43/#51 nodes (Learning 94). This S100-coda commit (recording the merge) is itself committed + pushed, so add-methodology ends 0/0 with origin again.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. Creating a PR changes NO runtime behavior on this machine (no R//test change); it publishes already-verified work for review. The verification appropriate to the deliverable = the three-part merge-safety proof (merge-tree clean + no master-only files + mirror diff) and confirming the PR landed correctly (gh pr view 51 --json ‚Üí base=master, head=add-methodology, state=OPEN, mergeable=MERGEABLE, 54 commits). (2) add-methodology is the long-lived working branch ‚Äî it continues after any merge; do NOT delete it. The ‚Äúmaster is N ahead‚Äù divergence in git rev-list is benign (prior merge-commit nodes, not master-only work) ‚Äî verify any re-merge with merge-tree + no-master-only-files + mirror-diff, NOT the ahead/behind count (Learning 94). (6) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards / stateReason not a supported --json field ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json state,body,...; for PRs gh pr view N --json number,title,state,baseRefName,headRefName,mergeable,commits,url.

Learnings (3C): none new (a push is well-documented; S91/S96 added none ‚Äî judged, not skipped). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. A git push publishes already-committed, already-verified work (S98 = a verified issue-body edit; S97 = a read-only audit); it changes no runtime behavior on this machine. Verification appropriate to the deliverable = confirming the remote ref advanced to the exact local HEAD (origin/add-methodology == 49740e03). FM #24 N/A (no build/compile step to mistake for verification).

Session 98 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 99): Score 9/10. S98 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1) with the exact ahead-count and commit list: ‚ÄúPush add-methodology to origin ‚Äî this close-out leaves the branch 2 ahead of origin (S97 02728e92 + this S98 commit).‚Äù Every element held firsthand: the branch WAS 2 ahead, both hashes matched exactly (02728e92, 49740e03), clean fast-forward. The gotchas (branch model: work on add-methodology, master via PR; the renv-unmaterialized + merge=TRUE method notes; the 2-untracked-files + .DS_Store disposition; the gh --json gotcha; ‚Äú#37 OPEN with an accurate body, future re-check is a delta‚Äù) were all accurate and load-bearing. ROI: high. The ‚àí1: purely structural ‚Äî a push is a trivial follow-on with almost no surface for a handoff to get wrong, so there was little to evaluate (symmetric with how S96‚ÜíS95 and S97‚ÜíS96 docked the trivial-follow-on handoffs).

Self-assessment (Session 99): 9/10. Re-verified the push-relevant state fresh (did NOT trust memory ‚Äî re-ran fetch, ahead/behind, the commit list) rather than assuming the ahead-count from the S98 handoff. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE the push). Pre-flight done properly: confirmed remote/commits/upstream AND fetched first to PROVE a clean fast-forward rather than blind-pushing. Verified the result both directions (rev-parse equality + 0/0). Declared TDD N/A honestly (no code surface). Scope discipline (1-and-done): pushed exactly the branch asked; did NOT open a PR, did NOT touch master, did NOT touch the 2 untracked files / .DS_Store, did NOT start any candidate issue. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO. The ‚àí1 (honest, inherent): the close-out itself necessarily creates a new commit (this handoff), so ‚Äúfully synced‚Äù requires a second push ‚Äî I do push it (ending 0/0), but a session whose deliverable is ‚Äúsync the branch‚Äù can never leave a zero-new-commit trace; inherent, not a defect, and stated transparently (same as S96).

Do NOT implement #28 (gated on #11/#12/#37/#46); do NOT close #37/#45/#1/#5/#9 without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence. #37‚Äôs body is accurate as of S98 ‚Äî a future re-verification is a clean DELTA against it, not a re-run (Learning 91/93).

Key files (this session): none changed except SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, test, NAMESPACE, man/, DESCRIPTION, CHANGELOG, or NEWS change. No transient files.

Gotchas: (1) add-methodology ends FULLY synced with origin (0/0 through this handoff commit) ‚Äî the next session‚Äôs close-out commit will again leave it 1 ahead until pushed; normal accumulate-then-push. (2) Branch model unchanged: work lives on add-methodology; master gets it via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), NOT via this push. No PR opened this session. (3) renv library is NOT materialized ‚Äî Rscript --vanilla + codetools for static analysis; renv::restore() before actual TESTS (Learning 92). (4) #37 is OPEN with an accurate body (S98) ‚Äî do NOT close without owner direction; future re-check is a delta. (5) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). What Session 98 Did

Deliverable: Update issue #37‚Äôs stale body ‚Äî keep it OPEN as the standing keep-as-public-API catalog (owner‚Äôs choice over ‚Äúclose #37‚Äù), refreshed to the HEAD snapshot. (DONE ‚Äî body edited via gh issue edit 37 --body-file; verified live + state=OPEN. Recomputed the authoritative unused set firsthand and reconciled the WHOLE body against it (programmatic identical() exact-match) rather than hand-applying S97‚Äôs delta-list. Result: 166 exported / 127 used / 39 unused** (35 fn + 4 S3) ‚Äî 5 strikes (modORIPReportingUI/modORIPReportingServer, getPotentialParents, removeAutoGenIds, chooseDate) + 2 additions (setAutoIdFormat, summary.nprcgenekeeprGV) + the getPedDirectRelatives docfix note + corrected totals/arithmetic. Plus a dated re-verification comment on the issue thread. No R//test/NAMESPACE/man//CHANGELOG/NEWS change.) Started / Completed:** 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Operational documentation task (edit a GitHub issue body), not a code change ‚Üí TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared every response). One AskUserQuestion ‚Äî to disambiguate candidate (1)‚Äôs two materially-different deliverables (close vs keep+update); this was a scope decision (the owner‚Äôs to make), NOT a TDD phase gate. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (one recompute + targeted firsthand checks + a body rewrite ‚Äî the audit fan-out already happened S95/S97; a Workflow would be theater). - Recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit applied to a PUBLICATION (‚Üí Learning 93): before editing a public issue body, I did NOT just apply S97‚Äôs recommendation list. I re-ran the issue‚Äôs own documented method ‚Äî codetools::findGlobals(merge = TRUE) over all 202 sourced R/*.R under Rscript --vanilla (/tmp/s98_reach.R; renv unmaterialized, Learning 92) ‚Äî independently reproducing 166 / 127 / 39 (35 fn + 4 S3), then reconciled every non-struck body entry against it with identical(body_nonstruck, authoritative) (/tmp/s98_check.R ‚Üí EXACT MATCH: TRUE). - The exact-match gate caught what the handoff‚Äôs delta-list could not: S97‚Äôs list was the delta-since-S78, but #37‚Äôs body was last updated at S65 (2026-06-12), older than S78 ‚Äî so two more entries were stale: chooseDate (left the unused set AT S78 via Map(chooseDate,‚Ä¶) setExit.R:54 ‚Üí invisible to a since-S78 delta) and setAutoIdFormat (a newer #44/#38 export never catalogued). Also fixed a pre-existing arithmetic error (the body claimed 39 but its origin-breakdown summed to 42; rebuilt so 22 + 17 = 39 holds) and a grep false-negative (NAMESPACE declares S3 methods as S3method(print,summary.x) ‚Äî comma form ‚Äî my first regex assumed print.summary.x and returned empty). - Every published claim verified firsthand: mounts (appUI.R:181,200 / appServer.R:286,302), the transitive removeAutoGenIds (getPotentialParents.R:42), the Map higher-order site (setExit.R:54), setAutoIdFormat‚Äôs no-direct-callers + app-reads-via-getAutoIdFormat (addUIds.R), all four S3 methods in NAMESPACE, the docfix commit (2a64770f, S87), and state=OPEN post-edit. - [news-vs-changelog]: an issue-body edit is dev-process bookkeeping (the underlying audit was logged by S97) ‚Üí no CHANGELOG/NEWS entry ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], S93/S94/S96 precedent). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Session 97 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 98): Score 8/10. Every baseline held firsthand: branch 1-ahead, 14 open issues, dashboard 98/100, ghost-check (02728e92=S97), the gh --json gotcha, the 2-untracked + .DS_Store disposition. The audit‚Äôs disposition table (incl.¬ÝsetAutoIdFormat at row 26) and recompute method were the raw materials that let me get the body right. ROI: high. The ‚àí2: S97‚Äôs explicit body-edit list (Finding #5 / Rec #2) said ‚Äústrike the 3 now-reached functions, add the 4th S3, note the docfix‚Äù ‚Äî but the real change-set was 5 strikes + 2 additions: it undercounted the strikes and never named chooseDate or setAutoIdFormat, because it was framed as a delta-since-S78 while the body lagged to S65. A maximal handoff for a ‚Äúkeep + update body‚Äù candidate flags ‚Äúthe body is at the S65 baseline ‚Äî updating to HEAD needs a full recompute-and-reconcile, not just the since-S78 delta‚Äù (exactly Learning 93). Light, fair dock ‚Äî the recompute method + disposition table were in hand; I had to apply them one step more completely than the literal list.

Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí 02728e92=S97), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs task; then disambiguated candidate (1)‚Äôs two-deliverable fork via AskUserQuestion rather than assuming a close vs a body-update. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit applied to a publication ‚Äî independently reproduced S97‚Äôs 39-set and reconciled the WHOLE body programmatically, which caught two stale entries beyond the handoff‚Äôs list (chooseDate, setAutoIdFormat), a 42‚âÝ39 arithmetic error, and an S3 grep false-negative; (2) verified every published claim firsthand before it went public; (3) made the body internally consistent, self-documenting (the ‚Äúwhat changed‚Äù block + audit reference), and left a dated thread comment for the otherwise-silent body edit; (4) produced Learning 93 (a genuinely non-obvious meta-lesson: a handoff delta is anchored to the handoff‚Äôs baseline, not the artifact‚Äôs). Scope discipline (1-and-done): edited exactly #37‚Äôs body + one comment; did NOT close #37, did NOT push, did NOT touch other issues / .DS_Store / the untracked files, did NOT start another candidate. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons. I reserve 9‚Äì10 for a harder delivery.

Gotchas: (1) #37 is OPEN with an ACCURATE body as of 2026-06-16 (S98) ‚Äî a future re-verification is a clean DELTA against THIS body (no longer stale); recompute + diff, don‚Äôt re-run (Learning 91/93). Do NOT close #37 without owner direction. (2) This close-out leaves add-methodology 2 ahead of origin (S97 02728e92 + this S98) ‚Äî push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (3) renv library is NOT materialized ‚Äî use Rscript --vanilla + codetools for static analysis; renv::restore() needed before actual TESTS (Learning 92). (4) Reachability method: findGlobals(f, merge = TRUE), NOT $functions-only ‚Äî higher-order args (Map/apply/do.call) are false-flagged unused (Learning 92/93). (5) NAMESPACE S3 form is S3method(print,summary.x) (comma, not print.summary.x) ‚Äî grep accordingly (Learning 93). (6) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (7) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards / stateReason not a supported --json field ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json state,body,.... (8) Branch model unchanged: work on add-methodology; master via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), not a push.

Deliverable: Audit issue #37 (‚ÄúExported functions not used by the app‚Äù). (DONE ‚Äî docs/audits/ISSUE_37_UNUSED_EXPORTS_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md written; read-only DELTA re-verification against the S78 triage. Headline: #37‚Äôs actionable surface is FULLY DRAINED ‚Äî both S78 wire-ins shipped + their tracking issues CLOSED (#47, #48), the surfaced docfix is fixed (S87 2a64770f), 39 unused exports = 0 wire-in ¬∑ 39 keep-as-public-API ¬∑ 0 retire. No R//test/NAMESPACE/man//issue-state/issue-body change.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Audit workstream (docs/methodology/workstreams/AUDIT_WORKSTREAM.md). TDD phase N/A (read-only audit, no code surface ‚Äî declared every response). No AskUserQuestion gates (no phase transitions). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (the per-function disposition was adversarially verified by S78 two days ago; a fan-out would be the exact Learning-91 redundancy ‚Äî the genuinely-new work is one deterministic recompute + targeted firsthand checks). - Scope = DELTA, set at Phase 1 (Learning 91 applied correctly ‚Äî the discipline S95 was flagged for): before any work I read the #37 body + its two triage comments (S65 2026-06-12, S78 2026-06-14) + both prior audit reports (BACKLOG_STALENESS_AUDIT_2026-06-12.md, IMPLEMENTED_BUT_OPEN_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md) + CHANGELOG.md. Established that S78 already verified the per-function disposition (2 wire-in ¬∑ 37 keep ¬∑ 0 retire) with an adversarial completeness critic. So I did NOT re-run S78‚Äôs triage ‚Äî I recomputed the set, diffed against S78, and confirmed the wire-in/docfix status. #37 has now been re-verified 4√ó (S65, S78, S95, S97). - Recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit caught a method bug (‚Üí Learning 92): the renv project library is NOT materialized in this checkout (renv.lock + renv/activate.R but no renv/library ‚Üí .Rprofile‚Äôs source("renv/activate.R") re-bootstraps an empty renv and pkgload::load_all/devtools die). So I recomputed reachability statically ‚Äî sys.source all 202 R/*.R into a throwaway env + codetools::findGlobals under Rscript --vanilla (base lib only; /tmp/s97_reach2.R). First pass used findGlobals(merge=FALSE)$functions and FALSE-flagged chooseDate unused; firsthand check found Map(chooseDate, ‚Ä¶) at R/setExit.R:54 (passed as a value ‚Üí $variables, not $functions). merge=TRUE (the correct conservative ‚Äúapp uses this‚Äù test) reconciled it with S78. Result: 127 app-used / 39 unused (35 functions + 4 S3 methods); 202/202 files sourced (100% coverage). - The delta vs S78, all verified firsthand: (1) #47 ORIP module mounted appUI.R:181/appServer.R:286, issue CLOSED ‚Üí left the unused set. (2) #48 getPotentialParents wired via NEW R/modPotentialParents.R:150, mounted appUI.R:200/appServer.R:302, issue CLOSED ‚Üí left the set (removeAutoGenIds follows transitively via getPotentialParents.R:42). (3) Docfix FIXED ‚Äî getPedDirectRelatives @examples now invokes itself (was the getLkDirectRelatives copy-paste defect), fixed S87 2a64770f. (4) Logging island unchanged ‚Äî safeExecute/logModuleEvent/savePlotToFile still 0 live callers (grep) ‚Üí safeExecute the lone conditional future-retire. (5) Minor: issue body lists 3 unused S3 methods; verified set is 4 (adds summary.nprcgenekeeprGV). - [news-vs-changelog]: internal audit findings doc = dev-process history ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S97 entry), no NEWS. [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the audit report + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md + SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): docs/audits/ISSUE_37_UNUSED_EXPORTS_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md (the deliverable). CHANGED (committed): CHANGELOG.md (S97 [Unreleased] entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 92 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Gotchas: (1) #37 is still OPEN ‚Äî its disposition (close vs keep-and-update-body) is an owner judgment call; do NOT close without owner direction. (2) renv library is NOT materialized in this checkout ‚Äî pkgload::load_all/devtools::* fail (the .Rprofile re-bootstraps an empty renv). To run actual TESTS you must renv::restore() first; for static analysis (like this audit) use Rscript --vanilla + codetools (base lib). (3) Reachability method: use codetools::findGlobals(f, merge=TRUE), NOT $functions-only ‚Äî functions passed to Map/apply/do.call (e.g.¬ÝchooseDate in setExit.R:54) are in $variables and get false-flagged unused (Learning 92). (4) This close-out leaves add-methodology 1 ahead of origin ‚Äî push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (5) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (6) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards / stateReason not a supported --json field ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json state,closed,.... (7) Branch model unchanged: work on add-methodology; master via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), not a push.

What Session 96 Did

Deliverable: Push add-methodology to origin ‚Äî published the 4 accumulated unpushed commits (S92 4f9a0fe1, S93 ecaa4006, S94 d06552ec, S95 fd8b260e). (DONE ‚Äî fast-forward 0a677c29..fd8b260e add-methodology -> add-methodology; verified local HEAD == origin/add-methodology == fd8b260e, 0 ahead / 0 behind. No code/test/doc change; pure git operation. THIS handoff commit is then committed AND pushed too, so add-methodology ends FULLY in sync with origin ‚Äî git status clean, 0 ahead / 0 behind.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Operational task (publish committed work), not a code change ‚Üí TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared as such every response). No AskUserQuestion gates (no phase transitions). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (one outward-facing git command ‚Äî a Workflow would be theater). Same shape as S91‚Äôs push. - Pre-flight (SAFEGUARDS): git remote -v (origin = https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr.git); git fetch origin add-methodology FIRST ‚Üí git rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...origin/add-methodology = 4 ahead / 0 behind = clean fast-forward (no surprise remote commits, no force-push needed); git log origin/add-methodology..HEAD confirmed exactly the 4 expected commits (S92/S93/S94/S95). - Push: plain git push origin add-methodology ‚Üí fast-forward 0a677c29..fd8b260e. Verified BOTH directions: git rev-parse HEAD == git rev-parse origin/add-methodology == fd8b260e, and post-push ahead/behind = 0/0. - No CHANGELOG/NEWS entry: the 4 pushed commits already carry their own CHANGELOG/NEWS entries (added S92‚ÄìS95); a git push of already-logged work is not itself a software change ‚Üí nothing new to log (consistent with [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). Learnings (3C): none new (a push is well-documented; S91 added none ‚Äî judged, not skipped). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. A git push publishes already-committed, already-verified work (S95 was a read-only audit; S92‚ÄìS94 each Phase-3E-verified or N/A at their own close); it changes no runtime behavior on this machine. Verification appropriate to the deliverable = confirming the remote ref advanced to the exact local HEAD, which I did (origin/add-methodology == fd8b260e). FM #24 N/A (no build/compile step to mistake for verification).

ROI: high. The ‚àí1: purely structural ‚Äî a push is a trivial follow-on with almost no surface for a handoff to get wrong, so there was little to evaluate; not a fault of S95 (symmetric with how S90‚ÜíS91 and S92‚ÜíS93 docked the trivial-follow-on handoffs).

Self-assessment (Session 96): 9/10. Re-oriented to the push-relevant state fresh (did not trust memory ‚Äî re-ran git status, fetch, ahead/behind, the commit list) rather than assuming the ahead-count from the S95 handoff. Verified the result both directions (rev-parse equality + 0/0). Declared TDD N/A honestly (no code surface). Right-sized SOLO under ultracode. The ‚àí1 (honest, inherent): the close-out itself necessarily creates a new commit (this handoff), so ‚Äúfully synced‚Äù requires a second push ‚Äî I do push it, but a session whose deliverable is ‚Äúsync the branch‚Äù can never leave a zero-new-commit trace; that‚Äôs inherent, not a defect, and I‚Äôm transparent about it.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. Branch is published and FULLY in sync (0/0 through this handoff commit); nothing mid-flight. 14 open issues. Do NOT re-run a whole-backlog implemented-but-open sweep ‚Äî do a DELTA against the S95 audit if anything closes (Learning 91).

Partly inherited (S62 wasn‚Äôt in any recent handoff, and a #49-close session wouldn‚Äôt naturally re-audit docs/audits/), so not a full collapse ‚Äî but a steeper dock than the recent 8s because the omission had a concrete, owner-visible cost.

Redundant work is a judgment defect even under ultracode‚Äôs unconstrained budget (FM #5 helpfulness-as-volume / FM #21 greenfield applied to process artifacts).
