(DONE ‚Äî issue CLOSED via gh issue close 49 with an evidence comment; verified state=CLOSED closed=true closedAt=2026-06-16T21:28:21Z. No R//test/NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION/CHANGELOG/NEWS change ‚Äî the gating feature itself shipped + was logged in S84 b980f998 (E2E hardened S86 4cea5691); this session is the administrative close of already-completed, already-logged work, plus a PROJECT_LEARNINGS Learning 90.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. The deliverable CHANGED mid-session on a recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit finding: it arrived framed as an implementation (‚Äúwork on closing #49‚Äù; I had ‚Äî wrongly, previous turn ‚Äî called #49 ‚Äúimplementation-ready, real implementation remains‚Äù), but a grep-first check BEFORE declaring any RED revealed #49 was already fully implemented in S84. So it became an operational close (close a GitHub issue), not a code change ‚Üí TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared as such every response ‚Äî same shape as S90‚Äôs close of #4 and S93‚Äôs close of #33). One pre-close AskUserQuestion (close-path confirmation, NOT a TDD phase gate ‚Äî the premise had changed). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (grep + git log + firsthand re-verify + one gh command ‚Äî a Workflow would be theater and can‚Äôt pause for the confirmation). - THE finding (recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit, applied to a NEGATIVE claim): I did NOT trust my own prior-turn assertion that #49 ‚Äúneeds implementation.‚Äù Before declaring RED, grep -rn "ORIP\|orip" R/ tests/ + git log --diff-filter=A -- R/shouldShowOripTab.R surfaced S84 b980f998 (‚Äúfeat: gate ORIP Reporting tab to ONPRC-only (#49); close #47‚Äù). Verified FIRSTHAND: (1) R/shouldShowOripTab.R:34-36 = isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center == "ONPRC") ‚Äî resolves the issue‚Äôs flagged ‚Äúopen product fork‚Äù as option (b) (the getSiteInfo() default ONPRC fallback with NO config file does NOT show the tab); (2) R/appUI.R:13,17-18,177-183 ‚Äî appUI(siteInfo=) computes showOrip and includes the ORIP tabPanel only if (isTRUE(showOrip)); (3) R/appServer.R:281-292 ‚Äî modORIPReportingServer mounted only inside if (shouldShowOripTab(...)). Re-ran the tests green at HEAD (did not trust S84‚Äôs green claim blindly): test_shouldShowOripTab.R 5/5, test_modORIPReporting.R 9/9 (behavioral ‚Äî renders real appUI(siteInfo=) HTML for ONPRC/SNPRC/no-config), test_modSiteConfig.R 30/30; plus the opt-in test-e2e-orip-module.R browser suite. - Owned the prior-turn error: last turn I told the owner #49 was an ‚Äúimplementation-ready TDD candidate‚Äù / ‚Äúreal implementation remains‚Äù ‚Äî that was WRONG (I checked issue/sibling states + the S86 commit message but did NOT grep for the implementation). Corrected it explicitly to the owner before closing. This near-miss is Learning 90 (grep for the implementation before asserting an open issue needs work; implemented-but-unclosed is the repo default ‚Äî #4/#33/#49). - [news-vs-changelog]: closing an issue adds no new software work ‚Äî the gate was logged in S84‚Äôs CHANGELOG/NEWS at commit time ‚Üí no new CHANGELOG/NEWS entry (consistent with [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] + S90/S93 precedent). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

The recovery was clean and the discipline that caught it (recompute-before-RED) is real, but a 10 doesn‚Äôt get awarded for needing to walk back a claim I should never have made; I reserve it for getting the negative right the first time.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 90 at tail). Read firsthand (verification): R/shouldShowOripTab.R (full), R/appUI.R:1-18,165-204 (the siteInfo param + conditional ORIP tabPanel), R/appServer.R:278-299 (the gated server mount), R/getSiteInfo.R (the center="ONPRC" no-config fallback at :66), tests/testthat/test_shouldShowOripTab.R, tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R, tests/testthat/test-e2e-orip-module.R (grep-level), plus git log --diff-filter=A -- R/shouldShowOripTab.R. No R//test/NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION/CHANGELOG/NEWS change. Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s94_close49.md (the close comment body).

Gotchas: (1) #49 is CLOSED (this session) ‚Äî do not reopen without owner direction. (2) #49‚Äôs ONPRC gate shipped S84 b980f998 (predicate R/shouldShowOripTab.R, UI gate appUI.R:177-183, server gate appServer.R:281-292); the no-config fork is resolved as hide-unless-an-actual-config-file-names-ONPRC (the getSiteInfo() default ONPRC fallback does NOT show the tab). (3) appUI() now takes a siteInfo param (appUI(siteInfo = getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE))) ‚Äî tests inject list(center=, configFile=) to make the gate deterministic; file.exists(siteInfo$configFile) is the ‚Äúconfig present‚Äù signal (contents irrelevant). (4) This close-out leaves add-methodology 3 ahead of origin (S92+S93+this S94) ‚Äî push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (5) Learning 90: when a deliverable is ‚Äúclose #N,‚Äù GREP/git log for the implementation FIRST ‚Äî implemented-but-unclosed is the repo default (#4/#33/#49); and a session that implements-but-leaves-OPEN must carry ‚Äúimpl SXX, OPEN pending close‚Äù in its handoff until close. (6) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (7) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards and stateReason is NOT a supported --json field in this gh version ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json state,closed,closedAt,.... (8) Branch model unchanged: work lives on add-methodology; master gets it via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), NOT via a push.

Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Operational task (close a GitHub issue), not a code change ‚Üí TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared as such every response ‚Äî same shape as S91‚Äôs push). No AskUserQuestion gates (no phase transitions). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (one gh command + a firsthand re-verify ‚Äî a Workflow would be theater). - The owner confirmation: the owner selected SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1) ‚Äî phrased by S92 as ‚ÄúClose #33 on owner confirmation that the completed @return doc is acceptable‚Äù ‚Äî by replying 1 to the orientation report. Selecting a candidate that IS ‚Äúclose #N on owner confirmation‚Äù constitutes the firsthand owner-confirmed evidence the standing close-only-on-owner-confirmation rule requires. - Recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit (applied to S92‚Äôs completion claim before asserting it publicly): did NOT trust S92‚Äôs ‚Äúdone + verified‚Äù claim blindly. Verified FIRSTHAND before closing: (1) R/fillBins.R:6-10 now holds the \describe{} block (males/females = integer count vectors per age bin), no TODO (grep -n TODO R/fillBins.R ‚Üí none); (2) the body actually returns that shape ‚Äî R/fillBins.R:36 list(males = maleBins, females = femaleBins), maleBins/femaleBins built from integer(0L) + nrow(...) over seq_along(lowerAges) (‚Üí one integer count per bin); (3) git log -- R/fillBins.R confirms 4f9a0fe1 (S92) is the commit that completed it. The close comment cites all of this + the consumer (getMaxAx() reads them as per-bin counts) + the @noRd source-read doc-completeness test (test_fillBins.R 11/11). - [news-vs-changelog]: closing an issue adds no new software work ‚Äî the fillBins doc change was logged in S92‚Äôs CHANGELOG entry when it was committed ‚Üí no new CHANGELOG/NEWS entry (consistent with [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] and S91‚Äôs push precedent: only completed work goes to CHANGELOG, and this work was logged at commit time). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). - Learnings (3C) ‚Äî none new to PROJECT_LEARNINGS (judged, not skipped): the close-on-owner-confirmation pattern is already well-documented across handoffs; S91 (the analogous operational push) added none. The one micro-point worth carrying ‚Äî a SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate phrased ‚ÄúClose #N on owner confirmation,‚Äù when the owner selects it, IS the firsthand evidence the standing rule needs ‚Äî is captured here in the handoff rather than bloating PROJECT_LEARNINGS with a near-obvious entry.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. Closing a GitHub issue changes NO runtime behavior on this machine (no R/ change, no man page renders, no app surface). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = confirming the issue actually transitioned to CLOSED, which I did (gh issue view 33 --json state,closed ‚Üí state=CLOSED closed=true). FM #24 N/A ‚Äî there is no build/compile step to mistake for verification.

What Session 92 Did

Deliverable: Complete the fillBins() @return roxygen documentation (#33) ‚Äî replaced the #' @return A list with two TODO: RMS provide description placeholder at R/fillBins.R:6 with a \describe{} block documenting both returned elements (males/females = integer vectors of counts per age bin). (DONE ‚Äî docs-only on the @noRd internal fillBins(); NO R/ behavior, NAMESPACE, man/, or DESCRIPTION change. Two NEW test_that blocks + a returnBlock() helper added to tests/testthat/test_fillBins.R: (1) a doc-completeness test (RED driver) reading R/fillBins.R via test_path("..","..","R","fillBins.R") + skip_if(!file.exists), scoped to the extracted @return block, asserting no TODO + \bmales\b/\bfemales\b; (2) a behavioral return-contract guard (green at HEAD, honestly classified). test_fillBins.R 11/11; document() no-op (no NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION change); lintr 0; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Development workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; gates via AskUserQuestion: a separate pre-RED test-approach scope question (doc-completeness + contract vs doc-completeness only) + PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR; 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. #33 left OPEN pending owner confirmation. - Recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit (applied to the issue‚Äôs claim): verified FIRSTHAND that the issue‚Äôs suggested @return is accurate before writing it ‚Äî R/fillBins.R:32 returns list(males = maleBins, females = femaleBins); maleBins/femaleBins are built from integer(0L) + nrow(...) (‚Üí integer vectors, one element per seq_along(lowerAges) bin); the existing values test confirms integer (c(0L,0L,2L,1L,0L)); the caller R/getMaxAx.R:18 consumes them as bins$male/bins$female (R $ partial-match of the plural names) = per-bin counts. ‚úì - RED design (the load-bearing craft): since fillBins is @noRd there is no man/fillBins.Rd, so S87‚Äôs tools::Rd2ex(man/<fn>.Rd) pattern (test_examples_invoke_documented_fn.R) is UNAVAILABLE ‚Äî the doc lives only in the SOURCE file, read via test_path + skip_if-installed (RED in dev, inert under R CMD check where R/*.R isn‚Äôt shipped). The false-pass trap I scoped around: fillBins.R‚Äôs TITLE already says list of two lists \code{males} and \code{females}, so a whole-file grepl("males", ...) would be GREEN with the TODO still present ‚Äî the test extracts ONLY the @return block (from the @return line to the next #' @ tag) and uses \bword\b boundaries (so the males inside females doesn‚Äôt satisfy the males assertion). All 3 assertions RED at HEAD for the right reason; the RED failure output literally printed the isolated @return line, proving the extractor scoped correctly. - GREEN: the issue‚Äôs verified \describe{\item{males}{...}\item{females}{...}} wording (matches the package‚Äôs existing @return convention ‚Äî 4 files use \describe, e.g.¬ÝR/getPossibleCols.R), all lines ‚â§80. REFACTOR = confirmed no-op (idiomatic, lint 0, lines ‚â§80). - [news-vs-changelog]: a @noRd internal‚Äôs docs never render to a user-facing man page ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S92 entry), no NEWS. [macos-dupe-scan]: commit only the doc/test/notes files; exclude .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. A roxygen comment on a @noRd internal changes NO runtime behavior (the function body is untouched; no man page even renders). The verification appropriate to a docs-only change = the test suite (incl.¬Ýthe doc-completeness test that PROVES the doc is now complete) + document() no-op + lint + full regression, all run and green. FM #24 does not apply ‚Äî there is no build-vs-runtime gap to mistake for verification.

(2) The doc-completeness test reads R/fillBins.R via test_path("..","..","R","fillBins.R") and skip_if(!file.exists) ‚Äî so it is RED-capable in the dev/source tree but SKIPS under an installed package / R CMD check (where R/*.R source isn‚Äôt shipped). Key files (this session): none changed except SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, test, NAMESPACE, man/, DESCRIPTION, CHANGELOG, or NEWS change.

Gotchas: (1) add-methodology ends FULLY synced with origin (0 ahead / 0 behind through this handoff commit) ‚Äî the next session‚Äôs close-out commit will again leave it 1 ahead until pushed; that‚Äôs the normal accumulate-then-push pattern (this session resolved the 3 that had accumulated S88‚ÄìS90). (2) Branch model unchanged: work lives on add-methodology; master gets it via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), NOT via this push. No PR was opened this session. (3) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (4) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json <fields>. (5) #4 is CLOSED (S90).

What Session 90 Did

Deliverable: Finalize #4 ‚Äî CLOSED the issue (owner-confirmed; S89‚Äôs fix 8a3e3631 was Phase-3E-verified) and added behavioral regression coverage for the Shiny upload path that S89 had covered only structurally (a deparse(body(modInputServer)) grep). (DONE ‚Äî two NEW test files, test-only; no R//NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION change. (1) tests/testthat/test_modInput_incomplete_final_line.R ‚Äî in-process shiny::testServer driving modInputServer‚Äôs getData with a TINY no-trailing-newline upload through read.csv + read.table; asserts the warning does NOT escape + all 3 records survive QC; FIX-SPECIFIC teeth PROVEN by un-muffle round-trip (both blocks RED un-muffled, green restored). (2) tests/testthat/test-e2e-input-incomplete-final-line.R ‚Äî opt-in shinytest2 browser E2E uploading a no-trailing-newline ExamplePedigree.csv through both reader paths; asserts the cleaned studbook flows end-to-end to all 3,694 records in the Pedigree Browser. testServer 6/6, browser E2E 2/2, full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error, lines ‚â§80.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Development workstream (regression backfill of already-shipped behavior) under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; gates via AskUserQuestion: a separate pre-RED scope question (both reader paths) + PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR, + a mid-GREEN reframe question when the investigation changed what was testable; 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. #4 CLOSED. - #4 closed first (owner gave the confirmation S89 deferred): the fix shipped + Phase-3E-verified in S89; closed with an evidence comment referencing 8a3e3631, the root cause, and the surgical muffle. - THE load-bearing discovery (cost ~6 browser diagnostics + several install/load_all round-trips ‚Äî see Learning 88): read.table/read.csv emit "incomplete final line found by readTableHeader" only for SMALL files ‚Äî the header/type-detection scan must actually REACH the unterminated final line; a realistic-size file (3694-row ExamplePedigree) never triggers it. AND the warning is a console-only artifact: in the assembled app it never reaches the DOM or app$get_logs(). ‚áí a browser get_logs warning-absence assertion is a TAUTOLOGY (it stayed green even un-muffled ‚Äî the un-muffle round-trip exposed it). Root self-critique: I chased ‚Äúthe app swallows the warning‚Äù for many cycles before validating the FOUNDATIONAL assumption (does my fixture even reproduce the warning in a plain read.csv? It didn‚Äôt). De-risk the FIXTURE, not just the observation mechanism. - Resulting honest division of labor (surfaced to the owner via the mid-GREEN reframe AskUserQuestion): the warning-suppression teeth live in the in-process testServer test (TINY 4-line fixture, where the warning IS observable via withCallingHandlers); the browser E2E owns end-to-end data integrity (a no-trailing-newline upload processes correctly to all records). Both reader paths covered in each. - testServer test (normal suite): drives getData with a data.frame(name,size,type,datapath) fileInput value + session$setInputs(getData=1) wrapped in withCallingHandlers; asserts !any(grepl("incomplete final line", captured)) and qcSummary()$records == 3, $errors == 0, for read.csv (fileTypeExcel) and read.table (fileTypeText). Teeth round-trip: un-muffle BOTH modInput.R read sites ‚Üí load_all ‚Üí both blocks RED (warned=TRUE); git checkout R/modInput.R ‚Üí 6/6 green. - browser E2E (opt-in NPRC_RUN_E2E): after upload+wait_for_module_ready, navigate_to_tab(app, "Pedigree Browser") ‚Üí #pedigree-pedigreeTable shows of 3,694 entries (the modInput QC outputs are in SUSPENDED hidden nested tabs and don‚Äôt reliably un-suspend; a top-level consumer is the robust signal ‚Äî Learning 88). - Verification: testServer 6/6; browser E2E 2/2 (real Chrome, INSTALLED app); full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (195 files; 5 warnings = the designed loadSiteConfig safety-net logs); all test lines ‚â§80 (REFACTOR reflowed two 81‚Äì82-char lines). [news-vs-changelog]: test-only (the user-facing fix landed in NEWS at S89) ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S90 entry). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit only the 2 test files + docs; exclude .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED ‚Äî PASS. The browser E2E IS the runtime verification (drove the INSTALLED app via real Chrome, both reader paths, end-to-end to the Pedigree Browser); the testServer test drives the real modInputServer reactive graph in-process. FM #24 answered ‚Äî did not treat compile/lint-clean as verification.

Session 89 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 90): Score 8/10. S89 pre-named this exact deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (2): ‚ÄúBehavioral E2E for the upload path: a shinytest2 file-upload of a no-trailing-newline file through modInput to close the one structural-only gap from this session (opt-in NPRC_RUN_E2E, mirroring test-e2e-). Small TDD/test-only.‚Äù ‚Äî which (with candidate (1), close #4) is exactly what the owner picked. Load-bearing elements that held firsthand: the precise gap (the modInput upload path readDataFile is a server-internal closure covered only structurally ‚Äî gotcha #5), the candidate to close #4 on confirmation, and every baseline (dashboard 98/100, ghost-check ‚Üí 8a3e3631=S89, the gh issue view --json gotcha, the 2-untracked-files disposition, the close-out-on-add-methodology branch model, the standing‚Äùdon‚Äôt close #1/#5/#9/#45/#28-gated without owner evidence‚Äù list). The ‚àí2: S89 framed it as ‚ÄùSmall TDD/test-only,‚Äú* which badly mis-sized it ‚Äî the muffle is not browser-observable and the warning only fires for small files, two facts that made a naive‚Äùupload a file, assert no warning in the browser‚Äù approach a tautology and forced the testServer pivot + the tiny-fixture discovery. Genuinely the implementer‚Äôs to discover (S89 never investigated browser-observability), so it‚Äôs a light, fair dock ‚Äî but a maximal handoff would have flagged ‚Äúthe warning is a console-only artifact; a browser may not even see it‚Äù rather than implying a straightforward browser assertion. ROI: very high on what/where; the sizing was optimistic.

Self-assessment (Session 90): 8/10. Oriented fully, reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick; closed #4 on the owner‚Äôs confirmation with an evidence comment; claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). Used AskUserQuestion for the pre-RED scope (both reader paths) + all three TDD gates + an honest mid-GREEN reframe when the discovery changed what was testable ‚Äî declared the phase every response; 0 stakeholder corrections. The strength = honest teeth: the un-muffle teeth round-trip caught a TOOTHLESS browser assertion before it shipped (it stayed green un-muffled), and I refused to ship a test claiming warning-teeth it didn‚Äôt have ‚Äî I surfaced the finding and split the deliverable into a testServer test that PROVABLY catches the regression (both blocks RED un-muffled) + a browser E2E that owns end-to-end. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (a contained two-file backfill with interactive gates; a Workflow would be theater AND can‚Äôt pause for the gates). The ‚àí2 (honest): I burned many cycles on a wrong hypothesis (‚Äúthe assembled app swallows the deferred warning‚Äù) before checking the cheapest foundational thing ‚Äî does my chosen fixture reproduce the warning in a plain read.csv? (it didn‚Äôt; the file was too large). A single plain-R fixture check up front would have saved most of the investigation. That self-critique IS Learning 88: de-risk the fixture, not just the observation mechanism; a teeth round-trip that won‚Äôt go RED means a toothless assertion OR a non-triggering fixture ‚Äî check the fixture first.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. #4 finalized (closed + behaviorally covered); nothing mid-flight. 16 open issues (#4 closed this session). Candidates: - (1) Push add-methodology to origin (now ahead of origin by several commits after this close-out; S51 promoted prior work via PR #41). - (2) Open issues #33 (complete fillBins @return docs ‚Äî small docs TDD), #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid settings ‚Äî overlaps open #46), #29 (rename makeGrpNum‚ÜímakeGroupNum ‚Äî a rename ‚Üí run a cross-reference grep inventory FIRST per the SESSION_RUNNER planning rules + SAFEGUARDS rename caution). - (3) #37 (exported functions not used by the app) ‚Äî an audit-shaped candidate. Do NOT implement #28 (still gated on #11/#12/#37/#46), do NOT close #45, and do NOT close #1, #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence.

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): tests/testthat/test_modInput_incomplete_final_line.R (testServer warning-suppression teeth + data integrity; local tiny_no_final_newline() + run_upload() helpers), tests/testthat/test-e2e-input-incomplete-final-line.R (opt-in browser E2E, both reader paths ‚Üí Pedigree Browser count; local write_without_final_newline()). CHANGED (docs): CHANGELOG.md (S90 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 88 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Read firsthand: R/modInput.R (the modInputUI controls ‚Äî fileType default fileTypeExcel, fileContent default pedFile‚ÜípedigreeFileOne, separator default ,; the getData observer; readDataFile with the two muffled read sites at :274 read.table / :281 read.csv; the qcSummaryUI/cleanedDataTable/downloadCleaned outputs in nested tabs; the module return cleanedStudbook/qcSummary), tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R (create_test_app opt-in gate, create_app_driver, upload_and_wait, wait_for_module_ready, navigate_to_tab, get_html_safe, E2E_TIMEOUT), tests/testthat/test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R (the sibling pattern: navigate_to_tab("Pedigree Browser"), of %d entries), R/appServer.R:38-43 (the flog.logger file appender ‚Äî why flog.debug does NOT reach get_logs). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s90_derisk/, /tmp/s90_derisk_tc/, /tmp/s90_diag*.R, /tmp/s90_ts.R, /tmp/s90_tiny.R.

Gotchas: (1) The "incomplete final line" warning fires ONLY for SMALL files (‚â§ ~5 lines ‚Äî the readTableHeader scan must reach the unterminated final line) AND is a console-only artifact (never in the DOM or app$get_logs() from the app). ‚áí it canNOT be teeth-tested in a browser; a browser get_logs absence assertion is a TAUTOLOGY. The suppression teeth live in the testServer test (TINY fixture). (2) modInput‚Äôs QC outputs are in SUSPENDED hidden nested tabs (dataInput-mainTabs; suspendWhenHidden=TRUE) ‚Äî qcSummaryUI/cleanedDataTable/downloadCleaned are empty/disabled until their tab is active, and neither set_inputs(mainTabs=...) nor a JS anchor-click reliably un-suspends them (bslib BS4 + broken shinyBS JS). Read counts via a top-level consumer: #pedigree-pedigreeTable in the Pedigree Browser (DataTables formats the count WITH a comma ‚Üí match "of 3,694 entries", not 3694). (3) data-ready=true is set server-side (in the getData observer) and does NOT imply the hidden-tab outputs rendered or that cleaned is non-NULL. (4) Teeth round-trip recipe (testServer): un-muffle BOTH modInput.R read sites ‚Üí pkgload::load_all ‚Üí run the testServer file ‚Üí both blocks RED ‚Üí git checkout R/modInput.R ‚Üí green. No install needed for testServer; the browser E2E needs devtools::install() first (drives the INSTALLED app). (5) The browser E2E uses the BIG fixture (3694 rows; warning never fires) = a pure end-to-end data-integrity net; the testServer uses TINY fixtures (warning fires) = the suppression teeth. Two complementary vehicles BY DESIGN ‚Äî don‚Äôt ‚Äúconsolidate‚Äù them. (6) flog.* writes to a FILE appender (~/nprcgenekeepr.log via appServer.R:38-43), NOT stderr ‚Äî so flog.debug("File read successfully. Rows: N") does NOT reach app$get_logs(). (7) #4 is CLOSED. (8) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (9) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json <fields>. (10) Installed package = muffled HEAD (reinstalled at the end of this session). Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 89 Did

Deliverable: Fix #4 ‚Äî reading an animal list or pedigree file whose final line lacks a trailing newline emitted "incomplete final line found by readTableHeader on '...'" (reported from a Shiny text upload, 0.txt). (DONE ‚Äî NEW non-exported @noRd helper muffleIncompleteFinalLine() muffles ONLY that warning; wrapped all 4 read sites; NEW tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R 7/7 RED‚ÜíGREEN; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error; lint 0 on the 5 changed R/ files; no NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION change; Phase-3E on the INSTALLED app confirms the readers no longer emit the warning, rows preserved, and the muffle is surgical.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Development workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR) + a separate pre-RED scope question (all-readers-via-helper vs named-readers vs app-only); 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - Reproduce-first (recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit, applied to the bug): before designing anything, wrote a no-trailing-newline CSV and read it ‚Äî confirmed the warning is noise, not data loss (read.table/read.csv read every row including the last; nrow == 3). That decided the fix shape: suppress the warning, not recover a row. Also confirmed grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed = TRUE) matches, so the muffle can target exactly this warning. - Owner scope decision (separate pre-RED AskUserQuestion): All readers, shared helper ‚Äî root-cause over symptom-only. The same bug exists identically at 4 sites (getPedigree.R:30 / getGenotypes.R:30 read.table; getFocalAnimalPed.R:44 read.csv; modInput.R:274+:279 upload readDataFile). The mechanism (surgical withCallingHandlers muffle) was settled by reproduction, so only scope was the owner‚Äôs fork. - RED: NEW tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R (7 tests / 12 expectations). Helper teeth: no-newline read ‚Üí no warning + all rows; an unrelated warning("...") still propagates (surgical, not blanket suppressWarnings); returns expr unchanged. Integration: getPedigree/getGenotypes ‚Üí no warning + rows preserved; getFocalAnimalPed ‚Üí no "incomplete final line" warning (its post-read LabKey call returns an errorLst without a DB ‚Äî the unrelated config-missing warning is acceptable, so I assert absence of the SPECIFIC warning); modInputServer body references the helper (structural lock for the server-internal readDataFile closure). All 7 RED at HEAD for the right reason (helper missing ‚Üí 3 errors object 'muffleIncompleteFinalLine' not found; readers warn ‚Üí fail; body lacks wrap ‚Üí fail). The 2 passing expectations at HEAD are the nrow == 3 data-preservation guards (data was never lost). - GREEN: NEW R/muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R ‚Äî non-exported @noRd internal helper (matches getRecordStatusIndex/readExcelPOSIXToCharacter convention) = withCallingHandlers(expr, warning = \(w) if (grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed=TRUE)) invokeRestart("muffleWarning")). No new imports (base fns). Wrapped each read: x <- muffleIncompleteFinalLine(<existing read call>) at all 4 sites. document() ‚Üí no NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION change (@noRd; reader signatures unchanged ‚Äî git status-confirmed). REFACTOR = confirmed no-op (helper + wraps minimal/idiomatic/lint-clean; one roxygen line-length reflow done in GREEN to reach lint 0). - Verification: new file 7/7 green (12 expectations); full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (192 files / 1046 tests / 167 skips; the 5 warnings are S85‚Äôs designed loadSiteConfig safety-net flog.warn logs ‚Äî confirmed by the ‚ÄúCould not find center‚Äù text); lintr 0 lints on all 5 changed R/ files. - [news-vs-changelog]: user-facing file-reading bug fix ‚Üí BOTH NEWS.Rmd‚ÜíNEWS.md (new bullet under 1.1.0.9000 ‚Üí Data input / quality control; rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY the new bullet ‚Äî pandoc‚Äôs straight‚Üícurly-quote + --‚Üíen-dash conversion is expected, not churn) AND CHANGELOG.md (S89 entry). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only source/test/doc/notes; excluded .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED ‚Äî PASS. devtools::install(quick=TRUE) then exercised the exported readers in a fresh session with library(nprcgenekeepr) (the INSTALLED package, loaded from the renv library cache, NOT load_all): getPedigree/getGenotypes on no-trailing-newline files ‚Üí no incomplete-final-line warning, all 3 rows preserved; getFocalAnimalPed ‚Üí incomplete-final-line warning gone but the unrelated ‚Äúconfiguration file is missing‚Äù warning STILL propagates (the surgical proof ‚Äî the muffle is not a blanket suppression); a control file WITH a trailing newline still reads cleanly (no regression). The Shiny upload path (readDataFile) is a server-internal closure ‚Äî covered by the structural test; a full shinytest2 file-upload E2E is the heavier alternative (not done ‚Äî stated, not silently skipped). FM #24 answered: did not treat lint/compile-clean as verification ‚Äî ran the installed readers.

Session 88 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 89): Score 8/10. S88 closed out the version-housekeeping cleanly and its ‚ÄúSUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick‚Äù listed #4 as a small-TDD candidate (candidate (2): ‚Äú#4 (incomplete-file no-trailing-newline)‚Äù), which is what the owner picked. Load-bearing elements that held firsthand: every baseline was accurate (dashboard 98/100, 17 open issues, ghost-check clean ‚Üí cef325d7 = S88), the gh issue view projectCards gotcha (used --json), the 2-untracked-files disposition (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html), the branch model (close out on add-methodology), and the standing ‚Äúdo NOT close any OPEN issue without owner-confirmed evidence‚Äù rule ‚Äî which directly shaped my decision to leave #4 OPEN. S88 also correctly retired the stale-version gotcha it resolved, so I wasted no time on it. The ‚àí2: #4 was a bare one-line parenthetical with zero analysis ‚Äî no hint that the warning originates in read.table/read.csv, that it‚Äôs noise-not-data-loss, that it spans 4 read sites, or that the user report came from the app upload. All genuinely the implementer‚Äôs to discover, but a maximal candidate could have noted ‚Äúthe warning is from read.table; check the app upload vs the exported readers.‚Äù ROI: high on orientation/baselines, low on the specific issue (expected for a multi-candidate handoff).

Self-assessment (Session 89): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 17 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí none), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick. Used AskUserQuestion to pick the ONE deliverable (#4) among the 4 small-TDD candidates ‚Äî honored ‚Äú1 and done‚Äù rather than treating ‚Äúsmall TDD issues‚Äù (plural) as a license to batch. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). Recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit applied to the BUG: reproduced firsthand to learn it‚Äôs noise-not-data-loss (which set the entire fix shape) before writing a line ‚Äî and confirmed the muffle is surgical via the grepl match. Posed the scope fork as a SEPARATE pre-RED AskUserQuestion (all-readers vs named vs app-only), then gated all 3 TDD transitions; declared the phase every response. RED discriminating & honest: strong behavioral teeth on the helper + a ‚Äúan unrelated warning still propagates‚Äù assertion (proves surgical), confirmed all 7 RED for the right reason, and honestly classified the 2 always-green nrow guards. GREEN minimal, matched the package‚Äôs @noRd internal-helper convention (so document() is a no-op ‚Äî scope-checked). Did NOT treat build-clean as runtime-clean (FM #24): real Phase-3E on the INSTALLED package, including the surgical-propagation proof. Scope discipline (1-and-done): wrapped exactly the 4 read sites; did NOT touch the 2 untracked files, did NOT push, did NOT close #4 (left OPEN per standing rule), did NOT chase the other 3 candidate issues. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO even under ultracode (a one-bug fix with interactive gates ‚Äî a Workflow would be theater). The ‚àí1 (honest): the modInput upload path ‚Äî the very site the user hit ‚Äî is covered only structurally (deparse-grep), not by a behavioral browser E2E; a shinytest2 file-upload test would close that gap (noted as the heavier alternative, deferred).

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. #4 is implemented, verified, and runtime-smoke-tested; nothing mid-flight. 17 open issues (none opened/closed this session). Candidates: - (1) Close #4 on owner confirmation that the fix is acceptable (it is implemented + Phase-3E-verified; left OPEN only per the standing close-only-on-owner-confirmation rule). - (2) Behavioral E2E for the upload path: a shinytest2 file-upload of a no-trailing-newline file through modInput to close the one structural-only gap from this session (opt-in NPRC_RUN_E2E, mirroring test-e2e-*). Small TDD/test-only. - (3) Push add-methodology to origin (now ahead by ~43 after this close-out; S51 promoted prior work via PR #41). - (4) Remaining small TDD issues: #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid settings ‚Äî overlaps open #46), #33 (complete fillBins @return docs), #29 (rename makeGrpNum‚ÜímakeGroupNum ‚Äî needs a cross-reference grep inventory first per the planning rules + SAFEGUARDS rename caution). Do NOT implement #28 (still gated on #11/#12/#37/#46), do NOT close #45, and do NOT close #1, #5, #9, or any OPEN issue (incl.¬Ý#4) without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence.

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): R/muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R (non-exported @noRd helper), tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R (7 tests / 12 expectations; local write_no_final_newline() + captured_warnings() helpers). CHANGED (code): R/getPedigree.R (:30 read.table wrapped), R/getGenotypes.R (:30 read.table wrapped), R/getFocalAnimalPed.R (:44 read.csv wrapped), R/modInput.R (readDataFile text read.table :274 + CSV read.csv :279 wrapped). CHANGED (docs): NEWS.Rmd‚ÜíNEWS.md (Data-input bullet), CHANGELOG.md (S89 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 87 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Read firsthand: all 4 read-site functions; R/getRecordStatusIndex.R + R/readExcelPOSIXToCharacter.R (the @noRd internal-helper convention I matched); NAMESPACE (confirmed modInputServer exported, the 3 readers exported). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s89_probe.R, /tmp/s89_red_probe.R, /tmp/s89_phase3e.R, /tmp/news_before_s89.md.

Gotchas: (1) The ‚Äúincomplete final line‚Äù warning was NOISE, not data loss ‚Äî read.table/read.csv always read the last row correctly; the fix only suppresses the warning. Do not ‚Äúfix‚Äù it by appending newlines / normalizing files. (2) muffleIncompleteFinalLine() is surgical ‚Äî it muffles ONLY warnings whose message contains "incomplete final line"; every other warning still propagates (a malformed-file warning, the LabKey config-missing warning, etc.). Do NOT replace it with suppressWarnings(). (3) The helper is @noRd (non-exported) ‚Äî reference it in tests as nprcgenekeepr:::muffleIncompleteFinalLine; document() does NOT generate a man/*.Rd or a NAMESPACE entry for it (the package‚Äôs convention for internals). (4) getFocalAnimalPed on a no-DB machine emits TWO warnings (incomplete-final-line + ‚Äúconfiguration file is missing‚Äù); assert absence of the SPECIFIC incomplete-final-line warning, not ‚Äúno warnings‚Äù. (5) The modInput upload path (readDataFile) is a server-internal closure ‚Äî tested STRUCTURALLY (deparse(body(modInputServer)) grep), not behaviorally; a shinytest2 upload E2E is the heavier behavioral alternative if desired. (6) #4 is left OPEN pending owner confirmation. (7) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (8) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 88 Did

Deliverable: Housekeeping ‚Äî replace the stale Version 1.0.8 strings (carried since S56) with the current package version. (1) Runtime: the Shiny About panel (R/appUI.R:230) now renders paste("Version", getVersion(date = FALSE)) ‚Äî reusing the existing exported getVersion() helper so the displayed version tracks DESCRIPTION (1.1.0.9000) dynamically and can never drift again; (2) docs: CLAUDE.md:18 overview prose ‚Üí 1.1.0.9000. (DONE ‚Äî 2 edits; NEW tests/testthat/test_appUI_version.R (3 expectations) RED‚ÜíGREEN; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error; lint 0 on R/appUI.R; no document()/NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION change; Phase-3E AppDriver boot of the INSTALLED app confirms the live About panel shows Version 1.1.0.9000, stale 1.0.8 gone.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Development workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR) + a separate pre-RED approach question (dynamic vs hard-coded ‚Äî it changes both the test and the impl); 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - Owner approach decision (separate pre-RED AskUserQuestion): Dynamic from DESCRIPTION over a hard-coded literal ‚Äî root-cause fix (permanent-over-recurring; the string had drifted since S56). De-risked firsthand: grep getVersion( found the package ALREADY has an exported getVersion(date=) wrapping packageVersion("nprcgenekeepr") (R/getVersion.R), and appUI.R:47 already uses getVersion() (with build date) in the title bar ‚Äî so GREEN reuses the helper (getVersion(date = FALSE) ‚Üí clean "1.1.0.9000"), no new inline utils::packageVersion. - RED: NEW tests/testthat/test_appUI_version.R renders as.character(appUI()) and asserts the version scoped to the About panel (regexpr("About GeneKeepR") + substring(..., i, i + 200L)): it shows Version <packageVersion> and NOT Version 1.0.8, plus a guard that the About panel exists. Scoping was load-bearing: my FIRST RED grep‚Äôd the whole rendered page and the positive assertion FALSE-PASSED at HEAD ‚Äî appUI.R:47‚Äôs dynamic title-bar getVersion() already renders "Version 1.1.0.9000 (2026-06-16)", so ‚Äúversion present‚Äù was true regardless of the broken About tab. After scoping to the About region, both assertions failed for the right reason at HEAD (About region has 1.0.8, not 1.1.0.9000). See Learning 86. - GREEN (2 edits, no document()): R/appUI.R:230 p("Version 1.0.8") ‚Üí p(paste("Version", getVersion(date = FALSE))); CLAUDE.md:18 (Version 1.0.8) ‚Üí (Version 1.1.0.9000). Body-only change to appUI() ‚Üí no .Rd/NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION change (utils already in Imports; getVersion/appUI already exported). REFACTOR = confirmed no-op (reuses the existing helper; minimal/idiomatic). - Verification: new test 3/3 green; full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (the 5 warnings are the designed loadSiteConfig safety-net flog.warn logs from S85‚Äôs test_loadSiteConfig.R; 167 skips = opt-in/browser tests); lintr 0 lints on R/appUI.R. - [news-vs-changelog]: user-facing (the displayed app version) ‚Üí BOTH NEWS.Rmd‚ÜíNEWS.md (new bullet under 1.1.0.9000 ‚Üí Shiny application; rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY the new bullet ‚Äî pandoc‚Äôs straight‚Üícurly-quote conversion is expected, not churn) AND CHANGELOG.md (S88 entry). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only source/test/doc/notes; excluded .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED ‚Äî PASS. devtools::install() then a shinytest2::AppDriver boot of the installed stock app (shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)): the live page body contains <p>Version 1.1.0.9000</p> (the About panel) and does NOT contain Version 1.0.8. The version is set at UI construction (not via server reactivity) and Shiny renders all tabPanels into the DOM at load, so the body-HTML check is definitive ‚Äî no tab navigation needed. (The /tmp/s88_phase3e.R navigate-click leg had a run_js multi-arg bug ‚Üí returned FALSE; cosmetic only ‚Äî the body-HTML check is the substantive proof.) FM #24 answered: I did not treat lint/compile-clean as verification ‚Äî I booted the installed app.

(2) The About-tab version is now DYNAMIC (getVersion(date = FALSE) ‚Üí reads packageVersion): it always matches DESCRIPTION, no manual bump needed. The title bar (appUI.R:47) uses getVersion() WITH the build date. (3) RED-test scope matters: as.character(appUI()) contains the version string in TWO places (title bar :47 with date, About tab :230 without) ‚Äî any test asserting on rendered appUI() HTML MUST scope to the element under test or the positive assertion false-passes (Learning 86). (4) test_appUI_version.R runs everywhere ‚Äî under load_all/dev AND an installed package (it only renders appUI() + reads packageVersion; no man/ dependency, unlike S87‚Äôs Rd2ex test), and it is NOT opt-in (no browser). (5) Phase-3E for a build-time UI string = AppDriver body-HTML check on the INSTALLED app (tabPanels are in the DOM at load ‚Üí no navigation needed); install dev first. (6) NEWS workflow: edit NEWS.Rmd (source), rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd") ‚Üí NEWS.md, diff against a pre-render backup ‚Äî pandoc converts straight‚Üícurly quotes (expected, not churn); diff was EXACTLY the new bullet here. (7) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (8) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json <fields>. (DONE ‚Äî 3 R/ @examples edits + 3 regenerated man/*.Rd; 3 NEW test files; the 4 example assertions RED‚ÜíGREEN; coverage tests pass + teeth-checked; all 3 fixed examples run clean; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error; lint 0 on changed R/; NAMESPACE unchanged. Test+docs only ‚Äî no behavior change.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Development workstream (documentation + test backfill) under Strict TDD. Owner chose Full sweep + tests via AskUserQuestion. - RED: NEW tests/testthat/test_examples_invoke_documented_fn.R ‚Äî extracts each function‚Äôs @examples from man/<fn>.Rd via tools::Rd2ex (skips under installed pkg with no man/) and asserts the example calls <fn>( + that getPedDirectRelatives does NOT call getLkDirectRelatives(. 4 assertions failed for the right reason at HEAD. Plus 2 coverage files (test_kinshipMatrixToKValues.R, test_getAncestors.R) ‚Äî honest degenerate cycle (functions already correct): hand-derived expected values, teeth-checked by perturbation (perturbed value ‚Üí FAIL), declared up front. - GREEN (3 @examples edits + document()): getPedDirectRelatives ‚Üí getPedDirectRelatives(ids = "E", ped = nprcgenekeepr::lacy1989Ped); cumulateSimKinships ‚Üí final call cumulateSimKinships(ped, allSimParents, pop, n = 10); getIdsWithOneParent ‚Üí added closing getIdsWithOneParent(p). document() regenerated ONLY the 3 corresponding man/*.Rd (verified git status; NAMESPACE untouched). Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A ‚Äî STATED, not silently skipped. The deliverable is documentation (roxygen @examples) + tests only; it changes no runtime behavior (no startup, service registration, dispatch, or config-resolution change). The build-equivalent for a docs change is rendering/running, which I performed: document() regenerated the 3 man/*.Rd cleanly and all 3 corrected examples execute without error (R CMD check‚Äôs own example-runner equivalent). Key files (this session): NEW (committed): tests/testthat/test_examples_invoke_documented_fn.R (3 structural example tests + 1 negative assertion; local exampleCode() Rd2ex helper), tests/testthat/test_kinshipMatrixToKValues.R (shape/row-count/coefficient/named+unnamed; local getK() lookup), tests/testthat/test_getAncestors.R (founder/NA‚Üícharacter(0), 1- & multi-gen lineage exact+setequal, createPedTree(lacy1989Ped) integration via F). CHANGED (code/docs): R/getPedDirectRelatives.R R/cumulateSimKinships.R R/getIdsWithOneParent.R (@examples only), man/getPedDirectRelatives.Rd man/cumulateSimKinships.Rd man/getIdsWithOneParent.Rd (regenerated), NEWS.Rmd‚ÜíNEWS.md (Documentation bullet), CHANGELOG.md (S87), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 85), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Read firsthand: R/getLkDirectRelatives.R (the copy-source example), tests/testthat/test_getPedDirectRelatives.R (the ids="E"/lacy1989Ped usage I mirrored), R/getAncestors.R + R/kinshipMatrixToKValues.R + R/cumulateSimKinships.R + R/getIdsWithOneParent.R bodies (to ground expected values + runnable example calls). Transient (NOT committed): none (used /tmp only via tempfile() inside probes).

Gotchas: (1) The example-invokes-its-own-fn test reads man/*.Rd via tools::Rd2ex ‚Äî it RUNS under load_all/dev (the project‚Äôs standard test invocation) and SKIPS under an installed package (no man/). So it does NOT protect under R CMD check‚Äôs installed-pkg test run; the example correctness there is enforced by R CMD check‚Äôs own example runner. (2) For a roxygen-example fix, ‚Äúexample runs without error‚Äù is a WEAK test ‚Äî broken examples often still run (getPedDirectRelatives‚Äôs buggy one soft-failed to NULL); the discriminating test is STRUCTURAL (example calls its own fn). (3) Use grepl(..., fixed=TRUE) for the <fn>( check ‚Äî "\\(" triggers ‚Äúunrecognized escape‚Äù depending on quoting, and fixed=TRUE also requires the literal ( (a call, not a mention). (4) document() can drift unrelated .Rd under a different roxygen2 version ‚Äî git status-check after and restore any file you didn‚Äôt intend to change; here it cleanly touched only the 3 intended .Rd. (5) NEWS workflow: edit NEWS.Rmd (source), rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd") ‚Üí NEWS.md, and diff against a pre-render backup to confirm ONLY the new bullet changed (render was stable here). (6) lacy1989Ped E is a FOUNDER (no ancestors) ‚Äî use F (sire=D, dam=E ‚Üí {A,B,D,E}) for a non-trivial getAncestors integration check. (7) CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:216 still say ‚ÄúVersion 1.0.8‚Äù (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) ‚Äî stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (9) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 86 Did

Deliverable: Durable opt-in E2E for the ONPRC-gated ORIP Reporting tab ‚Äî NEW tests/testthat/test-e2e-orip-module.R mirroring test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R (S82), driving the tab end-to-end in a real browser (shinytest2::AppDriver) and asserting the build-time #49 gate both polarities. (DONE ‚Äî 4 opt-in blocks / 9 expectations; GREEN run 9/9 PASS against the installed app via real Chrome; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error with the file 4-skipped in normal mode; lint clean. Test-only ‚Äî no R/, man/, NAMESPACE, or NEWS changes.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Development workstream (regression backfill of already-shipped behavior) under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR); 0 stakeholder corrections. Per the S82 precedent (closest analog ‚Äî opt-in E2E backfill), no separate pre-RED approach question ‚Äî the author decisions (helper placement, block scope) were folded into the RED gate‚Äôs Option 1. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - Honest degenerate RED‚ÜíGREEN (per Learning 80): the ORIP tab already ships (#47/#49), so there is no production code and the classic red-to-green does not literally apply ‚Äî declared at the PRE-RED‚ÜíRED gate. RED = author teeth-bearing assertions + confirm all 4 blocks self-skip cleanly with opt-in OFF (SSSS, 0 fail/error). GREEN = run green against the shipped/installed feature, re-deriving the gate outcomes first. REFACTOR = line-length tidy. - Four opt-in blocks (each skip_if_not_installed("shinytest2"/"chromote") + skip_on_cran()): (1) ONPRC ‚Üí tab accessible (assert_active_pane + the module‚Äôs unique body text ‚ÄúOffice of Research Infrastructure Programs‚Äù); (2) ONPRC ‚Üí content + download ‚Äî #oripReporting-siteInfo reports Center=ONPRC, the Export ORIP Report button is in the pane, and app$get_download("oripReporting-downloadORIPReport") yields a Category/Metric/Value CSV with a Center=ONPRC Site row (deterministic ‚Äî the handler writes the Site section even with no pedigree loaded); (3) no config (stock app) ‚Üí tab ABSENT (navigate_to_tab‚ÜíFALSE AND grepl("oripReporting-", body)‚ÜíFALSE); (4) SNPRC config ‚Üí tab ABSENT (proves the gate keys on center, not mere config presence). - Config-injecting fixture (the load-bearing design move): a LOCAL build_config_app_dir(center) writes a temp app.R that Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with a complete documented-format .nprcgenekeepr_config>) BEFORE shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) ‚Äî S84/S85‚Äôs HOME-override Phase-3E recipe promoted to a reusable test fixture. The POSITIVE case can‚Äôt use the stock create_test_app() app (no config ‚Üí tab hidden); the NO-CONFIG negative case rides the stock app for free. The builder reuses the opt-in gate by calling create_test_app() for its skip side-effect (path discarded) ‚Äî NO change to helper-shinytest2.R. Config dir lives INSIDE the app dir so one unlink(recursive=TRUE) (registered AFTER app$stop()) cleans both. - GREEN verification: independently confirmed the three gate outcomes in-process FIRST (getSiteInfo() parse ‚Üí shouldShowOripTab(): ONPRC‚ÜíTRUE, SNPRC‚ÜíFALSE, no-config‚ÜíFALSE) ‚Äî Learning 80b applied to a predicate, so the browser run passed first try; devtools::install(quick=TRUE); then NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true single-file ‚Üí 9/9 expectations PASS (real Chrome). - REFACTOR (owner-approved): reflowed 6 over-length lines to ‚â§80 (split the long lkPedColumns config line into the shipped example config‚Äôs multi-line format, which getTokenList parses); re-verified parsing (center=ONPRC, 7 lkPedColumns tokens, gate TRUE) + a fresh 9/9 browser run (the config line is content the app reads) + full clean-regression 0/0 + lint clean. - [news-vs-changelog]: test-only (no R/ code, no user-facing feature; the ORIP feature landed in NEWS at S83/S84) ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S86 entry). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only the test file + doc/notes; excluded .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED ‚Äî PASS. The browser E2E run IS the runtime verification (strongest kind): real Chrome via chromote, real reactive graph, real config-driven ONPRC gating, real CSV download. All 4 blocks pass against the installed app ‚Äî 9/9 expectations ‚Äî including the ONPRC-gated tab present+navigable, #oripReporting-siteInfo rendering ONPRC, the report CSV‚Äôs Center=ONPRC row, and the tab genuinely ABSENT under both no-config and SNPRC. shinytest2+chromote are installed here, so this ran for real (NOT_CRAN=true + install-first required ‚Äî Learning 78).

Session 85 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 86): Score 9/10. S85 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (2) with the exact file/pattern/constraint: ‚ÄúDurable opt-in E2E for the ORIP tab ‚Äî tests/testthat/test-e2e-orip-module.R mirroring test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R (S82): must set an ONPRC config (the tab is ONPRC-gated since #49). Now easier ‚Äî loadSiteConfig means the e2e app.R can use the FULL documented config format (no need for the read.table-safe single-line workaround). Small TDD/test-only.‚Äù Every load-bearing element held firsthand: the file name + sibling pattern, the central ‚Äúmust set an ONPRC config‚Äù constraint, and ‚Äî the single most valuable pre-load ‚Äî ‚ÄúloadSiteConfig lets the e2e use the FULL documented config format‚Äù: S85‚Äôs own #50 fix is exactly what lets my documented multi-line ONPRC config boot clean end-to-end (the appServer observer no longer crashes), so I could write a real documented config instead of a stripped single-line one. The reusable HOME-override-in-app.R recipe (gotcha 4) was reused verbatim as the test fixture; getSiteInfo requires all 7 params when a file exists (Read-firsthand list) saved me a parse error. Baselines all accurate (dashboard 98/100, 17 open issues, ghost-check, gh --json gotcha, 2-untracked-files, stale version, branch model). Zero time lost on what to build, the config mechanism, or the runtime recipe. The ‚àí1: ‚Äúmirroring test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R‚Äù under-specifies that the POSITIVE case CANNOT reuse create_test_app() (the stock app has no config ‚Üí tab hidden), so I had to build a config-injecting app fixture ‚Äî a real structural divergence from the sibling; and conversely that the no-config NEGATIVE case is free from that same stock app. Both genuinely the implementer‚Äôs to discover by reading the gate. ROI: very high.

Self-assessment (Session 86): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 17 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí none: 504f0a6a = S85 close-out), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). Declared the TDD phase every response and gated all 3 transitions via AskUserQuestion with exact next-phase actions. Honest degenerate-cycle framing up front (no production code; RED = teeth + clean self-skip), folded the author decisions into the RED gate per the S82 precedent (no theatrical separate approach question for a test-only backfill). Recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit: read the sibling E2E, the ORIP module, helper-shinytest2.R, shouldShowOripTab, the appUI/appServer gating, getSiteInfo/getConfigFileName, and the example config firsthand ‚Äî did NOT trust the ‚Äúmirror the sibling‚Äù framing, and so discovered the positive case needs a config-injecting fixture (and the no-config negative is free from the stock app). GREEN‚Äôs real work first: independently confirmed all THREE gate outcomes in-process BEFORE the slow browser run (Learning 80b applied to a predicate) ‚Üí browser run passed first try. Did NOT treat skip/build-clean as runtime-clean (FM #24): ran the real 4-block browser E2E (9/9) ‚Äî the strongest Phase-3E. Two-polarity teeth: positive (namespace renders ONPRC + deterministic report-CSV Center=ONPRC row) AND negative (grepl("oripReporting-", body) ABSENT under no-config/SNPRC ‚Äî not a body-grepl tautology); the SNPRC block proves the gate keys on center, not config presence. No shared-helper growth: reused the opt-in gate via create_test_app()‚Äôs skip side-effect, keeping the file self-contained (sibling idiom, Learning 80c). Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (project methodology governs; one-file test backfill with interactive gates ‚Äî a Workflow would be theater). Scope discipline (1-and-done): no production code, did NOT touch the stale version string, the 2 untracked files, did NOT push or close/open issues. 0 stakeholder corrections. The ‚àí1 (honest): (a) the deliverable adds no NEW user-facing value ‚Äî it‚Äôs a regression net (though it closes the literal full-browser-chain gap for the ONPRC gate, incl.¬Ýthe negative cases the unit tests only cover at UI-string level); (b) the ONPRC content assertion is a substring match on #oripReporting-siteInfo HTML ‚Äî robust but coupled to that output rendering; (c) 4 browser boots make the opt-in suite slower than strictly necessary (block 1 could have merged into block 2) ‚Äî a deliberate failure-isolation trade-off.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. The ORIP E2E ships, is runtime-verified (9/9), and skips cleanly in the normal suite; nothing mid-flight. 17 open issues (none closed/opened this session ‚Äî test-only). Small TDD. - (2) Housekeeping: CLAUDE.md + the appUI.R About tab (R/appUI.R:216) still say ‚ÄúVersion 1.0.8‚Äù vs DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000 (carried since S56). - (3) Push add-methodology to origin (now ahead by ~40 after this close-out; S51 promoted prior work via PR #41). - (4) Open issues #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid settings), #33 (complete fillBins docs), #29 (rename makeGrpNum‚ÜímakeGroupNum), #4 (incomplete-file no-trailing-newline) are small/self-contained TDD candidates. Key files (this session): NEW (committed): tests/testthat/test-e2e-orip-module.R (4 opt-in AppDriver blocks / 9 expectations + the local build_config_app_dir(center) fixture). CHANGED (docs): CHANGELOG.md (S86 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 84 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Read firsthand (unchanged): tests/testthat/test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R (the sibling pattern), R/modORIPReporting.R (UI body text + output$siteInfo + the two downloadHandlers :231/:294), tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R (create_test_app opt-in gate :200, create_app_driver, navigate_to_tab, assert_active_pane, get_html_safe, E2E_TIMEOUT), tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R (the injected-siteInfo mount tests), R/shouldShowOripTab.R (isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center=="ONPRC")), R/appUI.R:13-18 (the showOrip gate), R/appServer.R:281-292 (gated modORIPReportingServer mount), R/getSiteInfo.R (parses the file when present; requires all 7 params), R/getConfigFileName.R:16 (HOME-based), inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config (documented multi-line format, center=SNPRC), inst/shinytest/app.R (the stock app: appUI()/appServer, no config). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s86_confirm.R, /tmp/s86_confirm2.R.

Gotchas: (1) The ORIP E2E is OPT-IN (NPRC_RUN_E2E=true); it skips cleanly otherwise (4 skips, reason ‚ÄúOn CRAN‚Äù when NOT_CRAN unset). To run it: NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true + devtools::install() FIRST (it drives the INSTALLED app). (2) The POSITIVE/SNPRC cases boot a config-injecting app.R (HOME-override) ‚Äî the stock create_test_app() app has NO config so it can only serve the no-config-NEGATIVE block. The builder reuses the opt-in gate via create_test_app()‚Äôs skip side-effect (no shared-helper change). (3) The config the fixture writes must have all 7 params (getParamDef stop()s on a missing one when a file exists); center is the only one the GATE reads, but all 7 must be present to parse. (4) getConfigFileName reads Sys.getenv("HOME") ‚Äî that‚Äôs the lever; the generated app.R must Sys.setenv(HOME=...) BEFORE shinyApp(appUI(), appServer), and the HOME path is embedded as a normalizePath(winslash="/") absolute literal. (5) .lintr excludes tests/ ‚Äî lintr::lint() on a test file returns ‚ÄúNo lints found‚Äù regardless of content; I still kept all lines ‚â§80 by hand (the sibling‚Äôs max is 82). (6) The browser E2E run IS the Phase-3E verification (FM #24) ‚Äî there is no separate smoke needed. (7) CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:216 still say ‚ÄúVersion 1.0.8‚Äù (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) ‚Äî stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (9) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 85 Did

Deliverable: Fix #50 ‚Äî the modular app‚Äôs config-loading observer (R/appServer.R:58-68) used read.table(sep="="), which crashes runModularApp() on boot whenever a config file in the documented format (inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config: comments, blank lines, multi-line quoted/comma values) is present. (DONE ‚Äî NEW exported loadSiteConfig() routes config through the tolerant getSiteInfo() parser wrapped in tryCatch; observer collapses to a one-liner; new tests 8/8; full suite 0 failed/0 error; lint 0; Phase-3E AppDriver boots the REAL documented config that previously crashed ‚Üí PASS. #50 CLOSED on owner confirmation 2026-06-16.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-15 Status: DONE. Development workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR) + a separate pre-RED approach question (single-source-of-truth vs minimal-tryCatch, posed before RED); 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - Pre-RED decision (owner, via AskUserQuestion): Single source of truth (issue‚Äôs suggested fix #1) over a minimal tryCatch-only patch ‚Äî parse via the tolerant getSiteInfo() path so documented-format configs actually LOAD, not merely fail-soft. De-risked firsthand: shared$config is passed to modInputServer/modPedigreeServer but referenced by NEITHER module body (grep -n "config" R/modInput.R R/modPedigree.R ‚Üí only @param/signature, default config = NULL, ignored), so reshaping it from a read.table data.frame to the getSiteInfo() named list has zero runtime consumer impact. - RED: NEW tests/testthat/test_loadSiteConfig.R (5 tests / 8 expectations): (1) no-file ‚Üí NULL; (2) documented example_nprcgenekeepr_config ‚Üí list, center=="SNPRC", lkPedColumns>=6 [THE crash case]; (3) malformed config (missing center) ‚Üí NULL, no throw [tryCatch safety net]; (4) characterization guard ‚Äî read.table(sep="=") errors on the documented file [root-cause lock, green throughout, honestly classified as NOT a RED driver]; (5) structural ‚Äî deparse(appServer) contains loadSiteConfig, NOT read.table. Confirmed RED: 1/2/3 error (could not find function "loadSiteConfig"), 5 fails (observer still on read.table), 4 passes. Rest of suite untouched (only a new test file added). - GREEN (2 edits + document()): NEW exported R/loadSiteConfig.R = getConfigFileName(Sys.info()) ‚Üí NULL if no file, else tryCatch(getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE), error ‚Üí futile.logger::flog.warn + NULL); rewrote the observer to observe({ shared$config <- loadSiteConfig() }). document() ‚Üí NAMESPACE export + man/loadSiteConfig.Rd. REFACTOR (owner-chosen small readability tweak): added a bidirectional @seealso \link{loadSiteConfig} to appServer‚Äôs roxygen, re-document()‚Äôd; no behavior change, full suite still 0/0. - Verification: new tests 8/8 green; full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (the 5 warnings are the designed flog.warn safety-net logs from test 3); lintr 0 lints on all 3 changed files (the object_usage warning for the brand-new fn cleared after devtools::install()). - [news-vs-changelog]: user-facing (a startup-crash bug fix) ‚Üí BOTH NEWS.Rmd‚ÜíNEWS.md (new bug-fix bullet under 1.1.0.9000, rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY that bullet) AND CHANGELOG.md (S85 entry). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only source/test/doc/notes; excluded .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED ‚Äî PASS. AppDriver boot of the installed app with the REAL documented example_nprcgenekeepr_config (center=SNPRC) present (HOME-override-in-app.R recipe; NOT_CRAN=true; dev installed first) ‚Äî the exact file that crashed boot before this fix. App reached a stable state (AppDriver$new returned, mainNavbar=‚ÄúHome‚Äù), navigation to ‚ÄúGenetic Value Analysis‚Äù worked (reactive graph alive), 0 read.table/‚Äúdid not have 2 elements‚Äù crash lines, 0 non-shinyBS error-level logs. This is the boot S84 had to SIDESTEP with a stripped single-line config; the documented format now boots clean ‚Äî the designed payoff of S84‚Äôs flag-and-file.

Session 84 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 85): Score 9/10. Zero time lost on what to build, the root cause, or the runtime recipe. The ‚àí1: S84 didn‚Äôt flag that shared$config is passed-but-UNUSED by both modules ‚Äî the fact that determined the reshape was safe (I found it by grep); and ‚Äúparse through the observer‚Äôs path‚Äù slightly under-specifies that an observe() isn‚Äôt unit-testable in isolation, so the real move is to extract a pure helper (loadSiteConfig) for the test seam. Both genuinely the implementer‚Äôs to discover. ROI: very high.

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): R/loadSiteConfig.R (exported helper), tests/testthat/test_loadSiteConfig.R (5 tests / 8 expectations), man/loadSiteConfig.Rd. CHANGED (code): R/appServer.R (config observer ‚Üí one-line shared$config <- loadSiteConfig() ~:58-64; @seealso loadSiteConfig added to roxygen :24), NAMESPACE (export), man/appServer.Rd (seealso). Read firsthand (unchanged): R/getSiteInfo.R (tolerant parser :30-88; requires all 7 params when a file exists), R/getParamDef.R (stop()s on a missing param), R/getTokenList.R (handles comments/blank/multi-line/parens/quotes), R/getConfigFileName.R (HOME-based :16-22), R/modInput.R (config param :228, UNUSED in body), R/modPedigree.R (config param :198, UNUSED in body), inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config (documented multi-line format, center=SNPRC), tests/testthat/test_getSiteInfo.R (covers only the no-file DEFAULT path). Gotchas: (1) #50 is CLOSED (owner-confirmed 2026-06-16; fix d440e504, Phase-3E-verified). (2) shared$config is now a getSiteInfo() named list (was a read.table data.frame) when a config file is present, else NULL. Safe TODAY because both consumer modules ignore the config param ‚Äî but if a FUTURE change makes a module actually USE config, it now receives the named-list shape, not a data.frame. (3) loadSiteConfig() fails SOFT: a missing OR malformed config ‚Üí NULL + a futile.logger::flog.warn to the nprcgenekeepr logger (no R warning/error surfaced to the user). A partial config (missing one of the 7 required params) also no longer crashes ‚Äî getParamDef‚Äôs stop() is caught ‚Üí NULL. (4) Phase-3E HOME-override recipe (reusable): temp app.R does Sys.setenv(HOME=<dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>) BEFORE shinyApp(appUI(), appServer); AppDriver$new(<appdir>); NOT_CRAN=true; install dev first. The DOCUMENTED example_nprcgenekeepr_config now boots clean ‚Äî no longer needs S84‚Äôs stripped single-line workaround. (5) Test idiom: withr::local_tempdir() + withr::local_envvar(c(HOME=tmp)) (withr is available via testthat); name the config file by basename(getConfigFileName(Sys.info())[["configFile"]]) for OS-correctness. (6) object_usage_linter false-positives a brand-new package function (‚Äúno visible global function‚Äù) until devtools::install(); re-lint after install. (7) CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:216 still say ‚ÄúVersion 1.0.8‚Äù (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) ‚Äî stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call). (9) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards ‚Äî use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 84 Did

Deliverable: (1) Owner-confirmed close of #47 (ORIP wire-in accepted), then (2) implement #49 ‚Äî gate the ‚ÄúORIP Reporting‚Äù tab to show only for ONPRC ‚Äî under Strict TDD. (BOTH DONE ‚Äî #47 CLOSED; #49 = NEW exported predicate + parameterized appUI + gated server mount; new tests 5/5 + 4/4; full suite 0 failed/0 error; lint 0; Phase-3E AppDriver 3/3 config scenarios PASS. Discovered a pre-existing config-observer crash ‚Üí filed NEW #50, did NOT fix it.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-15 Status: DONE. Development workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR) + a separate pre-RED scope/approach question (the product fork AND the gating mechanism, posed together before RED); 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - Owner-authorized first action ‚Äî #47 CLOSED: the owner‚Äôs ‚Äú1‚Äù was the firsthand confirmation the standing close-rule requires; closed #47 with a comment referencing S83 commit 6fd16715 + the 13/13 Phase-3E smoke, noting ONPRC-gating is tracked in #49. (Not a workstream deliverable ‚Äî a clerical close; #49 is the substantive work.) - Two pre-RED decisions (owner, via one AskUserQuestion): (a) product fork ‚Üí Hide unless a real ONPRC config ‚Äî show only when an actual config file exists AND center=="ONPRC"; the getSiteInfo() default fallback (center="ONPRC" with no file, true in dev/CI) hides the tab. (b) approach ‚Üí Build-time conditional tabPanel (not dynamic insertTab) ‚Äî appUI() is evaluated ONCE at shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) construction and the deployment center is fixed per server, so the tab‚Äôs presence is a per-deployment CONSTANT, not a per-session reactive (dynamic insertTab is for DATA-driven tabs like Error List). - RED: NEW tests/testthat/test_shouldShowOripTab.R (5 pure-predicate cases) + rewrote tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R to INJECT a siteInfo list ‚Äî tab PRESENT under ONPRC+existing-file, ABSENT under SNPRC / non-existent-file ‚Äî plus a deparse(appServer) assertion that the server mount is gated on shouldShowOripTab. All failed for the right reason (undefined function / unused siteInfo arg / un-gated mount); rest of suite unaffected (only test files changed). - GREEN (3 edits): NEW exported R/shouldShowOripTab.R = isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center == "ONPRC") (mirrors shouldShowChangedColsTab; isTRUE(==) not identical ‚Üí names/NA/NULL/length-safe); parameterized appUI(siteInfo = getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE)) with if (isTRUE(showOrip)) tabPanel("ORIP Reporting", ‚Ä¶); gated the modORIPReportingServer mount in appServer.R on the same predicate. document() ‚Üí NAMESPACE export + man/shouldShowOripTab.Rd + man/appUI.Rd. REFACTOR = confirmed no-op (kept the sibling inline-call idiom; no helper extraction ‚Äî would break the appServer structural test‚Äôs intent). - Verification: new tests 5/5 + 4/4 (9 expectations); full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error; lintr 0 lints on all 5 changed files (the object_usage warning for the brand-new fn cleared after devtools::install(), like its installed siblings). - [news-vs-changelog]: user-facing (visible-tab behavior change) ‚Üí BOTH NEWS.Rmd‚ÜíNEWS.md (ORIP bullet augmented with the ONPRC-gating note; rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY that bullet) AND CHANGELOG.md (S84 entry). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only source/test/doc/notes; excluded .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED ‚Äî PASS (3/3 config scenarios). AppDriver boots of the installed app, each via a generated app.R that Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>) BEFORE shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) (controls the real getSiteInfo‚Üíconfig path in-process): ONPRC config ‚Üí ORIP tab present + navigable (active pane = ORIP), all 5 module outputs register values, 4 siblings intact; SNPRC config ‚Üí tab ABSENT (UI + server), siblings intact; no config ‚Üí tab ABSENT, siblings intact. 0 ORIP-namespaced ERROR-level logs (the 5 oripReporting lines are INFO shiny:value registrations; the only errors are the pre-existing app-wide shinyBS noise). First app boot ever done WITH a config file present ‚Üí surfaced pre-existing bug #50 (below).

Discovered (out of scope) ‚Üí NEW #50: the pre-existing config-loading observer (appServer.R:63, from 6457a3a3, 2025-12-29) uses read.table(configFile, header=TRUE, sep="="), which cannot parse the documented config format (example_nprcgenekeepr_config: comments, blank lines, multi-line quoted values) and crashes the modular app on boot whenever such a file is present ‚Äî latent in dev/CI only because no config file exists there. NOT fixed (unrelated to #49); sidestepped for the smoke with a stripped single-line key=value config both read.table AND getParamDef accept; filed #50 with root cause + repro + suggested fix.

Session 83 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 84): Score 9/10. S83 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (2): ‚ÄúImplement #49 (ONPRC-gate the ORIP tab) ‚Ä¶ resolve the ‚Äòshow or hide when no config file (default ONPRC)‚Äô product fork first (likely via AskUserQuestion). Design options recorded on #49 ‚Ä¶ Phase-3E must confirm present-under-ONPRC / absent-under-SNPRC.‚Äù Every load-bearing element held firsthand: the product fork was pre-flagged (saved me from discovering it cold), the issue carried evidence-based design options, and the baselines (dashboard 98/100, the dynamic-tab reference appServer.R:163-242, getSiteInfo.R:66 default ONPRC, the AppDriver Phase-3E recipe + shinyBS-noise separation, gh --json gotcha, 2-untracked-files disposition, stale version, branch model) were all accurate. Zero time lost on what to build or the core seams. The ‚àí1: S83 leaned toward the dynamic insertTab approach (‚Äúmost consistent with the codebase‚Äù) ‚Äî a minor over-steer I had to evaluate and override, because the firsthand fact that appUI() is built ONCE makes build-time gating both simpler and semantically correct for a per-deployment constant; and S83 couldn‚Äôt pre-load the pure-predicate test idiom, the getSiteInfo-requires-all-7-params strictness, or the #50 observer crash (only surfaces on a config-present boot). All genuinely the implementer‚Äôs to find. ROI: very high.

Self-assessment (Session 84): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 18 issues, ghost-check ‚Üí none: 6fd16715 = S83 close-out), reported, STOPPED for the owner‚Äôs pick. Honored ‚Äú1 then 2‚Äù without bleeding scope: closed #47 as the owner-authorized clerical action, made #49 the substantive deliverable, claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). Resolved BOTH pre-RED forks (product + approach) via a SEPARATE AskUserQuestion before RED, then gated all 3 TDD transitions via AskUserQuestion with exact next-phase actions; declared the TDD phase every response. RED clean & isolated (failures for the right reason; rest of suite untouched since only test files changed). GREEN minimal (3 edits + document()), mirroring the sibling pure-predicate idiom with a names/NA/NULL-safe predicate. Recompute-don‚Äôt-inherit: read appUI/appServer/getSiteInfo/getConfigFileName/the dynamic-tab test/the example config firsthand, and verified appUI()-built-once + the config strictness firsthand rather than trusting the handoff‚Äôs approach steer.
