‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 65 (update #37, DONE; full detail below):

‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 65 (update #37, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 65 ‚Äî 2026-06-12): Execute S64 SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 ‚Äî update GitHub issue #37 (‚ÄúExported functions not currently used by app‚Äù): firsthand-verify its 70-function inventory against current code, strike the now-resolved rows, keep the still-accurate unused inventory. (DONE ‚Äî #37 UPDATED, kept open.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Issue-grooming session (no production code or tests written; TDD phase = N/A declared every response ‚Äî same classification S57/S61/S62/S63/S64 used). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - The finding ‚Äî far staler than the handoff anticipated: S64 (inheriting the S62 audit) framed #37 as ‚Äústrike the resolved Shiny-module + genotype rows; keep the still-accurate inventory‚Äù (‚âà7 functions). A from-scratch call-graph reachability recomputation found 45 of the 70 listed functions are now used by the app ‚Äî the inventory was ~64% stale, not 2 clusters. Verified totals (current code): 155 exports / 116 used / 39 unused (the issue claimed 108 / 38 / 70). - Method (firsthand, reproducible): pkgload::load_all, seed the call graph at {runModularApp, runGeneKeepR, appUI, appServer}, take the transitive closure of codetools::findGlobals(f)$functions ‚à© package-functions; exported names outside the closure = unused. Produced concrete call paths for the flipped set (e.g.¬ÝrankSubjects: appServer ‚Üí modGeneticValueServer ‚Üí reportGV ‚Üí orderReport ‚Üí rankSubjects; the 4 Shiny modules called directly by appServer; the genotype trio getGenotypes/addGenotype/hasGenotype via modInputServer; filterKinMatrix/Report/Threshold via the breeding-group + GV modules). Confirmed all 22 ‚Äústill unused‚Äù are genuinely unreachable. - Adversarial check of the static method‚Äôs blind spots: findGlobals misses dynamic + S3 dispatch, so I checked both explicitly: the ONLY string-dispatched call in R/ is do.call("rbind", ‚Ä¶) (base), and none of the 22 still-unused names appear as a string literal anywhere in R/ ‚Üí no dynamic invocation the closure missed; the 3 S3 methods (print.summary.*, summary.nprcgenekeeprErr) aren‚Äôt dispatched on the app path (the app‚Äôs summary() calls hit summary.default on numeric vectors). Classification held: 45 flipped / 22 unused / 3 S3 = 70. - Applied (owner-chosen via AskUserQuestion): strikethrough-in-place + dated verification note in the body (gh issue edit 37 --body-file) ‚Äî struck the 45 now-used rows (verified: exactly 45 ~~‚Ä¶~~ pairs in the saved body; obfuscateId unstruck, rankSubjects struck), corrected the counts + the 5 ‚ÄúNotable findings‚Äù, and folded in the 17 newer unused exports (added since 2026-01-25: the sim-kinship subsystem, modORIPReporting*, calcFE/calcFG, makeFounderStatsTable/makeGeneticSummaryTable, getAncestors, create_wkbk, savePlotToFile, logModuleEvent, safeExecute) so the inventory is complete (39 total). Posted a timeline pointer comment (#issuecomment-4696756359) so the silent body edit is visible to watchers. Issue kept OPEN ‚Äî the 39 genuinely-unused exports remain the actionable surface. - [news-vs-changelog] ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (S65 entry under [Unreleased]; dev-process/grooming history, not a user-facing release note ‚Üí NOT NEWS.md; BACKLOG.md has no #37 reference ‚Äî open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add of only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked: ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated ‚Äî no runtime behavior changed (edited a GitHub issue + build-ignored docs; no R/ code). The runtime fact the issue documents IS verified, though: pkgload::load_all loaded the package cleanly and the reachability closure is computed against the working-tree source, so the ‚Äúused by the app‚Äù set reflects the live call graph, not a stale install.

‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 64 (verify + close #8, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 64 ‚Äî 2026-06-12): Execute S63 SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 ‚Äî verify issue #8 (‚ÄúImprove handling of non-founder animals without either parents assigned‚Äù) firsthand against current code, then CLOSE #8 with a caveat note. (DONE ‚Äî #8 CLOSED, strengthened caveat.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Verify-and-close session (no production code or tests written; TDD phase = N/A declared every response ‚Äî same classification S57/S61/S63 used closing #30/#34/#14). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - The finding ‚Äî re-graded from the audit‚Äôs footnote-caveat to a STRENGTHENED caveat (with a reproduction): #8‚Äôs two proposed solutions are genuinely implemented, BUT firsthand verification + a direct reproduction showed the fix is gated on the optional origin column and silently recreates the original bug in the default (no-origin) case ‚Äî plus the issue‚Äôs 2021 simulated-kinship thread is orphaned (the audit never mentioned it). The owner picked the strengthened-caveat close via AskUserQuestion. - What‚Äôs implemented (verified firsthand): Solution 1 (origin/‚ÄúFrom Center‚Äù segregation) ‚Äî ONPRC-born founders with no offspring are split into a noParentage bucket: R/orderReport.R:31,44-54 (is.na(rpt$origin) & totalOffspring==0L & id %in% founders), placed last. Solution 2 (don‚Äôt rank them) ‚Äî R/rankSubjects.R:38 sets value="Undetermined", :44 sets rank=NA. Live ‚Äî reportGV() ‚Üí orderReport(finalData, ped) (R/reportGV.R:146); finalData carries origin only when the source ped does (R/reportGV.R:119 intersect(getIncludeColumns(), names(ped))). Green ‚Äî pkgload::load_all runs: test_orderReport.R 8/0/0/0, test_rankSubjects.R 5/0/0/0. - The caveats (firsthand, beyond the audit‚Äôs one-liner): (1) origin-gating silently no-ops ‚Äî REPRODUCED. Whole noParentage block wrapped in if ("origin" %in% names(rpt)) (R/orderReport.R:31); origin is documented optional (R/getPossibleCols.R:29, R/qcStudbook.R:34), absent from most bundled datasets. I built a synthetic ped+rpt (a no-parent founder, 0 offspring, high gu) and ran orderReport(): without origin ‚Üí value="High Value", rank=1 (the exact #8 bug, no warning); with origin (NA for the ONPRC animal) ‚Üí "Undetermined"/NA. (2) No regression pin ‚Äî test_orderReport.R:15-26 runs the origin path but asserts only positional unknown-ID counts; test_rankSubjects.R never exercises noParentage; the only ‚ÄúUndetermined‚Äù in tests is a comment in a usually-skipped e2e (test-e2e-genetic-value-tutorial.R:109). (3) Orphaned thread B ‚Äî the 2021 simulated-kinship subsystem (getPotentialParents, createSimKinships, kinshipMatricesToKValues, summarizeKinshipValues, countKinshipValues, addKinshipValueCount, cumulateSimKinships) is exported but has NO live caller in R/ (only tests + inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R + vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd); the issue‚Äôs ‚Äúdiscuss with Matt ‚Ä¶ display/use simulated kinship coefficients‚Äù design item is unresolved. All three documented in the close comment as candidate future enhancements; none filed (new-issue creation outside the approved ‚Äúclose with caveat‚Äù scope ‚Äî S63 lesson; I did not even attempt it this time). - Adversarial close gate (3-lens refute workflow wf_f37f1b72-b6a, Explore skeptics vs the working tree): SPLIT ‚Äî 1 hold-open + 2 close-with-caveat ‚Äî reconciled by REPRODUCTION, not vote. (i) headline-implemented ‚Üí refuted=true, hold-open (high): the origin-gating makes the fix illusory in the common case; claimed to have reproduced a no-parent founder ranked ‚ÄúHigh Value‚Äù. I reconstructed it myself (the table above) ‚Üí upheld, which is what upgraded the close from clean‚Üístrengthened-caveat. (ii) test-authenticity ‚Üí refuted=false: no assertion pins the branch ‚Üí upheld, caveat not blocker. (iii) intent-completeness ‚Üí refuted=false: simulated-kinship subsystem orphaned (also surfaced the vignette + example file) ‚Üí upheld, severable. The lone dissenter was the decisive one ‚Äî verifying it firsthand beat tallying 2-of-3. - Closed: gh issue comment 8 --body-file + gh issue close 8 with the strengthened-caveat resolution comment (both solutions implemented + the reproduced origin-gating failure + the missing test + the orphaned thread B + why-close-anyway). ‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 63 (verify + close #14, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 63 ‚Äî 2026-06-12): Execute S62 audit recommendation #1 ‚Äî verify #14 (‚ÄúAdd ability to provide genotypes for animals within the pedigree and track them‚Äù) is genuinely resolved against the current code, then CLOSE issue #14. (DONE ‚Äî #14 CLOSED, caveated.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Verify-and-close session (no production code or tests written; TDD phase = N/A declared every response ‚Äî same classification S57/S61 used closing #30/#34). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - The finding ‚Äî re-graded from the audit‚Äôs ‚Äúclean‚Äù to CAVEATED: #14‚Äôs genotype provide+track ability is genuinely implemented and test-pinned, BUT firsthand verification (deeper than the audit‚Äôs per-issue classifier) surfaced a deliberate limitation that made it an #8-grade close-with-caveat rather than a clean close. The owner picked the caveated close via AskUserQuestion. - What‚Äôs implemented (verified firsthand): Provide ‚Äî the modular app‚Äôs separate-genotype-file mode reads via getGenotypes, validates via checkGenotypeFile (degrade-to-NULL), integer-codes + merges via addGenotype (R/modInput.R:384-396); same functions exported for scripting. Track ‚Äî integer first/second ride the cleaned studbook into reportGV() ‚Üí getGVGenotype (R/reportGV.R:78) ‚Üí geneDrop(genotype=‚Ä¶) (:92) ‚Üí calcGU/calcFG. Live ‚Äî runModularApp() ‚Üí shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) ‚Üí modInputUI/Server("dataInput") (appUI.R:123, appServer.R:104); module exposes cleanedStudbook/genotypeData reactives. Pinned ‚Äî I ran the genotype tests via pkgload::load_all: 278 assertions, 0 fail / 0 err / 0 skip, incl.¬Ýthe end-to-end modInputServer separate-file test (test_modInput_qcStudbook.R:536-545 asserts hasGenotype(cleaned), integer first/second, populated genotypeData()). The 0 skips are load-bearing (the testServer tests skip_if_not_installed("shiny"); shiny is installed ‚Üí they actually drove the module). - The caveat (deliberate, owner-authored): commonPedGenoFile (combined ped+geno file) UI mode does not integer-code string alleles ‚Üí those genotypes never satisfy hasGenotype() / reach gene-drop. This is at parity with the legacy monolith and explicitly intentional per the owner‚Äôs own commit c9019d51: ‚Äúneither app integer-codes string alleles for a combined file, so common-mode genotypes never reach gene-drop in either.‚Äù Documented in the close comment; combined-file tracking noted there as a candidate future enhancement (NOT filed ‚Äî new-issue creation was outside the approved ‚Äúclose with caveat‚Äù scope; the auto-mode classifier correctly denied my attempt to gh issue create). - Adversarial close gate (3-lens refute workflow wf_9d00a37a-6d0): 1 confirmed, 2 refuted ‚Äî and I reconciled EACH verdict firsthand. (i) live-path ‚Üí refuted=false (matched my read). (ii) intent-completeness ‚Üí refuted=true, REAL ‚Üí this surfaced the combined-file caveat ‚Üí re-graded the close. (iii) test-authenticity ‚Üí refuted=true, but an ARTIFACT: the agent decompiled the stale installed binary (missing the merge block) instead of the source; my load_all run (90/0/0/0) + my read of modInput.R:384-396 proved the source is correct and green. Agents inspect what‚Äôs installed; the parent runs load_all against the working tree. - Closed: gh issue close 14 with a resolution comment (implemented-ability evidence + the parity caveat + the firsthand/adversarial verification). The refute pass earned its keep: of 3 STALE candidates it confirmed 2 (#14, #8) and KNOCKED DOWN 1 (#1) ‚Äî #1‚Äôs Clear Focal Animals checkbox clears focalIds() but never resets the file-browser input, exactly as the owner‚Äôs own 2020 GitHub comment states (‚Äúa partial fix ‚Ä¶ does not clear file names read in with the file browser‚Äù). Closing #1 on the checkbox alone would have re-buried a documented gap. - Firsthand verification of all close-relevant calls (#14/#8/#1): re-read source + ran gh api repos/:owner/:repo/issues/1/comments to CONFIRM the agent-cited #1 owner comment is real (a bare gh issue view 1 printed empty ‚Äî a TTY/pager quirk; the API showed 1 comment). My independent spot-checks agreed with the classifier on every issue I checked. S61 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 with the exact framing (‚Äú#34 proved the issue tracker lags the code; worth a read-only pass verifying whether other ‚Äòopen‚Äô issues are already done ‚Ä¶ before treating any as implementation work. Could close several‚Äù), even naming example issues to check (#33, #35/#36). That made orientation‚Üíexecution near-frictionless. Its gotchas held: #34 is closed (didn‚Äôt re-treat it), the tracker-lags-code insight was the audit‚Äôs whole premise, and ‚ÄúREAD THE CODE before treating a backlog bug as implementation work‚Äù was exactly the discipline this session operationalized for all 21. The ‚àí1: ‚ÄúCould close several‚Äù was slightly optimistic ‚Äî the audit found only 1 clean close (#14) + 1 caveated (#8); the example issues S61 named (#33/#36 OPEN, #35 PARTIAL) are NOT closeable. Not a defect (determining that IS the audit‚Äôs job), just a calibration note: a staleness hunch is not a staleness finding. ROI: very high.

Self-assessment (Session 62): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, all 21 issue bodies), reported, and STOPPED for the owner to pick the deliverable (‚Äú1‚Äù). Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared TDD phase (N/A, with reason) every response. Verify-first was load-bearing and the design paid off: built my own evidence base on the high-signal issues in parallel with the workflow (not trusting agents), and the adversarial verify pass caught the one false-STALE (#1) that would have been a wrong outward-facing close ‚Äî then I confirmed its evidence (the owner comment) firsthand via gh api rather than propagating an unverified citation. Right-sized orchestration: a 24-agent classify‚Üírefute fan-out is proportionate for a 21-issue tracker-wide sweep under ultracode (a solo pass would have under-covered; a heavier per-issue multi-lens would be ceremony for the 14 obvious OPENs). Scope discipline (1-and-done): produced the audit REPORT and RECOMMENDED closes; did NOT bulk-close issues (outward-facing follow-up), did NOT touch the 2 untracked artifacts, did NOT fix the stale CLAUDE.md version. Stakeholder corrections: 0. Dings (both self-caught): (a) my first Workflow launch passed args:[...] which arrived as a non-array ‚Üí pipeline() threw instantly (0 agents) ‚Äî one self-corrected round-trip (hardcoded the issue list in the script, re-launched from scriptPath); should have defended against the args-shape up front. (b) Minor: the gh issue view 1 empty-output quirk cost a moment‚Äôs doubt about the agent‚Äôs owner-comment citation before gh api confirmed it. Clean, authoritatively-verified, 0-correction deliverable with one avoidable round-trip ‚Üí honest 8. Met the S57‚ÄìS61 discipline bar.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick. The audit is done; nothing mid-flight. The audit‚Äôs own prioritized recommendations (in docs/audits/BACKLOG_STALENESS_AUDIT_2026-06-12.md), in value order: (1) Close #14 ‚Äî clean STALE, #34-grade, firsthand + adversarially verified; ~2-min gh issue close 14 with a resolution comment (a verify-and-close session like S61‚Äôs #34, but the verification is already done ‚Äî re-confirm lint/tests aren‚Äôt needed since no code changed). (2) Close #8 with a caveat note ‚Äî both solutions live; note the optional-origin gating + missing regression assertion in the close comment; optionally file a 1-line follow-up for a test asserting value=="Undetermined"/rank==NA. (3) Update #37 ‚Äî strike the resolved Shiny-module + genotype rows; keep the still-accurate unused-export inventory. (4) Re-scope #35 to ‚Äúinclude descendants‚Äù (ancestors are done). (5) Consolidate the ID cluster (#38/#32/#26/#31) into one umbrella design issue. (6) Housekeeping (carried since S56): CLAUDE.md says ‚ÄúVersion 1.0.8‚Äù but DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000. Do NOT close #1, #5, #9 (PARTIAL ‚Äî real remaining work) or any of the 14 OPEN issues.

Key files (this session): docs/audits/BACKLOG_STALENESS_AUDIT_2026-06-12.md (THE deliverable ‚Äî full per-issue evidence, the Items-Audited table, structural observations, prioritized recommendations); CHANGELOG.md (S62 entry under [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 62); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Evidence anchors cited in the report: #14 R/modInput.R:384-396 + R/reportGV.R:78 + tests/testthat/test_modInput_qcStudbook.R:538-545; #8 R/orderReport.R:44-54 + R/rankSubjects.R:38,43; #1 R/modPedigree.R:84-88,205-210,225-227; #35 R/modPedigree.R:292-302 + R/trimPedigree.R:51 + R/getProbandPedigree.R:24-40; #37 R/appUI.R:159,177 + R/appServer.R:276,292. Workflow script: ‚Ä¶/workflows/scripts/backlog-staleness-audit-wf_73e5f620-ba8.js (TRANSIENT; run wf_7339f4ca-2a5). No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes.

Gotchas: (1) The audit RECOMMENDS closes; it did NOT execute them ‚Äî #14/#8 are still OPEN on GitHub. Don‚Äôt assume they‚Äôre closed. (2) #1 is a confirmed false-STALE ‚Äî do NOT close it on the basis of the Clear Focal Animals checkbox; the file-browser input is not reset (owner flagged this in 2020). (3) #8‚Äôs close is CAVEATED ‚Äî functionally resolved but test coverage of the noParentage branch is cosmetic and the behavior is gated on the optional origin column; close with a note, not silently. (4) #26 and #32 are near-duplicate issues (both: configurable auto-gen-ID detection); #38 adds the format-generation side, #31 is the dam-ID consumer ‚Äî treat as ONE feature when implementing (consolidate). (5) Workflow args can arrive non-array ‚Üí pipeline() throws instantly; hardcode the work-list in the script or guard with Array.isArray(args). (6) Audit/report files belong under docs/ (build-ignored via ^docs$) ‚Äî a top-level dated file re-adds to the ‚Äúnon-standard top-level files‚Äù NOTE S60 eliminated. (7) gh issue view N can print empty in this environment (TTY/pager) ‚Äî use gh api repos/:owner/:repo/issues/N/comments for the authoritative read. (8) CLAUDE.md still says ‚ÄúVersion 1.0.8‚Äù (DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000) ‚Äî stale, carried since S56. (9) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) remain ‚Äî build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call; left untouched. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 61 (verify + close #34, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 61 ‚Äî 2026-06-12): Verify the qcStudbook() integration in modInput is genuinely complete/working, then CLOSE issue #34 (‚ÄúIntegrate qcStudbook() in modInput Shiny module‚Äù, bug/high). (DONE ‚Äî #34 CLOSED.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Verify-and-close session (no production code or tests written; TDD phase = N/A declared every response ‚Äî RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR does not apply, same classification S57 used closing #30). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/ changes. - The finding: #34 was already resolved and never formally closed. The placeholder QC it describes (# TODO: Replace with actual qcStudbook() call + results$cleaned <- rawData) is gone; modInput.R:408 calls qcStudbook(rawData, minParentAge, reportChanges=TRUE, reportErrors=TRUE) and :423 calls the two-pass wrapper runQcStudbook() (R/runQcStudbook.R ‚Üí R/processQcStudbookResult.R for UI shaping). The integration landed during the Shiny-module conversion (commit 7da01afe, extended c9019d51 genotype-merge / bb7f2be6 LabKey parity). CHANGELOG.md:1216-1217 (Session-20 entry) already said ‚Äú#34 ‚Ä¶ is stale (already integrated)‚Äù ‚Äî but the GitHub issue stayed open. The handoff chain kept listing #34 as the ‚Äúhighest-value open BUG to implement‚Äù; it was actually a verify-and-close. - Live path confirmed: appUI.R:123 (modInputUI("dataInput")) ‚Üí appServer.R:104 (modInputServer("dataInput", ‚Ä¶)) ‚Äî the canonical runModularApp() flow, not dead code. minParentAge wired from the UI input with safe coercion (modInput.R:398-404). - Verification (firsthand, authoritative): ran the two QC test files myself ‚Äî test_qcStudbook.R 38/0/0/0 and test_modInput_qcStudbook.R 90/0/0/0 (pass/fail/err/skip). The 0 skips were load-bearing: the shiny::testServer module tests carry skip_if_not_installed("shiny"), so a skip would have meant the end-to-end module path never ran ‚Äî shiny is installed, so they DID drive modInputServer. The integration is regression-pinned by test_modInput_qcStudbook.R:296 ("gen" %in% names(cleaned)) ‚Äî gen is added ONLY by qcStudbook() (qcStudbook.R:267), so that assertion would FAIL if the module were reverted to the placeholder. - Adversarial close gate: a 3-lens refute-the-close workflow (wf_c60c7bf1-521: residual-placeholder/live-path ‚Äñ functional-completeness-vs-issue-intent ‚Äñ test-authenticity, each an Explore skeptic told to find a reason NOT to close) ‚Üí all refuted=false, high confidence, 0 gaps; closeRecommended=true. The test gate was run by me, not trusted from the agents. - Closed: gh issue close 34 with a resolution comment (placeholder‚Üíreal integration, commits, firsthand verification, the pin test, the 3-lens result). Verified state=closed, closed_at=2026-06-12T20:52:27Z. [news-vs-changelog] ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (Session 61 entry under [Unreleased]; BACKLOG.md has no #34 reference ‚Äî open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add of only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked: ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated ‚Äî no runtime behavior changed (closed a GitHub issue + edited build-ignored docs; no R/ code). The runtime behavior #34 concerns IS verified, though: the shiny::testServer tests actually drove modInputServer end-to-end (0 skips) ‚Äî a stronger, cheaper gate for a pure verification than a manual app boot.

Session 60 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 61): Score 8/10. S60‚Äôs handoff made orientation frictionless: accurate clean-tree report, the 2 pre-existing untracked files named with their disposition (build-ignored / owner‚Äôs keep-call), the dashboard/branch state, and a priority-ordered candidate list that put #34 first. The build-config gotchas were irrelevant to my task but correct. What helped most: the candidate list pointed me straight at #34, and the standing housekeeping flag (stale CLAUDE.md version) is well-tracked. The ‚àí2 (a systemic gap S60 inherited, not originated): the SUGGESTED-NEXT framed #34 as ‚Äúintegrate qcStudbook() in modInput (highest-value open bug)‚Äù ‚Äî i.e., implementation work ‚Äî when CHANGELOG had recorded it as ‚Äústale (already integrated)‚Äù since Session 20. Every session S20‚ÜíS60 carried the stale ‚Äúimplement #34‚Äù characterization forward without reconciling it against CHANGELOG or the code. An executor trusting the backlog pointer verbatim would have opened modInput.R expecting a placeholder to replace and found the work already done. The verify-first reflex caught it (I read the code before treating it as implementation), but the handoff chain should have flagged #34 as verify-and-close. ROI: high ‚Äî orientation cost was near-zero; the mischaracterization cost a few minutes of discovery, not a wrong deliverable.

Self-assessment (Session 61): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard, git, issues), reported, and STOPPED for the owner. Treated the owner‚Äôs first message (‚Äúexplain #34‚Äù) as a question (FM #23) ‚Äî explained without modifying and surfaced the ‚Äúalready done ‚Üí candidate to close‚Äù finding before any action. On ‚Äúwork to close #34‚Äù: claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared TDD phase (N/A, with reason) every response. Verify-first was load-bearing: ran the authoritative test gate firsthand (not trusted from the handoff or agents), located the regression-pin test (the gen-column assertion that the placeholder would fail), and ran a 3-lens adversarial refute-the-close pass ‚Äî only closing after all evidence converged. Right-sized orchestration: a small 3-lens workflow for an irreversible high-priority-bug close (proportionate under ultracode), authoritative gate by me. Scope discipline: did NOT touch the 2 untracked artifacts; did NOT fix the stale CLAUDE.md version (surfaced as follow-up); pre-committed to NOT fixing in-session if a gap surfaced. Stakeholder corrections: 0. Dings (both minor, self-caught): (a) my first Workflow launch passed an unsupported run_in_background arg ‚Üí one self-corrected round-trip (Workflow always backgrounds ‚Äî should have read the signature); (b) the ‚Äú#34 is stale‚Äù CHANGELOG note could have surfaced during Phase-0 orientation rather than during the explain step, though the explain step is exactly where reading the code revealed it. Clean, authoritatively-verified, 0-correction deliverable with one avoidable round-trip ‚Üí honest 8. Met the S57‚ÄìS60 discipline bar.

Gotchas: (1) #34 is CLOSED ‚Äî don‚Äôt re-add it to any backlog or re-treat it as a bug to implement. (2) The issue tracker LAGS the code ‚Äî #34 was resolved at commit 7da01afe / documented in CHANGELOG since S20 but stayed open until S61; when picking a backlog ‚Äúbug,‚Äù READ THE CODE to confirm it‚Äôs still live before treating it as implementation work (other open issues may also be stale ‚Äî see SUGGESTED-NEXT #1). (3) The integration‚Äôs regression-pin is test_modInput_qcStudbook.R:296 (the gen-column assertion) ‚Äî if a future modInput refactor touches the QC path, keep that assertion. (4) The testServer module tests skip_if_not_installed("shiny") ‚Äî a green run with skips on that file would be hollow; confirm shiny is installed so they actually run. (5) CLAUDE.md still says ‚ÄúVersion 1.0.8‚Äù (DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000) ‚Äî stale, carried since S56. (6) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) remain ‚Äî build-ignored / owner‚Äôs standing keep-call; left untouched. ‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 60 (.Rbuildignore top-level-files NOTE elimination, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 60 ‚Äî 2026-06-12): Execute S59‚Äôs deferred SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 (‚Äúscope-B‚Äù) ‚Äî add .Rbuildignore patterns for the currently-UNignored, non-shipping top-level files so they are dropped from the build tarball, eliminating the R CMD check ‚Äúnon-standard files/directories found at top level‚Äù NOTE (and dupe-guarding the synced docs). (DONE.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Build-config-only change (.Rbuildignore), behavior-none for installed/runtime code; TDD phase = N/A declared every response ‚Äî .Rbuildignore is dropped from the built tarball, so there is no shippable testthat assertion and RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR does not apply (same as S58/S59). One close-out commit on add-methodology. - The change (.Rbuildignore): the built tarball previously carried 8 non-standard top-level files (the entire NOTE): 20250504_cran-comments.md, methodology_dashboard.py, dashboard.html, nprcgenekeepr_notes.txt, RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.md, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.{md,html}, TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md. Two edits: (1) consolidated the 7 dated cran-comments exact lines (^YYYYMMDD_cran-comments\.md$) into one ^[0-9]+_cran-comments\.md$ (covers the missed 20250504 + any future dated file); (2) appended a tooling/audit/notes block at EOF ‚Äî ^methodology_dashboard\.py$, ^dashboard\.html$, ^nprcgenekeepr_notes\.txt$, ^RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.*\.md$, ^PED_GV_AUDIT.*, ^TECH_DEBT_AUDIT.*. Result: the tarball top level is now only the 5 standard files (DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE/NEWS.md/README.md/LICENSE) ‚Üí the NOTE is eliminated. - Owner decisions (AskUserQuestion, before editing): (1) scope = exclude all 8 (none are package content) ‚Üí eliminate the NOTE (vs.¬Ýkeeping the audit reports shipping); (2) pattern style = broad dupe-guarded + consolidate ‚Äî <NAME>.* forms for the synced methodology/audit docs so macOS dupes (RECOMMENDED_SKILLS 2.md, ‚Ä¶) are also caught, plus the dated-cran-comments consolidation (vs.¬Ýminimal exact-match per file). - Verification (firsthand, authoritative at the build level): staged 3 real spaced dupes (RECOMMENDED_SKILLS 2.md, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30 2.md, TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026-05-30 2.md), ran R CMD build --no-build-vignettes --no-manual . ‚Üí RC=0 (proves no regex-comment abort); tar tzf of the resulting nprcgenekeepr_1.1.0.9000.tar.gz: top level = exactly the 5 standard files, zero of the 8 excluded files, zero of the 3 staged dupes; 685 files total (down from the 693 baseline = the 8 removed). The ‚Äúnon-standard top-level files‚Äù NOTE is a pure function of the tarball‚Äôs top-level entries (the same input R CMD check uses), so this directly confirms the NOTE is gone ‚Äî full devtools::check() deliberately NOT re-run (nothing affecting tests/examples/DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE changed; same build-not-check logic as S58/S59, avoiding S57‚Äôs self-flagged over-check). Baseline tarball built first to ENUMERATE the real 8-file set before posing the scope question (S59‚Äôs candidate list named only 4 of the 8). trap cleanup EXIT removed the 3 dupes + both tarballs; git status clean except the intended mods + 2 build-ignored/pre-existing untracked (..Rcheck/00check.log, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). - [news-vs-changelog] ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (Session 60 entry under [Unreleased]; dev-process/packaging history, not a user-facing release note ‚Üí NOT NEWS.md). [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add of only .Rbuildignore + SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (excluded ..Rcheck/00check.log, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated ‚Äî no runtime behavior changed. Edited only .Rbuildignore (build config; not shipped/installed) and docs (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES). No R/ code ‚Üí no app boot; the authoritative gate is the clean R CMD build + tarball inspection above.

Session 59 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 60): Score 8/10. S59 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 (‚Äúscope-B‚Äù): the action (add currently-UNignored top-level docs to .Rbuildignore), the rationale (removes them from the tarball ‚Üí shrinks the top-level-files NOTE AND dupe-guards them), the correct framing (‚Äúa tarball-contents change, so weigh whether those files SHOULD ship‚Äù ‚Äî exactly the owner decision I gated), and the load-bearing method via Learnings 58b/59 (verify a .Rbuildignore change at the BUILD level: stage dummy ‚Üí R CMD build ‚Üí tar tzf; .Rbuildignore is a regex file so comments must be paren-safe; zsh quirks). Every method held firsthand. The ‚àí2: S59‚Äôs candidate-file list named only 4 of the actual 8 NOTE contributors (RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.md, TECH_DEBT_AUDIT, PED_GV_AUDIT.{md,html}) ‚Äî it omitted 20250504_cran-comments.md, methodology_dashboard.py, dashboard.html, nprcgenekeepr_notes.txt. An executor trusting the list verbatim would have under-scoped and left the NOTE half-present. The full set was recoverable (S57‚Äôs note enumerated it, and S59‚Äôs own prescribed build-the-tarball method surfaces it), so the gap was self-correcting if you follow the method ‚Äî which I did (built the baseline first). Not misleading, but incomplete on the one thing the executor most needs enumerated. ROI: high.

Self-assessment (Session 60): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard, git, issues), reported, and STOPPED for the owner to pick the deliverable (‚Äú1‚Äù). Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE any file modification; declared TDD phase (N/A, with reason) every response. Enumerate-from-the-artifact: built the BASELINE tarball to get the true 8-file NOTE set BEFORE posing the scope question, so the AskUserQuestion options were grounded in reality, not S59‚Äôs partial list. Posed BOTH genuine author-decisions (scope + pattern style) via AskUserQuestion with concrete regex previews before editing. Verify-first was load-bearing: the build with 3 staged dupes simultaneously proved (a) the NOTE is eliminated (top level = only the 5 standard files), (b) the dupe-guard works, and (c) no regex-comment abort (RC=0). Right-sized: no workflow for a build-config edit (ultracode trivial-mechanical exemption). Kept comments paren-free (Learning 59b). Cleaned up all temp artifacts (trap + verified clean tree). Did NOT scope-creep into deleting PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (owner‚Äôs standing call). Stakeholder corrections: 0. Ding (one, self-inflicted, caught immediately): my first build attempt used rm -f nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz with no tarball present ‚Üí zsh nomatch ABORTED the script before R CMD build ran (the exact zsh-glob class Learning 59d warned about ‚Äî I applied it to the dupe array but forgot it for the cleanup glob). Fixed with unsetopt nomatch. Avoidable given the explicit handoff warning ‚Üí honest 8 (clean, authoritatively-verified deliverable, 0 corrections, but one round-trip the handoff had pre-warned). Met the S57‚ÄìS59 discipline bar; one notch below on first-try cleanliness.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner‚Äôs pick from the backlog. This deliverable is done; the ‚Äúnon-standard top-level files‚Äù NOTE is eliminated (tarball top level is now only the 5 standard files). Nothing is mid-flight. Candidates (rough value order): (1) #34 ‚Äî integrate qcStudbook() in modInput (highest-value open bug). (2) #38/#32/#26 ‚Äî configurable auto-generated ID format (overlapping enhancements; consolidation pass). (3) #37 ‚Äî exported-but-unused functions. (4) #30 ¬ß4C #16 ‚Äî convert the kept makeGeneticDiversityDashboard line-range exclusion to inline # nolint start/end (behavior-none). (5) (optional, ~2 min) confirm the full check-state with one devtools::check() ‚Äî expect 0 errors / 0 warnings / 2 environmental NOTEs (clock-skew future-timestamps + spelling; the top-level-files NOTE should now be gone ‚Äî this would be the belt-and-suspenders confirmation I skipped as redundant). Housekeeping (carried since S56): CLAUDE.md still says ‚ÄúVersion 1.0.8‚Äù but DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000 ‚Äî stale one-liner.

Key files (this session): .Rbuildignore (cran-comments consolidation in the dated-files block near :10; new tooling/audit/notes block appended at EOF, :81-88); CHANGELOG.md (Session 60 entry under [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 60); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). /tmp/s60_build.log + /tmp/s60_build_baseline.log (TRANSIENT build logs ‚Äî regenerate if needed). No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes.

Gotchas: (1) .Rbuildignore is a REGEX file ‚Äî every line incl.¬Ý# comments is a perl regex; an unbalanced paren ABORTS R CMD build. Keep comments balanced/paren-free; re-build after ANY edit (carried, S59). (2) zsh nomatch ABORTS on an unmatched glob ‚Äî rm -f <glob> with no match kills the whole script (even with -f); prefix unsetopt nomatch 2>/dev/null; setopt null_glob 2>/dev/null before any cleanup glob. (3) Dated cran-comments are now covered by one ^[0-9]+_cran-comments\.md$ ‚Äî do NOT re-add per-date exact lines; a new dated file is auto-ignored. NOTE: this tight pattern does NOT dupe-guard (20250504_cran-comments 2.md would NOT be ignored) ‚Äî intentional (cran-comments aren‚Äôt macOS-synced). (4) Tight vs broad patterns: ^methodology_dashboard\.py$/^dashboard\.html$/^nprcgenekeepr_notes\.txt$ are exact (no dupe-guard); the <NAME>.* ones (RECOMMENDED_SKILLS, PED_GV_AUDIT, TECH_DEBT_AUDIT) ARE dupe-guarded ‚Äî matched to whether the file is sync-prone. (5) PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html remains untracked on disk (now build-ignored) ‚Äî owner‚Äôs standing keep/remove call; ..Rcheck/00check.log is build-ignored check residue. (6) DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000 (CLAUDE.md‚Äôs ‚Äú1.0.8‚Äù is stale) ‚Äî glob nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz. (7) Verify any .Rbuildignore change at the BUILD level (stage dummy ‚Üí R CMD build ‚Üí tar tzf), NOT via devtools::check() and NOT by eye. ‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 59 (generalize .Rbuildignore dupe-guard to all methodology .md, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 59 ‚Äî 2026-06-12): Generalize Session 58‚Äôs .Rbuildignore macOS-dupe guard to the OTHER top-level methodology .md files (S58 SUGGESTED-NEXT #1) ‚Äî broaden the 7 remaining exact-match patterns to <NAME>.*\.md$ so a <NAME> 2.md / <NAME> copy.md sync dupe of any of them can never re-raise the R CMD check ‚Äúnon-portable file names‚Äù WARNING. (DONE.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Build-config-only change, behavior-none; TDD phase = N/A declared every response (no production R code; .Rbuildignore is dropped from the built tarball, so there is no shippable testthat assertion ‚Äî RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR does not apply, same as S58). One close-out commit on add-methodology. - The change (.Rbuildignore:78-85): broadened 7 patterns ^NAME\.md$ ‚Üí ^NAME.*\.md$ for PROJECT_LEARNINGS, CLAUDE, SESSION_RUNNER, SAFEGUARDS, BACKLOG, ROADMAP, CHANGELOG. After S58 only SESSION_NOTES (:81) was .*-guarded; this kills the dupe-WARNING class for the whole methodology cluster (8 files now). Also rewrote the block‚Äôs comment to be regex-safe and added an inline NOTE ‚Äî see the finding below. - Owner decisions (AskUserQuestion, before editing): (1) pattern style = loose .* (matches the S58 line; over ( [0-9]+| copy)? targeted form); (2) scope = the already-ignored methodology cluster only (pure behavior-none ‚Äî those 7 are already excluded; only their dupe forms get newly covered). Explicitly DEFERRED adding currently-UNignored docs (RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.md, audit files) ‚Äî that would change tarball contents + shrink the top-level-files NOTE, a DIFFERENT change. - In-flight finding (caught by the build step, fixed, NOT shipped): every line in .Rbuildignore is a perl regex ‚Äî including # comments (they only ‚Äúwork‚Äù because each is a valid regex matching no real path). My first multi-line comment held an unbalanced ( ‚Üí R CMD build aborted with invalid regular expression. Fixed to balanced/paren-free comment lines + an inline NOTE warning future editors. (Learning 59b.) - Verification (firsthand, both levels): (a) RED-equivalent regex probe across all 7 names (grepl, ignore.case, perl ‚Äî mirrors R CMD build‚Äôs case-insensitive relative-path match): OLD exact patterns MISS every dupe form (the leak); NEW .* form matches <NAME> 2.md AND <NAME> copy.md, does NOT over-match <NAME>.Rmd/<NAME>_archive.txt, canonical names stay excluded ‚Äî all 7 OK. (b) Build-equivalent (authoritative): staged 14 real spaced dupes (both forms √ó 7 names), ran R CMD build --no-build-vignettes --no-manual . (RC=0); the resulting nprcgenekeepr_1.1.0.9000.tar.gz (693 files) contained zero of the 7 names as .md ‚Üí all dupes + canonicals excluded, real content (DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE) present. trap cleanup EXIT removed all 14 dupes + the tarball; git status clean except the intended mods + the 2 pre-existing untracked (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). Did NOT run a full devtools::check() ‚Äî the WARNING is a pure function of tarball contents (verified directly); S58 established the dupe‚ÜíWARNING link firsthand and self-dinged the redundant check. - [news-vs-changelog] ‚Üí CHANGELOG only (Session 59 entry under [Unreleased]; dev-process history, not a user-facing release note ‚Üí NOT NEWS.md). [macos-dupe-scan]: no dupe present this session; explicit git add of only .Rbuildignore + SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (excluded ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Every method held firsthand. Verify-first was load-bearing: ran the all-7-names regex probe BEFORE editing (proving the leak), then the authoritative build-level test (14 REAL dupes ‚Üí R CMD build ‚Üí tarball) ‚Äî which caught my own invalid-regex comment bug before it could ship. Dings (two self-inflicted round-trips, both caught by verification before shipping, both turned into Learning 59): (a) I introduced the unbalanced-paren comment that aborted the first build ‚Äî avoidable had I known .Rbuildignore is a regex file; (b) my first verify loop used an unquoted $NAMES scalar that zsh did NOT word-split ‚Üí 1 bogus file instead of 7 (same shell-quirk class as S58‚Äôs ding). Core deliverable is clean and authoritatively verified, but two avoidable round-trips ‚Üí honest 8 (vs S58‚Äôs 9 with one). Met the S52‚ÄìS58 bar on discipline; below it on first-try cleanliness.

Nothing is mid-flight. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes.

Gotchas: (1) .Rbuildignore is a REGEX file ‚Äî every line incl.¬Ý# ‚Äúcomments‚Äù is a perl regex; a comment with an unbalanced ( ABORTS R CMD build (invalid regular expression). Keep comments balanced/paren-free (there‚Äôs now an inline NOTE saying so); re-build after ANY .Rbuildignore edit. (2) This environment‚Äôs shell is zsh ‚Äî for n in $VAR does NOT word-split an unquoted scalar (bash does); use an explicit literal list. (3) The dupe-WARNING class is now covered for the 8 .Rbuildignore-listed methodology .md files (SESSION_NOTES + the 7); it is NOT covered for top-level docs that aren‚Äôt in .Rbuildignore at all (RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.md, audit files) ‚Äî a dupe of THOSE would still WARN; see SUGGESTED-NEXT (1). (4) Verify any .Rbuildignore change at the BUILD level (stage dummy ‚Üí R CMD build ‚Üí tar tzf), NOT via devtools::check() and NOT by eye (S58 gotcha, still true). (5) DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000 (CLAUDE.md‚Äôs ‚Äú1.0.8‚Äù is stale) ‚Äî glob nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

Gotchas: (1) the macOS dupe SESSION_NOTES 2.md will likely RE-APPEAR (sync conflict) and re-raise the check WARNING until the .Rbuildignore pattern is broadened ‚Äî see SUGGESTED NEXT (1); rm it again or apply the permanent fix. (2) PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html + the build-ignored ..Rcheck/ remain untracked ‚Äî the HTML is a real audit artifact, left for the owner to keep/remove; it only contributes to the accepted top-level-files NOTE, not a WARNING. (3) the live SESSION_NOTES.md is the Jun-12 581 KB file ‚Äî beware sorting/globbing picking up a future macOS dupe again. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology (historical dev branch); master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 56 (IMPLEMENT #30 Phase 4, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 56 ‚Äî started 2026-06-11, completed 2026-06-12): IMPLEMENT Phase 4 of the issue #30 plan (¬ß5 Phase 4) ‚Äî the 6 behavior-sensitive .lintr line-exclusions (4 verified-safe REFACTORs + 2 RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR slices). (DONE ‚Äî all 6 removed; only makeGeneticDiversityDashboard remains excluded; lint_package() = 0.) Started: 2026-06-11 ¬∑ Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Two commits on add-methodology: 17e3fa06 (checkRequiredCols RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR) + 69c8d759 (5 refactors + de-exclude). The ¬ß4 line-specific-exclusion deliverable of #30 is now COMPLETE ‚Äî every R-file line exclusion is gone except the deliberately-kept makeGeneticDiversityDashboard. - checkRequiredCols.R (#1, genuine RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR): RED test pins the owner-chosen robust NA contract (c("dam","sex","birth",NA)‚Üíc("id","sire"); c("id",NA)‚Üíc("sire","dam","sex","birth") with reportErrors=TRUE ‚Äî errored before with "missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed"). GREEN = requiredCols[!requiredCols %in% cols] + delete the .lintr entry. REFACTOR = @details documents the intentional robust NA behavior + document(). Non-NA byte-identical; reportErrors=FALSE untouched. - 3 fully behavior-none REFACTORs: correctParentSex.R (if/else inverted to a guard clause ‚Äî report body falls through), fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R (else { if }‚Üíelse if, inline # nolint deleted), setExit.R (mapply‚Üíunlist(Map(...)) ‚Äî chooseDate always length-1). Each fix + de-exclude in the same change ([lint-net-zero]). - addSexAndAgeToGroup.R (#11) ‚Äî DOWNGRADED from the plan‚Äôs RED slice to a REFACTOR, then ADOPTED-ROBUST: my firsthand probe proved sapply‚Üíped$sex[match(ids,ped$id)] behavior-none on the documented domain (the age vapply already errors identically on any ‚âÝ1-row id, so the planned match()-error concern doesn‚Äôt occur). BUT the adversarial pass found it diverges on empty ids: old sapply dropped the sex column (2-col result) ‚Üí CRASHED the sole caller modBreedingGroups.R:438 (colnames(gp) <- c(<3 names>)); new keeps the documented 3-col schema ‚Üí empty table renders. Owner ADOPTED the new behavior (latent crash-fix + matches @return); pinned by an empty-ids contract test + a happy-path characterization test; @details documents it. - create_wkbk.R (#13) ‚Äî ACCEPTED cosmetic divergence: inner if(replace)‚Üíif(!replace) guard clause. On a non-logical non-coercible replace while the file exists, both error but the message text differs ("argument is not interpretable as logical"‚Üí"invalid argument type"); replace is documented logical, coercible values identical. Owner ACCEPTED (out-of-contract; no clean lint-passing rewrite preserves the message). - Verification (all firsthand/authoritative): lint_package() = 0; the 6 files lint-clean (parse_settings=FALSE); full suite 0 fail / 0 err / 159 skip (S49 baseline + 5 new passing expectations ‚Üí zero regression); devtools::check() 0 errors (1 WARNING + 2 NOTEs ‚Äî all pre-existing stray-file/clock-skew, chiefly the macOS SESSION_NOTES 2.md dupe; NOT mine). Adversarial verification wf_168f8dcf-1e5 (6 skeptics, each told to REFUTE + run R): 4 fully equivalent (6000-iter fuzz / 146 seeded / 21 / ~50 inputs), the 2 divergences owner-reviewed. Phase-3E: addSexAndAgeToGroup‚Äôs runtime path is covered by test_modBreedingGroups.R:1015-1122 (the viewGrp member view ‚Üí c("Ego ID","Sex","Age in Years") colnames), green in the full suite ‚Äî no app boot needed (pure fn). Process slip (self-caught): committed 17e3fa06 after adding @details without re-linting ‚Üí an 81-char line landed in it, fixed-forward in 69c8d759. [news-vs-changelog] ‚Üí CHANGELOG only. [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add (excluded ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html, SESSION_NOTES 2.md). #30 stays OPEN (close decision below).

Session 55 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 56): Score 9/10. What helped enormously: S55‚Äôs handoff named Phase 4 with the EXACT 6 files + dispositions (4 verified-safe + 2 slices), the precise per-file fixes, the plan reference, and the gotchas (the parse_settings=FALSE probe ‚Äî load-bearing again; [lint-net-zero] fix-and-de-exclude; always reconcile a sub-probe count against the real lint_package(); the checkRequiredCols casing already fixed S55; the macOS-dupe WARNING). Orientation was fast and every named fact held. The ‚àí1: S55 (following the plan) listed BOTH addSexAndAgeToGroup AND checkRequiredCols as RED slices, citing for addSexAndAge ‚Äúsapply‚Üívapply changes error semantics‚Äù ‚Äî but the real situation is subtler: match() is behavior-none on the documented domain (the age-vapply guard), and the ACTUAL divergence is on EMPTY ids (a shape/column-drop the plan never mentioned). The ‚ÄúRED slice‚Äù caution was warranted but for a different, unstated reason ‚Äî neither S55 nor the plan flagged the empty-ids case (it took the adversarial pass). Not misleading ‚Äî S55‚Äôs general verify-first/adversarial discipline is exactly what surfaced it. Also gotcha #5 (‚Äúnone of Phase 4‚Äôs files are Shiny runtime‚Äù) was slightly off (addSexAndAgeToGroup IS called by modBreedingGroups), but the spirit (unit/module suite is the gate) held. ROI: very high.

Self-assessment (Session 56): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard, git, issues) before any work; claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work. Declared the TDD phase every response; gated the PRE-RED scope decisions + every PRE-RED‚ÜíRED / RED‚ÜíGREEN / GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR transition via AskUserQuestion with exact actions spelled out. Verify-first was load-bearing twice: (1) before classifying, firsthand-probed both ‚ÄúRED‚Äù slices ‚Äî correctly downgrading addSexAndAge to behavior-none on the documented domain and confirming checkRequiredCols genuine RED, AND catching that my own first checkRequiredCols NA probe used the wrong length-case (masking the divergence) ‚Üí re-probed to nail the exact trigger; (2) ran a 6-skeptic adversarial workflow AFTER my firsthand checks ‚Äî which caught TWO divergences my probes MISSED (empty-ids column-drop + abuse-input error-message), then I REPRODUCED both firsthand and TRACED the caller (proving empty-ids is a latent crash-fix) before surfacing them. Honored the strict-TDD contract: did NOT silently ship the refutations ‚Äî paused, surfaced both via AskUserQuestion with caller-traced consequences; owner chose adopt/accept. AUTHORITATIVE gate (lint=0, suite=baseline, check=0-errors) run by me, not trusted from agents. Checkpoint-committed the RED slice before the 5-file batch (SAFEGUARDS ‚â§5-files-uncommitted + commit-before-mode-switch). Stakeholder corrections: 0 (4 AskUserQuestion gates + 2 finding-decisions, all answered as posed). Dings: (a) I MISSED the empty-ids divergence in my OWN firsthand probe (only happy/missing/dup) ‚Äî the skeptic caught it; my ‚Äúfully behavior-preserving‚Äù claim for addSexAndAge was premature (should have probed character(0) myself). (b) Committed 17e3fa06 without re-linting after the @details add ‚Üí an 81-char line slipped in (fixed-forward, but the checkpoint wasn‚Äôt clean). Both captured as Learning 56. Met/exceeded the S47‚ÄìS55 bar.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner decision on CLOSING #30 (then, only if desired, the optional ¬ß4C #16 inline-nolint conversion). Phase 4 completes the plan‚Äôs PRIMARY deliverable (¬ß4 line-specific exclusions): every R-file .lintr line exclusion is now gone except the deliberately-kept makeGeneticDiversityDashboard (#16, author won‚Äôt-delete NEW-20), and lint_package() = 0 ‚Üí the CI lint check is GREEN. Two paths: (A) CLOSE #30 ‚Äî the deliverable is complete and the ¬ß7 residual is already 0 today (no remaining lint to fix); or (B) convert the makeGeneticDiversityDashboard line-range exclusion to a line-shift-proof inline # nolint start/end block (plan ¬ß4C #16, ‚Äúoptional, lowest priority‚Äù; behavior-none) then close. Recommendation: CLOSE #30 and file the inline-nolint conversion as a trivial follow-up only if wanted. Other standing backlog (unchanged): #34 (integrate qcStudbook() in modInput, bug), #38/#32/#26 (configurable auto-gen ID format), #37 (exported-but-unused functions). Key files (this session): R/checkRequiredCols.R (+@details, %in%), R/addSexAndAgeToGroup.R (+@details, match()), R/correctParentSex.R, R/create_wkbk.R, R/fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R, R/setExit.R; .lintr (now only inst/application, inst/extdata, makeGeneticDiversityDashboard = 12L:55L, tests, vignettes); tests/testthat/test_checkRequiredCols.R + test_addSexAndAgeToGroup.R (new contract tests); plan ¬ß5 Phase 4 (marked DONE) + ¬ß4 dispositions; /tmp/probe_phase4.R + /tmp/probe_crc.R + /tmp/repro_refute.R (TRANSIENT firsthand probes ‚Äî regenerate if needed). Gotchas: (1) the macOS dupe SESSION_NOTES 2.md (untracked, 563 KB) is STILL the cause of the only devtools::check() WARNING ‚Äî rm it to clear the warning (NOT package code; out of Phase-4 scope so I left it ‚Äî a 30-second owner/next-session cleanup). (2) addSexAndAgeToGroup now ALWAYS returns the 3-col schema (sex an empty factor on empty ids) ‚Äî the only caller (modBreedingGroups:438) REQUIRES 3 cols, so this is a fix, but any NEW caller should expect 3 cols on empty input. (3) create_wkbk‚Äôs error message on a non-logical replace changed (cosmetic, out-of-contract). (4) re-lint (lint_package()) after EVERY edit incl.¬Ýroxygen, before committing (Learning 56e). Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology (the historical dev branch); master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 55 (IMPLEMENT #30 Phase 3, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 55 ‚Äî 2026-06-11): IMPLEMENT Phase 3 of the issue #30 plan (¬ß5 Phase 3) ‚Äî behavior-none .lintr line-exclusion removals + the underlying lint fixes. (DONE ‚Äî 6 of the 7 planned files; the 7th reclassified to Phase 4. lint_package() = 0.) Started: 2026-06-11 Status: DONE. Shipped 6 behavior-none REFACTORs, each deleting its .lintr line-exclusion in the SAME change ([lint-net-zero]); lintr::lint_package() stays 0. The 6: convertFromCenter.R/fillGroupMembers.R/hasGenotype.R (unnecessary_nesting collapses ‚Äî drop an else after an unconditional stop()/return(); else { if }‚Üíelse if); getLkDirectAncestors.R/getLkDirectRelatives.R (rename local var source‚ÜímsgSource ‚Äî undesirable_function_linter flags the symbol shadowing base::source; also deleted the 2 now-redundant inline # nolints in the latter); saveDataframesAsFiles.R (unnecessary_lambda ‚Üí vapply(dfList, inherits, logical(1L), what = "data.frame")). A per-file adversarial-verifier workflow (wf_eb654565-758, 7 skeptics each told to REFUTE behavior-equivalence + allowed to run R) returned refuted=FALSE for all 6 (each with empirical before/after R comparison). The 7th planned file, checkRequiredCols.R (¬ß4A #1), was RECLASSIFIED to Phase 4 mid-session: the verifier + my firsthand repro proved its sapply‚Üí%in% ‚Äúbehavior-none‚Äù fix actually changes EXPORTED error semantics ‚Äî on out-of-contract NA-in-cols the old if (!any(col == cols)) throws "missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed" while %in% returns the missing cols cleanly (non-NA output byte-identical, 5 cases). Per the strict-TDD REFACTOR contract (no behavior change) I paused, surfaced 3 options via AskUserQuestion; owner chose DEFER to Phase 4 (RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR w/ an NA-contract test) ‚Üí I reverted that file to its exact pre-session state (sapply + exclusion restored). Owner ALSO flagged the .lintr casing bug (R/CheckRequiredCols.R capital-C suppresses nothing on case-sensitive CI) ‚Üí fixed to lowercase R/checkRequiredCols.R (config-only, behavior-none; the deferred file‚Äôs L34 lint now suppressed on Linux too). Verification (all firsthand): lint_package() = 0; the 6 files lint-clean under parse_settings=FALSE; full suite 2140 pass / 0 fail / 0 err / 159 skip (= S49 baseline EXACTLY ‚Üí zero regression); devtools::check() 0 errors (1 WARNING + 1 NOTE both from stray top-level files ‚Äî chiefly the macOS dupe SESSION_NOTES 2.md, 563 KB, untracked, created 16:49; NOT from this change); man/ untouched (no roxygen edits). Plan updated to reflect the reclassification (¬ß4A #1, ¬ß5 Phase 3 done / Phase 4 +checkRequiredCols, ¬ß3 casing resolved, ¬ß6 dragon #6). [news-vs-changelog] ‚Üí CHANGELOG only. [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add (excluded ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html, SESSION_NOTES 2.md). #30 stays OPEN.

Session 54 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 55): Score 9/10. What helped enormously: S54‚Äôs handoff + the S53 plan gave me the EXACT per-file fixes for all 7 Phase 3 files, the parse_settings=FALSE auditing method (the load-bearing probe technique all session), the [lint-net-zero] fix-and-de-exclude discipline, and the gotcha ‚Äúalways reconcile a sub-probe lint count against the real lint_package().‚Äù The plan‚Äôs ¬ß6 dragons (the source-rename-not-stale, the casing bug, makeGeneticDiversityDashboard-keep) all held under firsthand probing. The suggested-next named Phase 3 with its precise file list, so orientation was fast. What was slightly off (the ‚àí1, and it became THE finding of this session): the plan classified checkRequiredCols as behavior-none, but it is NOT ‚Äî its %in% fix changes exported NA-edge error semantics; the plan‚Äôs ‚Äúagent-verified across present/absent/empty cases‚Äù never tested NA. Neither handoff nor plan flagged this ‚Äî it took adversarial verification to surface (the plan caught addSexAndAgeToGroup as the ‚Äúonly real behavior risk‚Äù ¬ß6 #4, but checkRequiredCols is a second). Not misleading ‚Äî the plan‚Äôs own verify-first discipline is what caught it. ROI: very high.

Self-assessment (Session 55): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard, git, issues) before any work; claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work. Honored the strict-TDD contract: when the mid-REFACTOR behavior change surfaced I did NOT ship it ‚Äî paused, surfaced 3 options, took the owner‚Äôs deferral, reverted that file cleanly. Honored TWO owner interjections precisely (the Fable-5 question; the casing-bug flag ‚Äî fixed it as a behavior-none config fix consistent with the deferral). The AUTHORITATIVE gate (lint=0, suite=baseline, check=0-errors) was run by me, not trusted from agents. Stakeholder corrections: 0 (3 AskUserQuestion gates + 2 interjections, all answered as posed). Dings: (a) I initially restored the capital-C casing bug as a literal ‚Äúrevert‚Äù and flagged it for Phase 4 rather than fixing it ‚Äî the owner had to point it out; fixing it was the better call and I should have proposed it proactively (behavior-none, independent of the deferred code). (b) Delivered 6 of 7 planned files ‚Äî correct given the finding, but Phase 3 is ‚Äúmostly done‚Äù with one file pushed to Phase 4. Met/exceeded the S47‚ÄìS54 bar.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = Phase 4 of the #30 plan (¬ß5 Phase 4, now SIX files): the 4 verified-safe REFACTORs (correctParentSex.R #12, create_wkbk.R #13, fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R #14, setExit.R #15) + TWO RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR slices (addSexAndAgeToGroup.R #11 AND checkRequiredCols.R #1 ‚Äî both exported, both change error semantics on out-of-contract input; each needs an AskUserQuestion at PRE-RED‚ÜíRED and RED‚ÜíGREEN per the TDD contract). Gotchas: (1) checkRequiredCols (#1) is now a Phase-4 RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR like #11 ‚Äî its .lintr entry casing is FIXED but the entry stays until the code is refactored + the entry deleted in the SAME commit ([lint-net-zero]). Also removed the stale .lintr getPyramidPlot.R = 25:27 exclusion (suppressed 0 lints) myself. Owner decisions (AskUserQuestion, plan ¬ß9): (1) KEEP implicit_integer_linter ON, fix all 74 with L (counts/indices/widths/lengths) or .0 (reals ‚Äî ped$age * 12.0, score/progress) ‚Äî NOT disable; (2) targeted inline # nolint for the 16 verified false-positives/idioms. Mechanical fixes: line_length wraps (roxygen + code, ALL verified as WRAPS not truncations), brace, keyword_quote (unquote symbol names ‚Äî display labels unchanged), return (drop terminal return()), paste(collapse=", ")‚ÜítoString(), sapply‚Üívapply(...,logical(1L)), and an if/else if chain ‚Üí switch() in logModuleEvent.R (fall-through ERROR= ,WARN=message + unnamed default = INFO; behavior-identical). # nolint (all PROVEN non-bugs): object_usage √ó6 (package-internal calcFounderContributions/gatedSeed lintr can‚Äôt resolve + founderStats which IS a modSummaryStatsServer formal, modSummaryStats.R:293), nonportable_path √ó3 (MIME strings), object_name √ó2 (base-R launch.browser arg), library() √ó2 (inst/shinytest/app.R harness), par() √ó3 (CRAN save/restore, getPyramidPlot). document() regenerated 3 man pages (getPyramidPlot/modBreedingGroupsServer/modPedigreeServer ‚Äî roxygen reflow, content identical; NAMESPACE unchanged). Session 53 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 54): Score 9/10. What helped enormously: S53‚Äôs plan (docs/planning/issue30-lintr-exclusion-cleanup-plan.md) was a high-quality evidence-based deliverable ‚Äî ¬ß4 gave the EXACT per-file fixes, ¬ß6 here-be-dragons (don‚Äôt delete makeGeneticDiversityDashboard; set_seed.R:11 is a #'-strip not a stale-exclusion; getLkDirectAncestors is a source rename) all held, ¬ß7 gave the residual linter-type table that drove my fan-out, ¬ß8 the verification commands, and ¬ß9 surfaced the two author decisions I then posed to the owner verbatim. Phase 1 was trivial to execute because the plan nailed it. The parse_settings=FALSE auditing method (Learning #53) was the load-bearing technique all session. What was slightly off (the ‚àí1): the plan‚Äôs residual was ‚Äú152‚Äù but the real lint_package() is 154 (a 2-lint drift ‚Äî undesirable_function is 6 not the plan‚Äôs 4); and ¬ß7 characterized implicit_integer as ‚Äúall in Shiny UI layout‚Äù when ~half are business logic (comparisons errors > 0, durations, counts) ‚Äî I found both firsthand; neither misled. The plan RECOMMENDED disabling implicit_integer (¬ß9 #1) but the owner chose to fix ‚Äî the plan correctly surfaced it as a decision, so this isn‚Äôt a plan error. ROI: very high ‚Äî the plan turned a sprawling cleanup into a mechanical execution.

Self-assessment (Session 54): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard, git, issues) before any work; claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work. Phase 1 = clean behavior-none REFACTOR, verified and committed ALONE as a boundary before the architect-mode residual work (SAFEGUARDS commit-before-scope-switch). When the owner expanded scope, honored it as a deliberate expansion (not scope creep) and surfaced the two genuine author decisions via AskUserQuestion per the plan. Right-sized the orchestration: a per-file editor‚Üíadversarial-verifier workflow for 18 files (the parallel structure + built-in adversarial verification fit the breadth), with the AUTHORITATIVE verification done by me, not trusted from agents. Verified, didn‚Äôt assume: reconciled the agents‚Äô lint claims against the real lint_package() CI command (caught the spurious coalesce_linter), spot-checked EVERY structural transform myself (switch conversion, sapply‚Üívapply, return removal, if/else bracing, and TWO logic-bearing modInput string-wraps ‚Äî chasing each apparent ‚Äútruncation‚Äù to confirm it was a clean wrap), and ran the full gate (lint + suite + check + man-consistency + runtime app boot). Stakeholder corrections: 0. Dings: (a) the workflow‚Äôs FIRST launch failed instantly on an args-as-string bug ‚Äî my scripting error, fixed + relaunched with 0 agents wasted; (b) I relied on agents for the 150+ edits rather than doing them all myself ‚Äî defensible given the scale + adversarial verify + my spot-checks + global verification, but it means I trust the workflow‚Äôs breadth; (c) one editor correctly edited inst/shinytest/app.R (outside R/) and flagged a constraint I‚Äôd written too narrowly. Met/exceeded the S47‚ÄìS53 bar.

‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = owner decision on #30 (plan ¬ß9 #4), then (if continuing) Phase 3 of the plan. The CI lint check is now GREEN (lint_package() = 0), so #30‚Äôs ‚Äúmany lint remain‚Äù is resolved. BUT the green relies on ~12 .lintr line-exclusions that still EXIST (suppressing ~28 lints in the Phase 3/4 files) ‚Äî the plan wanted most of those FIXED, not left excluded. So two paths: (A) the owner judges the green lint check sufficient and closes #30, filing the remaining exclusion cleanup as a follow-up; or (B) continue the plan: Phase 3 (behavior-none exclusion files ‚Äî checkRequiredCols.R [+ the .lintr casing bug CheckRequiredCols‚ÜícheckRequiredCols], getLkDirectAncestors.R [rename local source var], getLkDirectRelatives.R, saveDataframesAsFiles.R, convertFromCenter.R, fillGroupMembers.R, hasGenotype.R), then Phase 4 (behavior-sensitive: correctParentSex.R, create_wkbk.R, fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R, setExit.R + the ONE RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR: addSexAndAgeToGroup.R sapply‚Üímatch() with a contract test ‚Äî plan ¬ß4B #11 / ¬ß6 dragon #4), after which only makeGeneticDiversityDashboard (kept) remains ‚Üí then close #30. Key files: the plan (full ¬ß4/¬ß5/¬ß6/¬ß8/¬ß9); .lintr:13-31 (remaining exclusions); /tmp/lint_dump.R + /tmp/lint_phase1.R + /tmp/wf_residual_lint.mjs (this session‚Äôs reusable lint dump / per-file lint / the fan-out workflow ‚Äî TRANSIENT, regenerate if needed). Gotchas: (1) ALWAYS reconcile any agent/sub-probe lint count against the real lintr::lint_package() ‚Äî a parse_settings=FALSE probe can fire linters NOT in the project tag set (the coalesce_linter false alarm). (2) implicit_integer is KEPT ON now ‚Äî new code must type literals (L for counts, .0 for reals). (3) After ANY roxygen edit run devtools::document() and commit the regenerated man/ (R-CMD-check enforces code/doc match). (4) Phase-3E for Shiny runtime edits = BOOT the app from load_all (the unit suite doesn‚Äôt exercise the mod servers). (5) inst/shinytest is NOT .lintr-excluded ‚Üí inst/shinytest/app.R IS linted. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR.

‚ü¢ Carried ‚Äî Session 53 (PLAN #30 lintr cleanup, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 53 ‚Äî 2026-06-11): PLAN the cleanup of issue #30 (lintr) ‚Äî examine each file = line entry in .lintr‚Äôs exclusions: list() (18 R-file line-specific suppressions), identify the specific suppressed lint at each, classify fix-vs-keep, and write a plan to docs/planning/ to fix most/all of them. (DONE ‚Äî plan delivered) Started: 2026-06-11 Status: DONE. Deliverable = docs/planning/issue30-lintr-exclusion-cleanup-plan.md (planning session; NO R//tests//.lintr content changed ‚Äî implementation is the subsequent sessions, FM #18/#25). Evidence base (firsthand): lint_package(parse_settings=FALSE) bypassing the .lintr exclusions = 41 lints suppressed by the 18 line-excludes + 152 residual = 193 total in R/; cross-checked by an 18-file parallel examination workflow (wf_c7863094-8f1, 28 agents ‚Äî one examiner per excluded file proposing the EXACT fix + behavior-change risk, then an adversarial verifier on every behavior-affecting fix and every commented-code deletion). THREE agent conclusions were corrected by adversarial verification / my own reproduction (the value of verify-first): (1) examine said ‚Äúdelete the all-commented makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R‚Äù ‚Üí verifier caught it reverses an explicit author won‚Äôt-delete decision (NEW-20, .Rbuildignore‚Äôd, would trigger namespace fallout #35) ‚Üí KEEP-EXCLUDE; (2) examine said set_seed.R:11 exclusion is stale ‚Üí verifier proved removing it alone RE-FIRES the lint (a stray trailing #' that also leaks into man/set_seed.Rd fools commented_code_linter) ‚Üí FIX = strip the #'; (3) a getLkDirectAncestors.R agent (ran lint() with .lintr active ‚Üí the exclusion hid the lints) wrongly called L26/29/35 stale ‚Üí my parse_settings=FALSE repro proved undesirable_function_linter flags the local variable named source (it flags the SYMBOL shadowing base::source) ‚Üí FIX = rename the variable. Dispositions of the 18 entries: FIX 15 (~38 lints ‚Äî 10 behavior-none, 5 low-risk verified-safe), KEEP-EXCLUDE 1 (makeGeneticDiversityDashboard), REMOVE-STALE+fix-real 1 (getPyramidPlot.R = 25:27 suppresses 0 lints; real lints at L16/38/41-43). 4 config bugs found: (a) .lintr lists "R/CheckRequiredCols.R" wrong-case ‚Üí misses on case-sensitive CI; (b) the getPyramidPlot 25:27 dead entry; (c) the source ‚Äúundesirable function‚Äù hits = local var rename; (d) commented_code_linter IS active via tags ‚Äî the #commented_code_linter = NULL line is a dead no-op (resolves #30‚Äôs confusion). Plan phases (vertical slices, 1 session each): P1 comment/doc-only + config hygiene ¬∑ P2 getPyramidPlot ¬∑ P3 behavior-none logic refactors (7 files) ¬∑ P4 behavior-sensitive (5 files; addSexAndAgeToGroup is the ONE RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR ‚Äî exported, sapply‚Üímatch() w/ a contract test) ¬∑ P5 optional residual-152. Phase-3E: N/A ‚Äî planning doc, no runtime behavior changed; the plan‚Äôs lint counts/line numbers were all firsthand-verified (build-equivalent for a plan = the evidence holds, not a render). Learning #53 added (parse_settings=FALSE auditing trap; line-drift both ways; verify-first). [news-vs-changelog] ‚Üí CHANGELOG only. Commit: one docs: close-out (the plan + SESSION_NOTES + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS #53) on add-methodology, explicit git add ([macos-dupe-scan]: .DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html excluded). #30 stays OPEN.

The gotcha ‚Äúlint #30 is known/accepted style debt, independent of #42‚Äù held and correctly told me the lint red is a style backlog, not a correctness problem. The branch model note (close-outs live on add-methodology; master got #42 via PR #43 but not the notes) was accurate and unchanged this session. What was missing (the ‚àí2): no #30-specific detail ‚Äî no pointer to the .lintr exclusion structure, the commented_code_linter history, or the [lint-net-zero]/Learning #7 line-shift trap that turned out central (I found those via PROJECT_LEARNINGS + firsthand probing). That absence is expected ‚Äî S52 was a pkgdown session, not a #30 session, so #30 detail wasn‚Äôt its job; the score is 8 not 9 only because a one-line ‚Äúsee Learning #7 for the line-shift trap before touching .lintr‚Äù would have saved a few minutes.
