(NEW-46 ‚úÖ S14 ‚Äî geneDrop rejects duplicate ids; audit‚Äôs ‚Äúwrong values‚Äù was empirically a CRASH at rownames(ped)<-ids L97, doubly-masked in the pipeline by removeDuplicates+kinship‚Äôs guard. Learning #14.) Remaining correctness/debt candidates from PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md (also listed in BACKLOG.md), with the load-bearing gotchas preserved below: 1. Other correctness fixes (test-first): NEW-34 ‚úÖ (S4, dc695a3b). NEW-40 ‚úÖ (S5, ea5d28fa). NEW-37 ‚úÖ (S6, 6b0ae333). NEW-48 ‚úÖ (S7, 19350559 ‚Äî clear stop() guard in calcFEFG/calcFE/calcFG). NEW-25 ‚úÖ (S8, 587ba042 ‚Äî clear stop() on empty input in getProportionLow). NEW-52 ‚úÖ (S9, e3c7e8b3 ‚Äî n<2 sd ‚Üí NA+warning, n<1 ‚Üí stop in cumulateSimKinships; audit‚Äôs M2 cancellation DISPROVED as unreachable for dyadic-rational kinship values, Learning #10). Remaining: NEW-53 ‚úÖ (S11 ‚Äî fixed in makeSimPed/getPotentialParents/createSimKinships; setDT(ped)‚Üíped <- as.data.table(ped). Audit‚Äôs ‚Äúsire/dam overwritten‚Äù + ‚Äúadds population column in place‚Äù both DISPROVEN ‚Äî the only leak was a by-reference data.frame‚Üídata.table CLASS flip; content preserved by $<- shallow-copy. Learning #12), NEW-45 ‚úÖ (S13 5e228bd9/2527d6bc/ceab030f ‚Äî enforce ‚Äúno period in IDs‚Äù at qcStudbook+geneDrop+both generators+the 2 error renderers. This time the audit‚Äôs mechanism was CORRECT (period truncates the strsplit(rownames, ".") id/parent rebuild); the real decision was a NEW class ‚Äî a DOMAIN-DEFINITION question (does the ID domain permit .?), settled by the project‚Äôs own spec input_format.html ‚ÄúAlphanumeric characters (no symbols)‚Äù ‚Üí PRECLUDED ‚Üí reject, not support. Learning #13). ‚öÝ Several remaining items are masked by upstream invariants/non-default inputs ‚Äî verify reachability with real data BEFORE ‚Äúfixing‚Äù. NEW-34/40/37/48 ALL had MIXED, per-mechanism verdicts ‚Äî the confirmed template: enumerate each distinct trigger and confirm reachability through the full qcStudbook pipeline first (Learning #6), AND empirically reproduce the ACTUAL failure mode ‚Äî the audit‚Äôs stated mechanism AND its prescribed fix can BOTH be WRONG (NEW-48 was a crash not ‚Äúsilent NA‚Äù; NEW-25‚Äôs ‚Äúadd a terminal else‚Äù would NOT have fixed the crash, which is at the first if (NA) ‚Äî Learnings #8a/#9). ‚öÝ But reachability is NOT always a pipeline-masking question: NEW-25‚Äôs getProportionLow had ZERO live callers (orphaned by dead NEW-20 code), so ‚Äúverify reachability‚Äù meant an exhaustive caller-trace (incl.¬Ýdynamic dispatch + commented-out consumers), not a qcStudbook-masker analysis (Learning #9). Known maskers in qcStudbook: addUIds (qcStudbook.R:180 ‚Äî eliminates partial parentage, NEW-48), convertSexCodes (qcStudbook.R:184 ‚Äî folds H‚ÜíU / NA‚ÜíU, NEW-37/S6 gotcha #2), addParents (injects founder lines for missing parents, NEW-40). For NEW-53, recall checkParentAge is NOT an integrity guard (drops NA-birth rows, R/checkParentAge.R:91 ‚Äî S5 gotcha #2). - Small cleanup (found Session 4, NOT fixed): tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R test "works with records with no potential parent" has a copy/paste slip ‚Äî it recomputes into a local ped but then asserts the OLD top-level potentialParents[[1L]]$id instead of anything about ped, so it verifies nothing about its own scenario. Trivial to fix; out of NEW-34‚Äôs scope. 2. Dead code ‚Äî NEW-20: delete makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R (+ its fully-commented test). 3. Quick-win duplication: extract getFounders(ped)/isFounder(ped) (PED-1 / NEW-17 / Session 1 KIN-2). ‚öÝ Do NOT naively unify the adjacent descendants lines ‚Äî calcRetention.R:27 filters by ped$population; the calc* copies do not. 4. Consolidation / overhauls (own sessions): NEW-13/23 (calcFE/calcFG delegate to calcFEFG ‚Äî when doing this, collapse the now-triplicated S7 partial-parentage guard into the single calcFEFG; S7 deliberately kept 3 inline copies per the user‚Äôs scope decision), NEW-12 / XARCH-3 (Shiny progress hook), XARCH-1 (two coexisting Shiny apps ‚Äî planning session). 5. Pre-existing test-infra debt (FOUND this session, NOT fixed): the 22 test-app-* / test-e2e-* files all call create_test_app(), which is defined nowhere in the repo ‚Üí 154 suite ERRORS whenever shinytest2+chromote are installed (they are on this machine). Independent of any compute code. Either define the missing helper or gate the tests behind skip_if_not_installed/existence checks. Worth its own session. All findings + per-finding verdicts are in PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md and the workflow artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/w9oz3tkdf.output.

How You Will Be Evaluated

The user rates every session‚Äôs handoff on: 1. Was the ACTIVE TASK block sufficient to orient the next session? 2. Were key files listed with line numbers? 3. Were gotchas and traps flagged? 4. Was the ‚Äúwhat‚Äôs next‚Äù actionable and specific?

Session history accumulates below this line. Newest session at the top.

Session 45 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 46)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most (load-bearing): S45‚Äôs GOTCHA 1 (‚Äúthe 14-file R/ reformat is uncommitted, behaviorally inert ‚Äî decide commit/discard BEFORE starting a code slice, especially 8e-5 which edits the SAME files‚Äù) set the session‚Äôs entire opening move. It correctly framed the reconcile-first decision the owner then made (‚Äúcommit reformat; then 8e-5‚Äù), and it‚Äôs why the reformat was committed as a clean baseline before any 8e-5 edit. GOTCHA 6 (‚Äú8e-5 is PRODUCTION R/ ‚Üí full RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR + devtools::check() + owner go/no-go‚Äù) + the ‚áí line‚Äôs pointer to plan ¬ß5/¬ß7 were decision-complete on the PROCESS: I knew before reading code that this needed an owner gate + the full TDD cycle + a check, and the plan ¬ß2.5/¬ß7/¬ß8e-5 carried every implementation detail (the exact gate code, the hook placement, that set_seed exists and is exported, that both modules have ZERO set.seed). - What helped (applied directly): GOTCHA 4 (‚Äúread the regression as expectation_success with NOT_CRAN=true; baseline is 2166, never sum(nb)‚Äù) ‚Äî I read 2166 first try and correctly attributed the +14 to the new tests, no false jump. GOTCHA 5 + [macos-dupe-scan] (commit only your own files via explicit git add) ‚Äî applied to all 3 commits. GOTCHA 2 (namespace discriminators) wasn‚Äôt needed for 8e-5 but is good 8e-6 context. - What was below handoff altitude (the one deduction): S45 characterized the reformat as '‚Ä¶'‚Üí"‚Ä¶" / 0‚Üí0L / round(x,2)‚Üíround(x,2L) ONLY. It did not mention the reformat had ALSO rewrapped #' roxygen comments and changed savePlotToFile‚Äôs formals defaults to integer (8L) ‚Äî which desynced man/ and surfaced (via document()) as a real savePlotToFile codoc drift requiring a regen commit to keep check() clean. S45 couldn‚Äôt fully have known (it never ran document() on the reformatted tree), but a note that ‚Äúthe reformat touched roxygen/signatures too ‚Äî run document() before check()‚Äù would have saved a discovery step. Minor. - What was wrong: nothing. The reformat-is-inert claim held (re-verified 2166‚Üí2180); the 2166 baseline, the ‚ÄúPRODUCTION ‚Üí full check + owner gate‚Äù classification, and the commit-hygiene exclusions all verified correct firsthand. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî GOTCHA 1 made the reconcile-first call obvious, GOTCHA 6 + the plan pointer made the TDD/owner-gate shape decision-complete, and the regression-read guidance prevented a measurement artifact.

Session 46 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 47)

Score: 9/10. The S46 ‚áí line was a near-complete spec for 8e-6: it named the exact flow (upload_and_wait‚Üí#dataInput-getData‚ÜíGVA‚Üíbreeding), the deferred-block inventory (4 pedigree S40 + 2 GV S42 + 3 BG S43), the env vars, the ‚Äú8e-6 does NOT require 8e-5 ‚Üí use Option-C invariants‚Äù steer, and the üêâüêâ dragons (runtime/flake, fixture-needed). What helped: the Option-C steer let me keep asserts structural without chasing the (unproven) seed channel; the deferred-block list scoped the work instantly; the 2180 baseline + ‚Äúexpectation_success, never sum(nb)‚Äù matched my regression EXACTLY. What was missing (the ‚àí1): no mention that the AppDriver subprocess resolves the package from the SYSTEM library (/Library/Frameworks/...) ‚Äî NOT the renv cache ‚Äî under RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=false, and that this install can be badly stale (mine was Jul-2025); I discovered this firsthand and reinstalled before spiking. What was inaccurate (plan-level, not the handoff): the plan‚Äôs ¬ß2.3 ‚Äúoutput tier‚Äù framing (get_value(output=) empty when hidden) is only half-true ‚Äî the output un-suspends to non-NULL on tab-activation without data, so is.null() does not discriminate data; the rendered-DOM content is the real discriminator. ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the ‚áí line + recon workflow saved a half-session of discovery.

What Session 47 Did

Deliverable: plan slice 8e-6a (docs/planning/phase8e-assertion-strengthening-subplan.md ¬ß5/¬ß8e-6, issue #40) ‚Äî the FIRST of three vertical 8e-6 flows: wire the real upload+QC ‚Üí pedigree-table flow end-to-end and revive the 3 NULL‚Äôd pedigree blocks (S40) as genuine data-bearing E2E assertions. Owner-scoped to 8e-6a (pre-RED AskUserQuestion; GVA=8e-6b, breeding=8e-6c are later sessions per the plan‚Äôs per-flow boundary). Date: 2026-06-10. Branch: add-methodology (NOT on remote). Commit (1): one test:/docs: close-out (test-e2e-pedigree-module.R + -detailed.R + -tutorial.R + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS #47 + these notes), explicit git add. TDD flow (4 AskUserQuestion gates): pre-RED scope (‚Üí 8e-6a) ¬∑ PRE-RED‚ÜíRED ¬∑ RED‚ÜíGREEN ¬∑ REFACTOR declined (the 3-line upload duplication is idiomatic with these files‚Äô inline setup; factoring only it would be inconsistent + scope creep). RED was confirmed FIRSTHAND (empty/suspended #pedigree-pedigreeTable div ‚Üí every content assert fails) before GREEN. Recon first (read-only, PRE-RED): an 8-agent census + adversarial completeness critic (wf_855f37cd-4ac, ~349k subagent tokens) mapped the fixture, every module output id, the deferred blocks, the reportGV/groupAddAssign return shapes, and the helper surface; the critic caught 3 category-killing errors in the raw census (DT value levels, the numGp+1 myth, the unattainable strict-kinship invariant) that would have produced a wrong RED test downstream. Then a live-browser SPIKE (the 8e-6 hard gate) settled G2/G4/G5 (see ACTIVE TASK above). Key files: tests/testthat/test-e2e-pedigree-module.R (A1 ‚Äúhas data table‚Äù ‚Äî upload at :71, asserts "of 375 entries" :82 + "sire" :86); tests/testthat/test-e2e-pedigree-detailed.R (A2 ‚Äî upload :74, "of 375 entries" :85 + "sire" :89 + "dam" :93; A4 ‚Äústatus filter‚Äù UNCHANGED, honest pane-active); tests/testthat/test-e2e-pedigree-tutorial.R (A3 ‚Äúrow count display options‚Äù ‚Äî upload :36, "dataTables_length" :46 + "of 375 entries" :50). Driver per block: fixture <- system.file("extdata","obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv", package="nprcgenekeepr"); loaded <- upload_and_wait(app, fixture); if (!loaded) skip(...) BEFORE the existing navigate_to_tab(app, "Pedigree Browser"). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/spike_8e6a.R, /tmp/spike_8e6a_v2.R, /tmp/mutation_8e6a.R. Verification: 3/3 files GREEN (module 6/6 ¬∑ detailed 8/8 ¬∑ tutorial 9/9, 0 fail/0 error/0 skip under the e2e env). [mutation-check] ALL PASS (right content TRUE; wrong row-count 999 & 374, foreign genotype, foreign-pane Breeding Groups, same-pattern-on-a-different-element ‚Üí all FALSE). Non-e2e regression 2180 expectation_success / 0 failed / 0 error / 156 skip / 5 modPyramid warn / 0 non-e2e offenders (S46 baseline EXACT; test-only). Phase-3E = the live GREEN AppDriver run (#31). Reinstalled current source into the SYSTEM lib first (the subprocess‚Äôs library; was Jul-2025 stale). Self-assessment: strong. Ran the 8e-6 hard gate (the live spike) BEFORE writing any RED ‚Äî exactly as the plan/8e-1 precedent demands ‚Äî and let the spike CORRECT a plan assumption (the is.null output-tier) rather than enshrine it; the resulting assertions are all discriminating (mutation-proven), not green-on-arrival. Caught the stale system-lib install before it could test phantom behavior. Held all 4 gates via AskUserQuestion. Kept strict 1-flow scope (did NOT bleed into 8e-6b/c despite the shared driver). Could improve: the recon workflow was generous (8 agents) for a slice that ultimately touched 3 blocks ‚Äî though the critic‚Äôs corrections justified it, and they seed 8e-6b/c. GOTCHAS for 8e-6b/c (full ‚áí plan above): 1. Reinstall current R/ into the SYSTEM lib before any e2e spike (RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=false R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --no-docs --library=/Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library .). The renv-cache install is a DIFFERENT, separately-stale copy. 2. get_value(output=DToutput) returns a json-class atomic string and un-suspends to non-NULL even with NO data ‚Äî assert rendered-DOM CONTENT via get_html_safe(app, "#<ns>-<id>") (the row-count info / column headers / length menu), not is.null(get_value(...)). 3. suspendWhenHidden: an output renders only once its (possibly NESTED) tab is active. Pedigree/Rankings are default-visible; GVA Summary/Visualizations + breeding Statistics/Group-Detail are hidden inner tabs ‚Üí set_inputs(<inner tabsetPanel id>=...) first (the inner-tabset inputIds were NOT captured by recon ‚Äî get them firsthand). 4. Fixture obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv renders exactly 375 pedigree rows ("of 375 entries"); upload via the helper default (pedFile/pedigreeFileOne, fileType Excel routes .csv‚Üíread.csv). It flows clean QC‚ÜíGVA‚Üíbreeding (recon-verified). 5. Commit ONLY your own files via explicit git add (.DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html are owner cruft).

What Session 46 Did

Deliverable: plan slice 8e-5 (docs/planning/phase8e-assertion-strengthening-subplan.md ¬ß7, issue #40) ‚Äî the FIRST 8e PRODUCTION R/ change [production-in-disguise]: an env/option-gated set_seed() determinism hook (Option A) in modGeneticValueServer + modBreedingGroupsServer, owner-gated (approach choice + full RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR, all four transitions via AskUserQuestion). Also, on the owner‚Äôs ‚Äúcommit reformat; then 8e-5‚Äù instruction, committed the owner‚Äôs concurrent 14-file R/ reformat as a clean baseline + a follow-on docs: man-regen the reformat desynced. Date: 2026-06-10. Branch: add-methodology (NOT on remote). Commits (3): d0989408 (style: ‚Äî the owner‚Äôs 14 R/ files + test_modPyramid.R formatter pass) ‚Üí a docs: man-regen (3 files) ‚Üí the 8e-5 feat:/test: close-out ‚Äî all staged via explicit git add (.DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html excluded). The TDD flow (5 AskUserQuestion gates): (1) pre-RED scope/approach ‚Äî Option A (env/option gate) vs Option B (UI seed input) vs Option C-only (no production change) ‚Üí Option A; (2) PRE-RED‚ÜíRED; (3) RED‚ÜíGREEN; (4) GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR. RED was confirmed FIRSTHAND before GREEN (6 genuine failures + 2 guards passing); no synthetic RED. What was built: the gate seed <- getOption("nprcgenekeepr.gva_seed", as.integer(Sys.getenv("NPRC_GVA_SEED", NA))); if (!is.na(seed)) set_seed(seed) (+ bg_seed/NPRC_BG_SEED) at the top of each eventReactive (after req(), ahead of withProgress); REFACTORed into one @noRd helper gatedSeed(optionName, envName); 8 browser-free testServer tests (3 RED + 1 guard per module). Key files: R/set_seed.R (NEW @noRd gatedSeed(optionName, envName) after set_seed, ~:29-44; calls the exported set_seed); R/modGeneticValue.R:137-139 (gatedSeed("nprcgenekeepr.gva_seed","NPRC_GVA_SEED") in gvResults eventReactive, after req(pedigree()) :135, ahead of withProgress ‚Äî the engine reportGV() is at ~:189); R/modBreedingGroups.R:193-195 (gatedSeed("nprcgenekeepr.bg_seed","NPRC_BG_SEED") in breedingGroups eventReactive, after req(pedigree()) :191 ‚Äî engine groupAddAssign() at ~:280); tests/testthat/test_modGeneticValue.R (+4 tests at EOF ‚Äî determinism/mock-called/no-op-guard/env-fallback); tests/testthat/test_modBreedingGroups.R (+4 tests at EOF, mirror); man/{appServer,modSummaryStatsServer,savePlotToFile}.Rd (regenerated ‚Äî reformat desync, in the docs: commit). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/app_smoke_8e5.log. Verification: 8/8 seed tests GREEN / 14 expectations (RED proven first: 6 fail + 2 guard pass at HEAD). Non-e2e regression 2180 expectation_success / 0 failed / 0 error / 156 skip / 5 pre-existing modPyramid warn / 0 non-e2e offenders (2166 + 14; default path unchanged = R2 mitigation). devtools::check() 0 errors / 0 warnings / 3 NOTEs (stale spelling.Rout.save + future-timestamps + top-level dev files = S35 baseline; no gatedSeed NOTE). Phase-3E: runModularApp() working-tree source HTTP 200, gate unset. document() no delta for the @noRd helper. Lint net-zero on changed R/. Self-assessment: strong. Held all 5 gates (scope + 3 TDD transitions) via AskUserQuestion; confirmed RED firsthand and guarded against a vacuous identical(NULL,NULL) pass with an explicit length>0 assertion (caught the subtle stochastic-RED-capture trap before it could fake green); committed the owner‚Äôs reformat only after RE-verifying it inert (didn‚Äôt trust S45‚Äôs memory, per FM #11); diagnosed the man/ desync to its true cause (reformat rewrapped #' comments + savePlotToFile 8L) via git show rather than assuming, and committed the regen separately with explicit causality; correctly classified the gatedSeed lintr flag as a [stale-namespace] transient and confirmed via check() rather than ‚Äúfixing‚Äù it; kept 1-deliverable scope (did NOT bleed into 8e-6). Could improve: nothing material; the man/ desync was an unforeseeable second-order consequence of committing the reformat, handled cleanly once surfaced. GOTCHAS for the next session (8e-6 or 8e-7; full plan in the ‚áí line above + plan ¬ß5/¬ß8e-6): 1. 8e-5 is DONE and 8e-6‚Äôs upload ids are correct (8e-4 fixed dataInput-). To add OPTIONAL exact-value E2E assertions, set the seed via withr::local_envvar(NPRC_GVA_SEED="42") (and NPRC_BG_SEED) AROUND AppDriver$new() ‚Äî the subprocess inherits the parent env (reliable); AppDriver$new(seed=) does NOT control the click-triggered RNG (plan ¬ß2.5). But 8e-6 does NOT require 8e-5 ‚Äî Option-C structural invariants are robust and usable regardless. 2. The gate is a no-op by default. With no option/env, getOption(..., NA) ‚áí NA ‚áí no set_seed. The 2180-pass regression IS the default-path-unchanged proof; don‚Äôt re-litigate it. 3. The gatedSeed object_usage_linter ‚Äúno visible global function‚Äù is a [stale-namespace] artifact (single-file/installed-namespace can‚Äôt resolve a just-added cross-file helper). devtools::check() (full namespace) emits no such NOTE. Do NOT ‚Äúfix‚Äù it; reinstall + re-lint if you must confirm. 4. ‚öÝ After committing ANY R/ reformat, run document() + diff man//NAMESPACE BEFORE check() ‚Äî a formatter can rewrap #' comments and change signature defaults (8‚Üí8L), desyncing generated docs (a codoc WARNING no content grep catches). This session‚Äôs docs: commit fixed exactly that. 5. Read the regression as expectation_success with NOT_CRAN=true + load_all ‚Äî baseline is now 2180 (NOT 2166; +14 from the 8e-5 tests). Never sum(nb) (Learning #43e). 6. Commit ONLY your own files via explicit git add ([macos-dupe-scan]: .DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Session 44 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 45)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most (load-bearing): S44‚Äôs ‚áí line was decision-complete on the namespace fix ‚Äî it named all 5 sites with exact files + line numbers (helper:150/:154, error-states:24/:45, boundary:43), drew the dataInput-vs-data-module="input" distinction (appUI.R:123 vs modInput.R:31), gave the ¬ß2.4 DO-NOT-CHANGE list, the RED‚ÜíGREEN classification, and ‚Äúread R/modInput.R observeEvent(getData) validation ~301-419 FIRSTHAND‚Äù ‚Äî which led me straight to the no-file showNotification path that became es#1‚Äôs assertion. GOTCHAS 1-7 reinforced every load-bearing point. - What helped (applied directly): the browser env triplet; ‚Äúread the regression as expectation_success with NOT_CRAN=true‚Äù (I read it right the first time and correctly attributed the +4 to the new helper tests rather than reporting a false jump); the [macos-dupe-scan] commit-hygiene list (which became load-bearing for an UNEXPECTED reason ‚Äî the concurrent formatter; see below). - What was below handoff altitude (the deductions): (1) the plan/handoff framed the namespace wrong id as one that ‚Äúthrows‚Äù ‚Äî it actually WARNS (set_inputs on an unbound id) and never sets, so the real discriminator is the value read-back, not a caught throw (the click path DOES throw). (2) ‚Äúconfirm the modInput validation surfaces a visible error/state‚Äù + ‚Äúsome boundary blocks need data ‚Üí defer to 8e-6‚Äù ‚Äî neither fully held: WITHOUT a file there is NO error-state except the no-file toast; the zero/non-numeric-age blocks surface nothing (the textInput just reflects), so the genuine assertion is the round-trip + pane-active, not an error-state; and ZERO blocks needed 8e-6 deferral. Both surfaced by the firsthand spike the handoff correctly told me to run. - What was wrong: nothing material ‚Äî the 5 sites, the namespace, the DO-NOT-CHANGE list, and the env all verified correct firsthand. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the namespace fix was decision-complete; the only surprises (warn-not-throw; no pre-data error-state; 0 deferrals) were resolved by the spike the handoff directed me to.

What Session 45 Did

Converted all 26 browser-booting blocks in test-e2e-error-states.R (13) + test-e2e-boundary-conditions.R (13) from nchar(html)>100 / dead-grepl / interaction-noop-tryCatch to behavioral assert_active_pane(...), and fixed the dataInput- namespace at all 5 ¬ß2.4 sites + upload_and_wait. Strict TDD, gated PRE-RED‚ÜíRED then RED‚ÜíGREEN via AskUserQuestion. Date: 2026-06-10. Branch: add-methodology (NOT on remote). Commit: one test:/docs: close-out (4 test-tree files + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS #45 + these notes), staged via explicit git add. ‚öÝ The session‚Äôs biggest non-deliverable event ‚Äî a concurrent external formatter (FM #22 in the wild): orientation showed a clean tree (only .DS_Store), but my first spike‚Äôs pkgload::load_all FAILED on a parse error in makeFounderStatsTable.R:68. Investigation: an automated style pass ('‚Ä¶'‚Üí"‚Ä¶", 0‚Üí0L) had rewritten 14 R/ production files and broke 2 by converting single-quoted HTML strings to double quotes without escaping the inner class="‚Ä¶"; the modified set was GROWING (11‚Üí13‚Üí15‚Üí17) across read-only polls ‚Üí running CONCURRENTLY (likely an editor/styler/another agent on the open #30 lint work). Per SAFEGUARDS / FM #22 I did NOT touch/revert/fix the unauthored uncommitted edits ‚Äî STOPPED and gated with the owner. The formatter then SELF-HEALED both broken files and settled; the regression confirmed the reformat is behaviorally inert (2162 baseline held). The 8e-4 commit excludes all 14 R/ files (the owner‚Äôs in-progress work). Key files: tests/testthat/test-e2e-error-states.R (13 blocks rewritten); tests/testthat/test-e2e-boundary-conditions.R (13 blocks rewritten); tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R (upload_and_wait ~:149-167 ‚Äî default module_id="dataInput" + do.call/stats::setNames-derived upload id; docstring updated); tests/testthat/test_helper_shinytest2.R (new fake_app_upload_recorder stub + 2 upload_and_wait tests, +4 expectations); R/modInput.R:305-308 (no-file showNotification ‚Äî the es#1 assertion target), :119-121 (minParentAge textInput, default ‚Äú2.0‚Äù), :130 (getData actionButton); R/appUI.R:123 (modInputUI("dataInput")). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/spike_8e4.R, /tmp/mutation_8e4.R, /tmp/regression_8e4.R. Verification: helper unit 63/0/0; e2e error+boundary 26 blocks / 29 expectations GREEN, 0 failed / 0 error / 0 skip. RED proven first (3 ns e2e blocks + 2 helper tests failed with the old input- ids). [mutation-check] PASS (wrong-pane / foreign-content ‚Üí FALSE; OLD whole-body grepl ‚Üí TRUE content-blind contrast; ns read-back + notification + wrong-selector). Non-e2e regression 2166 / 0 / 0 / 156 / 5 (= 2162 baseline + 4 new helper expectations). Phase-3E satisfied (live browser run + 2 spikes ARE the runtime, #31). Self-assessment: strong. Caught the broken/concurrently-modified tree firsthand at the FIRST spike (didn‚Äôt blame my own work, didn‚Äôt silently fix the owner‚Äôs files), surfaced + gated it cleanly, and verified the reformat inert before resuming rather than proceeding blind. Held the TDD gates (two AskUserQuestion transitions), the spike-first discipline (refined the plan‚Äôs ‚Äúthrows‚Äù ‚Üí ‚Äúwarns/read-back‚Äù and ‚Äúneeds data ‚Üí 0 deferrals‚Äù firsthand), the [mutation-check] rigor, and the 1-deliverable scope (did NOT bleed into 8e-5/8e-6). Commit hygiene held under pressure (explicit git add excluded 14 unauthored R/ files). Could improve: my first parse-check loop auto-printed successful parses (noisy) ‚Äî a one-line guard fix; cost one extra command. GOTCHAS for the next session (8e-5 or 8e-6; full plan in the ‚áí line above + plan ¬ß5/¬ß7): 1. ‚öÝ The working tree carries the owner‚Äôs 14-file R/ reformat (uncommitted, behaviorally inert). Phase 0 will see it. Decide with the owner whether to commit/discard BEFORE starting a code slice ‚Äî especially 8e-5, which edits the SAME R/ files the formatter just touched (modGeneticValue.R, modBreedingGroups.R). Reconcile first to avoid mixing. 2. Namespace discriminators (now firsthand-established): namespaced textInput ‚Üí get_value read-back (shinytest2 WARNS, never sets ‚Äî does NOT throw); actionButton ‚Üí app$click throw. Use the read-back for input assertions. 3. upload_and_wait is now correct (module_id="dataInput", derived upload id) ‚Üí 8e-6‚Äôs real upload flow is unblocked: upload_and_wait(app, <file>) ‚Üí #dataInput-getData ‚Üí wait_for_module_ready(app, "dataInput"). 4. Read the regression as expectation_success with NOT_CRAN=true ‚Äî baseline is now 2166 (NOT 2162; +4 from the new helper tests). Never sum(nb) (Learning #43e). 5. Commit ONLY your own files via explicit git add ([macos-dupe-scan]: .DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html + the owner‚Äôs R/ reformat must NOT ride along). 6. 8e-5 is PRODUCTION R/ ‚Üí full RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR + devtools::check() + owner go/no-go (plan ¬ß7, [production-in-disguise]). 8e-6 is RED‚ÜíGREEN (flows reach real outputs) + Option-C structural invariants.

Session 43 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 44)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most (load-bearing): S43‚Äôs ‚áí line named this cut precisely as ‚ÄúNOT mechanical‚Äù and front-loaded the navbarMenu dragon ‚Äî ‚ÄúVERIFY FIRSTHAND that a navbarMenu(‚ÄòMore‚Äô) child becomes the lone .tab-pane.active‚Ä¶ if it renders differently, assert_active_pane returns FALSE and you must adapt the helper/approach (bring it to the owner, don‚Äôt silently redesign).‚Äù That one instruction set the session‚Äôs shape: spike the live DOM FIRST (hard gate) before any conversion. It resolved TRUE, keeping the cut pure run-and-observe (docstring-only). - What helped (exact + applied directly): the docstring location helper-shinytest2.R:283-299 (precise); the [mutation-check] inversion idiom (I reused the spike‚Äôs wrong-pane/wrong-content arms verbatim); the Learning #43e measurement trap (‚Äúreport expectation_success, NEVER sum(nb)‚àísum(failed)‚Äù) ‚Äî I read the regression right the first time because of it; the browser env; ‚Äúread plan ¬ß5 FIRST‚Äù; the file/block counts (4+7=11, verified). - What was below handoff altitude (the one deduction): S43 framed workflow-integration as just ‚Äúconvert the visit-N loop to per-pane asserts.‚Äù That undersold its heterogeneity ‚Äî the file also has 2 is.list() responsiveness tautologies (W2/W3) and a navbar-BRAND check (W4) that lives OUTSIDE any pane (a carve-out, NOT active-pane). I had to census those firsthand and decide the brand‚Üí.navbar-brand scoping myself. Minor ‚Äî plan ¬ß3 classified the file ‚Äú5 hidden-DOM, 2 tautology‚Äù and S43 said ‚Äúcensus firsthand‚Äù, so the info was reachable. - What was wrong: nothing. The navbarMenu dragon, the docstring line range, the file counts, the env ‚Äî all verified correct firsthand. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the dragon flag converted a load-bearing unknown into a spike-first discipline, and the #43e warning prevented a sum(nb) measurement artifact (a fake +161 ‚Äújump‚Äù).

What Session 44 Did

Deliverable: the 8e-3 FINAL cut (docs/planning/phase8e-assertion-strengthening-subplan.md, issue #40, the LAST of three 8e-3 cuts ‚Üí 8e-3 COMPLETE) ‚Äî converted all 11 browser-booting blocks across test-e2e-settings-about.R (4) + test-e2e-workflow-integration.R (7) from the content-blind navigate_to_tab ‚Üí grepl(get_html_safe(app,"body")) idiom to behavioral assert_active_pane(...); finalized navigate_to_menu_item as a confirmed visible-pane check. Strict TDD, PURE run-and-observe ([refactor-only], green-on-arrival; gated PRE-RED‚Üírun-and-observe via AskUserQuestion; [mutation-check] = the rigor). Date: 2026-06-09/10. Branch: add-methodology (NOT on remote). Commit: one test:/docs: close-out (the 2 test files + helper docstring + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS #44 + these notes), staged via explicit git add (NOT .DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). The load-bearing move ‚Äî the navbarMenu dragon spike (R1 / ¬ß2.3 item 4): before touching any test, I rendered the live navbarPage DOM via two Rscript‚ÜíAppDriver spikes. Spike 1 confirmed set_inputs(mainNavbar="Settings") makes the Settings pane the lone active top-level .tab-pane (top-level .tab-content count==1; get_active_pane_value/innerText == the child content) ‚Äî idem About/Help; all 4 settings assertions + the wrong-pane/wrong-content mutation arms passed firsthand. Spike 2 verified every workflow pattern against the real active-pane innerText (ALL_OK). So navigate_to_menu_item‚Äôs delegate body was already correct ‚Üí docstring-only finalization, body unchanged ‚Üí the cut stayed pure run-and-observe (a [hard-gate-spike] in its CONFIRM mode, vs 8e-1‚Äôs falsify). Map (10 keep-regex-rescope ¬∑ 1 navbar-chrome carve-out): full per-block detail in the ACTIVE TASK block above + CHANGELOG (2026-06-09) + PROJECT_LEARNINGS #44. Headlines: settings-about 4 KEEP verbatim (navbarMenu child panes); W1 visit-N loop ‚Üí 6 per-pane asserts + threshold >=3‚Üí==6L; W2/W3 is.list() tautologies ‚Üí pane-switch asserts; W4 navbar brand ‚Üí .navbar-brand-scoped carve-out (strictly stronger than whole-body); W5/W6/W7 KEEP verbatim. Key files: tests/testthat/test-e2e-settings-about.R (4 blocks rewritten ‚Äî navbarMenu children Settings/About/Help); tests/testthat/test-e2e-workflow-integration.R (7 blocks rewritten); tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R:283-292 (navigate_to_menu_item docstring finalized ‚Äî BODY UNCHANGED, still delegates to navigate_to_tab; assert_active_pane :407-413, navigate_to_tab :253, get_html_safe :266, create_test_app self-skip :196 ‚Äî all UNCHANGED, just called); R/appUI.R:183-212 (navbarMenu("More") with static inline tabPanels Settings :187-192 / About :194-201 / Help :203-211; brand title :17 renders as <span class="navbar-brand">GeneKeepR</span>). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/spike_navbarmenu_8e3.R, /tmp/spike_workflow_patterns.R, /tmp/mutation_check_8e3_final.R. Verification: browser run 11/11 blocks GREEN / 12 expectations (net 0), 0 error / 0 skip (filter="^e2e-(settings|workflow)"). [mutation-check] PASS (settings arms via spike; W1 wrong-pane‚ÜíFALSE; W4 scoped grepl("Breeding", brand)‚ÜíFALSE while old whole-body‚ÜíTRUE). Non-e2e regression (NOT_CRAN=true) 2162 expectation_success / 0 failed / 0 error / 156 skipped / 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings / 0 non-e2e offenders ‚Äî S40‚ÄìS43 baseline held EXACTLY (read via expectation_success, not sum(nb)=2323, per Learning #43e). Phase 3E (runtime smoke): SATISFIED ‚Äî test-infra deliverable; the live browser run (11 blocks via real AppDriver) + the 2 DOM spikes + the live mutation-check spike ARE the runtime (#31 pattern). Drove the REAL app. Self-assessment: strong. Resolved the carried navbarMenu üêâ firsthand by rendering the live DOM (the strongest refutation for a DOM question ‚Äî it subsumed S43‚Äôs source-reasoning skeptic-panel idiom); pre-verified every pattern against the real active-pane innerText before the gate (spike v2 ALL_OK); chose the .navbar-brand scoping as a genuine strengthening (mutation-proved stronger than the whole-body tautology it replaced) rather than a lazy carve-out; held the protocol (orient‚Üíone deliverable‚Üíclose out), the TDD gate, and the 1-cut scope (did NOT bleed into 8e-4). Met/exceeded the S38‚ÄìS43 bar. Could improve: nothing material this session; the heterogeneity of the workflow file (is.list tautologies + brand) was the only thing I had to discover rather than inherit. GOTCHAS for the next session (8e-4 = namespace fix + error-states/boundary interaction revival; full plan in the ACTIVE TASK ‚áí line + plan ¬ß5/¬ß2.4): 1. NOT pure run-and-observe ‚Äî this cut is RED‚ÜíGREEN (namespace fix). The corrected-id interaction newly SUCCEEDS where the swallowed wrong-id one did nothing ‚Üí write the assertion that requires the real interaction first (RED on the wrong id), then fix the id (GREEN). 2. The 5 namespace fix sites (input-‚ÜídataInput-): helper-shinytest2.R:150 + :154, error-states:24 + :45, boundary:43. The input module is namespaced dataInput (appUI.R:123); data-module="input" (modInput.R:31) is a LABEL, not the namespace. 3. ¬ß2.4 DO-NOT-CHANGE list (verified-correct as-is): data-ready:27-32/43, home-navigation:95 (#goto_input app-level button). Don‚Äôt ‚Äúfix‚Äù a correct site. 4. Read R/modInput.R observeEvent(getData) validation (~301-419) FIRSTHAND ‚Äî these files probe INVALID input (non-numeric/zero parent age); confirm the validation surfaces a visible error/state to assert against. Some boundary blocks need data loaded ‚Üí defer those to 8e-6. 5. [mutation-check]: wrong id ‚Üí must FAIL (proves the interaction is real, not tryCatch-swallowed). 6. Read the regression as expectation_success with NOT_CRAN=true ‚Äî never sum(nb) (Learning #43e/#42d). 7. Commit ONLY the test/helper files + docs via explicit git add ([macos-dupe-scan]: .DS_Store/..Rcheck//PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html must NOT ride along).

Every line applied directly and held against the source. - What helped most (load-bearing): (1) the [mutation-check] inversion carry ‚Äî I reused ‚ÄúFocal Animals‚Äù (grep-confirmed Pedigree/Input-only, foreign to BG) and it discriminated on the first run. That‚Äôs true, but it did NOT flag that some STATIC features (the export downloadButtons) are present-but-hidden in an INACTIVE nested tab ‚Äî a DISTINCT NULL reason from data-dependency (‚Üí new Learning #43a). I discovered it firsthand from the module read S42 directed me to, so the gap was minor (the pointer to read the module surfaced it). - What was wrong: nothing. File counts, tab title, idiom, the Phase-5/6 surface ‚Äî all verified against source firsthand. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî one read put me on the right slice with the idiom, the mutation-check inversion, the pre-gate discipline, and the module-read instruction in hand.

(3) The helper-mechanism carry (‚Äúthe active-pane mechanism = the IMPLEMENTED helper :309-413, NOT the plan selector; later cuts just CALL it‚Äù) meant zero helper work ‚Äî I read it once to confirm the contract and moved on. - What was below handoff altitude (the one deduction): S41 did not flag that 8e-3 is ~3√ó a single 8e-2 session and should be split one family per session. S41 correctly noted it had NOT censused the 8e-3 files (correct division of labor); my census matched plan ¬ß5 exactly (7/7/8/7/7/9/4/7). - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî one read put me on the right slice with the idiom, the mutation-check inversion, the pre-gate-refute discipline, and the helper fact in hand.

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: S40‚Äôs ‚Äú‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = continue 8e-2 with the PYRAMID family ONLY‚Äù was decision-complete ‚Äî it named the 2 files (module/detailed = 12 per plan ¬ß5), the tab title ‚ÄúAge-Sex Pyramid‚Äù, the idiom, the canonical static anchors with exact lines (modPyramid.R:25-47 + the pyramidPlot.html guidance :55-58), the browser env, and don‚Äôt-bundle-8e-3 (FM #18/#25). Every line-ref held against the source (the title string is at appUI.R:139, within the :138-139 block S40 cited ‚Äî harmless). - What helped most (load-bearing): GOTCHA #4, the [mutation-check] INVERSION warning ‚Äî ‚ÄúPyramid is the TARGET pane next session, so ‚ÄòColor Scheme‚Äô/‚ÄòBin Size‚Äô are IN-pane; the wrong-content mutant must use a Pedigree/Input-only label (e.g.¬Ý‚ÄòFocal Animals‚Äô).‚Äù I used ‚ÄúFocal Animals‚Äù immediately, grep-confirmed it foreign, and the mutation-check discriminated on the first try ‚Äî no wasted spike. GOTCHA #5 (pre-gate refute the map) also paid off directly: it caught 2 over-NULLs before the gate. - What was below handoff altitude (NOT a defect, but the one deduction): GOTCHA #3 framed the pyramid family as needing the data-dependent‚ÜíNULL+defer rule (‚Äúthe rendered plot is data-dependent ‚Üí assert STATIC controls only; apply Learning #40‚Äôs data-dependent‚ÜíNULL+defer‚Äù). That correctly told me to assert static only ‚Äî but it subtly biased my FIRST map toward NULLing D3/D6, which the pre-gate refutation then had to correct. A sharper handoff might have noted that the pyramid pane‚Äôs static surface (controls + UNCONDITIONAL guidance) is rich enough that the only data-dependent thing ‚Äî the rendered plot/table ‚Äî is targeted by NONE of the 12 blocks, so 0 NULLs were actually needed. Minor: the handoff explicitly told me to census firsthand, which surfaced exactly this. - What was slightly imprecise: S40 said ‚Äúdid NOT census the pyramid files this session‚Äù and handed that to me ‚Äî correct division of labor; I censused firsthand (6+6=12, matching plan ¬ß5). - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî one read put me on the right slice with the idiom, the mutation-check inversion, and the helper fact in hand; the pre-gate refutation then did the rest.

The pyramid pane‚Äôs static surface is rich (sidebar controls modPyramid.R:25-47 + UNCONDITIONAL includeHTML(pyramidPlot.html) :55-58), so every block has a default-visible anchor. None of the 12 blocks targets the data-dependent rendered plot (plotOutput) or Statistics table (tableOutput), both req(pedigreeData())-gated (modPyramid.R:90-118) ‚Äî so nothing defers to 8e-6. The two dragons keep their keywords against always-rendered static text: detailed:25 male|female|sex ‚Üê guidance ‚Äú‚Ä¶males are plotted on the left and females on the right‚Äù + h3 ‚ÄúAge-Sex Pyramid Analysis‚Äù (NOT the data-dependent plot axis labels); detailed:44 max|maximum|age|limit ‚Üê always-visible ‚ÄúAge Unit:‚Äù/‚ÄúAge Label Size:‚Äù/‚ÄúAge-Sex‚Äù ‚Äî there is NO dedicated max-age control (the max=10/1500/2.0 are numeric widget bounds, not innerText), so the genuine regex is kept verbatim, not renamed. Pre-gate adversarial verification CORRECTED the map (Learning #41d): ran a 4-agent workflow (3 source-grounded skeptics defaulting-to-refuted + a cross-checking critic) over the 12-block map BEFORE the gate ‚Üí 2/12 refuted, both my proposed NULLs (D3 ‚Äúmaximum age setting‚Äù, D6 ‚Äúdata requirement message‚Äù). The skeptics were right: D3‚Äôs regex matches static ‚Äúage‚Äù (‚Üí KEEP, don‚Äôt NULL), and D6‚Äôs pane has always-rendered guidance (‚Üí anchor ‚ÄúAge Plot‚Äù, don‚Äôt NULL+defer). Verified the critic‚Äôs reasoning firsthand against modPyramid.R + the guidance HTML, adopted both corrections ‚Üí 0-NULL map. (Contrast S40‚Äôs 0/19-refuted confirmation ‚Äî this session the pre-gate EARNED its keep by correcting, not just confirming.) Patterns drawn from the innerText VISIBILITY-MAP (Learning #39) ‚Äî default-visible sidebar controls, the nested-tab nav labels, and the active ‚ÄúInput Format‚Äù tab‚Äôs includeHTML(input_format.html) guidance. Two prior tautologies (expect_true(TRUE)): input-detailed ‚Äúexample data option‚Äù ‚Üí NULL-pattern assert_active_pane(app,"Input") (no such feature exists ‚Äî honest pane-active check, not a forced match); input-tutorial ‚Äúgenotype file support‚Äù ‚Üí real "genotype" (backed by the File-Content radio + genotype-format docs). Static-pattern sources (read firsthand): R/modInput.R ‚Äî h3 ‚ÄúData Input and Quality Control‚Äù (:42), radio labels ‚ÄúFile Type‚Äù/‚ÄúFile Content‚Äù + the visible pedFile fileInput ‚ÄúSelect Pedigree File‚Äù (:55-93), ‚ÄúMinimum Parent Age‚Äù (:119), ‚ÄúRead and Check Pedigree‚Äù (:130), the nested tabsetPanel labels ‚ÄúInput Format‚Äù/‚ÄúQC Summary‚Äù/‚ÄúErrors‚Äù/‚ÄúWarnings‚Äù/‚ÄúCleaned Data‚Äù (:142-195); inst/extdata/ui_guidance/input_format.html ‚Äî ‚Äúcomma-delimited‚Äù/‚Äútab-delimited‚Äù/‚ÄúExcel‚Äù/‚Äúgenotype‚Äù (the active nested-tab content); R/appUI.R:120-124 (‚ÄúInput‚Äù tab title). Key files: tests/testthat/test-e2e-input-module.R, -input-detailed.R (the NULL-pattern honest conversion is at its line ~76), -input-tutorial.R (all rewritten); tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R:309-413 (active-pane helpers ‚Äî UNCHANGED, just called). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/mutation_check_8e2_input.R. Verification: baseline 19/19 GREEN ‚Üí post-conversion 19/19 blocks GREEN / 19 expectations (1:1 swap, net 0), 0 error/0 skip (filter="^e2e-input", env NPRC_RUN_E2E=true NOT_CRAN=true RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=false). [mutation-check] PASS (/tmp/mutation_check_8e2_input.R): correct‚ÜíTRUE; wrong-pane (Age-Sex Pyramid)‚ÜíFALSE; wrong-content (Input,"Color Scheme")‚ÜíFALSE; old whole-body grepl("Color Scheme",body)‚ÜíTRUE (content-blind contrast); active-pane innerText grepl‚ÜíFALSE (sanity). Non-e2e regression 2122 passed / 0 failed / 0 error (159 e2e-skipped, 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings ‚Äî unchanged S38 baseline; 0 non-e2e offenders). Phase 3E (runtime smoke): SATISFIED ‚Äî test-infra deliverable; the live browser run (19 blocks via real AppDriver) + the live mutation-check spike ARE the runtime (#31 pattern). Drove the REAL app, not just build-clean. GOTCHAS for the next session (continue 8e-2 ‚Äî PEDIGREE family ONLY; see the ACTIVE TASK ‚áí line for the full plan): 1. Use the IMPLEMENTED helper (helper-shinytest2.R:309-413), NOT the plan ¬ß2.3/¬ß4 selector (superseded). Just CALL assert_active_pane; no helper work. 2. innerText VISIBILITY-MAP (Learning #39): only DEFAULT-VISIBLE content is assertable ‚Äî conditionalPanels with a false condition + non-active nested tabs are display:none and contribute nothing. Read modPedigree.R for the DEFAULT input state BEFORE picking a pattern; the browser run is the [verify-first] (a hidden-subtree pattern ‚Üí assert_active_pane=FALSE ‚Üí caught). 3. Honest-tautology rule (Learning #39): a tautology naming a NONEXISTENT feature ‚Üí NULL-pattern assert_active_pane(app,"<Tab>") + flag; a real feature ‚Üí assert it. pedigree-tutorial is 7/8 tautology+dead ‚Üí expect several honest conversions. 4. Content-coupled REAL asserts (plan ¬ß2.2): pedigree-detailed:57 and pedigree-tutorial:174 keep their keywords ‚Äî just rescope to the active pane (don‚Äôt NULL them). 5. [mutation-check] a sample every session: wrong-pane‚ÜíFALSE; the wrong-CONTENT mutant must use text in NO sibling control of the target pane (innerText carries every static label ‚Äî #38b); SOURCE it by reading a DIFFERENT pane‚Äôs module UI, don‚Äôt guess. 6. Browser run is opt-in + slow (each test_that boots its own Chrome). 19 pedigree blocks is comfortable; do NOT add pyramid (FM #18/#25). Pyramid family (module/detailed = 12) is a separate session. 7. Branch add-methodology still NOT on remote. Two S34 live-run CI watch items still pending the first master run (Phase-8 CI baked-in note below). Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Oriented fully before touching anything; posed the pre-RED gate via AskUserQuestion with the COMPLETE per-block pattern mapping in hand, so the owner could review/adjust before any edit. (+) Sourced every pattern firsthand and built the innerText visibility-map ‚Äî read the DEFAULT input state, recognized the Separator panel is display:none (fileType defaults Excel), and avoided ‚ÄúComma‚Äù/‚ÄúTab‚Äù in favor of the guidance-HTML ‚Äúcomma-delimited‚Äù/‚Äútab-delimited‚Äù; the 19/19 browser run confirmed the map on the first try (zero false picks). (+) Caught the honesty issue (detailed ‚Äúexample data‚Äù names a feature that doesn‚Äôt exist) and converted it to a NULL-pattern pane-active check + flag, rather than force-matching incidental ‚Äúexamples‚Äù doc text ‚Üí distilled into Learning #39‚Äôs honest-tautology rule. (+) [mutation-check] discriminating at both arms; applied #38b from the start (Pyramid-only ‚ÄúColor Scheme‚Äù), avoiding the in-pane trap S38 hit. (+) Held the S38 baseline exactly; committed only intended files. (‚àí) Completed 1 of the 3 remaining 8e-2 families (Input) ‚Äî but that IS the correct non-bundling boundary (plan R3 / FM #18/#25); pedigree + pyramid are separate sessions. (‚àí) The pre-RED gate message was long. New: Learning #39.

Session 37 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 38)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: S37‚Äôs ‚Äú‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = implement slice 8e-2 ONLY‚Äù was decision-complete ‚Äî it named the conversion idiom (navigate_to_tab ‚Üí assert_active_pane), said ‚Äúno helper work ‚Äî helpers EXIST + spike-confirmed,‚Äù flagged the navbar-label carve-out, pointed at plan ¬ß5/¬ß4 to read FIRST, and warned don‚Äôt-bundle (FM #18/#25). The CRITICAL HANDOFF FACT (plan ¬ß2.3/¬ß4 selector SUPERSEDED ‚Üí trust the helper, not the plan text) was load-bearing: I never even tried the plan‚Äôs broken selector, and the helper‚Äôs own inline comments (helper-shinytest2.R:321-331) reinforced it. Every count/pointer I re-derived held EXACTLY. - What helped (the GOTCHAS list in ‚ÄúWhat S37 Did‚Äù): items 1-7 were each actionable. Item 5‚Äôs ‚Äúsplit input|pedigree|pyramid into separate sessions if oversized‚Äù directly seeded my scope decision ‚Äî without it I might have bundled all 64 blocks. - What was missing (below handoff altitude, NOT a defect): the handoff framed 8e-2 as ‚Äúinput/pedigree/pyramid + home-navigation‚Äù but didn‚Äôt pre-state the magnitude (11 files / 64 browser-booting blocks) ‚Äî I had to census it to discover the split was needed. Correct: sizing a picked-up slice is the executing session‚Äôs job, and ‚Äúsplit if oversized‚Äù covered it. Minor: the carve-out was cited as ‚Äúhome-navigation:155-156‚Äù but the real carve-out is two WHOLE tests (‚ÄúNavbar has all main tabs‚Äù 143-173 + ‚ÄúMore menu exists‚Äù 175-189) ‚Äî I generalized correctly. - What was wrong: nothing material. (The handoff didn‚Äôt claim pedigree/pyramid were defect-free; I verified firsthand they pass the FULL titles as the ignored fallback arg ‚Äî no wrong-tab defect.) - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî one read put me on the right slice with the idiom, the carve-out, and the split-guidance in hand.

Session 36 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 37)

Score: 8/10. - What helped most: the ACTIVE TASK‚Äôs ‚Äú‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = implement slice 8e-1 ONLY‚Äù was decision-complete ‚Äî it named the exact files, framed the browser spike as a HARD GATE with an explicit STOP-if-it-fails instruction, pointed at plan ¬ß4/¬ß5(8e-1)/¬ß2.3 to read FIRST, and warned don‚Äôt-bundle (FM #18/#25). The plan doc was an executable spec: ¬ß4‚Äôs helper contracts compiled almost verbatim, and every firsthand-verified count held EXACTLY (7 wrong-tab at lines 40/61/82/103/124/146/168, 8 test_that, the static-UI labels). - What helped (reflexes via CLAUDE.md): [verify-first] (re-ran the inventory greps + confirmed every static label in the INSTALLED package before asserting), the #31 fake-AppDriver-stub idiom, [author-decision] (the spike-failure scope fork), [discriminating-RED]/[mutation-check], [phase-3E-smoke] (suite+app = runtime). - What was MISSING ‚Äî the load-bearing one: the plan‚Äôs ¬ß2.3/¬ß4 active-pane mechanism was WRONG (.tab-content > .tab-pane.active is non-unique ‚Äî 5 nested .tab-content; innerText-visibility assumption mis-stated). The handoff/plan could NOT have known ‚Äî ¬ß2.3 was explicitly flagged ‚Äúbrowser-confirm pending‚Äù / R1, and the non-uniqueness is invisible on the static DOM the planner read. That is the spike‚Äôs job, and the gate WORKED (it stopped me before the conversion). Not a handoff defect ‚Äî but the R1 dragon under-weighted that the corrected mechanism would be findable same-session. - What was wrong: nothing material ‚Äî every count/pointer held; the one falsified thing (the selector) was the explicitly-unconfirmed item. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî one read put me on the right slice with the spike correctly framed as the gate.

Converted test-e2e-summary-statistics-module.R ‚Äî fixed the 7 wrong-tab navs (‚Üí‚ÄúSummary Statistics‚Äù), dropped the false ‚Äúembedded in another tab‚Äù GVA fallback, replaced all 8 tautologies/hidden-DOM asserts with assert_active_pane() on STATIC UI (export labels / h3 / guidance-HTML founder-equivalent terms). Browser run 8/8 GREEN; mutation-check PASS (wrong-tab‚ÜíFALSE, correct-tab‚ÜíTRUE). Key files: tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R:310-409 (the helpers + .active_pane_js builder ‚Äî THE deliverable, spike-corrected); tests/testthat/test_helper_shinytest2.R:65-110,206-330 (stubs + 11 unit tests); tests/testthat/test-e2e-summary-statistics-module.R (rewritten, 8 behavioral tests); R/modSummaryStats.R:22-223 (static-UI source for the patterns); R/appUI.R:16-19,156-159 (navbarPage BS4 + the SS tabPanel). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/spike_active_pane.R (17/17 confirmation), /tmp/spike_dom_diag.R (DOM diagnostic), /tmp/mutation_check.R. Phase 3E (runtime smoke): SATISFIED ‚Äî the deliverable is test-infra (the suite + the live AppDriver spike ARE the runtime, the #31 pattern); I drove the REAL app (17/17 spike + 8/8 e2e browser run), not just build-clean. GOTCHAS for the next session (implement 8e-2 ONLY): 1. The plan‚Äôs ¬ß2.3/¬ß4 active-pane selector is SUPERSEDED ‚Äî use the IMPLEMENTED helper, not the plan‚Äôs selector text. The helper is spike-confirmed; 8e-2/8e-3/8e-6 just CALL assert_active_pane(app, "<Tab>", "<pattern>"). No helper work. 2. Conversion idiom: success <- navigate_to_tab(app, "<Tab>"); if (!success) skip(...); expect_true(assert_active_pane(app, "<Tab>", "<static regex>"), info=...). Always mutation-check a sample (point one at the wrong tab ‚Üí must FAIL). 3. Assert STATIC UI only (labels/headings/guidance that render WITHOUT data); data-bearing tables/plots are 8e-6. Confirm each pattern is static by reading the module UI first. 4. home-navigation:155-156 carve-out: those assert navbar <ul> labels NOT inside any pane ‚Üí assert_active_pane would correctly FAIL them ‚Üí leave as whole-DOM grepl. Convert only pane-CONTENT asserts; convert the 3 #goto_* clicks to assert the active pane switched (highest-value). 5. Browser run is opt-in: NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=false Rscript -e '...test_dir(filter="^e2e-(input|pedigree|pyramid|home)"...)'. NOT_CRAN is mandatory (else ‚ÄúOn CRAN‚Äù abort). Each test_that boots its own AppDriver ‚Äî 8e-2 is ~11 files; split input|pedigree|pyramid into separate sessions if oversized (plan ¬ß5 8e-2 dragon), don‚Äôt bundle past one comfortable session. 6. 8e-5 (determinism) still touches PRODUCTION R/ ‚Äî its own owner-gated full RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR+check() session. 7. Branch add-methodology still not on remote. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Honored the HARD GATE ‚Äî STOPPED on spike failure instead of barrelling into the conversion, diagnosed the real DOM (read-only), and brought the mechanism deviation to the owner as a scope fork rather than silently redesigning (SAFEGUARDS mode-switch / FM #8). (+) The corrected mechanism is fully spike-proven (17/17 through the real helpers, incl.¬Ýthe navbarMenu child the naive selector got wrong) BEFORE any conversion rode on it. (+) Browser-free unit tests designed to survive a mechanism rewrite (stubs key on JS invariants) ‚Üí stayed 59/0 across the selector change. (+) Discriminating proof at every layer: unit (liar-stub guard), spike (wrong-tab=FALSE), mutation-check (test-file level). (+) Every TDD transition gated; static-only assertions with data-bearing deferred to 8e-6 as planned. (‚àí) The browser-free unit tests can‚Äôt pin the SELECTOR correctness (only the contract) ‚Äî selector truth rests on the spike, which is inherent but means a future selector regression wouldn‚Äôt trip the unit layer (acceptable; noted). (‚àí) Spent two browser spikes (falsify, then diagnose) before the fix ‚Äî unavoidable given the plan‚Äôs selector was wrong, but a sharper first spike could have counted .tab-content up front. New: Learning #37 + glossary [hard-gate-spike].

What Session 36 Did

Deliverable: Plan sub-phase 8e (GitHub issue #40) ‚Üí docs/planning/phase8e-assertion-strengthening-subplan.md ‚Äî decompose #40‚Äôs 5 work items + the CI-stability comment into 7 risk-ordered vertical TDD slices, with a MANDATORY grep-based evidence inventory and the active-pane-assertion browser spike identified. COMPLETE. (The plan is the deliverable; implementation is slices 8e-1‚Ä¶8e-7 in later sessions.) Date: 2026-06-07. Branch: add-methodology (NOT on remote). Commit: this docs: close-out (the plan + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md Learning #36 + glossary [production-in-disguise] + these notes). Nature: Planning/architecture session ‚Äî TDD code-phases INAPPLICABLE (no test/app code changed; mirrors S30). One owner AskUserQuestion (plan vs implement-a-slice vs CI-stability ‚Üí owner chose PLAN). One deliverable; did NOT start implementation (FM #18). Evidence base (firsthand + verified): (1) firsthand greps ‚Äî 41 literal expect_true(TRUE), ‚âà18 expect_true(nchar(html)>100) near-tautologies, 41 dead-grepl blocks, 7 wrong-tab (summary-statistics-module tests 2‚Äì8: lines 40/61/82/103/124/146/168 ‚Üí ‚ÄúGenetic Value Analysis‚Äù), 159 total test_that. (2) A 23-agent census workflow wf_4ebcdb7f-f4b (per-file census + 3 deep-dives: active-pane spike / namespace / determinism + an adversarial completeness critic). (3) An adversarial plan-review agent over the written doc ‚Üí caught a ¬ß3 block-count error (table summed 163, prose said 159; 4 rows off-by-one from inflated census n=), a home-navigation navbar-label trap (:155-156 assert <ul> labels not in any pane ‚Üí assert_active_pane would correctly fail them), and a missing get_js/wait_for_js contract note ‚Äî all fixed. The census‚Äôs ‚Äú49 tautologies‚Äù was a conflation (41 literal + 8 of the nchar class); the plan cites the faithful 41 + ‚âà18. Key findings baked into the plan: - Active-pane mechanism (the load-bearing crux): get_html/body serialize the whole hidden navbarPage DOM (BS4; every pane in the boot DOM) ‚Üí grepl(kw, body) is content-blind. Only app$get_js(".tab-content > .tab-pane.active ‚Ä¶ innerText") honors visibility; gate on data-value==title. New helpers get_active_pane_text/get_active_pane_value + wait_for_active_pane + assert_active_pane (plan ¬ß4). Confirmed shinytest2 0.5.0 has get_js/wait_for_js; jsonlite NOT a dep ‚Üí use encodeString(x, quote="'"). The BS4 runtime .active toggling + innerText-visibility need a live-Chrome confirm in 8e-1 (HARD GATE). - input‚ÜídataInput namespace fix (helper:150/154, error-states:24/45, boundary:43) + a DO-NOT-CHANGE list (data-ready:27-32/43; home-nav:95). - [production-in-disguise]: #40 item 5 (determinism) is a PRODUCTION change ‚Äî both modules have ZERO set.seed; 8e-5 ADDS a gated set_seed() (existing exported wrapper) to modGeneticValue.R:177/modBreedingGroups.R:272, owner-gated, full TDD ‚Äî distinct from the test-only conversion slices. Breaks the prior ‚Äú8a‚Äì8d test-only‚Äù boundary (flagged in plan ¬ß11). Key files: docs/planning/phase8e-assertion-strengthening-subplan.md (THE deliverable ‚Äî ¬ß2 inventory, ¬ß3 per-file‚Üíslice table, ¬ß4 helper contracts, ¬ß5 the 7 slices, ¬ß6 TDD-classification table, ¬ß7 determinism, ¬ß8 CI, ¬ß9 risks); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning #36 + glossary [production-in-disguise]); evidence artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/w8yc4nofm.output (NOT committed). Phase 3E (runtime smoke): N/A ‚Äî a planning doc changes no runtime behavior (the plan is the deliverable). Stated, not skipped. GOTCHAS for the next session (implement 8e-1 ONLY): 1. Read the plan ¬ß4/¬ß5(8e-1)/¬ß2.3 FIRST. 8e-1 = 4 active-pane helpers (browser-free RED‚ÜíGREEN) ‚Üí CONFIRM the browser spike as a HARD GATE ‚Üí fix summary-statistics wrong-tab + convert it. If the spike fails, close out with just the helper+spike finding (a valid deliverable) and re-plan the mechanism. 2. Do NOT bundle slices (FM #18/#25). 8e-2/8e-3/8e-4 each need only 8e-1; 8e-6 needs 8e-4; 8e-5 is owner-gated PRODUCTION; 8e-7 is orthogonal/live-runner-only. 3. 8e-5 touches R/ production code ‚Äî its own owner-gated full RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR+check() session; default path must stay unchanged. 4. The two S34 live-run CI watch items still stand (renv lib-path + the 23-in-one-process flake) ‚Äî 8e-7 addresses the flake but can only be validated once the branch is on master. 5. Branch add-methodology still not on remote. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Right-sized the scope decision to the owner (plan vs implement) before any work ‚Äî #40 was genuinely multi-session (6 items, a production change, a browser spike). (+) MANDATORY grep-inventory done firsthand AND cross-checked by a 23-agent census + adversarial critic; then [verify-first] applied to the workflow‚Äôs OWN headline counts (caught 49-vs-41 + 163-vs-159), reconciled against firsthand greps + the parent plan‚Äôs counts. (+) Ran a SECOND adversarial review over the SYNTHESIZED PLAN (not just the evidence) ‚Üí caught + fixed the propagated ¬ß3 count error, the navbar-label trap, the missing get_js note. (+) Surfaced [production-in-disguise] as a new glossary reflex + isolated determinism as an owner-gated slice. (+) Slices are vertical, risk-ordered (load-bearing spike first), each with DONE/verify/TDD-classification. (‚àí) The census agents inflated 4 per-file nTestBlocks by 1 and I propagated them into ¬ß3 before the plan-review caught it ‚Äî a stronger move would have been to derive ¬ß3‚Äôs block counts from a firsthand grep -c test_that from the start, not the census n=. (‚àí) The active-pane mechanism, though firsthand-grounded on the static DOM + method surface, still rides on a live-browser confirmation deferred to 8e-1 ‚Äî unavoidable for a planning session, but the plan‚Äôs central helper is not yet proven.

Session 34 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 35)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: the ACTIVE TASK‚Äôs ‚Äú‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = parent Phase 9 only‚Äù was decision-complete on the HARD parts ‚Äî it flagged Phase 9 as üêâ IRREVERSIBLE + its-own-session + ‚Äúconfirm with the owner before deleting anything‚Äù + the MANDATORY grep-evidence-inventory + ‚Äúmay warrant a planning session first,‚Äù and pointed at docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md ¬ß9/¬ß10 (which carried the actual file/symbol inventory, the alias design, and the ¬ß16 open decisions). That turned my pre-flight into a RE-verification (sweep + firsthand) rather than a from-scratch discovery. The Phase-8 CI baked-in note (renv + flake watch items) correctly stayed relevant. - What helped (reflexes via CLAUDE.md): [verify-first] (re-ran the ¬ß10 inventory + firsthand-checked load-bearing claims before any delete), [completeness-workflow] (the sweep‚Äôs completeness-critic + my firsthand re-checks), [author-decision] (4 owner gates), [discriminating-RED], [regression-read], [phase-3E-smoke], [right-sized-orchestration] (a read-only sweep for the irreversible delete; solo edits for the rest). - What was missing (NOT a defect ‚Äî below handoff altitude): the shorthand ‚ÄúDELETE the monolith inst/application/server.R+ui.R‚Äù understated the blast radius ‚Äî the monolith is 17 tracked files (server.r, ui.r, global.R, 8 uitp*.R, example_1.R, the dead modPyramid.R, www/√ó4); the accurate list was in plan ¬ß10, which the handoff pointed to. Neither the handoff, plan ¬ß10, NOR my own pre-flight sweep predicted the @import shiny NAMESPACE fallout of deleting getMinParentAge.R (the regression run caught it ‚Äî Learning #35); a reference-grep inventory structurally can‚Äôt (it reasons about callers, not roxygen output). - What was wrong: nothing material ‚Äî the ¬ß10 inventory held (0 new refs since S21), inst/www ‚âÝ inst/application/www, lifecycle was a dep, all 17 files tracked ‚Äî exactly as documented. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the handoff + plan ¬ß10 made an irreversible deletion safe and fast.

What Session 35 Did

Commits: 3db018d1 (refactor!: alias+orphans) ¬∑ 24992e0b (feat!: delete monolith, standalone ¬ß15) ¬∑ 53a9e5e0 (docs) ¬∑ a1618c48 (fix: a2interactive vignette) + this docs: close-out. Nature: strict TDD (RED‚ÜíGREEN gated; the pre-RED‚ÜíRED + RED‚ÜíGREEN AskUserQuestion gates) + 4 owner scope gates (go/no-go on the irreversible delete ¬∑ shouldShowErrorTab=delete ¬∑ modMinimalTest=delete ¬∑ one-session-multi-commit) + 1 owner gate on the pre-existing-vignette fix. Firsthand-verified: system.file("application") SOLE ref (R/runGenekeepr.R:19); inst/www/ (modular data-ready.js) is a DIFFERENT dir from inst/application/www/ (must survive); lifecycle a dep; all 17 monolith files tracked ‚Üí revertible. (3) Deleted orphans getMinParentAge/getLogo/shouldShowErrorTab/modMinimalTest + their tests; stripped the dead qcResults build (appServer.R:172-177) + the 2 @seealso \link{shouldShowErrorTab} refs (appServer.R, shouldShowChangedColsTab.R); removed the 7 shouldShowErrorTab blocks from test_appServer_dynamicTabs.R. (4) document() ‚Üí NAMESPACE drops 4 exports + 4 man pages; updates runGeneKeepR/appServer/shouldShowChangedColsTab .Rd. (5) Docs: _pkgdown.yml, inst/WORDLIST, CLAUDE.md, ROADMAP.md, NEWS.Rmd/NEWS.md (re-knit), vignette _running_shiny_application.Rmd‚ÜírunModularApp(), README.md (re-knit). ‚öÝ NAMESPACE fallout (‚Üí Learning #35): getMinParentAge.R was the SOLE carrier of @import shiny; deleting it dropped import(shiny) ‚Üí modular UI errored could not find function "h5" (caught by the regression run, NOT the inventory). After GREEN: regression 2135 / 0 / 0 (5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings); runtime smoke runGeneKeepR() ‚Üí modular HTTP 200; devtools::check() 0 errors / 0 warnings, creating vignettes ... OK (pre-existing NOTEs: non-standard top-level dev files; stale spelling baseline); grep no system.file("application"). Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Caught + fixed the @import shiny NAMESPACE fallout via the regression run and distilled it into Learning #35 + a new [deletion-namespace-fallout] glossary reflex ‚Äî a side-effect a reference-grep can‚Äôt see. (+) Ran the FULL devtools::check() (not just the regression-read); it earned its keep by surfacing a pre-existing vignette defect, fixed as a SEPARATE fix: commit (no scope-bundling). (+) 3 clean Phase-9 commits + the deletion isolated for single-revert (¬ß15); explicit git add (no .DS_Store). (‚àí) My pre-flight sweep, despite a completeness critic, did NOT predict the @import shiny fallout ‚Äî the regression run did; a stronger pre-flight would grep each to-delete file‚Äôs roxygen for sole-carrier @import/@importFrom. (‚àí) Left a3manual/a2interactive rendered outputs stale-by-design (functional but source-vs-output divergent) rather than a full release re-knit ‚Äî a deliberate noise-avoidance call, noted.

Session 33 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 34)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: the ACTIVE TASK‚Äôs ‚Äú‚áí SUGGESTED NEXT = implement PHASE 8d only‚Äù was decision-complete ‚Äî it named all 5 files with their extra deps + test counts (home-navigation/click_element_safe on #goto_*; settings-about/navigate_to_menu_item; workflow-integration/get_values_safe; error-states+boundary-conditions/E2E_TIMEOUT+input-), flagged the navbarMenu spike ¬ß8.2 as the crux, gave the exact verify command (BOTH env vars), the classification hint (‚Äúclassify TDD AFTER the spike ‚Äî don‚Äôt force a synthetic RED‚Äù), four accurate ‚ÄúWatch‚Äù items (top-level E2E_TIMEOUT, the #goto_* observers appServer.R:73-95, boundary‚Äôs named height/width, the input- tryCatch no-ops), and the DONE criteria (green-or-clean-skip ‚Üí close #39 + file 8e ‚Üí broaden CI to full ^(app|e2e)-). I went orientation ‚Üí spike ‚Üí classification gate ‚Üí CI broaden ‚Üí validate with near-zero rediscovery; the adversarial review independently re-confirmed all four Watch items. - What helped (reflexes via CLAUDE.md): [verify-first] (the spike settled ¬ß8.2 BEFORE I classified ‚Äî and proved the helper needs NO change), [author-decision] (the config-vs-RED‚ÜíGREEN gate + the CI/flake scope forks), [regression-read] (both env vars; summed failed+error+skip), [completeness-workflow] (the ultracode review CAUGHT the flake a single run masked), [phase-3E-smoke] (validated the exact run-step end-to-end). - What was missing / what I‚Äôd add (NOT a defect ‚Äî within-session discovery): the handoff flagged the ¬ß5(8c) AppDriver-process-count dragon for 8c but did NOT predict that broadening to 23-in-one-process would actually SURFACE it as a low-rate Chrome flake under stop_on_failure=TRUE. The handoff‚Äôs ‚Äúbroaden to full tier + run once‚Äù implicitly assumed the broaden was safe ‚Äî and ‚Äúrun once‚Äù is exactly what hides an intermittent flake. My single 193/0/0/0 masked it; the 4-lens review (run #1 = 1 error) caught it. - What was wrong: nothing ‚Äî the navbarMenu read-back DID work (settings-about green), all Watch items held, the verify command was exact. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî precise scope + spike-first discipline meant the only real ‚Äúwork‚Äù was the spike + the review-surfaced flake decision; everything else was run-and-observe.

The ¬ß8.2 spike + the 53/53 green proved the provisional navigate_to_menu_item is already correct ‚Üí no R unit to write test-first; the only code touch is a comment-only docstring + the CI YAML filter. One deliverable; did NOT start Phase 9 (FM #18/#2). The ¬ß8.2 navbarMenu spike (RESOLVED ‚Äî verify-first, before classifying): set_inputs(mainNavbar="Settings"/"About"/"Help") ‚Üí get_value(input="mainNavbar") reads back the child label TRUE for all 3 ‚Üí navigate_to_menu_item‚Äôs delegate-to-navigate_to_tab body is FINAL (no DOM dropdown-open+click needed). click("#goto_input") navigates for real (mainNavbar==‚ÄúInput‚Äù, active pane switches). Honesty nuance (‚Üí 8e/#40): after set_inputs on a navbarMenu child the input value reaches the child but the VISIBLE pane does not truly switch (navbar ‚ÄúMore‚Äù highlights; grepl(body) passes via ¬ß2.3 hidden-DOM) ‚Äî the ¬ß8.3 navigation-false-positive limit (do NOT overstate the spike as ‚Äúnavigation works‚Äù). The 5 8d files ‚Äî GREEN opt-in: NPRC_RUN_E2E=true NOT_CRAN=true ‚Üí home-navigation + settings-about + workflow-integration + error-states + boundary-conditions = 47 test_that blocks / 53 expectations, 0 fail/0 error/0 skip (block-vs-expectation gap: ‚ÄúNavbar has all main tabs‚Äù has 6 expect_true, ‚ÄúApp maintains state‚Äù has 2). All four S33 Watch items confirmed benign firsthand (E2E_TIMEOUT defined + used only inside test blocks; the 6 #goto_* observers wired; boundary‚Äôs named height/width handled by create_app_driver(...,height=,width=); the input- selectors stay tryCatch-swallowed no-ops ‚Äî 8e). The CI broaden (.github/workflows/shinytest2.yaml): run-step filter 18-file include-list ‚Üí ^(app|e2e)- (verified the regex selects EXACTLY the 23 test-{app,e2e}-* files ‚Äî replicating testthat‚Äôs stripped-name match in R ‚Äî and excludes the appServer near-miss via the trailing -). Step name + comments + cat label updated; job env, stop_on_failure=TRUE, the sum(passed)==0 guard UNCHANGED. Full tier re-validated in ONE process: 193 passed / 0 fail/0 error/0 skip, 23 files. ‚öÝ FLAKE found + owner decision: an ultracode 4-lens adversarial review (wf_ef031b1d-edc, 4 lenses: diff-minimality ‚Äñ #39-close ‚Äñ #40-scope ‚Äñ completeness) caught that the 23-in-one-process run is intermittently flaky ‚Äî review run #1 = 191/0/0/1 error (workflow-integration.R ‚ÄúApp maintains state when switching tabs‚Äù), run #2 clean, isolated 8/8/8 ‚Äî the ¬ß5(8c)/R2 AppDriver-process-count dragon; under stop_on_failure=TRUE it can red the scheduled job. Reproduced firsthand (my 2 fresh dedicated runs BOTH clean ‚Üí low-rate ~1/5 + contention-sensitive; the review‚Äôs 4 concurrent agents likely pressured Chrome). Owner decision (AskUserQuestion): close #39 now + document the flake [over harden-CI-now / keep-narrower] ‚Äî ship the broaden as-is (harness executable + CI wired = scope ¬ß1.1 met), record the flake as a live-run watch item, route CI-stability hardening (per-¬ß5(8c) ‚Äúrun grouped‚Äù in fresh processes ‚Äî can only be validated on the live runner) to #40. Close-out GitHub actions: #39 CLOSED (gh issue close 39 --reason completed + a validation/watch-item comment). 8e filed as #40 (‚ÄúStrengthen shinytest2 E2E assertions‚Ä¶‚Äù, label enhancement) ‚Äî captures the ¬ß2.4/¬ß2.5/¬ß6 deferred items + today‚Äôs navbarMenu false-positive; a CI-stability comment added to #40 for the flake hardening. Verification: ¬ß8.2 spike (read-backs TRUE); 5-file 53/53 green; full-tier 193/0/0/0 single-process; non-e2e regression (NOT_CRAN=true, NPRC_RUN_E2E unset ‚Üí e2e clean-skip) = 0 failed / 0 error, 0 non-e2e offenders, 2159 passed, 156 e2e-skipped, 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings (unchanged S31/S32/S33 baseline). The review‚Äôs Lens-1/3 confirmed the diff is comment-only + minimal and #40 is complete; Lens-4 caught a .DS_Store BLOCKER ‚Üí committed with explicit git add of only the 2 files (NOT -A). document() N/A (no roxygen), tests/+.github lint-exempt, no * 2.* source dupes (only .Rproj.user/, gitignored). Phase 3E: the suite IS the runtime for a test-infra/CI change ‚Äî validated locally end-to-end; the live GitHub run is DEFERRED (branch not on remote), now with TWO watch items (renv lib-path + the flake).
