Session 21 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 22)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: S21‚Äôs ACTIVE-TASK + the plan ¬ß9 Phase 1 scoped the session precisely ‚Äî the four sub-items, the z-score column-name TRACE gotcha (#2), the kinship-download ¬ß16.8 fork, the dropped MK/GU table gap, the founder-table-placement ¬ß16.6 fork, the ‚Äúaudit is stale‚Äù warning (¬ß11), the NOT_CRAN+load_all regression reflex (#6), and the 1a/1b split option. The plan was a near-perfect work-list: S21‚Äôs completeness-critic had caught exactly the 4 gaps that became Phase 1 (dead kinship button, dropped MK/GU tables, founder-placement, req(NULL)) ‚Äî the planning grep-inventory paid off directly as the executor‚Äôs task list. - What helped (reflexes transferred): the z-score trace gotcha was exactly right ‚Äî reportGV emits zScores (plural), arrives unrenamed at modSummaryStats, which checked zScore (singular). The Learning #15/#20 ‚Äúa pre-existing test passes on the bug‚Äù pattern was live (test_modSummaryStats_ggplots.R passes because makeTestGVData() injects BOTH names) ‚Üí drove the discriminating RED (ONLY the plural name). The verify-first reflex (Learnings #5/#8a/#9a) extended to (a) empirically confirming the emitted column name and (b) probing the testServer renderUI/downloadHandler surfacing before asserting. - What was missing (minor, implementation-detail): the plan didn‚Äôt spell out the testServer surfacing mechanism (output$<renderUI>‚Üí$html; output$<downloadHandler>‚Üíwritten-file-path) ‚Äî I probed it. Nor that matching the file‚Äôs reactive({x()}) return idiom adds brace_linter debt ‚Äî found via the touched-files stash-diff. Both are below the altitude a plan should carry. - What was wrong: trivial line drifts only ‚Äî the plan cited modSummaryStats.R:595 for the kinship req; the actual was L596. The zScore checks cited L396/477 ‚Äî exact. Negligible. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the precise four-item scope + the z-score trace + the two pre-identified author forks meant the session went straight to RED with zero rediscovery.

What Session 22 Did

Deliverable: Implement PHASE 1 of docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md ‚Äî Summary Statistics tab parity (the conversion plan‚Äôs first implementation slice; S21 was the plan). (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-02‚Üí03. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 596f6bc9 (feat: impl ‚Äî 4 files) + this docs: close-out. Nature: an implementation session under strict TDD (RED‚ÜíGREEN; REFACTOR gated + skipped). Followed DEVELOPMENT_WORKSTREAM + plan ¬ß9 Phase 1 + the RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR phase gates (each gate via AskUserQuestion). One deliverable; did NOT bundle Phase 2+ (FM #18). Author decisions (USER, via AskUserQuestion): (1) founder table ‚Üí add to the Summary tab (thread founderStats; keep the GVA subtab too); (2) kinship download ‚Üí use the module-internal getKinshipMatrix() (smallest change; avoided the plan‚Äôs ‚Äúthread reportGV kinship‚Äù dragon ‚Äî reportGV filters to probands, so identical() would likely fail and it would silently change the relationship-derivation basis); (3) REFACTOR skipped (the only duplication ‚Äî the dual-name zScore block in two reactives ‚Äî PRE-EXISTED and matches the file‚Äôs self-contained-reactive style; out of Phase 1‚Äôs parity scope). The bug (item 1, verified empirically ‚Äî Learning #15/#20 + matrix-cbind naming): reportGV.R:89 zScores <- scale(indivMeanKin) is cbind‚Äôd at L144 ‚Üí a column literally named zScores (scale() returns an unnamed 1-col matrix; cbind names it from the deparsed symbol ‚Äî confirmed firsthand: names(report) contains zScores, class numeric, no zScore). modGeneticValue.geneticValues() renames only indivMeanKin/gu, so zScores arrives unchanged; modSummaryStats checked "zScore" (singular) ‚Üí z-score hist+box ALWAYS NULL (‚ÄúZ-scores not available‚Äù). The existing _ggplots test passed on the bug because its fixture injects BOTH names. The four fixes (5 edits in R/modSummaryStats.R, 1 in R/appServer.R): (1) dual-name z-score lookup (prefer zScores) in zscoreHistogramPlot + zscoreBoxPlotGG; (2) mkSummaryData/guSummaryData reactives + rendered MK/GU quartile tables (Min/1stQ/Mean/Median/3rdQ/Max) in output$summaryStats (monolith server.r:545-630); (3) new founderStats=NULL param + rendered founder table (Known/Female/Male + FE/FG) in summaryStats, wired in appServer.R:278 as gvResults$founderStats; (4) downloadKinship writes getKinshipMatrix() not the NULL arg; (5) return list gained mkSummary/guSummary (passed through directly, not reactive({‚Ä¶})-wrapped ‚Üí lint-clean). TDD trail: PRE-RED ‚Äî read plan ¬ß9 + the 4 modular/monolith files firsthand; empirical probes (real column name; founderStats fields; testServer surfacing). Pre-RED author forks (¬ß16.6/¬ß16.8) via AskUserQuestion. RED ‚Äî tests/testthat/test_modSummaryStats_parity.R (6 tests), all failing for the RIGHT reasons (NULL plots; mkSummary non-function; "Quartile"/labels absent; founderStats ‚Äúunused argument‚Äù; req(NULL) error) ‚Äî confirmed before any impl. GREEN ‚Äî the 6 edits ‚Üí 6/6 green (22 expectations). REFACTOR ‚Äî gated, skipped. Verification (gold standard for a module slice): full suite under pkgload::load_all+NOT_CRAN=true = 0 failed / 0 error / 2071 passed (+22 = the new file), 0 non-e2e offenders, e2e skipped (156), only the 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings. Touched files all green (modSummaryStats 33, _ggplots 31, _popovers 23, _relationships 43, appServer_dynamicTabs 23, parity 22). Lint net-zero proven by touched-files-only git stash + lint-HEAD-in-place (Learning #16c): modSummaryStats 60=60, appServer 18=18 (initial +2 brace_linter from the wrapped return entries ‚Üí fixed by direct pass-through; diagnosed via line-CONTENT diff, not the misleading line-RANGE filter). devtools::document() ‚Üí only man/modSummaryStatsServer.Rd. Phase 3E runtime smoke: runModularApp() binds (‚ÄúListening on ‚Ä¶:7613‚Äù) + HTTP 200, no startup error (the appServer founderStats wiring is a runtime change ‚Äî build/test-clean ‚âÝ runtime-clean, FM #24). Key files: R/modSummaryStats.R (signature L275; zCol blocks ~L395 & ~L484; mkSummaryData/guSummaryData/quartileRow + summaryStats renderUI ~L544-665; downloadKinship; return mkSummary/guSummary), R/appServer.R:274-280 (founderStats wiring), man/modSummaryStatsServer.Rd, NEW tests/testthat/test_modSummaryStats_parity.R. Docs: CHANGELOG.md (S22), CLAUDE.md (Learning #22), BACKLOG.md, docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md (¬ß9/¬ß17 annotated). Throwaway (/tmp, NOT committed): lint_now.txt, lint_head.txt. RED = appUI() HTML lacks the GvAndBgDesc tab; DONE = tab renders + test_appServer_dynamicTabs.R still green. When the user said ‚Äúplan completing the conversion to using Shiny modules,‚Äù that framing instantly mapped the task to the Architecture/Planning workstream (not an implementation session) ‚Äî exactly right. - What helped (reflexes transferred): S19/S20‚Äôs NOT_CRAN=true + E2E-gate gotchas (Learning #19b) and the create_test_app opt-in gate were directly operative ‚Äî they fed my understanding of the E2E suite‚Äôs current state, which became Phase 8 (and the discovery that the suite is unwritten theatre). The carried verify-first reflex (Learnings #5/#8a/#9a/#13/#20) was the spine of this session: I disproved three audit ‚Äústaleness‚Äù premises and verified every load-bearing parity claim firsthand before it entered the plan. - What was missing (expected ‚Äî S20 wasn‚Äôt working this item): no detail on the actual modular-vs-monolith parity gap or current module state (I discovered all of it via the discovery workflow + firsthand verification). And the TECH_DEBT audit S20 pointed to turned out stale in key ways (ui.r line count, the ‚Äúlowercase duplicate,‚Äù the XARCH-3/4/7-first sequencing) ‚Äî but S20 couldn‚Äôt have known without doing the work. - What was wrong: nothing in S20‚Äôs handoff was inaccurate. (The audit it referenced was stale, but S20 faithfully relayed it.) - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the ‚Äúthis is planning work‚Äù framing + the E2E/NOT_CRAN reflexes oriented the session correctly from the first message.

What Session 21 Did

Deliverable: PLAN completing the Shiny-module conversion (issue #27 / XARCH-1) ‚Äî the architecture/planning document docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md (356 lines, 9 vertical-slice phases). (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-02. Branch: add-methodology. Commit: this docs: close-out (the plan + session docs). Nature: the project‚Äôs first planning/architecture deliverable (Sessions 1‚Äì20 were all audit-fix/refactor/test code work). Followed docs/methodology/workstreams/ARCHITECTURE_WORKSTREAM.md + the SESSION_RUNNER Planning Session protocol (evidence-based grep inventory, per-phase completion criteria, vertical slices, plan-is-the-deliverable). Strict-TDD code phases (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR) did NOT apply ‚Äî no production code was written (declared at the top of the response); the discipline that applied was the planning protocol. Did NOT implement (FM #18/#19 ‚Äî the plan is the deliverable; implementation is later sessions). Author scope decisions (USER, via AskUserQuestion): (1) Full conversion: parity + E2E enablement + retire monolith; (2) Exclude ORIP-reporting wiring + functional Settings/About (parity = match monolith; both are out-of-scope future features); (3) GU-threshold = re-expose the user selector, default 4 (monolith parity, replacing the hardcoded 1L). Method (ultracode, right-sized): (a) read SAFEGUARDS/SESSION_RUNNER/ACTIVE-TASK + the ARCHITECTURE workstream + the XARCH/APP audit findings; (b) structural recon (file inventory, exports, issue #27 = title-only); (c) a read-only 8-mapper discovery workflow (wf_2b9863c9-615, 9 agents, ~717K tok) ‚Üí synthesis (parity gap / deletion inventory / prereqs / dragons / proposed phases); (d) firsthand verification of every load-bearing claim (z-score bug, E2E helpers undefined, breeding-group hole, GvAndBgDesc unwired, guThresh, genotype/focal-animal gap, orphans, getLogo, NAMESPACE, vignette refs); (e) wrote the plan; (f) a 3-agent completeness-critic workflow (wf_f2de6bd6-29f) that found real omissions; (g) verified the critic‚Äôs findings firsthand and patched the plan (10 patches). What the completeness-critic CAUGHT (real omissions the single-pass synthesis missed) ‚Äî all verified + patched: (1) the modular kinship-matrix download is a dead button (modSummaryStats.R:595 req(kinshipMatrix) with appServer.R:278 passing NULL); (2) the modular Summary-Stats tab drops the MK/GU quartile distribution tables (monolith server.r:545-630; modular shows 3 scalars only); (3) FE/FG founder table placement gap (modular shows it on the GVA subtab, monolith on the Summary tab); (4) breeding-sim iterations default is 10 (gpIter) not 1000 ‚Äî modular fallback 1000L is a 100√ó drift, distinct from the GVA gene-drop count; (5) the inert debug toggle; (6) the GU-threshold selectInput offset mapping (label N‚Üívalue N+1). Also corrected: getMinParentAge is @noRd/unexported (no man, no NAMESPACE ‚Äî simpler deletion); a3manual.{R,md,html} ARE knitted outputs containing runGeneKeepR (edit the .Rmd child + re-knit). The plan in one paragraph: declare the modular app canonical; close the ¬ß6 parity gaps over Phases 1‚Äì7 (1 Summary-Stats tab; 2 wire GvAndBgDesc tab; 3 GVA threshold+subset export; 4 genotype-file merge; 5 breeding-group downloads+per-group-kinship+selector; 6 breeding-group seed-groups+inert-controls; 7 üêâ focal-animal/LabKey build); Phase 8 üêâ author the missing E2E driver helpers + run the shinytest2 tier (= issue #39); Phase 9 üêâ (irreversible) alias runGeneKeepR via lifecycle::deprecate_soft, delete inst/application/ + confirmed orphans, update docs/vignettes/CI. Each phase has goal / done-criteria / verify-command / session-boundary / risk / discriminating-RED guidance. Key files (the plan + its evidence): docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md (THE deliverable ‚Äî read it). Workflow artifacts (NOT committed): ‚Ä¶/tasks/wvavm9ljt.output (discovery synthesis), ‚Ä¶/tasks/wyud7g1yg.output (completeness critic). Evidence cited in-plan: modular core R/appUI.R/R/appServer.R/R/runModularApp.R/R/runGenekeepr.R; modules R/mod*.R; monolith inst/application/server.r(1304L)/ui.r(53L)/8 uitp*.R/global.R; reportGV.R:89,144 (zScores); modSummaryStats.R:396,477,595; modBreedingGroups.R:188,203; modGeneticValue.R:165,220-232; inst/application/uitpBreedingGroupFormation.R:155 (gpIter=10). Docs updated: CHANGELOG.md, BACKLOG.md, CLAUDE.md (Learning #21). GOTCHAS for the next session (implementing Phase 1): 1. Implement PHASE 1 ONLY ‚Äî Summary Statistics tab parity (4 sub-items in plan ¬ß9). Strict TDD applies now (RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR, phase gates). Do NOT bundle Phase 2+. Split 1a/1b if long. 2. The z-score bug needs a column-name TRACE first: data flows reportGV(zScores) ‚Üí modGeneticValue(renames some cols) ‚Üí modSummaryStats(zScore). Read the name that ACTUALLY arrives (print names(gv)), then write a discriminating RED that fails on the NULL-plot path (Learning #15/#20 ‚Äî a pre-existing test may pass on NULL). 3. The kinship download (req(kinshipMatrix) + kinshipMatrix=NULL) is dead ‚Äî fix by threading gvResults$kinshipMatrix OR using the module‚Äôs internal kinship (plan ¬ß16.8). If threading, prove identical(). 4. The audit is STALE (plan ¬ß11) ‚Äî plan against the real files. The modular app is much more complete than the audit implies. 5. E2E (Phase 8) is unwritten theatre ‚Äî create_app_driver/navigate_to_tab/get_html_safe defined nowhere (verified git log -S); 155 e2e test_that would error before asserting. This is issue #39‚Äôs real scope. 6. Verify under NOT_CRAN=true + pkgload::load_all(".") (Learnings #15b/#19b). Regression read command is in plan ¬ß9. 7. renv ‚Äúout-of-sync‚Äù on bare Rscript is pre-existing noise; authoritative read is under load_all. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Honored the planning protocol ‚Äî recognized this as Architecture/Planning (not implementation), declared TDD code-phases inapplicable, wrote the plan and did NOT implement (FM #18/#19), completed the mandatory grep-based inventory (¬ß10) with references run not assumed. (+) 1B stub landed this time (S20‚Äôs documented miss not repeated). (+) Verify-first paid off massively ‚Äî the audit was stale on 3+ load-bearing points and the discovery synthesis missed 4 real parity gaps; firsthand verification + the completeness-critic caught them all before they reached the executor (the whole point of the planning grep-inventory). (+) Right-sized ultracode orchestration ‚Äî a genuine broad fan-out (discovery 8-mapper) + an adversarial completeness-critic, both warranted by the scale (~5500 lines, a plan driving 9 sessions); not ceremony. (+) Surfaced the real author/scope decisions via AskUserQuestion with evidence in hand (not blind). (+) Vertical slices, not horizontal layers (FM #25) ‚Äî each phase ships a working feature. (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: read-only until the plan write; left the pre-existing .DS_Store/..Rcheck//audit-html noise untouched. (‚àí) Phase 3E (runtime smoke): N/A ‚Äî a planning document changes no runtime behavior; stated, not silently skipped (NOT FM #24). (‚àí) Three user interactions (the scope AskUserQuestion) ‚Äî appropriate for genuine author/product scope forks, but a recurring cost. (‚àí) Did not file the deferred XARCH items as GitHub issues (outward-facing; left as a user decision per the carried tracker-reconciliation question).

Session 19 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 20)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: S19‚Äôs ACTIVE-TASK suggested-next named the trivial test_getPotentialParents.R assertion fix as the first option, with the exact symptom (‚Äúrecomputes a local ped but asserts the old top-level potentialParents[[1L]]$id‚Äù) and provenance (found Session 4). Precisely accurate ‚Äî the defect was exactly that at lines 61-68; the session scoped instantly. - What helped (reflexes transferred): S19 gotcha #2 ‚Äî verify under NOT_CRAN=true, not bare test_dir (Learning #19b) ‚Äî was directly operative: the regression ran with NOT_CRAN=true and confirmed 0/0 with the e2e files skipped (S19‚Äôs gate working as designed). The campaign verify-first reflex (Learnings #5/#8a/#9a) carried to a TEST defect ‚Äî I empirically reproduced the scenario (BRI2MW dropped, 50‚Üí49) before writing the assertion rather than trusting the test‚Äôs name. The load_all-before-test_dir (#15b) and in-place-mutation-not-worktree (#3) reflexes both fired. - What was missing (expected ‚Äî S19 wasn‚Äôt working the item): the handoff framed it as a one-liner ‚Äúassertion fix‚Äù without the strict-TDD subtlety ‚Äî because production is already correct the corrected test is green-on-arrival, so it‚Äôs REFACTOR-only (not RED‚ÜíGREEN) and needs a mutation check for rigor. I surfaced that as the author‚Äôs pre-RED classification decision. (‚Üí Learning #20.) Fair: S19 was relaying the carried Session-4 note, not working the item. - What was wrong: nothing. File, test name, line region, and defect description all checked out exactly. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the precise pointer + the carried verify-first / NOT_CRAN / mutation reflexes produced a fast, correctly-classified, mutation-verified one-deliverable session.

What Session 20 Did

Deliverable: Fix the vacuous/tautological assertion in tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R test "works with records with no potential parent" (a copy/paste slip flagged Session 4, never fixed) so it genuinely verifies its named scenario, under strict TDD. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-02. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 6049445d (test), + this docs: close-out. Nature: a test-correctness fix (not a compute bug, not a production refactor) ‚Äî the campaign‚Äôs first vacuous-test repair. REFACTOR-only under strict TDD: no RED/GREEN (author-classified). Gated PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR via AskUserQuestion. Author decision (USER): chose REFACTOR-only over a formal RED‚ÜíGREEN. Rationale accepted: getPotentialParents is already correct ‚Üí a correct assertion is green-on-arrival; declaring RED on a passing test violates ‚ÄúRED tests must fail,‚Äù and forcing a fail via a wrong expectation is a synthetic RED (Learning #18c). The defect (test_getPotentialParents.R:61-68): the test set pedOne$birth[1] <- 1950 and computed a local ped <- getPotentialParents(...), then asserted expect_equal(potentialParents[[1L]]$id, ids[1L]) ‚Äî referencing the top-level fixture potentialParents (built at lines 9-13 from the unmodified pedigree) and never touching ped. A tautology already covered by the first test (line 47); it verified nothing about its scenario. The empirical finding (verify-first): row 1 of rhesusPedigree IS BRI2MW ‚Äî a from-center founder with both parents unknown (potentialParents[[1L]]). Pushing its birth to 1950-01-01 makes its breeding-age candidate set ba = ped[birth ‚â§ 1948] empty, so getPotentialParents hits the if (nrow(ba) == 0L) next skip (R/getPotentialParents.R:54-56) and drops BRI2MW: scenario result = 49 entries (vs global 50), BRI2MW absent. Production is correct; the defect was purely the assertion. The fix (1 file, +10/‚àí1): replaced the single tautological line with a discriminating block ‚Äî precondition ("BRI2MW" %in% the global ids ‚Äî it normally appears), then after the birth edit expect_false("BRI2MW" %in% scenarioIds) (dropped) and expect_equal(length(ped), length(potentialParents) - 1L) (exactly one fewer). Self-documenting, code-token-free comment. Verification (gold standard for a test fix): (1) corrected test passes (its 3 expectations; 15 in the file). (2) MUTATION CHECK ‚Äî disabled the next-skip in-place (perl -i ‚Üí git checkout restore; Learning #3, no worktree); both new assertions FAILED (BRI2MW present; length 50‚âÝ49), proving the test discriminates ‚Äî and the OLD assertion would have PASSED that same mutant (the contrast proves added coverage). (3) full suite under pkgload::load_all(".") + NOT_CRAN=true: 0 failed / 0 error, 0 non-e2e offenders, 2049 passed (S19‚Äôs 2047 + 2 net new expectations), 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings, e2e files skipped. Lint: tests is .lintr-excluded (.lintr:35) ‚Üí net-zero by config (the #16c /tmp-lint is doubly-invalid here). No document(), no NEWS.md (test-only, no user-facing effect). Key files: tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R:61-77 (the corrected test). Production R/getPotentialParents.R:54-56 (the next-skip ‚Äî read & mutation-tested, NOT changed). Docs: CHANGELOG.md (S20 entry), BACKLOG.md (trivial item ‚Üí resolved; suggested-next refreshed), CLAUDE.md (Learning #20). GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. Fixing a vacuous / green-on-arrival test is REFACTOR-only under strict TDD ‚Äî don‚Äôt fake a RED (Learning #20a, extends #18c from comments/dead-code to test corrections). Classify, gate PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR, prove discrimination via a mutation check. 2. tests is excluded wholesale in .lintr (line 35) ‚Äî test-file edits are lint-exempt by config; don‚Äôt run the #16c-invalid /tmp lint on them. 3. The audit compute/test campaign is now COMPLETE (all NEW-* + the trivial test fix resolved through S20). What remains is planning work ‚Äî NEW-12/XARCH-3 (Shiny progress hook), XARCH-1 (two coexisting apps; also gates issue #39) ‚Äî plus open GitHub issues (#34 bug, #30 lintr, #37 unused exports, #9). Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1‚Äì#39) still open. 4. renv reports the project ‚Äúout-of-sync‚Äù on every Rscript ‚Äî pre-existing noise (prior sessions saw it too); the authoritative test read is under pkgload::load_all("."), which works fine. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored ‚Äî declared the phase at the top of every response; recognized the green-on-arrival classification tension and surfaced it as the author‚Äôs pre-RED decision via AskUserQuestion (gated PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR); did NOT fake a RED (Learning #18c discipline). (+) Verify-first on a TEST defect ‚Äî empirically reproduced the scenario (BRI2MW dropped, 50‚Üí49) before writing the assertion, so it asserts real behavior, not the test‚Äôs name. (+) Mutation check proved the new assertion discriminates (and the old one didn‚Äôt) ‚Äî the rigorous substitute for a RED on green-on-arrival code; in-place + git checkout restore (Learning #3), production verified clean after. (+) Right-sized under ultracode ‚Äî NO multi-agent workflow for a trivial, deterministic, mutation-verified one-assertion fix (honest ‚Äútrivial + already-verified‚Äù); said so rather than reflexively orchestrating. (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: committed the isolated test fix BEFORE the multi-file doc change; staged only the intended file; left the pre-existing .DS_Store/..Rcheck//audit-html noise untouched. (+) Lint net-zero confirmed by .lintr config (the tests exclusion), not an invalid /tmp lint. (‚àí) 1B stub miss: my first attempt to write the session-claim stub FAILED (‚Äúfile not read yet‚Äù) and I proceeded to investigation without retrying it, so the mandatory 1B trace never landed (FM #14 mitigation incomplete) ‚Äî a mid-session crash would have left no Session-20 trace. The full handoff now supersedes it; I should have retried the stub immediately. (‚àí) Did not run full devtools::check() (e2e baseline + S7‚ÄìS19 precedent); mitigated by the NOT_CRAN=true test_dir 0/0 + mutation check + the targeted reproduction. (‚àí) Phase 3E: test-only change ‚Äî no production code touched at all, so no app-startup/registration/dispatch/config behavior changed; a live-app launch does not apply. Stated, not silently skipped ‚Äî NOT FM #24. (‚àí) One user interaction (the classification gate) ‚Äî mandated by strict TDD‚Äôs phase-gate contract; the recurring minor.

Session 18 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 19)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: S18‚Äôs ACTIVE-TASK suggested-next named the test-infra debt as a discrete ‚Äúits own session‚Äù deliverable with the exact symptom (create_test_app() undefined ‚Üí 154 suite ERRORS when shinytest2+chromote installed) and the file globs (test-app-*/test-e2e-*). That pointer scoped the session instantly and was accurate. - What helped (reflexes transferred): the campaign‚Äôs verify-first reflex (Learnings #8a/#9a ‚Äî ‚Äúthe audit‚Äôs stated mechanism can be wrong/incomplete‚Äù) was directly operative: I did NOT take ‚Äú154 errors, define the helper or gate‚Äù at face value. Empirical probing revealed the load-bearing mechanism nobody had documented ‚Äî skip_on_cran() keys on NOT_CRAN, so the suite is clean under bare test_dir but errors under devtools::test()/CI. The carried Learning #2/#4 (‚Äútest_local ERRORS / test_dir SKIPS‚Äù) recorded the symptom; this session found the cause. The load_all-before-test_dir reflex (Learning #15b) and the /tmp-lint-is-invalid reflex (#16c) both fired correctly. - What was missing (expected ‚Äî S18 wasn‚Äôt working the item): the handoff carried the audit‚Äôs binary ‚Äúdefine the helper OR gate the tests‚Äù without noting that (a) the effort was ~90% complete ‚Äî the app is instrumented (data-ready.js + 6 modules) and inst/shinytest/app.R already exists, making create_test_app() a near-trivial one-liner; (b) the e2e tests target the modular app and are entangled with XARCH-1 (two coexisting apps); (c) the chosen fix is really ‚Äúgate cleanly + preserve the plan,‚Äù not ‚Äúfinish the e2e suite.‚Äù I surfaced all three via investigation. (‚Üí Learning #19.) - What was wrong: trivial only ‚Äî ‚Äú22 files‚Äù vs the actual 23; ‚Äú154‚Äù is the call-site count (the file count is 23). - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the precise pointer plus the verify-first reflex turned a vaguely-scoped ‚Äútest debt‚Äù into a clean, correctly-scoped one-cycle deliverable with the deferred work captured (issue #39).

What Session 19 Did

Deliverable: Test-infra debt ‚Äî define create_test_app() (the missing entry point for the 23 test-app-*/test-e2e-* files) with an opt-in gate so the 154 suite ERRORS become clean skips, under strict TDD. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-01‚Üí02. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: a1ee8497 (test: helper + test_create_test_app.R), + this docs: close-out. Nature: test-infrastructure debt paydown (not a compute bug, not a refactor). One RED‚ÜíGREEN cycle; no REFACTOR (the helper is minimal/clean; extracting the tests‚Äô env-var boilerplate would need fiddly parent.frame()/on.exit and read worse ‚Äî gated GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR and chose skip). Author scope decision (USER): chose ‚ÄúGate + opt-in + issue‚Äù over (B) full e2e implementation+validation [campaign-sized, entangled with XARCH-1] and (C) minimal skip-only [discards the runnable path]. The user‚Äôs own framing ‚Äî ‚ÄúE2E coverage is prudent but perhaps not now‚Äù ‚Äî drove it; pursue the full e2e campaign later, sequenced with XARCH-1. Root cause (the discovery that reframed the item): create_test_app() was never defined (confirmed: git log -S 'create_test_app <-' --all empty; the e2e suite landed in 7da01afe without it). The errors are runner-dependent: each test guards with skip_if_not_installed("shinytest2"/"chromote") + skip_on_cran(); shinytest2+chromote+Chrome are all installed here, and skip_on_cran() only skips when NOT_CRAN is unset ‚Üí bare test_dir SKIPS (clean), but devtools::test()/test_local()/CI set NOT_CRAN=true ‚Üí no skip ‚Üí reach the undefined helper ‚Üí 154 errors. (This is the cause behind Learnings #2/#4‚Äôs recorded symptom.) The fix (2 files): appended create_test_app() to tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R (its natural home; auto-sourced for the suite): if (!identical(Sys.getenv("NPRC_RUN_E2E"), "true")) testthat::skip(...) else system.file("shinytest", package="nprcgenekeepr"). The returned inst/shinytest/app.R is shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) (the modular app) ‚Äî exactly what AppDriver needs. New browser-free tests/testthat/test_create_test_app.R (2 test_that): opt-in returns a 1-length char path to an existing dir containing app.R; gate raises a skip condition (caught via tryCatch(condition=‚Ä¶), asserted with expect_s3_class(cnd, "skip") ‚Äî the probe-confirmed class is c("skip","condition")). Env var scoped with base Sys.setenv/Sys.unsetenv+on.exit (withr is NOT a declared dep ‚Üí avoided). Deferred work ‚Üí GitHub issue #39 (‚ÄúComplete & validate the shinytest2 E2E test suite‚Äù): the existing assets (instrumented app, 159 tests, inst/shinytest/app.R, shinytest2.yaml CI), the remaining campaign (run+validate the 159 tests, wire CI), and the XARCH-1 dependency. This is the antidote to ‚Äúthe plan got lost.‚Äù Verification (gold standard for test-infra): new tests pass (test1: 5 expectations; test2: 1). Full suite under pkgload::load_all(".") + NOT_CRAN=true (the hard case): 0 failed / 0 error, E2E files error=0 / skipped=154 (was 154 errors), zero non-e2e offenders, 2047 passed, only the 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings. Lint net-zero (helper-shinytest2.R = 0 lints in-place; the /tmp-HEAD ‚Äú10‚Äù is the Learning #16c artifact ‚Äî /tmp loses .lintr+namespace; verified helper-shinytest2.R is NOT .lintr line-excluded ‚Üí #7 trap inapplicable). No document() (test helper, not package API ‚Üí no man/NAMESPACE). Key files: NEW tests/testthat/test_create_test_app.R (2 browser-free tests). tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R:176-201 (appended create_test_app()). Docs: CHANGELOG.md (S19 entry), BACKLOG.md (test-infra debt ‚Üí resolved/issue #39), CLAUDE.md (Learning #19). Throwaway (/tmp, NOT committed): helper_head.R. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. NPRC_RUN_E2E=true flips the E2E suite ON. With it set, the 23 e2e files run create_test_app() ‚Üí AppDriver launches Chrome and drives the modular app ‚Äî 159 never-validated browser tests. Expect a first-run flood to triage (issue #39). Do NOT set it in CI until XARCH-1 is addressed and the tests are validated. 2. The runner determines whether the suite looks clean ‚Äî skip_on_cran() keys on NOT_CRAN. Always verify ‚Äúno regressions‚Äù under NOT_CRAN=true (the devtools::test()/CI condition), not just a bare test_dir (refines Learnings #2/#4). 3. The E2E effort is ~90% complete and entangled with XARCH-1, not lost scaffolding. runGeneKeepR‚Üímonolith inst/application/; runModularApp‚ÜíshinyApp(appUI(),appServer); e2e tests target the modular app. See issue #39 + TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md XARCH-1. 4. Suggested next = the trivial test_getPotentialParents.R assertion fix, OR NEW-12/XARCH-3 / XARCH-1 (planning; XARCH-1 also gates #39), OR a GitHub issue (#34 bug, #30 lintr, #37 unused exports). Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1‚Äì#39) still open. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored ‚Äî declared every phase; gated PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR via AskUserQuestion + the separate pre-RED author scope decision; RED failed for the right reason (could not find function), GREEN minimal (no untested branches ‚Äî omitted a speculative ‚Äúapp.R missing‚Äù guard), REFACTOR honestly skipped. (+) Verify-first paid off ‚Äî probed the actual failure (the skip_on_cran/NOT_CRAN mechanism) and the actual completeness (instrumented app + inst/shinytest/app.R) instead of taking the audit‚Äôs binary framing, which reframed the deliverable from ‚Äúfinish e2e‚Äù to ‚Äúgate cleanly + preserve the plan.‚Äù (+) Right-sized under ultracode ‚Äî NO multi-agent workflow: this was a contained, deterministic grep/probe investigation (honest ‚Äútrivial + already-verified‚Äù), so a workflow would be ceremony; said so rather than reflexively orchestrating. (+) Preserved the plan (issue #39) ‚Äî directly addressing the user‚Äôs ‚Äúthe plan got lost‚Äù concern. (+) Probed before asserting (skip class, withr absence, system.file resolution) ‚Äî no theorizing. (+) Lint net-zero with both traps (#7, #16c) pre-checked. (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: GREEN (2 files) committed before close-out; staged only the intended files; left the pre-existing .DS_Store/..Rcheck//audit-html noise untouched. (‚àí) Did not run full devtools::check() (the e2e baseline + known precedent S7‚ÄìS18); mitigated by the NOT_CRAN=true test_dir 0/0 + lint + the targeted reproduction. (‚àí) Phase 3E: test-only change ‚Äî no app-startup/registration/dispatch/config behavior changed (the helper returns a path; it does NOT launch the app under the default gate), so a live-app launch does not apply. The opt-in browser path is intentionally deferred (issue #39). Stated, not silently skipped ‚Äî NOT FM #24. (‚àí) Outward-facing action (filed issue #39) ‚Äî done under the explicit author approval in the scope decision. (‚àí) Several user interactions (scope clarification + scope decision + 3 phase gates) ‚Äî mandated by strict TDD + an author scope decision; the recurring minor.

Session 17 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 18)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: S17‚Äôs ACTIVE-TASK suggested-next named NEW-22/NEW-30 with exact locations and the load-bearing framing that they ‚Äúnow live in ONE place‚Äù post-NEW-13. What Session 18 Did

Deliverable: NEW-22 / NEW-30 ‚Äî document the Mendelian ¬Ω factor (declined the named constant) and remove the dead UID.founders block, in R/calcFounderContributions.R + R/kinship.R, under strict TDD. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-01. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 04115d97 (refactor: comments + dead-code), + this docs: close-out. Nature: the campaign‚Äôs 3rd pure refactor (after PED-1, NEW-13/23) and the FIRST whose audit motivation was already dissolved by a prior session. Comment + dead-code only ‚Üí REFACTOR-only under strict TDD: no RED/GREEN (no new behavior, no new unit). Gated PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR via AskUserQuestion, plus a separate pre-RED author scope decision. Decisions (USER, package author): (1) NEW-22 = decline the named constant / comment-only (keep / 2L ‚Äî self-documenting; a shared constant would over-couple distinct formulas across the GV compute + the kinship engine); (2) NEW-30 = delete the UID.founders block, KEEP founderMatrix <- NULL (intentional memory free, not dead ‚Äî annotate it); (3) approved the PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR gate (RED/GREEN vacuous for a comments/dead-code change). The two findings that reshaped the item (audit partly outdated/wrong ‚Äî Learnings #8a/#10a pattern): 1. NEW-22‚Äôs ‚Äúduplication‚Äù was already resolved by S17. The 3 calc* copies ‚Üí 1 helper, so the 5 named /2L sites are now 4‚Äì5 DISTINCT Mendelian formulas ‚Äî parental-contribution avg (calcFounderContributions.R:61), parental-kinship avg (kinship.R:103), self-kinship (1+f)/2 (kinship.R:104), founder self-kinship init (kinship.R:78/80) ‚Äî not one shared knob. Grep-confirmed the inventory is contained to those 2 files; every other 0.5//2L in R/ is unrelated (date math, plot colors, UI sliders, thresholds, docs). 2. founderMatrix <- NULL is NOT a dead variable. founderMatrix is USED at d <- rbind(founderMatrix, d) (calcFounderContributions.R:45); the <- NULL frees the founders√ófounders identity block before the generation loop ‚Äî an intentional memory optimization the audit mislabeled. Kept + annotated (L46-48). The change (5 edits, 2 files): (NEW-22) keep every / 2L; add a 1-line Mendelian-¬Ω comment at calcFounderContributions.R:59-60 and kinship.R:75-76 (init) + kinship.R:100-102 (recurrence). (NEW-30) delete the dead ## UID.founders <- ‚Ä¶ block + its # nolint: commented_code_linter wrapper (was ~L36-40); annotate founderMatrix <- NULL (now L46-48). TDD trail: PRE-RED ‚Äî grep inventory + audit-claim scrutiny ‚Üí pre-RED author scope AskUserQuestion (decline constant / keep NULL). No RED/GREEN (declared + justified: comments + dead-code have no new behavior/unit; a synthetic ‚Äúassert the comment exists‚Äù RED would test implementation trivia ‚Äî anti-TDD). REFACTOR ‚Äî the 5 edits; committed AFTER proving behavior-preservation. Verification (gold standard for a parser-inert change): captured a HEAD reference, then proved byte-identical() on all 10 outputs ‚Äî calcFE/calcFG/calcFEFG (char+factor), calcFounderContributions $p+$ped, kinship() dense+sparse, and the full set.seed(42) reportGV() object (the live calcFEFG/kinship caller). Full suite under load_all = 0 failed / 0 error / 2001 passed (= S17‚Äôs exact count; no new tests), 0 non-e2e offenders, only the 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings. Lint net-zero (both files 0, = HEAD; verified neither is .lintr line-excluded ‚Üí the Learning #7 shift-trap is inapplicable; prose comments use no code-like tokens ‚Üí commented_code_linter stays clean). document() = no man/NAMESPACE change. No adversarial workflow ‚Äî right-sized: the change is parser-inert (BOTH trivial AND already-verified via the seeded reportGV() identical()), so a workflow would be ceremony (honest reading of ultracode‚Äôs ‚Äúunless trivial or already verified‚Äù). No NEWS.md bullet ‚Äî internal-only, no user-facing effect (NEWS = CRAN-facing per Learning #11); logged in CHANGELOG.md. Key files: R/calcFounderContributions.R (deleted dead block ~L36-40; annotated founderMatrix <- NULL L46-48; Mendelian comment L59-60), R/kinship.R (Mendelian comments L75-76 init, L100-102 recurrence ‚Äî all / 2L literals at L78/80/103/104 unchanged). Throwaway (/tmp, NOT committed): new2230_ref_capture.R, new2230_compare.R, new2230_ref.rds. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. An audit item tagged ‚Äúnow trivial because a prior session changed the code‚Äù may have had its MOTIVATION dissolved ‚Äî re-derive whether the reason still holds before doing it. NEW-22‚Äôs duplication was gone (S17 consolidated); the right output was a documented DECLINE, not an extraction. (‚Üí Learning #18a.) 2. The audit‚Äôs ‚Äúdead variable‚Äù can be a deliberate optimization ‚Äî founderMatrix <- NULL is a memory free, kept. Verify each claimed-dead var is truly unused before deleting. (‚Üí Learning #18b.) 3. A comment-only / dead-code cleanup is REFACTOR-only under strict TDD ‚Äî no RED/GREEN. Don‚Äôt fake a RED; gate PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR. (‚Üí Learning #18c.) 4. Suggested next = test-infra debt (create_test_app) OR NEW-12/XARCH-3 / XARCH-1 (planning) OR the trivial tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R assertion slip. Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1‚Äì#38) still open. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored ‚Äî declared every phase‚Äôs TDD state; recognized the change as REFACTOR-only and justified the absence of RED/GREEN honestly (NOT FM #17 erosion: there is no new behavior to develop); gated PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR + the separate pre-RED scope decision via AskUserQuestion. (+) Verify-first paid off twice ‚Äî disproved NEW-22‚Äôs duplication premise (already resolved by S17) and the ‚Äúdead variable‚Äù claim, turning a reflexive ‚Äúextract + delete‚Äù into a disciplined ‚Äúdecline + keep + annotate.‚Äù (+) Behavior-preservation proven to the gold standard ‚Äî identical() on all 10 outputs incl.¬Ýthe full seeded reportGV() object, despite the change being parser-inert. (+) Lint net-zero with the Learning #7 trap pre-checked (neither file .lintr-excluded) and prose comments kept code-token-free. (+) Right-sized under ultracode ‚Äî no adversarial workflow for a parser-inert change (both escape clauses apply); stated the reasoning, not silently skipped. (+) Correct doc separation ‚Äî CHANGELOG yes, NEWS no (internal-only). (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: baseline already committed; only the 2 intended R files staged; left the pre-existing .DS_Store/..Rcheck//audit-html noise untouched. (‚àí) Did not run full devtools::check() (e2e create_test_app baseline noise ‚Äî S7‚ÄìS17 precedent); mitigated by test_dir 0/0 + lint + document() + the identical() proof. (‚àí) Phase 3E: comment + dead-code change ‚Äî no app-startup/registration/dispatch/config behavior changed; the only live path (reportGV‚ÜícalcFEFG/kinship) verified identical() on the full seeded object, so a live-app launch does not apply. Stated, not silently skipped ‚Äî NOT FM #24. (‚àí) Two user interactions (scope decision + phase gate) ‚Äî mandated by strict TDD + an author scope decision; the recurring minor.

Session 16 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 17)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: S16‚Äôs ACTIVE-TASK suggested-next named NEW-13/NEW-23 AND pre-scoped it ‚Äî ‚Äúnow even cleaner post-PED-1: all three calc* share the IDENTICAL getFounders(ped) + descendants + matrix-build body, so the only real diff is FE-vs-FG output, making delegation low-risk.‚Äù Exactly right: the three bodies WERE byte-identical (the adversarial workflow re-confirmed line-for-line), so the consolidation scoped instantly. - What helped (reflexes transferred): S16 gotcha #2 ‚Äî object_usage_linter ‚Äúno visible global function‚Äù on a NEW helper is the stale-namespace transient; authoritative read = REINSTALL/load_all then re-lint, NOT the /tmp method (Learning #16c) ‚Äî was directly operative: the fresh-process lint flagged calcFounderContributions on all 3 wrappers; I diagnosed it as the transient in seconds and confirmed 0 via load_all-then-lint. S16‚Äôs identical()-vs-reference-incl-seeded-reportGV() reflex (Learning #16b) was my entire REFACTOR verification template. - What had a dragon S16 didn‚Äôt flag (expected, minor): S16 relayed the audit‚Äôs literal ‚ÄúcalcFE/calcFG delegate to calcFEFG‚Äù ‚Äî but calcFE(ped) has no alleles arg and is gene-drop-free, so literal delegation is UNIMPLEMENTABLE for calcFE (it would force an FG it can‚Äôt feed). I surfaced the approach fork (shared @noRd helper vs alleles-optional calcFEFG) as a pre-RED AskUserQuestion; the user chose the helper. (‚Üí Learning #17a.) S16 was faithfully relaying the audit and hadn‚Äôt worked the item ‚Äî fair. - What was missing (minor): nothing flagged the load-bearing WIRING risk ‚Äî calcFG/calcFEFG feed the toCharacter-coerced ped to calcRetention, so the helper must return that ped (else factor input silently diverges). I caught it by reading calcRetention before designing. (‚Üí Learning #17b.) - What was wrong: nothing. The suggested-next pointer, the ‚Äú3 bodies identical‚Äù claim, and the descendants/population context all checked out. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the accurate pre-scoping + the lint-transient and identical() reflexes shaped a fast, clean session.

What Session 17 Did

Deliverable: NEW-13 / NEW-23 ‚Äî consolidate the founder-contribution algorithm (shared near-verbatim by calcFE/calcFG/calcFEFG) into one @noRd helper calcFounderContributions() and collapse the triplicated Session-7 partial-parentage stop() guard, under strict TDD (one RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR cycle). (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-01. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 022afc8b (GREEN: R/calcFounderContributions.R + tests/testthat/test_calcFounderContributions.R), 2b27f4c3 (REFACTOR: 3 thin wrappers, +16/‚àí134), + this docs: close-out. Nature: the campaign‚Äôs 2nd pure refactor (after PED-1) ‚Äî no behavior change. The body toCharacter‚Üípartial-parentage guard‚ÜígetFounders/descendants‚Üícontribution-matrix build‚Üígeneration propagation loop (d[ego,]<-(d[sire,]+d[dam,])/2L)‚ÜícurrentDesc subset‚Üíp<-colMeans(d) was copied ~45 lines √ó 3; now it lives ONCE in the helper, which returns list(p, ped). Decisions (USER, package author): (1) approach = shared @noRd helper (over the literal ‚Äúmake calcFEFG alleles-optional & delegate‚Äù) ‚Äî keeps calcFE gene-drop-free and all 3 public contracts byte-identical; (2) approved all three TDD phase gates + the pre-RED approach fork. The load-bearing design (gotcha #1): the helper returns the toCharacter-coerced ped as $ped, so calcFG/calcFEFG call calcRetention(fc$ped, alleles) with the EXACT character pedigree the pre-refactor code passed ‚Äî identical-BY-CONSTRUCTION, independent of whether calcRetention is factor-robust (it is, via %in% coercion ‚Äî verified by reading R/calcRetention.R:26-44, but not relied upon). The single guard: parameterized by caller (default "calcFEFG") so each function keeps its byte-identical message ("calcFE requires complete parentage (no partial parentage): ‚Ä¶"). Used caller, NOT .caller ‚Äî a leading dot trips object_name_linter (.lintr styles snake/Camel/camel; cf.¬Ýkinship.R:69‚Äôs # nolint). TDD trail: RED ‚Äî tests/testthat/test_calcFounderContributions.R (3 test_that, 10 expectations): helper returns list(p, ped) with 1/sum(p^2)==2.9090909091 + character ped; identical p for factor vs char ped; guard stops naming the caller. All failed could not find function ‚Äî the genuine RED for a pure refactor (the cross-function characterization calcFE‚â°FEFG$FE already PASSES, so it can‚Äôt be RED; Learning #17c). GREEN ‚Äî R/calcFounderContributions.R (@noRd); document() (no NAMESPACE/man change); new file passes; full suite 0/0/2001; committed BEFORE the multi-file refactor (SAFEGUARDS). REFACTOR ‚Äî thinned the 3 functions to wrappers. Verification (gold standard): captured a pre-refactor reference at the GREEN commit, then proved every output byte-identical(): FE/FG on lacy1989Ped (character AND factor), the full set.seed(42L) reportGV(...) object (the only live calcFEFG caller, reportGV.R:133), and all 3 partial-parentage messages; + cross-consistency calcFE‚â°FEFG$FE, calcFG‚â°FEFG$FG. Full suite under load_all 0 failed / 0 error / 2001 passed (S16‚Äôs 1991 + 10 new). Lint net-zero (the object_usage finding on the 3 wrappers is the Learning #16c transient ‚Äî 0 under load_all/reinstall). document() no churn. An independent 3-agent adversarial-equivalence workflow (wf_8c251475, agentType=Explore): static body-diff vs 022afc8b ‚Äñ 20 empirical OLD-vs-NEW edge tests (transcribing the originals via git show) ‚Äñ contract/guard/namespace ‚Äî all ok=true, 0 divergences. Key files: NEW R/calcFounderContributions.R (@noRd, returns list(p, ped)); R/calcFE.R:43-47 (wrapper), R/calcFG.R:55-63 (wrapper, calcRetention(fc$ped, ‚Ä¶)), R/calcFEFG.R:45-53 (wrapper). Tests: tests/testthat/test_calcFounderContributions.R (NEW, 10 expectations). Docs: NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd (GV bullet), CHANGELOG.md (S17 entry), BACKLOG.md (NEW-13/23 removed, NEW-22/30 added as the now-trivial follow-on), CLAUDE.md (Learning #17). Throwaway (/tmp, NOT committed): new13_ref_capture.R, new13_compare.R, new13_ref.rds. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. A behavior-preserving refactor‚Äôs real risk is the WIRING you don‚Äôt relocate, not the body. Here calcFG/calcFEFG fed the toCharacter-coerced ped to calcRetention; the helper returns $ped to keep that identical-by-construction. When extracting a shared body, audit every value the OLD code passed downstream that the extraction would change. (‚Üí Learning #17b.) 2. A literal ‚Äúdelegate to X‚Äù audit item can be unimplementable ‚Äî check signature asymmetry. calcFE has no alleles; surfacing the approach fork as a pre-RED AskUserQuestion was correct. (‚Üí Learning #17a.) 3. calcFounderContributions is @noRd (internal) ‚Äî not exported, no .Rd. Its caller arg ONLY phrases the guard message; it returns list(p, ped). 4. Suggested next = NEW-22/NEW-30 (now trivial ‚Äî the / 2L ¬Ω-factor + dead vars live once in calcFounderContributions.R; ¬Ω also in kinship.R:98-99) OR the test-infra debt (create_test_app) OR NEW-12/XARCH-3 (planning). Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1‚Äì#38) still open. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase; gated all three transitions (AskUserQuestion) + the separate pre-RED approach fork; RED failed for the right reason (could not find function), GREEN minimal + committed before the multi-file change, REFACTOR proven identical(). (+) Caught the load-bearing wiring risk (calcRetention‚Äôs ped type) by reading the downstream BEFORE designing, and chose identical-by-construction (return list(p, ped)) over relying on factor-robustness. (+) Behavior-preservation proven to the gold standard ‚Äî identical() on char+factor + the full seeded reportGV() object + the 3 messages, then an independent adversarial workflow returned 0 divergences. (+) Lint net-zero with the object_usage transient correctly diagnosed (Learning #16c), and caught the .caller‚Üícaller naming lint before it landed. (+) Right-sized: deterministic identical() was conclusive; ran the adversarial workflow as CONFIRMATION under ultracode (the user opted in via the gate), not as discovery. (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: GREEN committed before the 3-file refactor; left the pre-existing artifacts (.DS_Store, ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_*.html) untouched. (‚àí) Did not run full devtools::check() (e2e create_test_app baseline noise ‚Äî S7‚ÄìS16 precedent); mitigated by test_dir 0/0 + lint + document() + the identical() proofs + the adversarial workflow. (‚àí) Phase 3E: behavior-preserving compute refactor ‚Äî the only live path (reportGV‚ÜícalcFEFG) verified identical() on the full SEEDED object; no app-startup/registration/dispatch/config changed, so a live-app launch does not apply. Stated, not silently skipped ‚Äî NOT FM #24. (‚àí) Several user interactions (approach fork + detail + 3 phase gates) ‚Äî all mandated by strict TDD + an author approach decision; the recurring minor.

Session 15 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 16)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: S15‚Äôs ACTIVE-TASK suggested-next named PED-1/NEW-17 with the single most load-bearing gotcha ‚Äî ‚Äúdo NOT naively unify the descendants lines: calcRetention.R:27 filters by ped$population, the calc* copies do not.‚Äù That was directly operative: I left calcRetention.R:27 untouched while swapping line 26, exactly as warned. The user picked PED-1 and it scoped instantly. - What helped (reflexes transferred): S15‚Äôs gotcha #1 (ALWAYS run test_dir under pkgload::load_all(".")) was applied on every suite run ‚Äî zero phantom-flood this session. S15‚Äôs gotcha #3 (prove a behavior-preserving refactor with identical() vs a captured reference, not just ‚Äúsuite passes‚Äù) was the template for my entire REFACTOR verification ‚Äî extended here to a seeded reportGV() full-object compare. - What was a partial mismatch (expected, not a deficiency): S15‚Äôs handoff was bug-fix-centric (reproduce-the-failure, ‚Äúaudit mechanism/fix can be wrong‚Äù, RED-on-divergent-input). Same per-deliverable mismatch every session notes. - What was missing (minor): nothing warned that grep founder would MISS the modSummaryStats sites (named males/females, not founders) ‚Äî I caught them via the broad is.na(...sire/dam...) sweep. And the handoff didn‚Äôt enumerate the 12 sites (expected ‚Äî a handoff points, the session inventories). - What was wrong: nothing. file:line, the descendants gotcha, and the suggested-next pointer all checked out. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the accurate pointer + the descendants gotcha prevented a wrong unification, and the load_all/identical() reflexes shaped a clean verification.

What Session 16 Did

Deliverable: PED-1 / NEW-17 ‚Äî extract two exported helpers isFounder(ped) / getFounders(ped) and de-duplicate the inline founder predicate across the codebase, under strict TDD (one RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR cycle, REFACTOR committed in 2 checkpointed batches). (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-01. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 2758ffe6 (GREEN: helpers + tests + NAMESPACE + 2 man), 77f13d51 (REFACTOR Batch A), a95828d6 (REFACTOR Batch B), + this close-out (docs:). Nature (NOT a bug fix): the campaign‚Äôs first pure-refactor deliverable ‚Äî a fix-all-instances DRY cleanup, no behavior change. The founder predicate (both parents unknown) was hand-written inline at 12 sites across 9 files; now isFounder(ped) <- is.na(ped$sire) & is.na(ped$dam) and getFounders(ped) <- ped$id[isFounder(ped)] hold it once. Decisions (USER, package author): (1) scope = all ped-object sites (12 substitutions; findPedigreeNumber excluded), over GV-compute-only or a dual-signature variant; (2) both helpers @exported (consistent with the package‚Äôs public pedigree-utility family); (3) the user asked for more problem/solution detail before choosing scope ‚Äî supplied a before/after table, then re-posed the scope question. The inventory (how it was settled): grep founder found 10 sites; the broad grep is.na(...sire/dam...) sweep caught 2 more (modSummaryStats.R:606/616, variables males/females). Separated the true idiom from false positives (roxygen #' examples, partial-parentage xor/|/exactly-one-parent, hasBothParents, birth/sex is.na). No non-is.na founder idiom exists (gen==0 etc. absent). The 4 call-site shapes: ids (ped$id[mask] √ó6 ‚Üí getFounders), rows (ped[mask,] reportGV ‚Üí isFounder), rows+sex (ped[sex & mask,] modSummaryStats √ó2 ‚Üí isFounder), counts (sum(mask[, na.rm]) modORIPReporting √ó4 ‚Üí isFounder). TDD trail: RED ‚Äî tests/testthat/test_isFounder.R (8 assertions) + test_getFounders.R (6) using a lacy fixture, a partial-parentage fixture (proves partial parentage is NOT a founder), all/none/empty edges, type guarantees, and getFounders(ped) ‚â° ped$id[isFounder(ped)]; all FAILED with could not find function. GREEN ‚Äî R/isFounder.R + R/getFounders.R (roxygen + @export + @examples), devtools::document() (NAMESPACE +2 exports, 2 new .Rd); both files green; full suite 0/0/1991; committed before the multi-file refactor (SAFEGUARDS). REFACTOR ‚Äî 12 single-expression swaps in 2 batches (A: calc*+orderReport, 5 files; B: reportGV+removeUninformativeFounders+2 Shiny modules, 4 files). Verification (gold-standard): captured a pre-refactor reference at the committed GREEN state, then proved every refactored output byte-identical(): calcFE/calcFG/calcFEFG/calcRetention on lacy1989; the full reportGV() object under set.seed(42L) (so the deterministic founder path is verified inside the stochastic gene-drop); the Shiny-module expressions (ped[sex & isFounder,], sum(isFounder(ped)[& sex])) on qcPed. Full suite under load_all = 0 failed / 0 error / 1991 passed, zero non-e2e offenders, only the 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings. New @examples run clean. Lint net-zero, proven rigorously (see gotcha #2). An independent 4-angle completeness-sweep workflow (wf_553e1ce4-389) re-derived the inventory; all 4 finders converged on the lone intentional exclusion findPedigreeNumber.R:35 ‚Üí 0 missed R/ sites, 0 out-of-scope inst/ sites. Key files: NEW R/isFounder.R, R/getFounders.R (+ man/isFounder.Rd, man/getFounders.Rd, NAMESPACE). Refactored: R/calcFE.R:52, R/calcFEFG.R:54, R/calcFG.R:64, R/calcRetention.R:26 (‚öÝ L27 untouched), R/orderReport.R:29, R/removeUninformativeFounders.R:40, R/reportGV.R:136, R/modSummaryStats.R:606/616, R/modORIPReporting.R:185/186/187/318 (counts; was 185-189/320 pre-collapse). Tests: test_isFounder.R, test_getFounders.R. Docs: NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd (‚ÄúPedigree curation‚Äù), CHANGELOG.md (S16), BACKLOG.md (PED-1 removed). Reference RDS (throwaway, /tmp/ped1_ref.rds, NOT committed). GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. Inventory a fix-all-instances refactor by the IDIOM, not the concept‚Äôs NAME ‚Äî grep founder missed modSummaryStats:606/616 (named males/females); the is.na(...sire/dam...) predicate sweep caught all. Filter false positives (roxygen, xor/| partial-parentage). (‚Üí Learning #16a.) 2. object_usage_linter ‚Äúno visible global function definition for ‚ÄòisFounder‚Äô/‚ÄògetFounders‚Äô‚Äù is the STALE-INSTALLED-NAMESPACE transient (Learning #7) ‚Äî and the Learning-#7 /tmp-copy method is INVALID for it (linting outside the package dir loses both .lintr and the namespace: HEAD-in-/tmp = 103 lints vs 21 in-package). Authoritative: R CMD INSTALL then re-lint (warnings vanish ‚Üí 0), and/or HEAD~1-vs-HEAD lint counts in-place (both Shiny modules = 60/17 at each ‚Üí 0 added). (‚Üí Learning #16c.) 3. findPedigreeNumber.R:35 is the one intentional exclusion (bare id/sire/dam vectors, no ped object). A future ‚ÄúPED-1 follow-on‚Äù could give the helpers a dual signature to cover it ‚Äî author opt-in. 4. Suggested next = NEW-13/NEW-23 ‚Äî calcFE/calcFG delegate to calcFEFG + collapse the triplicated S7 partial-parentage guard. Now low-risk post-PED-1: the three calc* share an IDENTICAL getFounders(ped)+descendants+matrix body; only FE-vs-FG output differs. Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1‚Äì#38) still open. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase; gated all three transitions (PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR) via AskUserQuestion, plus the separate pre-RED scope+export author decisions (one re-posed with a before/after table at the user‚Äôs request). RED failed for the right reason (could not find function); GREEN minimal; REFACTOR proven identical(). (+) Recon completeness win ‚Äî the broad predicate sweep caught the 2 modSummaryStats sites that grep founder missed; separated true idiom from false positives. (+) Behavior-preservation proven to the gold standard ‚Äî identical() on real fixtures incl.¬Ýthe full SEEDED reportGV() object, not just ‚Äúsuite passes‚Äù (S15 Learning #15c, extended). (+) Lint net-zero proven rigorously ‚Äî caught my OWN methodology error (the /tmp lint comparison is confounded by missing .lintr+namespace), corrected via reinstall + HEAD~1 in-place, and correctly diagnosed the object_usage warnings as the Learning-#7 transient rather than ‚Äúfixing‚Äù them. (+) Right-sized orchestration under ultracode ‚Äî ran a genuine 4-angle completeness-sweep workflow (the canonical fix-all-instances shape; distinct from the prior 6 single-fn fixes‚Äô solo grep), which independently confirmed 0 misses. (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: committed GREEN before the 9-file refactor; batched REFACTOR ‚â§5 files/commit; left the pre-existing uncommitted artifacts (.DS_Store, ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) untouched. (‚àí) Did not run full devtools::check() (e2e create_test_app baseline noise ‚Äî S7‚ÄìS15 precedent); mitigated by test_dir 0/0 + reinstall + lint + document() + @examples + the identical() proofs + the completeness sweep. (‚àí) Phase 3E: the two refactored Shiny modules‚Äô founder logic was verified at the EXPRESSION level (identical() on qcPed), not by launching the live app ‚Äî appropriate for a behavior-preserving expression swap inside download/render handlers (no app-startup/registration/dispatch/config behavior changed). Stated, not silently skipped ‚Äî NOT FM #24. (‚àí) Several user interactions (2 scope/detail rounds + export + 3 phase gates) ‚Äî all mandated by strict TDD + an exported-API author decision; the recurring minor.

Session 14 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 15)

Every detail checked out ‚Äî the user picked NEW-16 and it scoped the session instantly. - What helped (reflex): the carried audit-fix template (Learnings #8a/#9 ‚Äî ‚Äúempirically reproduce; the audit‚Äôs mechanism AND fix can both be wrong‚Äù). This time ‚Äî as with NEW-45 ‚Äî the audit was CORRECT on both counts; the verify-first reflex still paid off by redirecting attention to the REAL risk: the existing test silently passing on buggy output (the NEW-45 C.003 pattern). Probing the real data (5/153 rows change; row-10 unaffected) before writing RED was decisive ‚Äî it proved a naive RED on the existing fixture would not fail. - What was a partial mismatch (expected, not a deficiency): S14‚Äôs gotchas were duplicate/crash-centric (probe past precursor warnings; doubly-masked reachability). NEW-16 is neither a crash nor a reachability-masking item ‚Äî it‚Äôs a value mislabel with trivial reachability (exported; called in 1 vignette/1 test/1 dev script). Those specific gotchas didn‚Äôt apply, but the deeper reproduce/probe-first reflex did. Both surfaced via the probe / a BACKLOG read. Reasonable ‚Äî S14 wasn‚Äôt working NEW-16. - What was wrong: nothing. The file:line, both mechanism aspects, and the suggested-next pointer were all exact. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the accurate pointer + the verify-first reflex scoped the work and prevented a non-discriminating RED test.

What Session 15 Did

Deliverable: NEW-16 ‚Äî fix summarizeKinshipValues() so the secondQuartile column is the lower hinge (fivenum()[2]) not the minimum (fivenum()[1]), and refactor the O(n¬≤) rbind-in-loop to a single bind, under strict TDD (one RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR cycle). Plus a baseline housekeeping commit and recording the author‚Äôs NEW-20 retention decision. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-01. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 926f4606 (style: dashboard comment realign = clean baseline), dd89998c (fix: code+test+roxygen/man+NEWS/CHANGELOG/BACKLOG), + this close-out (docs:). The bug (audit mechanism AND fix BOTH correct ‚Äî the NEW-45 case, 2nd time): R/summarizeKinshipValues.R:105-106 assigned both min and secondQuartile to tukeys[1L]. fivenum() returns c(min, lower-hinge, median, upper-hinge, max); the sibling columns (median = tukeys[3L], thirdQuartile = tukeys[4L], max = tukeys[5L]) confirm the intended positional mapping, so secondQuartile should be tukeys[2L] ‚Äî it was silently duplicating min. Empirically confirmed: fivenum(1:5) = 1,2,3,4,5; synthetic input ‚Üí buggy secondQuartile=1, fixed =2. Real-data impact: 5 of 153 rows in the seeded example pipeline change (first is row 82). The existing test_‚Ä¶:75 asserts secondQuartile[10]==0 and still passes after the fix because that row‚Äôs distribution (0,0,0,0,0,0,.25,.25,.25,.25) has lower hinge = min = 0 ‚Äî i.e.¬Ýit was passing on corrupted output (NEW-45 C.003 pattern), which is why the RED test needed a divergent synthetic input. Reachability (trivial ‚Äî NOT a masking/crash question): summarizeKinshipValues is @exported and called directly in vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd, tests/testthat/test_summarizeKinshipValues.R, and the dev script inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R. A mislabel produces wrong-but-non-crashing output at every call site ‚Äî no upstream guard masks it. grep+probe-settled ‚Üí honest no workflow (6th consecutive). Decisions (USER, package author): (1) scope = correctness + perf refactor (the complete NEW-16), (2) handle the pre-existing uncommitted dashboard whitespace by committing it (clean baseline; file kept), (3) NEW-20 = retain makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R as early-development work (do not delete) ‚Äî already .Rbuildignore‚Äôd at L25. The fix: (correctness) R/summarizeKinshipValues.R:106 tukeys[1L] ‚Üí tukeys[2L]. (perf) collect each qualifying row‚Äôs data.frame into a preallocated list (rows <- vector("list", n); skipped/NA rows stay NULL), then stats <- do.call(rbind, rows) once; rbind drops the NULL gaps, and an all-skipped input yields NULL ‚Üí guarded back to data.frame() (empty contract preserved). (doc) roxygen @return now states secondQuartile/thirdQuartile are the lower/upper hinges. TDD trail: RED ‚Äî one test_that("‚Ä¶second quartile is the lower hinge, not the minimum (NEW-16)") in tests/testthat/test_summarizeKinshipValues.R using synthetic kValues=c(1,2,3,4,5)/kCounts=rep(1L,5); asserts secondQuartile==2 + min/median/thirdQuartile/max==1/3/4/5 + secondQuartile!=min. The secondQuartile==2 and !=min assertions FAILED for the right reason (actual 1=min) while the 4 already-correct columns PASSED ‚Äî proving the test sound, not broken. GREEN ‚Äî the one-line tukeys[2L] swap; the NEW-16 block passes and the existing row-10 line-75 assertion stays valid (verified unchanged). REFACTOR ‚Äî bind-once + roxygen; proven byte-identical() to a pre-refactor reference on the seeded pipeline, the synthetic input, AND the all-skipped/empty case. Verification: full suite via test_dir WITH pkgload::load_all(".") = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1977 passed (= S14‚Äôs 1971 + exactly the 6 new assertions); zero non-e2e offenders; only the 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings (my change trips no existing test, adds no warning). Lint net-zero (file not in .lintr exclusions; 0 before and after the roxygen edit, all roxygen lines ‚â§80 chars). devtools::document() regenerated only man/summarizeKinshipValues.Rd, no NAMESPACE change. @example pipeline runs clean (153√ó9, correct names). No full devtools::check() (e2e create_test_app baseline noise ‚Äî S7/S9/S11/S13/S14 precedent); mitigated by the above + the identical() proof. Key files: R/summarizeKinshipValues.R:106 (the fix) + the bind-once loop (L88-119) + roxygen @return hinge note (~L17-22). tests/testthat/test_summarizeKinshipValues.R (NEW-16 block, ~L83-101). man/summarizeKinshipValues.Rd (regen). NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd (new ‚ÄúGenetic value analysis‚Äù bullet), CHANGELOG.md (S15 entry), BACKLOG.md (NEW-20 removed; suggested-next updated). Reference RDS (throwaway, /tmp, NOT committed): new16_ref.rds. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. ‚öÝ ALWAYS run testthat::test_dir(...) under pkgload::load_all("."). A bare Rscript -e 'test_dir(...)' runs against the stale installed namespace, not your source ‚Äî it produced a PHANTOM 46-failed/68-error flood across ~40 untouched files (every prior session‚Äôs fix missing from the install) and inverted my own change. TELL: failures in files you never touched = environment/loading, not a regression. (‚Üí Learning #15b.) 2. When the audit mechanism AND fix are both correct (NEW-45/NEW-16), the trap is a pre-existing test PASSING ON BUGGY OUTPUT. Probe real data first; design the RED test on an input where buggy‚âÝcorrect. (‚Üí Learning #15a.) 3. Prove a behavior-preserving refactor with identical() vs a captured reference, not just ‚Äúthe suite still passes‚Äù; preserve the empty-input contract (do.call(rbind, all-NULL)‚ÜíNULL‚Üídata.frame()). (‚Üí Learning #15c.) 4. NEW-20 is CLOSED as won‚Äôt-delete ‚Äî do NOT delete R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R (author retains it as early-dev work; already .Rbuildignore‚Äôd L25, defines no live function). The audit‚Äôs ‚Äúdelete dead code‚Äù no longer applies. Note getProportionLow remains its only (dead) consumer ‚Äî NEW-25/NEW-20 entanglement (S8 gotcha) persists but both are retained. 5. Suggested next = PED-1/NEW-17 (extract getFounders/isFounder ‚Äî ‚öÝ don‚Äôt unify the descendants lines; calcRetention.R:27 filters by population) or NEW-13/NEW-23 (calcFE/FG ‚Üí calcFEFG + collapse the triplicated S7 guard). Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1‚Äì#38) still open. 6. summarizeKinshipValues naming oddity (NOT a bug, out of scope): the columns named secondQuartile/thirdQuartile hold the lower/upper hinges (‚âà Q1/Q3) ‚Äî the names track the 2nd/4th element of the five-number summary, not ‚ÄúQ2/Q3‚Äù. Renaming would be a breaking API change (vignette + tests reference them by name); left as-is. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase; gated all three transitions (PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR) via AskUserQuestion plus the separate pre-RED scope+housekeeping decision; the RED assertions failed for the RIGHT reason while the 4 already-correct columns + both existing blocks passed. (+) Caught and corrected my OWN tooling error ‚Äî the first test_dir (no load_all) showed 46-failed/68-error; I diagnosed it as the stale-installed-namespace artifact (not a regression) in ~1 min and re-ran correctly to 0/0/1977. Honest adversarial self-check, and it became Learning #15b. (+) Proved the refactor behaviour-preserving via identical() on three representative inputs incl.¬Ýthe empty edge ‚Äî gold standard beyond ‚Äúsuite passes.‚Äù (+) Verified the non-discriminating-existing-test trap by probing real data BEFORE writing RED, so the RED genuinely fails. (+) Right-sized: deterministic single-file fix, grep/probe-settled ‚Üí honest no workflow (ultracode‚Äôs ‚Äúunless already verified‚Äù). (+) Recorded the user‚Äôs NEW-20 retention decision accurately across BACKLOG/CHANGELOG/handoff ‚Äî prevents a future session deleting the file (FM #22 territory). (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: committed-clean baseline before starting, staged the fix explicitly (7 files, no .DS_Store), lint net-zero, clean document() with no NAMESPACE churn. (‚àí) Did not run full devtools::check() (e2e baseline noise ‚Äî established precedent); mitigated by test_dir 0/0 + lint + document() + @example + the identical() proof. (‚àí) Phase 3E: this is a compute-function output fix ‚Äî no app-startup/registration/dispatch/config behaviour changed, so a live-app launch does not apply; verified at the function/test level (RED proved the bug, GREEN the fix, identical() the refactor). Stated, not silently skipped ‚Äî NOT FM #24. (‚àí) My initial test_dir invocation error cost one verification round (caught fast). (‚àí) Several user interactions (scope+housekeeping bundle + 3 phase gates) ‚Äî all mandated by strict TDD; the recurring minor.

Session 13 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 14)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: the carried audit-fix template (Learnings #8a/#9 ‚Äî ‚Äúempirically reproduce the ACTUAL failure mode; the audit‚Äôs stated mechanism AND its prescribed fix can both be wrong‚Äù) was decisive again. NEW-46‚Äôs audit text (‚Äúparent lookup by rowname; duplicate ids ‚Üí wrong values‚Äù, geneDrop.R:82-104) was empirically a hard CRASH at rownames(ped) <- ids (geneDrop.R:97) ‚Äî the NEW-48 pattern repeating. Without the reproduce-first reflex I‚Äôd have written a RED test asserting silent corruption that never happens. - What helped (pointer accuracy): S13‚Äôs ACTIVE-TASK + gotcha #5 named NEW-46 as suggested-next with the exact location and the ‚Äúsibling of NEW-45 in consensus issue #7‚Äù framing; the user picked it and it scoped the session instantly. The R/geneDrop.R:76-80 reference (the NEW-45 period guard) was accurate ‚Äî I placed the new dup guard right beside it. - What was a partial mismatch (expected, not a deficiency): S13‚Äôs NEW-45 narrative was domain-sweep-centric (‚Äúwhen the audit mechanism is CORRECT, the live decision is a DOMAIN question‚Äù). NEW-46 was the inverse: the audit mechanism was WRONG again (crash, not corruption), and the domain was unambiguous (no sweep needed ‚Äî kinship() and removeDuplicates() already enforce uniqueness). So gotcha #1‚Äôs domain guidance didn‚Äôt apply, but the deeper ‚Äúreproduce first‚Äù reflex did. Same class of expected per-item mismatch every session notes. - What was slightly off (minor): gotcha #5 echoed the audit‚Äôs ‚Äúduplicate-id rowname lookup‚Äù framing ‚Äî but the crash is at the rownames(ped) <- ids assignment (L97), not the lookup (L118-119). A one-line ‚Äúverify the crash SITE ‚Äî the audit may finger the wrong line‚Äù would have pre-warned; but S13 was faithfully relaying the audit and hadn‚Äôt worked NEW-46. Caught in ~2 min by the backtrace. - What was wrong: nothing. Every commit hash, file:line, and the suggested-next pointer checked out. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the template prevented a wrong test; the accurate pointer scoped the work immediately.

What Session 14 Did

Deliverable: NEW-46 ‚Äî make geneDrop() reject duplicate animal IDs with a clear stop(), under strict TDD (one RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR cycle). (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-06-01. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: fix: (guard + test + roxygen/man + NEWS/CHANGELOG/BACKLOG) + this close-out (docs:). The bug (audit mechanism WRONG ‚Äî the NEW-48 pattern, Learning #8a): the audit (geneDrop.R:82-104) called NEW-46 ‚Äúparent lookup by rowname; duplicate ids ‚Üí wrong values.‚Äù Empirically it is a hard CRASH at R/geneDrop.R:97 rownames(ped) <- ids: R‚Äôs .rowNamesDF<- rejects duplicate row.names (a precursor warning "non-unique value when setting 'row.names'", then a hard error "duplicate 'row.names' are not allowed") ‚Äî it does not silently mangle. The crash happens before the ‚Äúparent lookup by rowname‚Äù (L118-119) and list-keying (L117) the audit fingered, so those downstream mechanisms are preempted/unreachable. Reproduced via /tmp/new46_probe*.R. METHOD TRAP recorded (‚Üí Learning #14a): probe v1 used tryCatch(expr, warning=, error=) ‚Äî the warning= handler short-circuited on the precursor warning and hid the terminal error (read as ‚Äúwarning‚Äù); probe v2 (suppress/let-pass the warning) revealed the hard error. Always probe PAST a precursor warning. Reachability ‚Äî DOUBLY-masked; direct-geneDrop()-call only (grep + deterministic probe; NO workflow): - Canonical qcStudbook ‚Üí reportGV ‚Üí geneDrop path = MASKED TWICE. qcStudbook calls removeDuplicates (qcStudbook.R:277) which unique()-collapses identical rows and stop()s on mismatched dup ids; AND kinship() ‚Äî called at reportGV.R:81, before the geneDrop call at reportGV.R:92 ‚Äî already guards with stop("All id values must be unique"). So a non-qc‚Äôd dup-id ped passed to reportGV crashes at kinship with a clear message, never reaching geneDrop‚Äôs L97. - Scope NARROWED to geneDrop-only by the grep, not widened (contrast NEW-53). A rownames(x) <- ‚Ä¶ inventory found a sibling site reportGV.R:122 rownames(ped) <- ped$id, but kinship (L81) crashes first ‚Üí it‚Äôs unreachable-with-dups ‚Üí no reportGV change needed. The grep proved scope rather than my guessing (NEW-53‚Äôs grep widened scope to 3 sites; here it narrowed to 1). The contract decision (USER, package author): reject with a clear stop() ‚Äî over silent-dedup and over ‚Äútolerate exact-dup, stop on mismatched.‚Äù Rationale (Learning #8b): today‚Äôs behavior is already a crash, so a clear stop() is contract-preserving (no currently-succeeding call changes; only the cryptic message improves) ‚Äî the NEW-48/NEW-45-reject side. The unique-id domain was unambiguous (unlike NEW-45‚Äôs sweep) because two sibling functions (kinship, removeDuplicates) already enforce it; kinship‚Äôs "All id values must be unique" supplied the message precedent. The fix (minimal, 4 lines): at R/geneDrop.R:96-100, immediately after the NEW-45 period guard: dupIds <- duplicated(as.character(ids)); if (any(dupIds)) stop("geneDrop(): animal IDs must be unique; ", "duplicated id(s): ", toString(unique(as.character(ids)[dupIds]))). Fires before rownames(ped) <- ids, so no precursor warning either. Style mirrors the adjacent period guard (toString/unique/as.character). TDD trail: RED ‚Äî one test_that("geneDrop rejects duplicate animal IDs (NEW-46)") in tests/testthat/test_geneDrop.R (after the NEW-45 block): expect_error(‚Ä¶, "must be unique") for (1) a mismatched dup id and (2) an exact-duplicate row (pinning reject-ALL-dups), + a no-false-positive guard (unique ids c("A","B","C") still return a data.frame with those ids). The 2 dup assertions FAILED for the right reason ‚Äî backtrace pinned geneDrop.R:97:3 rownames<-/.rowNamesDF<-, actual message "duplicate 'row.names' are not allowed" ‚âÝ "must be unique" ‚Äî while the guard PASSED. GREEN ‚Äî the 4-line guard; all 15 geneDrop tests pass, precursor warnings gone. REFACTOR (doc-only) ‚Äî roxygen unique-id paragraph + @param ids ‚Äúunique‚Äù; regen man/geneDrop.Rd; NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd/CHANGELOG.md bullets; removed NEW-46 from BACKLOG.md. Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1971 passed (+4 = exactly the new expectations; S13 ended 1967); zero non-e2e offenders; only the 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings (my stop() trips no existing test ‚Äî Learning #6 corollary clear). Lint net-zero: the one object_usage_linter warning on hasInvalidIdChar (now L92) is pre-existing from S13, HEAD-verified via scoped git stash (same warning at HEAD‚Äôs L86 ‚Äî it‚Äôs the stale installed-namespace not yet having the @noRd validator; Learning #7). devtools::document() regenerated only man/geneDrop.Rd, no NAMESPACE change. geneDrop @example runs clean (14 rows each). Key files: R/geneDrop.R:96-100 (the guard) + roxygen L34-39 (unique-id note) + L41 (@param ids). tests/testthat/test_geneDrop.R (NEW-46 block, ~L119-161). man/geneDrop.Rd (regen). NEWS.md / NEWS.Rmd / CHANGELOG.md (NEW-46 entry). BACKLOG.md (NEW-46 removed). Throwaway probes (/tmp, NOT committed): new46_probe.R, new46_probe2.R, new46_probe3.R. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. The audit‚Äôs ‚Äúduplicate/corruption‚Äù claim was a hard CRASH again (3rd time after NEW-48/NEW-25). R rejects duplicate data.frame row.names ‚Äî any rownames(x) <- <non-unique vector> idiom CRASHES, it does not mangle. When reproducing, probe PAST any precursor warning (a tryCatch with both warning= and error= hides the error after a warning). (‚Üí Learning #14a.) 2. Reachability can be DOUBLY-masked. geneDrop‚Äôs dup-id crash is unreachable through the pipeline because BOTH removeDuplicates (qcStudbook) AND kinship‚Äôs own guard (reportGV.R:81, before geneDrop) catch it. Trace all upstream guards before declaring an item reachable. (‚Üí Learning #14b.) 3. A fix-all grep can NARROW scope by proving a sibling site unreachable (here reportGV.R:122), not only widen it (NEW-53). Run the idiom inventory either way. (‚Üí Learning #14b.) 4. geneDrop now has TWO upfront input guards (period hasInvalidIdChar, then duplicate-id). They share the toString(unique(as.character(ids)[mask])) message idiom ‚Äî keep new id-validation guards consistent. 5. Suggested next = NEW-20 (delete dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R + its fully-commented test ‚Äî @noRd/no live fn) or NEW-16 (summarizeKinshipValues.R:105-106 ‚Äî secondQuartile mislabeled as the minimum, fivenum()[1] should be [2]; + rbind-in-loop O(n¬≤)). Remaining audit items + traps in ACTIVE TASK below. Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1‚Äì#38) still open. 6. geneDrop.R carries 1 pre-existing lint (object_usage_linter on the @noRd hasInvalidIdChar, from the stale installed namespace) ‚Äî it resolves once the package is reinstalled; don‚Äôt ‚Äúfix‚Äù it. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase, gated all three transitions via AskUserQuestion + the separate pre-RED contract decision; the 2 RED assertions failed for the RIGHT reason (backtrace-confirmed crash site) while the no-false-positive guard stayed green. (+) Empirically OVERTURNED the audit on BOTH counts (crash not corruption; site is rownames<- not the lookup) ‚Äî and caught my own probe-v1 error (the warning-handler masked the terminal error) by probing past the warning in v2; honest adversarial self-correction (the highest-value move). (+) grep+probe NARROWED scope to geneDrop-only by proving the reportGV sibling unreachable (kinship guards it first) ‚Äî evidence over guessing. (+) Right-sized orchestration: mechanism (deterministic base-R crash) + reachability (double-masked) + domain (two sibling guards) all ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù ‚Üí honest no-workflow (5th consecutive; ultracode‚Äôs ‚Äúunless already verified‚Äù). A fan-out would only re-run a deterministic probe. (+) HEAD-verified the lint is pre-existing (Learning #7) rather than assuming. (+) Contract-preserving stop() per Learning #8b, mirroring kinship‚Äôs message. (‚àí) Did not run full devtools::check() (e2e create_test_app baseline noise ‚Äî S7/S9/S11/S13 precedent); mitigated by test_dir 0/0 + net-zero lint + clean document() + @example run. (‚àí) Phase 3E: this is an input-validation guard on a compute function ‚Äî no app-startup/registration/dispatch/config behavior changed, so a live-browser launch does not apply; verified at the function/test level (RED proved the crash, GREEN proved the guard). NOT FM #24 ‚Äî stated, not silently skipped. (‚àí) 4 user interactions (1 contract decision + 3 phase gates) ‚Äî all mandated by the strict-TDD convention, the recurring minor.

Session 12 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 13)

Score: 8/10. - Context: S12 was doc-only (it added the AskUserQuestion phase-gate convention). That deliverable was directly operative this session ‚Äî I used a structured AskUserQuestion at every PRE-RED‚ÜíRED / RED‚ÜíGREEN / GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR transition (twice, across two cycles) plus the separate pre-RED scope/approach decisions, exactly as S12 specified. Immediate payoff. - What helped: the standing ACTIVE-TASK block named NEW-45 as the suggested-next code deliverable (the user picked it), and the carried audit-fix template (Learnings #8a/#9/#10/#12 ‚Äî ‚Äúempirically reproduce; the audit‚Äôs mechanism/fix can be wrong‚Äù) set the right reflex: I reproduced the bug first. - What was a partial mismatch (expected, not a deficiency): the reachability guidance is qcStudbook-masker-centric and value-domain-centric. NEW-45 was neither ‚Äî it was a DOMAIN-DEFINITION question (does the ID domain permit .?), a new class (‚Üí Learning #13). And ‚Äî first in the campaign ‚Äî the audit‚Äôs stated mechanism was CORRECT.
