The verify-first reflex still paid off: it sent me to investigate the domain, which reshaped the fix from ‚Äúsupport periods‚Äù to ‚Äúreject periods.‚Äù - What was missing: nothing flagged the pre-existing test_modGeneticValue special-char test (counter-evidence ‚Äî it was passing on silently-corrupted output) or the Shiny error-renderer display gap; both surfaced only through diligence/Phase 3E. Reasonable ‚Äî S12 was doc-only. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the phase-gate convention structured the entire session; the NEW-45 pointer scoped it fast.

What Session 13 Did

Deliverable: NEW-45 ‚Äî enforce + document the ‚ÄúIDs may not contain a period (.)‚Äù rule, under strict TDD across two RED‚ÜíGREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR cycles. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-31. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 5e228bd9 (fix: enforcement + tests), 2527d6bc (docs: rationale), ceab030f (feat: error-renderer display) + this close-out (docs:). The bug (audit mechanism CORRECT this time): geneDrop rebuilds the id/parent output columns by strsplit(rownames(alleles), ".", fixed=TRUE) then key[1]/key[2], where rownames are <id>.<sire|dam>. An id containing . (e.g.¬Ý"A.1"‚Üí"A.1.sire") splits to c("A","1","sire") ‚Üí id truncated to "A", sire/dam distinction lost. Empirically reproduced (/tmp/new45_probe.R). The KEY decision ‚Äî DOMAIN, not mechanism (Learning #13): the user GATED the ‚Äúfix-to-support periods‚Äù approach (A2) on whether the ID domain precludes .. A read-only multi-modal domain-sweep workflow (wf_21efc084-596; 4 searchers ‚Äî validation-code ‚Äñ docs ‚Äñ separator/format ‚Äñ example-data ‚Äî + adversarial synthesis) returned verdict PRECLUDED, decisive evidence verified firsthand: inst/extdata/ui_guidance/input_format.html:86-97 documents id/sire/dam (and L251 genotype-id) as ‚ÄúAlphanumeric characters (no symbols)‚Äù, live in the app via R/modInput.R:152-153, and the vignette manual_components/_input.Rmd:39-40 names that tab as the authority. Reinforced by deliberate authorial contrast (allele cols L141-150 explicitly allow ‚Äúunderscores, spaces and dashes‚Äù ‚Äî withheld from ids). So the correctness-aligned fix is to ENFORCE the documented-but-unenforced rule (reject), not re-engineer parsing to support .. Caveat the synthesis flagged: the rule was documentation-only (no code enforced it), so enforcing it is a deliberate behavior change ‚Äî which the author (user) signed off. User decisions: (1) approach = B-broad (geneDrop guard + qcStudbook validation), (2) .-only enforcement (NOT full alphanumeric ‚Äî lower risk; real data may legitimately use -/_), (3) document it as a feature enforced at data-input + in all auto-gen IDs (rationale: . breaks across software environments), (4) NEW-46 out of scope, (5) Phase-3E gap ‚Üí extend now to add the error display. Cycle 1 ‚Äî enforcement (5e228bd9 + docs 2527d6bc): - R/hasInvalidIdChar.R (NEW, @noRd): !is.na(ids) & grepl(".", ids, fixed=TRUE) ‚Äî single definition of the rule, reused everywhere (avoids the guard-triplication the audit dings elsewhere). - R/qcStudbook.R:180-189 (after unknown2NA, before addUIds): validates c(id,sire,dam); reportErrors=FALSE ‚Üí stop(), reportErrors=TRUE ‚Üí errorLst$invalidIdChars. (Restructured to satisfy unnecessary_nesting_linter.) - R/geneDrop.R:76-80: stop() guard on as.character(ids) (defense-in-depth ‚Äî the GV Shiny module reaches geneDrop without passing qcStudbook). - R/getEmptyErrorLst.R + R/checkErrorLst.R: add & count the new invalidIdChars field. - Generators (addUIds U####, obfuscateId) already period-free ‚Üí locked with characterization tests (pass before & after). - Two pre-existing tests my change disturbed (Learning #6 corollary, both expected): test_getFocalAnimalPed.R:112 errorLst field-count 9‚Üí10; test_modGeneticValue.R special-char fixture C.003‚ÜíC-003 (it had been passing on silently-corrupted geneDrop output ‚Äî NEW-45 in the wild!) + a new period-reject assertion. - Docs: roxygen on qcStudbook/geneDrop/addUIds/obfuscateId (+ regen those 4 .Rd), the live input_format.html spec (cells + a new <li> rationale note), NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd, CHANGELOG.md. Cycle 2 ‚Äî error display / Phase 3E (ceab030f): the two QC error renderers enumerate fields hardcoded, so the new invalidIdChars wasn‚Äôt shown to users (they‚Äôd see an error state without the reason). Added an invalidIdChars case to R/processQcStudbookResult.R (Shiny error table; toString to avoid adding to its 13 pre-existing lints ‚Äî issue #30) and R/summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R (addErrTxt summary line) + regen processQcStudbookResult.Rd. Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1967 passed (+24 net across both cycles); zero non-e2e offenders; only the 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings. Lint net-zero on every edited file (processQcStudbookResult.R stays at its 13 pre-existing lints). devtools::document() regenerated only the 5 intended .Rd, no NAMESPACE change (hasInvalidIdChar is @noRd). Ran the 4 changed exported functions‚Äô examples clean (check()-equivalent slice). HEAD-baseline-verified both regression offenders via git stash (both passed at HEAD ‚Üí both genuinely mine, both expected). Key files: R/hasInvalidIdChar.R (validator), R/qcStudbook.R:180-189, R/geneDrop.R:76-80, R/getEmptyErrorLst.R (invalidIdChars field), R/checkErrorLst.R, R/processQcStudbookResult.R (period block), R/summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R (addErrTxt). Tests: test_hasInvalidIdChar.R (NEW), test_geneDrop.R, test_qcStudbook.R (pedPeriod fixture), test_addUIds.R/test_obfuscateId.R (guarantees), test_modGeneticValue.R (swap+reject), test_getFocalAnimalPed.R (9‚Üí10), test_modInput_qcStudbook.R + test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R (display). Workflow artifact: ‚Ä¶/tasks/wixjhggi7.output (domain verdict). Probes (throwaway, /tmp, NOT committed): new45_probe.R. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. When the audit mechanism is CORRECT, the live decision often shifts to a DOMAIN-DEFINITION question ‚Äî consult the project‚Äôs own documented spec (here input_format.html, live via modInput) before fix-to-support vs reject. A read-only multi-modal domain sweep is the right tool. (‚Üí Learning #13.) 2. A new errorLst field is a 4-touch change, not 1: add to getEmptyErrorLst + the checkErrorLst count + both hardcoded renderers (processQcStudbookResult.R, summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R) ‚Äî Phase 3E catches the renderer gap. And it trips structure-count tests (getFocalAnimalPed 9‚Üí10). 3. processQcStudbookResult.R has 13 pre-existing lints (issue #30) ‚Äî use toString() (not paste(.., collapse=)) in any new line to avoid adding to the debt. 4. Enforcement is .-only, not full ‚Äúno symbols‚Äù ‚Äî -/_/space// ids still pass (see the test_modGeneticValue fixture). A future session could enforce the full alphanumeric spec, but that‚Äôs a bigger behavior change (would reject dash/underscore ids) ‚Äî its own decision. 5. Suggested next = NEW-46 (geneDrop.R:82-104 duplicate-id rowname lookup ‚Äî the sibling of NEW-45 in consensus issue #7) or NEW-20 (delete dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R). Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub #1‚Äì#38) still open. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end across two cycles ‚Äî declared every phase, gated every transition via AskUserQuestion; all RED tests failed for the right reason with no-false-positive guards passing; HEAD-baseline-verified the two regression offenders rather than assuming (Learning #3). (+) The domain sweep was the highest-value move ‚Äî the user gated A2 on it, and it found the decisive input_format.html spec, correctly pivoting the fix from support‚Üíreject; verified the load-bearing evidence firsthand (Learning #20). (+) Right-sized orchestration: a workflow for the genuine multi-location domain question; grep/probe (no workflow) for the mechanism. (+) Surfaced the counter-evidence (test_modGeneticValue was passing on corrupted output) and the Phase-3E renderer gap honestly rather than silently shipping. (+) DRY ‚Äî one validator, reused at 3 enforcement points + 2 renderers. (+) Lint net-zero by using the codebase-idiomatic toString instead of matching the file‚Äôs dirty paste(collapse=) pattern. (‚àí) Did not run full devtools::check() (e2e create_test_app baseline noise makes a clean signal impractical ‚Äî S7/S9/S11 precedent); mitigated by test_dir 0/0 + lint + clean document() + running the 4 changed examples. NOT FM #24: runtime verified at the testServer level (modGeneticValueServer rejects period-ids; the two renderers display them), though I did not launch a live browser. (‚àí) Many user interactions (multiple gates + clarifications across two cycles + the 3E decision) ‚Äî all warranted under strict TDD + a load-bearing domain decision + an explicit scope extension, but a high count.

What Session 12 Did

Deliverable: Added the TDD Phase-gate format convention to CLAUDE.md‚Äôs Development Process Contract ‚Äî at every phase transition, ask permission via AskUserQuestion (structured), not a prose question. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-31. Branch: add-methodology. Commit: docs: close-out (this entry‚Äôs commit). Why: the user runs a parallel TDD session that gates each transition with a structured AskUserQuestion (option 1 ‚ÄúYes, proceed to ‚Äù enumerating the exact next-phase actions + downstream verify; option 2 ‚ÄúHold / alternative‚Äù; harness auto-adds ‚ÄúOther‚Äù) and asked to adopt it here. In this conversation Session 11 had used prose gates (‚ÄúMay I proceed to GREEN?‚Äù). Decision (USER): gate all three transitions ‚Äî PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR (not just the two source/doc gates). A pre-RED scope/approach decision stays a separate AskUserQuestion. Mechanism note (important for a future session tempted to ‚Äúautomate‚Äù it): this is a followed CLAUDE.md convention, NOT a settings.json hook ‚Äî there is no ‚Äúphase transition‚Äù harness event, and a hook can‚Äôt author context-specific options. Per Learning #11 it lives in CLAUDE.md, never in the synced SESSION_RUNNER.md/SAFEGUARDS.md. Key file: CLAUDE.md ‚ÄúDevelopment Process Contract‚Äù ‚Üí new ‚Äú### Phase-gate format‚Äù subsection (right after Enforcement Rules); the ‚ÄúMUST ask permission before transitioning‚Äù bullet now points to it. Verification: docs-only; no code/tests touched, so no TDD cycle and no suite run (markdown convention ‚Äî nothing to test). CLAUDE.md reads coherently. 3A note: intentionally skipped the formal previous-handoff evaluation ‚Äî Session 11‚Äôs handoff was written minutes earlier in this same conversation, so a fresh-read evaluation would add nothing. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Right-sized a small process change: surfaced the one genuine design choice (which transitions) via AskUserQuestion with preview-rendered convention text, explained why a hook cannot implement it, and kept it in CLAUDE.md per the synced-files rule (Learning #11). (‚àí) Proportionately light close-out (no full 6-part handoff) ‚Äî appropriate for a one-paragraph doc convention, but flagged explicitly so it is not mistaken for protocol erosion (FM #17).

Session 10 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 11)

Score: 9/10. - Context: S10 was a methodology-update session; its handoff pointed the next session at NEW-53 as the suggested next code deliverable, which the user did pick. So unlike the S9‚ÜíS10 pivot, S10‚Äôs forward guidance was directly operative this session. - What helped most: the carried-forward audit-fix template (Learnings #8a/#9 ‚Äî ‚Äúempirically reproduce the ACTUAL failure mode; the audit‚Äôs stated mechanism AND prescribed fix can BOTH be wrong‚Äù) was again decisive. NEW-53‚Äôs audit text claims makeSimPed ‚Äúoverwrites sire/dam‚Äù and createSimKinships ‚Äúadds a population column in place‚Äù; a 2-minute probe disproved both ‚Äî the only real leak is a by-reference data.frame‚Üídata.table CLASS flip ($<- shallow-copies, so content survives). Without the reflex to probe first, I‚Äôd have written a RED test asserting content corruption that never happens. The ACTIVE-TASK pointer named NEW-53, the two files, and the exact mechanism (getPotentialParents.R:28, setDT no copy) accurately. S10‚Äôs GOTCHA #2 (orientation leads with gh issue list; tracker-reconciliation still open) set up Phase 0 correctly. - What was missing (minor): the pointer said ‚Äúfix both together‚Äù (makeSimPed + getPotentialParents) but the audit finding text also names createSimKinships as the same pattern ‚Äî the third sibling wasn‚Äôt flagged. I found it via the grep reference-inventory (workstream Q4) and surfaced the scope decision to the user. A one-line ‚Äúaudit also names createSimKinships‚Äù would have pre-scoped it. - What was a partial mismatch (expected, not a deficiency): the reachability guidance is qcStudbook-masker-centric (addUIds/convertSexCodes/addParents) and value-domain-centric (NEW-52). NEW-53 is neither ‚Äî it‚Äôs a side-effect-on-caller / call-graph-propagation question (does the mutation reach a caller who reuses the object? ‚Äî here cumulateSimKinships insulates it; direct API calls don‚Äôt). Same class of mismatch S7/S8 noted; the generic ‚Äúverify reachability + reproduce mechanism‚Äù still pointed right. - What was wrong: nothing. Every file:line and the mechanism checked out. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the template prevented a wrong test, and the accurate pointer scoped the session immediately.

What Session 11 Did

Deliverable: Fixed NEW-53 (makeSimPed/getPotentialParents/createSimKinships mutate the caller‚Äôs ped by reference) under strict TDD, scope = all three (user decision). (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-31. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 5f40b7af (fix: 3 R files + 3 test files + NAMESPACE); close-out docs in a follow-up docs: commit. The bug (empirically nailed; audit OVER-claimed the harm): each function ran setDT(ped), which converts a data.frame to a data.table by reference ‚Äî silently flipping the caller‚Äôs ped class. The audit said makeSimPed ‚Äúoverwrites sire/dam‚Äù and createSimKinships ‚Äúadds a population column in place‚Äù; both are FALSE ‚Äî the post-setDT ped$col[‚Ä¶] <- v triggers $<-.data.table‚Äôs shallow-copy-and-rebind, decoupling the local ped before any write, so neither content nor new columns reach the caller. The only leak is the class flip. Realized harm = [-semantics divergence in the caller‚Äôs later code (df[1]=column vs dt[1]=ROW; df[,"x"]=vector vs dt[,"x"]=1-col table) ‚Äî an exported-API value-semantics violation, NOT numerical corruption. Reachability ‚Äî verdict (probe + grep settled, NO workflow ‚Äî ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù, 3rd consecutive session): - Pattern inventory (grep): exactly 3 setDT( sites in R/; no other reference-mutation ops (setDF/:=/set(/setattr/setkey/setorder/setcolorder all absent). The complete cluster = makeSimPed.R:25 (guarded), getPotentialParents.R:28 (unconditional), createSimKinships.R:50 (unconditional). - Harm propagation (probe /tmp/new53_probe3.R): cumulateSimKinships (the GV sim pipeline) does NOT leak the flip to its caller ‚Äî its line-52 ped$population <- ‚Ä¶ copies before the loop ‚Äî and its Monte-Carlo result is correct (content preserved across iterations). The realized exposure is direct calls to the three @exported functions. getPotentialParents‚Äôs only in-repo caller is a dev script (inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R); makeSimPed/createSimKinships are GV-sim public functions. - Fix-idiom probe (/tmp/new53_probe2.R): as.data.table() COPIES for both data.frame AND data.table inputs; $<- never leaks a new column to the caller; so the minimal setDT‚Üías.data.table swap is sufficient (no unconditional copy() needed). makeSimPed output byte-identical (s1_1/s2_2/s3_1). Semantic/scope decision (USER, package author): scope = all three (the complete grep-verified cluster), over ‚Äúthe two from the handoff‚Äù or ‚ÄúmakeSimPed only‚Äù ‚Äî leaves no copy of the bug. There was NO stop-vs-warning contract decision (unlike NEW-40/48/52): a pure side-effect removal. The fix (minimal, identical √ó3): setDT(ped) ‚Üí ped <- data.table::as.data.table(ped) at R/makeSimPed.R:25 (inside the existing if (!inherits(ped, "data.table")) guard), R/getPotentialParents.R:28, R/createSimKinships.R:50. Plus roxygen @importFrom data.table setDT‚Üías.data.table √ó3 and NAMESPACE regen (dropped importFrom(data.table,setDT) ‚Äî no function uses setDT anymore; as.data.table was already imported by R/kinshipMatrixToKValues.R). TDD trail: RED ‚Äî one test_that per function asserting the caller‚Äôs class(ped) stays "data.frame" after the call (expect_false(inherits(pedDF,"data.table")) + expect_identical(class(pedDF),"data.frame")), plus an immutable content/structure guard (paste0(‚Ä¶) snapshot ‚Äî NOT before <- ped, which shares the SEXP and is mutated in place by setDT). All 3 failed for the right reason (class length 2, inherits data.table TRUE) while the content guards + preconditions stayed green. GREEN ‚Äî the 3 one-line swaps; all green. REFACTOR (user-approved, doc-only) ‚Äî @importFrom + NAMESPACE regen; no .Rd churn. Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1943 passed (+11 = exactly the new assertions; S9/S10 baseline 1932); zero non-e2e offenders. Warning column: only the 5 pre-existing test_modPyramid.R max()-on-empty warnings (unchanged since S4). Lint = 0 on all 3 R files (line-for-line edits ‚Üí no .lintr displacement, Learning #7 clear). The createSimKinships @example runs clean through the changed path. No devtools::check() (see self-assessment ‚àí). Key files: - R/makeSimPed.R:25, R/getPotentialParents.R:28, R/createSimKinships.R:50 ‚Äî the fix; @importFrom line in each updated to as.data.table. - tests/testthat/test_makeSimPed.R (makeSimPed does not mutate the caller's pedigree (NEW-53)), test_getPotentialParents.R, test_createSimKinships.R ‚Äî the 3 new non-mutation tests (self-contained, inline fixtures; as.data.frame(...) start state). - NAMESPACE ‚Äî importFrom(data.table,setDT) removed. - Probe scripts (throwaway, in /tmp, NOT committed): new53_probe.R (mutation repro), new53_probe2.R (copy semantics), new53_probe3.R (pipeline insulation + [-divergence demo). GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. data.table $<- shallow-copies ‚Äî so setDT/set* ‚Äúin-place‚Äù audit items usually leak only the CLASS/attrs, not content. When auditing any ‚Äúmutates/corrupts in place‚Äù finding, probe what actually leaks before writing the RED test; assert caller-object-unchanged with an immutable snapshot (paste0), because before <- ped shares the SEXP and setDT/setattr mutate it in place (a naive before/after identical can pass on buggy code). (‚Üí Learning #12.) 2. Reachability for a side-effect bug is a call-graph question, not a qcStudbook-masker or value-domain question. Trace whether internal callers insulate the leak (cumulateSimKinships does; createSimKinships did not before this fix). 3. NEW-50/51 still open in this cluster (per S9 gotcha #4): NEW-51 (positional matrix accumulation, no dimname/order assertion), NEW-50/KIN-4 (duplicated-and-diverging sim driver ‚Äî createSimKinships vs cumulateSimKinships; note they now BOTH avoid the by-ref flip, but cumulateSimKinships still never calls setDT/as.data.table at all ‚Äî it relies on ped$population <- ‚Ä¶ to copy, an asymmetry with createSimKinships). makeSimPed/createSimKinships/cumulateSimKinships are exported but public orphans (no live internal callers; the Monte-Carlo sim-kinship feature is exported-but-unwired). 4. Suggested next = NEW-45 (geneDrop period-in-id allele mis-assignment). Then NEW-20 (delete dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R + its commented test), PED-1/NEW-17 (extract getFounders/isFounder ‚Äî ‚öÝ don‚Äôt unify the descendants lines; calcRetention.R:27 filters by population). See ‚ÄúRemaining work + audit-fix guidance‚Äù in ACTIVE TASK. 5. Still-open from S10: reconcile the audit follow-ups (BACKLOG.md) vs the GitHub tracker (#1‚Äì#38) with the user ‚Äî they coexist. And the trivial test_getPotentialParents.R copy/paste-slip assertion (S4 find) is still unfixed. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase, asked permission RED‚ÜíGREEN and GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR; the 3 RED tests failed for the RIGHT reason (class flip) while content guards + existing tests stayed green. (+) Empirically disproved the audit‚Äôs harm claims (sire/dam overwrite + population-column leak) via deterministic probes BEFORE the RED test ‚Äî the highest-value move; correctly reframed the bug as a class-flip side effect, not corruption (‚Üí Learning #12). (+) Grep-inventoried the FULL reference-mutation pattern (3 sites, not the handoff‚Äôs 2) and surfaced the scope as an author decision with grounded options rather than guessing. (+) Answered the user‚Äôs ‚Äúis this actually a bug / is ped used later?‚Äù with a traced, evidence-based call-graph analysis (pipeline insulation + concrete [-divergence demo) rather than asserting severity. (+) Right-sized the work: probe + grep settled mechanism & reachability ‚Üí honestly no workflow (3rd consecutive session; ultracode‚Äôs ‚Äúunless already verified‚Äù). (+) Clean REFACTOR ‚Äî @importFrom honesty, NAMESPACE regen verified to touch nothing else, no .Rd churn, lint 0. (‚àí) Did NOT run devtools::check() ‚Äî justified for a 3-line internal change fully exercised by test_dir 0/0 + the example run + clean NAMESPACE regen, but it IS an exported-API + NAMESPACE change, so check() would be marginally more thorough (the one residual; this is NOT FM #24 ‚Äî no app-startup/registration/dispatch behavior changed, so Phase 3E runtime-launch does not apply; verified via suite + example). (‚àí) Four user interactions (clarify-why-a-bug, scope, RED gate, GREEN gate, REFACTOR gate) ‚Äî all warranted under strict TDD + an exported-API decision + the user‚Äôs substantive ‚Äúwhy is this a bug‚Äù question, but a looser contract could have bundled the gates.

Session 9 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 10)

Score: 8/10 (with a caveat: this session‚Äôs task was not derived from S9‚Äôs handoff). - Context: S9‚Äôs handoff pointed the next session at NEW-53 (audit campaign). The user instead directed a methodology-framework update, so the handoff‚Äôs audit-specific content wasn‚Äôt the operative input this session. That‚Äôs not an S9 deficiency ‚Äî a handoff can‚Äôt anticipate a meta/maintenance pivot. - What helped: the ACTIVE TASK block oriented me to true repo state in one read (clean tree, S9 = NEW-52 done, suggested-next NEW-53), and its careful treatment of the 10 Learnings as institutional memory is exactly what made me stop and preserve them (relocate to CLAUDE.md) before forcing the sync. The ‚ÄúWhat You Must Do‚Äù gotchas (maskers, reachability template) are high-value and I carried them forward intact. - What was missing (a chain-wide gap, not S9-specific): no session in the chain (1‚Äì9) noted that (a) the embedded methodology was stale vs canonical, or (b) the repo has a live GitHub issue tracker (gh issue list ‚Üí #34‚Äì38). Both are now surfaced. Also, none flagged the CHANGELOG.md-vs-NEWS.md scoring gap that the user caught ‚Äî that‚Äôs what triggered this session. - What was wrong: nothing. Every commit hash and file:line in S9‚Äôs handoff checked out. - ROI: positive ‚Äî fast, accurate orientation even though the task pivoted.

What Session 10 Did

Deliverable: Updated the embedded methodology framework to canonical rmsharp/methodology f32d780 and prepared the repo for a session restart. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-31. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 68ec2d05 (relocate customizations ‚Üí CLAUDE.md + Phase 1B claim), 15e9a793 (sync trio), 7133df0f (framework docs + workstreams), 0cd9838f (CHANGELOG/ROADMAP/RECOMMENDED_SKILLS + BACKLOG split), + this close-out (docs:). Why this session existed: the user asked ‚Äúwhere is the changelog?‚Äù; I answered from R convention (NEWS.md) without consulting the project‚Äôs own methodology, which keys the changelog on CHANGELOG.md (methodology_dashboard.py). The user (correctly) flagged that as substituting my judgment for the methodology, then directed a full methodology update from canonical. How (BOOTSTRAP ‚ÄúUpdating an existing project‚Äù runbook, evidence-based): cloned canonical to /tmp/methodology-canonical; ran bin/status/bin/sync --dry-run (all 3 synced files = locally modified ‚Üí sync refuses without --force); diffed every file to separate genuine customization from staleness BEFORE overwriting. Findings that drove the plan: - Synced trio customizations: SESSION_RUNNER.md carried only the 10 project Learnings (templates + FM #1‚Äì23 were stock); SAFEGUARDS.md carried 2 R-package build-equivalent rows; methodology_dashboard.py had no project customization. ‚Üí relocated the first two into CLAUDE.md, then bin/sync --force. - Framework docs all stale; 4 new upstream workstreams added. DESIGN/TEMPLATE workstreams were already identical. - Canonical dashboard still scores CHANGELOG.md (weight 5), NOT NEWS.md ‚Üí created a process CHANGELOG.md (NEWS.md stays CRAN-facing). Health 92‚Üí98. Key files: - CLAUDE.md ‚Äî SESSION PROTOCOL orient line updated; new ‚ÄúBuild / Test / Verify‚Äù (R build-equivalent) + ‚ÄúProject-Specific Methodology Adaptations‚Äù (10 Learnings verbatim + new Learning #11 on methodology-update mechanics). This is now the home for all project methodology customizations. - SESSION_RUNNER.md, SAFEGUARDS.md, methodology_dashboard.py ‚Äî byte-identical to canonical f32d780 (bin/status = all current). Do not edit these ‚Äî put changes in CLAUDE.md. - docs/methodology/ ‚Äî ITERATIVE_METHODOLOGY/HOW_TO_USE/README + 9 workstreams, all canonical. - CHANGELOG.md (process history), ROADMAP.md (milestone + feature inventory), RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.md (links adapted to this repo‚Äôs layout), BACKLOG.md (open work only + audit follow-ups). GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. RESTART before working ‚Äî the new CLAUDE.md/SESSION_RUNNER.md load only in a fresh session (BOOTSTRAP First-Session rule). 2. Orientation now leads with gh issue list (repo has a live tracker ‚Äî #34‚Äì38 open). BACKLOG.md is the fallback. The audit follow-ups (NEW-53 etc.) are in BACKLOG.md, NOT GitHub issues ‚Äî reconcile with the user. 3. Synced files must stay byte-identical to canonical. bin/sync/bin/status enforce it; drift blocks sync without --force. Customizations go in CLAUDE.md Adaptations (Learning #11). 4. bin/sync --source=github targets KJ5HST/methodology (hardcoded in the script), which differs from the rmsharp/methodology URL the user gave. I used --source=local against a clone of rmsharp/methodology, so it didn‚Äôt matter ‚Äî but for future GitHub-source syncs, confirm which repo is canonical. The /tmp/methodology-canonical clone is ephemeral (re-clone to sync again). 5. Scope held / NOT done (deliberate): did NOT file the audit follow-ups as GitHub issues; did NOT touch NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd; did NOT add CONTEXT.md/BOOTSTRAP.md to the project (methodology-repo docs); did NOT run the R test suite (no code changed); left the pre-existing untouched artifacts (.DS_Store, ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) alone. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Treated a destructive multi-file framework change with full SAFEGUARDS discipline: committed-clean baseline, evidence-based diff inventory before any overwrite, customizations preserved (10 Learnings) before --force, incremental commits (‚â§5 files each), verified byte-identical + dashboard-runs + health-improved after each step. (+) Used the project‚Äôs own intended mechanism (bin/sync) rather than ad-hoc copying ‚Äî the direct lesson from the CHANGELOG misstep (consult the project‚Äôs source of truth). (+) Was consultative on the two genuine decisions (scope, CHANGELOG/NEWS) rather than assuming ‚Äî rebuilding trust after the earlier substitution-of-judgment error. (+) Surfaced two latent chain-wide gaps (stale methodology, unused GitHub tracker). (‚àí) The RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.md link-rewrite is a judgment call (could have copied verbatim and accepted dangling links); it‚Äôs now non-identical to canonical by design. (‚àí) Could not self-verify the restart (the whole point of restarting) ‚Äî noted as a handoff gotcha rather than silently skipped (new SESSION_RUNNER Phase 3E).

Session 8 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 9)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: the two standing ‚öÝ in ‚ÄúWhat You Must Do‚Äù #1 were both decisive. (1) ‚Äúempirically reproduce the ACTUAL failure mode ‚Äî the audit‚Äôs stated mechanism AND its prescribed fix can BOTH be WRONG‚Äù (Learnings #8a/#9) ‚Üí NEW-52 has TWO mechanisms; M1 (n=1) reproduced (all-NaN sd), but M2 (catastrophic cancellation) does NOT reproduce ‚Äî kinship() returns dyadic rationals (exact in IEEE-754), so constant-across-sims cells cancel to exactly 0, never tiny-negative (probe: 0 negative numerators, 117 exactly-0, 0 NaN at n=10 and n=100). Without that reflex I might have shipped a speculative pmax(num,0) clamp for an unreachable path. (2) ‚Äúreachability is NOT always a pipeline-masking question ‚Ä¶ orphaned, zero callers‚Äù (Learning #9) ‚Üí cumulateSimKinships is @exported but has zero internal/live callers (grep-settled), so M1 reachability = a direct public-API call with n=1; per Learning #9‚Äôs corollary I did NOT spin up a 3-lens workflow (grep + a 2-min repro = ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù). - What helped (process): Learning #8b (stop-vs-warning by current failure mode) framed the author decision exactly ‚Äî n=1 currently succeeds silently (NaN sd) ‚Üí graceful NA+warning is backward-safe (NEW-40 side); n=0 already crashes ‚Üí clear stop is backward-safe (NEW-48 side). Learning #4‚Äôs test_dir+!grepl isolation gave a clean 0/0; the warning-column watch confirmed only the 5 pre-existing modPyramid warnings and that my new warning() trips no existing test (Learning #6 corollary). Learning #7 check (file not in .lintr) avoided a phantom line-displacement hunt. - What was slightly off (partial mismatch): the NEW-52 one-liner (‚Äúsd n=1 ‚Üí NaN‚Äù) captured M1 but not the audit‚Äôs second claimed mechanism (M2 cancellation) ‚Äî I found M2 only by reading the audit finding itself (PED_GV_AUDIT:114-116). Minor: the standing ‚öÝ ‚Äúaudit mechanism can be wrong‚Äù anticipated it, so the disproof cost ~2 min. - What was missing: no flag that cumulateSimKinships is a public orphan (@exported, zero live callers ‚Äî unusual), that the audit‚Äôs M2 is dubious for dyadic-rational values, or that n=0 is a separate adjacent crash. All three only surface on empirical repro; S8 wasn‚Äôt on NEW-52, so reasonable gaps (same class as S8‚Äôs own note about S7). - What was wrong: nothing. S8 faithfully relayed the audit pointer; the audit‚Äôs M2 over-claim is the audit‚Äôs, not S8‚Äôs. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the standing learnings (#4/#8a/#8b/#9) were the backbone; they prevented a speculative fix and framed the dual stop/warning contract correctly.

What Session 9 Did

Deliverable: Fixed NEW-52 (cumulateSimKinships standard deviation undefined for n < 2) under strict TDD, extended by author decision to also clear-error the adjacent n=0 crash; with a regression test block + roxygen/man update. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-31. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: e3c7e8b3 (fix + test block + man page); close-out docs in a follow-up docs: commit. The bug (M1, real): the sd formula sqrt(((n*squaredKinship) - sumKinship^2L) / (n*(n-1L))) is the n‚àí1 (sample) form. For n=1 the denominator n(n-1)=0 and numerator =0 ‚Üí 0/0 = NaN in every cell (289/289 on the test ped) ‚Äî silent (no error). The roxygen documented ‚Äústandard deviation‚Äù but didn‚Äôt address n<2. Reachability ‚Äî verdict MIXED; it DISPROVED the audit‚Äôs M2 (verified by grep caller-trace + direct empirical repro; NO workflow ‚Äî ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù per Learning #9 corollary): - M1 (n=1) = REACHABLE & CONFIRMED. cumulateSimKinships is @exported but has zero internal/live callers (full-repo grep: only its own def, its test using n=100, and docs/audits; no do.call/get/match.fun; NOT in the qcStudbook‚ÜíreportGV path). So n=1 is reachable via a direct public-API call. Empirically all-NaN sd. - M2 (catastrophic cancellation) = NOT REACHABLE. Audit claimed ‚Äúnear-constant cells ‚Üí tiny-negative under the root ‚Üí NaN.‚Äù Empirically FALSE here: kinship() returns dyadic rationals (sums of powers of ¬Ω), exact in IEEE-754, so constant cells produce numerator exactly 0 (probe: min numerator 0, 0 negative, 117 exactly-0; 0 NaN at n=10 and n=100). The formula is only reachable through cumulateSimKinships, so non-dyadic data can‚Äôt reach it. Under strict TDD, no failing test can be written ‚Üí no fix (a pmax(num,0) clamp would be speculative, untestable). ‚Üí Learning #10. - n=0 (adjacent, NOT in audit) = REACHABLE, distinct crash. seq_len(0) ‚Üí loop never runs ‚Üí minKinship/maxKinship never initialized ‚Üí "object 'minKinship' not found" before the sd code. Author chose to also guard this. Semantic decisions (USER, package author): (sd contract) NA matrix + warning for n<2 ‚Äî over silent-NA and over stop(). Reasoning (Learning #8b): n=1 currently succeeds (returns valid mean/min/max + NaN sd), so graceful NA+warning preserves that success while fixing the diagnostic; stop() would convert a currently-succeeding call to a failure and deny the valid mean/min/max ‚Äî the inverse of NEW-48/NEW-25 (already-crashing ‚Üí stop). Matches base R sd(c(5)) == NA. (scope) also clear-error n<1 ‚Äî n=0 already crashes cryptically, so a clear stop() is backward-safe (the NEW-48 side again). The fix (minimal): - R/cumulateSimKinships.R:42-44 ‚Äî if (n < 1L) stop("cumulateSimKinships() requires at least one simulation (n >= 1).") (top of function, before the loop). - R/cumulateSimKinships.R:73-89 ‚Äî sd branch: if (n < 2L) { warning("‚Ä¶ standard deviation is undefined for n < 2 simulations; sdKinship set to NA.") ; matrix(NA_real_, nrow=nIds, ncol=nIds, dimnames=dimnames(sumKinship)) } else { <original formula, byte-identical> }. Verified: n‚â•2 output byte-identical to pre-fix; n=1 ‚Üí all-NA (no NaN), dimnames preserved, mean/min/max intact. TDD trail: RED ‚Äî one test_that block (tests/testthat/test_cumulateSimKinships.R:72-101), 9 expectations: n=1 ‚Üí expect_warning("undefined for n < 2") + all(is.na(sd)) + expect_false(any(is.nan(sd))) (the KEY discriminator ‚Äî is.na(NaN) is TRUE, so only is.nan separates the NaN baseline from the NA fix) + mean/min/max not-NaN; n=0 ‚Üí expect_error("at least one simulation"); n=2 guard ‚Üí expect_warning(regexp=NA) + no-NA sd. The 3 bug assertions FAILED for the right reason on unfixed code (no warning / sd is NaN / cryptic n=0 message); the guards passed before AND after. GREEN ‚Äî the two guards; file 14/14. REFACTOR (user-approved, doc-only) ‚Äî roxygen @return (sdKinship NA for n<2) + @param n (n>=1 required, sd needs n>=2); regenerated man/cumulateSimKinships.Rd (only that Rd; no NAMESPACE change). Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1932 passed (+9 = exactly the new expectations; S8 ended 1923); zero non-e2e offenders. Warning column: only the 5 pre-existing test_modPyramid.R max()-on-empty warnings (unchanged) ‚Äî my new warning() trips no other test. Lint = 0 on R/cumulateSimKinships.R (NOT in .lintr) and the test file (blanket tests exclusion). Key files: - R/cumulateSimKinships.R:42-44 (n<1 stop), :73-89 (n<2 NA+warning sd branch); roxygen @return :10-14, @param n :19-21. - tests/testthat/test_cumulateSimKinships.R:72-101 ‚Äî the new test block (reuses file-level ped/allSimParents; set_seed(2L) for determinism; inline only). - man/cumulateSimKinships.Rd ‚Äî regenerated. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. An audit‚Äôs claimed mechanism can be UNREACHABLE for the function‚Äôs value domain (extends Learning #8a ‚Üí Learning #10). NEW-52‚Äôs M2 (catastrophic cancellation) cannot occur because kinship() yields dyadic rationals (exact in IEEE-754) ‚Üí constant cells cancel to exactly 0. When auditing numerical-instability findings, check whether inputs are exactly representable AND whether the unstable expression is reachable with non-exact data. DISPROVING > ‚Äúdefensively fixing‚Äù: under strict TDD, no failing test ‚Üí no code. 2. is.na(NaN) is TRUE in R ‚Äî to assert ‚ÄúNA but not NaN‚Äù you MUST use expect_false(any(is.nan(x))) alongside all(is.na(x)). A test that only checks is.na passes on the broken NaN baseline and proves nothing. 3. cumulateSimKinships is a ‚Äúpublic orphan‚Äù ‚Äî @exported but zero live callers (its sibling createSimKinships likewise ‚Äî verify before assuming a GV-report path). The Monte-Carlo sim-kinship feature appears exported-but-unwired. 4. NEW-50/51/53 still OPEN in this cluster (cumulateSimKinships.R/createSimKinships.R/makeSimPed.R): NEW-53 (makeSimPed in-place mutation ‚Äî also getPotentialParents.R:28 setDT-no-copy; fix both together), NEW-51 (positional matrix accumulation, no dimname/order assertion), NEW-50/KIN-4 (duplicated-and-diverging sim driver; note createSimKinships does setDT(ped) but cumulateSimKinships does not ‚Äî an existing divergence). If NEW-50/KIN-4 consolidates the two into a shared simulateOneKinship helper, fold the n-guards in once. 5. Scope held / NOT done (deliberate): did NOT add a defensive M2 clamp (unreachable + untestable); did NOT touch NEW-50/51/53; did NOT run devtools::check() (test_dir 0/0 + lint 0 + clean man regen deemed sufficient for a localized change ‚Äî but it IS an exported fn, see self-assessment ‚àí); did NOT touch the pre-existing modPyramid max() warnings or the test_getPotentialParents copy/paste-slip test (both still open). Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase (Pre-RED investigation, RED, GREEN, REFACTOR), asked permission RED‚ÜíGREEN and GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR; the 3 RED assertions failed for the RIGHT reasons while the n‚â•2 + mean/min/max guards stayed green as true-positive guards. (+) Empirically reproduced ALL THREE mechanisms FIRST and DISPROVED the audit‚Äôs M2 (dyadic rationals are exact ‚Üí constant cells cancel to exactly 0; formula only reachable through the function) ‚Äî correctly declined speculative untestable defensive code (the highest-value move; ‚Üí Learning #10). (+) Confirmed the candidate fix actually fixes the reproduced failure AND is byte-identical for n‚â•2 BEFORE the RED test (Learning #8a/#9). (+) Recognized the n<2 contract as a genuine author decision and surfaced it with grounded options + code previews + the Learning #8b reasoning; author chose NA+warning, consistent with the framework. (+) Right-sized reachability: @exported-but-zero-callers settled by grep + a 2-min repro = ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù; did NOT spin up a workflow (honest application of ultracode‚Äôs ‚Äúunless already verified‚Äù + Learning #9 corollary ‚Äî second consecutive session where the honest call was ‚Äúno workflow‚Äù). (+) Used the is.nan discriminator in the RED test (since is.na(NaN) is TRUE) ‚Äî a subtle correctness point. (+) Verified .lintr membership (Learning #7) and confirmed document() touched only the one Rd. (‚àí) Did NOT run devtools::check(); this touches an EXPORTED function + regenerates a man page, so check() is marginally more thorough than for S8‚Äôs @noRd helper ‚Äî but the example was unchanged, the Rd regenerated cleanly, lint=0, suite 0/0. The one residual. (‚àí) Four user interactions (the 2-question batch + two phase gates); all warranted under strict-TDD + a behavior-defining public-API decision, but a looser contract could have bundled them.

Session 7 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 8)

Score: 8.5/10. - What helped most: the standing Learning #8a carried in ‚ÄúWhat You Must Do‚Äù #1 ‚Äî ‚Äúempirically reproduce the ACTUAL failure mode ‚Äî the audit‚Äôs stated mechanism can be WRONG‚Äù ‚Äî was directly decisive. The audit (and its in-line note) framed NEW-25 as ‚Äúthe three-way if/else-if has no terminal else, so if (NaN > 0.5) raises‚Ä¶‚Äù, which reads as ‚Äúadd a terminal else.‚Äù Empirical repro in ~1 min showed the crash is at the FIRST if (NaN > 0.5) = if (NA) (because NaN > 0.5 is NA), so a terminal else would NOT fix it. S7‚Äôs gotcha #1 (‚Äúdon‚Äôt design the RED test off the audit‚Äôs prose‚Äù) trained exactly this reflex. Avoided shipping a non-fix. - What helped (process): Learning #4‚Äôs test_dir + !grepl("test-app-|test-e2e-", file) isolation gave a clean 0/0 with +3 passed (exactly the new expectations); the warning column again confirmed the 5 test_modPyramid.R max()-on-empty warnings are the SAME pre-existing ones S4‚ÄìS7 flagged. S7‚Äôs framing of the semantic decision as the package author‚Äôs call (NEW-48 approach choice) generalized perfectly ‚Äî empty-input contract was likewise a domain decision I surfaced with code previews rather than guessing. - What was a partial MISMATCH (not wrong, just not aimed here): the reachability template in #1 is qcStudbook-pipeline-centric (maskers addUIds/convertSexCodes/addParents). NEW-25‚Äôs getProportionLow is not in that pipeline at all ‚Äî it‚Äôs an @noRd helper with ZERO live callers. The right reachability question was an exhaustive caller-trace (incl.¬Ýdynamic dispatch + commented-out consumers), and the answer reshaped the task into ‚Äúharden an orphaned helper + decide the empty contract.‚Äù The generic ‚Äúverify reachability BEFORE fixing‚Äù still pointed me the right way; only the specific maskers didn‚Äôt apply. - What was missing: no note that getProportionLow‚Äôs only consumer is the dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard (NEW-20) ‚Äî i.e.¬ÝNEW-25 and NEW-20 are entangled (the orphan exists because its caller is commented out). I discovered this by grep; a one-line ‚Äúorphaned by NEW-20‚Äù tag would have saved a few minutes. Reasonable gap ‚Äî S7 wasn‚Äôt working NEW-25, and the audit itself didn‚Äôt surface the entanglement. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî Learning #8a alone prevented a wrong fix; the author-decision framing kept the contract correct.

What Session 8 Did

Deliverable: Fixed NEW-25 (getProportionLow empty-input crash) under strict TDD, with a regression test + roxygen note. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-31. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 587ba042 (fix + test + roxygen @param note); close-out docs in a follow-up docs: commit. The bug: getProportionLow(geneticValues) computes proportion <- length(grep "Low") / length(geneticValues). For empty input that is 0 / 0 = NaN. The function‚Äôs color logic is a three-way if / else if / else if with no terminal else ‚Äî BUT that is a red herring: NaN > 0.5 evaluates to NA, and if (NA) immediately errors ‚Äúmissing value where TRUE/FALSE needed‚Äù at the first if (R/getProportionLow.R, old line 18), before any fall-through. So the documented audit fix (‚Äúadd a terminal else‚Äù) would NOT have fixed it. Empty input was the only crashing case (confirmed empirically). Reachability ‚Äî verdict: NO LIVE PATH (orphaned helper), verified by direct evidence (exhaustive grep + dynamic-dispatch check + empirical repro ‚Äî no workflow needed, this was ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù): - getProportionLow is @noRd (internal, not exported). A full-repo search (*.R/*.Rmd/*.yml, substring ProportionLow, and do.call/get/match.fun/eval) found ZERO live callers. The only references are: its own def, its happy-path test, a commented-out call in test_makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R:8, and docs (_pkgdown.yml, WORDLIST, NEWS.*, audit files). - Its sole intended consumer, R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R, is 100% dead code (no non-comment, non-blank lines) = NEW-20. So NEW-25 is reachable only via a direct internal call or by reviving the dead dashboard. This is robustness-hardening of a retained-but-orphaned helper (it is intentionally retained ‚Äî listed in inst/_pkgdown.yml:192). - Adjacent latent bug found, NOT fixed (out of scope): getProportionLow(c(NA, "Low")) returns proportion = 1 silently ‚Äî NA elements are counted as ‚ÄúLow‚Äù via NA-subsetting. Not a crash, not NEW-25. Semantic decision (USER, package author): the code can‚Äôt say what empty input should return. Surfaced 3 grounded options with code previews ‚Äî (A) clear stop(), (B) graceful NA result, (C) empty = 0%‚Üígreen. User chose (A) clear stop() ‚Äî consistent with NEW-48 and Learning #8b: the input ALREADY crashes (cryptic message), so a stop() is backward-safe (no currently-succeeding call changes; only the message improves). The @param doc (‚Äúvector is to have already been filtered‚Äù) also implies a non-empty precondition, so empty = caller error worth surfacing. The fix (minimal): at R/getProportionLow.R:16-19, if (length(geneticValues) == 0L) stop("getProportionLow() requires at least one genetic value; ", "'geneticValues' is empty."). Existing proportion / if-else-if logic byte-for-byte unchanged. TDD trail: RED ‚Äî added one test_that block (tests/testthat/test_getProportionLow.R:24-42): 2 expect_error(‚Ä¶, "requires at least one") for character(0) and NULL (both FAILED for the right reason ‚Äî they DID throw, but the cryptic "missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed" ‚âÝ the intended message), plus a single-element getProportionLow("Low") ‚Üí list(1, "red", 1L) true-positive guard that PASSED before and after (proving the guard fires only on length-0). The 3 existing happy-path expectations stayed green. GREEN ‚Äî the length-0 stop guard; file 6/6. REFACTOR (user-approved, doc-only) ‚Äî roxygen @param precondition note (R/getProportionLow.R:9-12); NO man page (function is @noRd ‚Äî confirmed man/getProportionLow.Rd does not exist), so nothing to regenerate. Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1923 passed (+3 = exactly the new expectations; S7 ended at 1920); zero non-e2e offenders. Warning column: only the 5 pre-existing test_modPyramid.R max()-on-empty warnings (unchanged). Lint = 0 on R/getProportionLow.R (it is NOT in .lintr exclusions ‚Äî so no Learning #7 line-displacement risk; the test file is under the blanket tests exclusion). Key files: - R/getProportionLow.R:16-19 ‚Äî the guard; roxygen @param note at :9-12. Function is @noRd (line 14). - tests/testthat/test_getProportionLow.R:24-42 ‚Äî the new test block (deterministic; inline literals only, no fixtures). GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. The audit‚Äôs prescribed FIX can be as wrong as its mechanism (Learning #9, extends #8a). NEW-25‚Äôs ‚Äúno terminal else ‚Üí crash‚Äù implied ‚Äúadd an else‚Äù; the real crash is if (NaN > 0.5) = if (NA) at the FIRST if. NaN/NA comparisons yield NA, and if (NA) errors. Empirically confirm BOTH the mechanism and the remedy before writing the RED test. 2. Reachability is NOT always a qcStudbook-masking question. Some audit items target ORPHANED code (zero live callers). NEW-25‚Äôs getProportionLow is @noRd and its only caller is the dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard (NEW-20). For such items, ‚Äúverify reachability‚Äù = exhaustive caller-trace (grep all file types + do.call/get/match.fun + commented-out consumers), not pipeline-masker analysis. Don‚Äôt reflexively reach for the (heavier) 3-lens reachability workflow when grep already settles it ‚Äî that is ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù and a workflow would be ceremony. 3. NEW-25 / NEW-20 are entangled. getProportionLow exists only to serve the dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard. If a future session does NEW-20 (delete the dead dashboard), consider whether getProportionLow (+ its test) should go too, OR whether the dashboard should be revived to consume it. It‚Äôs retained in _pkgdown.yml:192 ‚Äî which, since the function is @noRd (no Rd page), is itself a likely-stale pkgdown reference worth checking. 4. Adjacent NA-element bug still open: getProportionLow(c(NA, "Low")) ‚Üí proportion 1 (silent, NA counted as Low). Separate from NEW-25; not in any audit finding I saw. Candidate for a future robustness pass. 5. Scope held / NOT done (deliberate): did NOT delete the dead makeGeneticDiversityDashboard (NEW-20); did NOT fix the NA-element bug; did NOT touch scattered-threshold items (NEW-21/26); did NOT touch the pre-existing modPyramid max()-on-empty warnings or the test_getPotentialParents copy/paste-slip test (both still open). Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase, asked permission RED‚ÜíGREEN and GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR; the 2 RED tests failed for the RIGHT reason (cryptic message ‚âÝ intended) while the single-element guard + happy-path tests stayed green as the true-positive guard. (+) Empirically reproduced the ACTUAL failure mode FIRST (Learning #8a) and caught that the audit‚Äôs prescribed remedy (‚Äúterminal else‚Äù) was a non-fix ‚Äî the highest-value move; generalized to Learning #9. (+) Recognized the empty-input contract as a genuine package-author decision and surfaced it with grounded options + code previews rather than guessing (cf.¬ÝS6/S7). (+) Right-sized the reachability work: a unique-named @noRd orphan with grep-clean callers is ‚Äúalready verified‚Äù ‚Äî did NOT spin up a redundant 3-lens workflow (honest application of ultracode‚Äôs ‚Äúunless already verified‚Äù carve-out). (+) Verified .lintr membership before editing (Learning #7) and confirmed @noRd ‚Üí no man page to regenerate, rather than assuming. (‚àí) Did not run the heavier devtools::check() build-equivalent ‚Äî justified for a one-line internal guard with test_dir 0/0 + lint 0, but noted. (‚àí) Three user prompts (semantic decision + two phase gates) ‚Äî all warranted under the strict-TDD contract + a behavior-defining decision, but a looser contract could have bundled them.

Session 6 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 7)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: ACTIVE TASK ‚ÄúWhat You Must Do‚Äù #1 named NEW-48 with a one-line mechanism AND the standing ‚öÝ ‚Äúverify reachability with real data BEFORE ‚Äòfixing‚Äô‚Äù + the confirmed MIXED per-mechanism template (Learning #6). That template was again exactly right ‚Äî NEW-48 split cleanly: M2 canonical qcStudbook‚ÜíreportGV (incl.¬Ýshipped app) MASKED by addUIds (qcStudbook.R:180), M1 direct call + M3 trimPedigree(removeUninformative=TRUE, addBackParents=FALSE) REACHABLE. S6 gotcha #2 (‚Äútreat convertSexCodes as a masking step‚Äù) generalized perfectly: the analogous masker here is addUIds (‚ÄúEliminates partial parentage situations‚Äù), which I found by tracing the pipeline as the gotcha trained me to. Learning #4‚Äôs test_dir + !grepl("test-app-|test-e2e-", file) isolation gave a clean 0/0 every run; the warning column confirmed the 5 test_modPyramid.R warnings are the SAME pre-existing ones S5/S6 flagged. - What was WRONG (but NOT S6‚Äôs fault): the NEW-48 one-liner ‚Äî ‚Äúpartial-parentage d[NA,] contaminates ego + all descendants‚Äù (silent corruption) ‚Äî described the wrong failure mode. Empirically it is a hard CRASH (subscript out of bounds): calcFEFG coerces ids to character and d[NA_character_, ] ERRORS (only logical/integer NA yields a silent NA row). BUT this came verbatim from the audit (PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md), which S6 faithfully relayed ‚Äî not an S6 fabrication. The 3-lens empirical workflow caught it at ~0 time cost because the template (‚Äúreproduce via the real pipeline BEFORE writing the test‚Äù) is designed for exactly this. New lesson ‚Üí Learning #8a (the audit‚Äôs mechanism itself can be wrong). - What was missing: S6 (via Learning #4) framed stop as ‚Äúnot backward-safe‚Äù ‚Äî true when the baseline is a silent success, but it inverts when the baseline is already a crash (NEW-48), where a clear stop() is the contract-preserving choice. New trap ‚Üí Learning #8b. - ROI: strongly positive ‚Äî the MIXED-template + reachability warning scoped the session immediately and steered me to the empirical workflow that corrected the audit before any test was written.

What Session 7 Did

Deliverable: Fixed NEW-48 (calcFEFG partial-parentage crash) under strict TDD, extended by user decision to the two identical-bug siblings calcFE/calcFG, with regression tests + doc updates. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-30. Branch: add-methodology. Commits: 19350559 (fix + 4 tests + 3 man pages); close-out docs in a follow-up docs: commit. The bug: calcFEFG/calcFE/calcFG define a founder as having BOTH parents NA (calcFEFG.R:46), so a row with exactly one NA parent is a descendant, enters the gen loop, and computes d[ego, ] <- (d[sire, ] + d[dam, ]) / 2L indexing the founder matrix by the lone NA_character_ parent. The roxygen documented ‚Äúno partial parentage‚Äù but did not enforce it. Reachability ‚Äî verdict MIXED, and it CORRECTED the audit (verified BEFORE fixing via 3-lens read-only workflow wf_20c72ee8-64e; artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/w5bxzeaxc.output): - The audit was WRONG about the mechanism: it claimed ‚Äúsilent NA corruption‚Äù (d[NA,] ‚Üí all-NA row contaminating FE/FG). Empirically it is a hard CRASH (subscript out of bounds) ‚Äî R‚Äôs matrix indexing errors on NA_character_ (character ids via toCharacter), whereas only logical/integer NA returns a silent NA row. FE/FG are NEVER produced; the loop aborts before colMeans/the FE/FG return, so the FG line‚Äôs na.rm=TRUE is irrelevant to this bug. All 3 lenses (empirical ‚Äñ static ‚Äñ adversarial-critic) converged on CRASH-not-corruption. - M2 canonical qcStudbook‚ÜíreportGV (incl.¬Ýshipped app, modGeneticValue.R:150-152, removeUninformative=FALSE) = MASKED by addUIds (qcStudbook.R:180), which rewrites every lone-NA parent to a U#### placeholder + adds a founder line. The adversarial critic confirmed a clean run (FE‚âà109.67/FG‚âà47.67) and found NO flag that bypasses addUIds. - M1 direct exported calcFEFG()/calcFE()/calcFG() call = REACHABLE (documented precondition unenforced; fails loud). - M3 trimPedigree(removeUninformative=TRUE, addBackParents=FALSE)‚ÜíreportGV = REACHABLE via removeUninformativeFounders.R:52-53 (NA-s child refs when an uninformative founder is dropped; addBackSecondParents repairs ONLY when addBackParents=TRUE). The critic verified kinship/geneDrop/offspringCounts all tolerate the NA ‚Äî calcFEFG is the FIRST step to die. The only removeUninformative=TRUE in the shipped app is commented out, so M3 is reachable via the exported API / scripts, not the default app path. Semantic decisions (USER, package author): (approach) clear stop() guard ‚Äî chosen over (warning + return NA) and (self-heal via addUIds). Key reasoning: the input ALREADY crashes, so stop() is backward-safe (no currently-succeeding call changes; only the cryptic message improves) ‚Äî the INVERSE of NEW-40, where the silent-NA baseline made warning the safe choice (‚Üí Learning #8b). (scope) fix all 3 identical-bug siblings, explicitly NOT the NEW-13/23 calcFE/FG‚ÜícalcFEFG consolidation. The fix (minimal, identical guard √ó3): after toCharacter, partial <- xor(is.na(ped$sire), is.na(ped$dam)); if (any(partial)) stop("‚Ä¶ requires complete parentage (no partial parentage): id(s) ‚Ä¶ ", toString(ped$id[partial]), " ‚Ä¶ qcStudbook()/addUIds() ‚Ä¶"). At R/calcFEFG.R:47-53, R/calcFE.R:45-51, R/calcFG.R:57-63. TDD trail: RED ‚Äî 4 tests added, all FAILED with the exact cryptic subscript out of bounds (no guard yet), proving the bug; the existing complete-parentage tests stayed green (they double as the no-false-positive guard). GREEN ‚Äî the xor/stop guard √ó3; all 4 pass. REFACTOR (user-approved) ‚Äî reflowed an 83-char stop() line to restore lint=0, and tightened the roxygen on all three to state the precondition is now ENFORCED (+ regenerated 3 man pages; document() touched ONLY those 3 .Rd). Declined (user decision) extracting a shared guard helper ‚Äî left to NEW-13/23. Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1920 passed (+4 = exactly the new tests); zero non-e2e offenders. Warning column: only the 5 pre-existing test_modPyramid.R max()-on-empty warnings (unchanged). Lint = 0 on all 3 R files + 3 test files. Masking (addUIds) + the M3 crash reproduced verbatim in the workflow artifact. Key files: - R/calcFEFG.R:47-53, R/calcFE.R:45-51, R/calcFG.R:57-63 ‚Äî the guard (identical; roxygen ‚Äúenforced‚Äù note added to each function‚Äôs description/@param ped). - tests/testthat/test_calcFEFG.R:52 (calcFEFG-stops), :66 (reportGV real-caller integration); test_calcFE.R:42; test_calcFG.R:52 ‚Äî the 4 new tests (inline 4-row partial-parentage data.frames; deterministic, no geneDrop needed because the guard short-circuits before calcRetention). - man/calcFEFG.Rd, man/calcFE.Rd, man/calcFG.Rd ‚Äî regenerated. - Workflow artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/w5bxzeaxc.output ‚Äî per-mechanism CRASH/MASKED verdicts + verbatim repro (incl.¬Ýthe d[NA_character_, ] vs d[NA_integer_, ] semantics proof). GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. The audit‚Äôs stated MECHANISM can be wrong ‚Äî reproduce the ACTUAL failure mode first (Learning #8a). NEW-48 was a crash, not the ‚Äúsilent NA‚Äù the audit described, because of character-vs-logical/integer NA matrix-indexing semantics. Don‚Äôt design the RED test off the audit‚Äôs prose. 2. addUIds (qcStudbook.R:180) is the partial-parentage masker ‚Äî like convertSexCodes (NEW-37) and addParents (NEW-40), it eliminates the very condition some calc* functions assume away. Any item about NA/partial parents reaching a GV function is likely MASKED through qcStudbook but REACHABLE via exported functions / trimPedigree(removeUninformative=TRUE, addBackParents=FALSE). 3. removeUninformativeFounders.R:52-53 independently NA-s child sire/dam refs when dropping an uninformative founder ‚Üí re-introduces partial parentage that trimPedigree‚Äôs default addBackParents=FALSE does NOT repair. Relevant to anything downstream of trimPedigree. 4. stop-vs-warning backward-safety depends on the CURRENT failure mode (Learning #8b): silent-wrong-success ‚Üí warning (don‚Äôt abort a success, cf.¬ÝNEW-40); already-crashing ‚Üí clear stop (preserve the contract, improve the message, cf.¬ÝNEW-48). 5. The S7 guard is triplicated across calcFEFG/calcFE/calcFG (3 inline copies, by the user‚Äôs scope choice). When NEW-13/23 consolidates calcFE/calcFG into calcFEFG delegation, collapse the guard into the single calcFEFG then. 6. Scope held / NOT done (deliberate): did NOT extract a shared guard helper (NEW-13/23); did NOT touch addUIds/removeUninformativeFounders/trimPedigree; did NOT fix the pre-existing modPyramid max()-on-empty warnings or the test_getPotentialParents copy/paste-slip test (both still open). Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase, asked permission RED‚ÜíGREEN and GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR; the 4 RED tests failed for the RIGHT reason (cryptic subscript out of bounds) while the existing complete-parentage tests stayed green as the true-positive guard. (+) Verified reachability per-mechanism BEFORE writing tests via a 3-lens read-only workflow, which corrected the audit‚Äôs mechanism (crash, not silent corruption) and converged unanimously ‚Äî the highest-value move of the session. (+) Recognized two genuine author decisions (approach + scope) and surfaced them with grounded options + code previews rather than guessing (cf.¬ÝS6/NEW-37). (+) Added a reportGV real-caller integration test, not just unit tests, guarding the pipeline path. (+) Clean REFACTOR ‚Äî restored lint=0, documented the enforced precondition, regenerated exactly 3 man pages (verified, not assumed). (+) Held scope (declined helper extraction; left NEW-13/23 untouched). (‚àí) The reportGV integration test runs the full GV pipeline (kinship+geneDrop, guIter=50) on a 7-row ped ‚Äî fast, but heavier than a pure unit test; acceptable for a real-path guard. (‚àí) Three user prompts (semantic+scope decision, then the two mandatory phase gates) ‚Äî all warranted under the strict-TDD contract + a public-API change, but a looser contract could have bundled them.

Session 5 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 6)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: ACTIVE TASK ‚ÄúWhat You Must Do‚Äù #1 named NEW-37 as suggested-next with an accurate one-line mechanism (correctParentSex silently rewrites H/U‚ÜíM/F) AND the ‚öÝ ‚Äúverify reachability ‚Ä¶ BEFORE ‚Äòfixing‚Äô‚Äù plus the explicit ‚ÄúNEW-40‚Äôs verdict was MIXED ‚Ä¶ enumerate each distinct mechanism separately ‚Äî reachability is rarely one yes/no‚Äù. That MIXED-template framing (echoed in Learning #6) was the session: I ran the 3-lens read-only reachability workflow first and, exactly as warned, NEW-37 split by mechanism ‚Äî U-sex parent REACHABLE through the canonical qcStudbook(reportErrors=FALSE) pipeline, literal-H MASKED by convertSexCodes(ignoreHerm=TRUE) folding H‚ÜíU at qcStudbook.R:184, H/U both REACHABLE via the exported public API. Without that framing I might have written a unit-only test and missed the pipeline-level U reachability (the part that actually matters to users). - What helped (process): Gotcha #1 (‚Äúreachability splits by MECHANISM‚Äù) applied directly. S5 gotcha #2 (checkParentAge drops NA-birth rows, isn‚Äôt an integrity guard) and gotcha #4 (@exported + warning is backward-safe, stop is not) framed the options I put to the user. Learning #4‚Äôs test_dir + !grepl("test-app-|test-e2e-", file) offender-isolation gave a clean 0/0 every run; watching the warning column (gotcha #3) confirmed my fix introduced no new warnings and that no existing test depended on the old H/U‚ÜíM/F behavior. - What was slightly off: The NEW-37 one-liner, like S5‚Äôs own NEW-40 note, didn‚Äôt pre-split the mechanism (that convertSexCodes masks literal-H while U stays reachable) ‚Äî but the generic MIXED ‚öÝ explicitly anticipated exactly this, so zero time lost. - What was missing: Nothing material. S5 couldn‚Äôt have known that a roxygen @details addition would displace the line-anchored .lintr exclusion (70L) and resurface a pre-existing suppressed lint ‚Äî that is the new, generalizable trap I‚Äôm adding as Learning #7 + gotcha #1 below. - ROI: Strongly positive ‚Äî the MIXED-template framing + accurate finding pointer + reusable test/lint gotchas scoped the session immediately and steered the test design toward the pipeline-reachable path.

What Session 6 Did

Deliverable: Fixed NEW-37 (correctParentSex silently rewrites H/U parents to M/F) under strict TDD, with regression tests + doc update. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-30. Branch: add-methodology. Commit: 6b0ae333 (fix + 3 tests + man page + .lintr); close-out docs in a follow-up docs: commit. The bug: R/correctParentSex.R correction branch (reportErrors = FALSE, old lines 90-91) used sex[((id %in% sires) & (sex != "M"))] <- "M" / (sex != "F") ‚Üí ‚ÄúF‚Äù, overwriting any non-M sire / non-F dam ‚Äî so hermaphrodite (‚ÄúH‚Äù) and unknown (‚ÄúU‚Äù) parents were silently forced to M/F. The report branch (reportErrors = TRUE, R/correctParentSex.R:71,73) instead uses !sex %in% c("H","U","M") / c("H","U","F"), deliberately exempting H/U. The two branches were inconsistent; the correction branch destroyed recorded H/U sex. Reachability ‚Äî verdict MIXED (verified BEFORE fixing via a 3-lens read-only workflow wf_50c7b30a-f43: caller-trace ‚Äñ empirical-repro ‚Äñ adversarial masking-critic; artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/wjahwpq5b.output): - U-sex parent via canonical qcStudbook(reportErrors=FALSE) = REACHABLE. convertSexCodes (qcStudbook.R:184) preserves U (NA‚ÜíU, Unknown‚ÜíU); an animal with its OWN line, sex ‚ÄúU‚Äù, listed as a sire/dam reaches the correction at qcStudbook.R:202 and is overwritten U‚ÜíM (sire) / U‚ÜíF (dam). End-to-end reproduced: own-line ‚ÄúU‚Äù sire emerges from qcStudbook as ‚ÄúM‚Äù. - U-sex parent via qcStudbook(reportErrors=TRUE) = REACHABLE through the inner reportErrors=FALSE call at qcStudbook.R:192 (the report branch never flags U/H, so all-NULL ‚Üí inner correction runs). - H & U via the exported correctParentSex() directly = REACHABLE (public API; the report branch‚Äôs c("H","U","M") whitelist proves H is a valid input). H‚ÜíM, U‚ÜíM, H‚ÜíF, U‚ÜíF all reproduced. - Literal H through default qcStudbook = MASKED: convertSexCodes(ignoreHerm=TRUE) folds H‚ÜíU at :184 before the correction (qcStudbook exposes no ignoreHerm param); the harm still lands but as the U-overwrite. The adversarial critic conceded every reachable mechanism and called the literal-H masking ‚Äúhollow‚Äù (U is the carrier). Semantic decision (USER, package author): the self-contradictory code can‚Äôt say what should happen to H/U parents, so I put a grounded 3-option choice (with code previews) to the user: (A) preserve H and U ‚Äî mirror the report branch; (B) preserve only H, keep inferring U‚ÜíM/F from parental role; (C) keep overwriting but warning(). User chose (A). This is a deliberate behavior change to qcStudbook output (a U-sex parent now stays U instead of becoming M/F), not just a diagnostic ‚Äî unlike NEW-40, whose fix only added a warning. The fix (minimal, consistency): R/correctParentSex.R:98-99 ‚Äî (sex != "M") ‚Üí !(sex %in% c("H", "U", "M")) and (sex != "F") ‚Üí !(sex %in% c("H", "U", "F")). Reuses the report branch‚Äôs exact, proven membership idiom; only true female-sires (F‚ÜíM) and male-dams (M‚ÜíF) are corrected. TDD trail: RED ‚Äî 3 tests added (tests/testthat/test_correctParentSex.R:81-138): (1) :95 ‚Äúleaves H/U sires and dams unchanged‚Äù ‚Äî 4 assertions (sH/sU/dH/dU) FAILED with exact overwrite signatures (M/F instead of H/U); (2) :122 ‚ÄúqcStudbook preserves a U-sex parent‚Äù ‚Äî integration test, FAILED M‚âÝU (proving real-pipeline reachability AND that the crafted studbook is well-formed ‚Äî qcStudbook returned a data frame, no error); (guard) :104 ‚Äústill corrects true female-sires and male-dams‚Äù ‚Äî PASSED before and after (true positives preserved). GREEN ‚Äî the 2-line membership fix; file 14/14. REFACTOR (user-approved scope = docs) ‚Äî added roxygen @details (:7-10) documenting H/U preservation; regenerated man/correctParentSex.Rd (clean, no NAMESPACE cascade). Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1916 passed (+7 = exactly the new expectations 4+2+1); zero non-e2e offenders. Warning column: only the 5 pre-existing test_modPyramid.R max()-on-empty warnings (unrelated, still open). Lint = 0 on R/correctParentSex.R and the test file (after the .lintr bump ‚Äî see gotcha #1). HEAD-version lint cross-checked to prove the resurfaced lint was pre-existing. Key files: - R/correctParentSex.R:98-99 ‚Äî the fix; roxygen @details at :7-10. - tests/testthat/test_correctParentSex.R:81-138 ‚Äî the 3 new tests (deterministic, inline data.frames only). - man/correctParentSex.Rd:36-41 ‚Äî regenerated \details{}. - .lintr:20 ‚Äî exclusion bumped 70L‚Üí75L to keep suppressing the pre-existing unnecessary_nesting_linter on if (reportErrors) (now line 75). - Workflow artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/wjahwpq5b.output ‚Äî the per-mechanism REACHABLE/MASKED verdicts + verbatim repro output. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. .lintr suppresses lints by HARDCODED LINE NUMBER (.lintr:17-34 lists ~19 files incl.¬ÝR/correctParentSex.R, R/getPyramidPlot.R, R/fillGroupMembers*.R, ‚Ä¶). Any edit that adds/removes lines above such a suppressed line (roxygen, imports, comments) shifts it, un-suppresses a pre-existing finding, and reads as ‚Äúa lint you introduced.‚Äù Countermeasure: after editing a file listed in .lintr, re-lint; if a lint appears, confirm it‚Äôs pre-existing by linting the HEAD copy (git show HEAD:path > /tmp/x.R; Rscript -e 'lintr::lint("/tmp/x.R")'), then update the exclusion‚Äôs line number ‚Äî do NOT refactor the suppressed (usually out-of-scope) structure. (See Learning #7.) 2. convertSexCodes is a masking step for ANY sex-code item. At qcStudbook.R:184 (default ignoreHerm=TRUE) it folds H‚ÜíU, NA‚ÜíU, Unknown‚ÜíU before most consumers. So a literal-H pathology is masked-as-U through the canonical pipeline but reachable via exported functions called directly. Treat it like addParents (NEW-40) ‚Äî a transform that can mask or re-establish a condition. qcStudbook exposes no ignoreHerm param. 3. NEW-36 / PED-6 is still OPEN in this same file and is what the suppressed lint flags: correctParentSex‚Äôs if (reportErrors) {...} else {...} returns different types (an error list vs a sex vector) by flag. A future overhaul session could split it into two functions. I deliberately did NOT touch it (scope) ‚Äî only updated the .lintr line number. 4. This fix CHANGES pipeline output (not just a diagnostic). A U-sex parent now stays U through qcStudbook. No current test relied on the old M/F (full suite clean), but if a future consumer assumes parents always have definite M/F sex, this is the change to remember. User approved it explicitly. 5. Scope held / NOT done (deliberate): did NOT fix NEW-36/PED-6 (dual return type); did NOT touch convertSexCodes; did NOT address the other report-vs-correction asymmetry (the report branch filters recordStatus == "original", the correction branch does not ‚Äî a separate latent inconsistency, not NEW-37); did NOT touch the pre-existing modPyramid max()-on-empty warnings or the test_getPotentialParents copy/paste-slip test (both still open). Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase, asked permission before RED‚ÜíGREEN and GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR; the bug-demonstrating tests failed for the right reason (H/U overwritten) while the guard proved true positives preserved. (+) Verified reachability per-mechanism with a 3-lens read-only workflow before writing the test (the NEW-40 template), surfacing the MIXED verdict; the adversarial critic conceded. (+) Recognized the fix encodes a domain decision the self-contradictory code couldn‚Äôt answer and put a concrete, empirically-grounded choice (with code previews) to the package author rather than guessing. (+) Added an end-to-end qcStudbook integration test, not just a unit test, so the user-facing reachability is regression-guarded. (+) Root-caused the lint 0‚Üí1 to a line-anchored .lintr exclusion displaced by my own @details and fixed it minimally (line-number bump), verified against HEAD rather than assuming, and resisted refactoring the out-of-scope NEW-36 structure. (‚àí) My @details is what displaced the .lintr line; I could have anticipated the line-anchored exclusions before regenerating docs (cost ~2 investigation turns ‚Äî though it produced Learning #7). (‚àí) Three user-facing prompts (semantic decision + two phase gates); the semantic one was necessary and the gates are mandated by the contract, but a looser contract could have bundled them.

Session 4 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 5)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: The ACTIVE TASK ‚ÄúWhat You Must Do‚Äù #1 listed NEW-40 with an accurate one-line mechanism (findGeneration silent NA) AND the ‚öÝ ‚ÄúSeveral are masked by upstream invariants/non-default inputs ‚Äî verify reachability with real data BEFORE ‚Äòfixing‚Äô‚Äù. That single warning shaped the whole session: I ran the read-only reachability/masking workflow first and it paid off precisely ‚Äî the simplest mechanism (dangling parent) turned out masked by addParents, while the cycle mechanism is genuinely reachable through the full qcStudbook pipeline. Without that warning I‚Äôd have written a weaker test against the masked path and ‚Äúfixed‚Äù something that can‚Äôt reach users via the canonical flow. - What helped (process): Gotcha #1 (verbatim: test_dir skips e2e; count failed AND error; isolate non-e2e offenders with !grepl("test-app-|test-e2e-", file)) gave the clean 0/0 read every time AND ‚Äî because I also watched the warning column ‚Äî surfaced that my new warning() had tripped an existing circular-reference test. Gotcha #4 (Phase 1B stub) honored. The fast pkgload::load_all + test_file invocation (Learning #4 / S3 gotcha #4) used throughout. - What was slightly off: The NEW-40 one-liner ‚Äúsilent NA‚Äù was accurate but undifferentiated ‚Äî it didn‚Äôt hint that reachability splits by mechanism (dangling = masked, cycle = reachable). Minor: the generic ‚öÝ ‚Äúverify reachability‚Äù line explicitly anticipated exactly this, so no time lost. - What was missing: Nothing material. The pre-existing test_modPedigree_processing.R circular-reference test that my warning would trip is not something S4 could have flagged for NEW-40 specifically. - ROI: Strongly positive ‚Äî the reachability warning + accurate finding pointer + reusable test/lint gotchas scoped the session immediately and steered me away from a masked-path fix.

What Session 5 Did

Deliverable: Fixed NEW-40 (findGeneration silent NA generations) under strict TDD, with regression tests + doc update. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-30. Branch: add-methodology. Commit: ea5d28fa (fix + tests + man page); close-out docs in a follow-up docs: commit. The bug: R/findGeneration.R initialises gen <- rep(NA, length(id)) and assigns a generation only to ids whose parents are all NA-or-already-placed; it breaks when no new id can be placed, leaving any unplaceable id as NA with no warning()/stop(). The roxygen precondition (l.20, ‚Äúdoes not work if the pedigree does not have all parent IDs as ego IDs‚Äù) was unenforced. The exported findGeneration (and the ~14 R/ + module callers) thus returned silent NA gen. Reachability ‚Äî verdict MIXED (verified BEFORE fixing via a 3-lens read-only workflow wf_ea9c8e19-b8f: caller-trace ‚Äñ empirical-repro ‚Äñ adversarial masking-critic; artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/wpyav39fd.output): - Dangling parent (parent id absent from id) = MASKED through the canonical pipeline: qcStudbook calls addParents (R/qcStudbook.R:181) before findGeneration (:249), and addParents injects a founder line (sire=dam=NA ‚Üí gen 0) for every missing parent ‚Äî empirically confirmed no NA results. - Cycle / self-loop = REACHABLE through the full qcStudbook pipeline: a cycle survives addParents (both ids already present), and checkParentAge only flags a too-young parent when birth dates exist (R/checkParentAge.R:91 drops NA-birth rows) ‚Äî so a cycle with NA birth dates slips through and qcStudbook returns normally with gen=NA. Empirically: data.frame(id=c("a","b"), sire=c(NA,"a"), dam=c("b",NA), sex=c("F","M"), birth=c(NA,NA)) ‚Üí silent NA gen. The adversarial masking-critic (whose job was to argue MASKED) conceded the cycle path and recommended the fix. - Downstream impact: the silent NA later detonates in kinship() as the cryptic "NAs are not allowed in subscripted assignments", far from the root cause ‚Äî so surfacing it at the source has real value. The fix (minimal, choke-point): after the loop, if (anyNA(gen)) build and emit a warning() naming the unplaced id(s) and (when present) the referenced-but-absent parent id(s). Covers cycle + dangling-parent + self-loop in one place. Return contract unchanged (still the gen vector with NAs), so all callers/tests are unaffected. Provably non-spurious: a valid acyclic self-contained pedigree always places every id (every ancestor chain ends at an NA-parent founder reachable by the iteration); lacy1989Ped and examplePedigree both yield zero NA. Chose warning over stop (user-confirmed) to preserve backward-compat for pipelines that currently complete with partial NA gen. TDD trail: RED ‚Äî added 3 tests (tests/testthat/test_findGeneration.R:14-46): (1) 2-cycle ‚Üí expect_warning + all-NA; (2) dangling parent ‚Üí expect_warning(regexp="GHOST") + orphan NA / founder 0; (3) happy path ‚Üí expect_warning(regexp=NA). Tests 1‚Äì2 failed for the right reason (no warning emitted) while their value assertions already passed ‚Äî proving the bug is exactly the missing diagnostic. GREEN ‚Äî added the guard; file passes 7/7. REFACTOR (user-approved scope = lint+docs) ‚Äî any(is.na())‚ÜíanyNA(), paste(.,collapse=", ")‚ÜítoString(.) (√ó2); extended roxygen @return and regenerated man/findGeneration.Rd (clean, no cascade). Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 1909 passed; zero non-e2e offenders. Warning column: the only remaining non-e2e warnings are the 5 pre-existing test_modPyramid.R "no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf" (unrelated to NEW-40 ‚Äî a max()-on-empty in the pyramid plot; candidate for a future session). Lint = 0 on R/findGeneration.R and both test files. devtools::document() touched only man/findGeneration.Rd. Key files: - R/findGeneration.R:55-72 ‚Äî the fix (the if (anyNA(gen)) warning block); roxygen @return at :22-26. - tests/testthat/test_findGeneration.R:14-46 ‚Äî the 3 new tests (deterministic, no fixtures beyond inline vectors + lacy1989Ped). - tests/testthat/test_modPedigree_processing.R:665-680 ‚Äî the existing circular-reference test, now wrapping session$setInputs(...) in expect_warning(regexp="could not be assigned a generation") and suppressWarnings(result$pedigree()). - man/findGeneration.Rd ‚Äî regenerated \value. - Workflow artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/wpyav39fd.output ‚Äî the three structured REACHABLE/MASKED verdicts + exact repro console output. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. Reachability splits by MECHANISM, not one yes/no. NEW-40 had a masked path AND a reachable path. When verifying any ‚Äúsilent NA / silent degradation‚Äù item, enumerate each distinct trigger and test reachability per-mechanism through the full qcStudbook pipeline. (See Learning #6.) 2. checkParentAge is NOT a cycle/integrity guard ‚Äî it drops NA-birth rows (R/checkParentAge.R:91), so any pedigree pathology among NA-birth animals (cycles, etc.) flows past it into findGeneration/kinship. Relevant to NEW-45/48/53 reachability too. 3. Adding a warning()/stop() can trip EXISTING tests that exercise the degenerate path. My warning hit test_modPedigree_processing.R‚Äôs circular-reference test (passed, but as an unexpected warning). Always re-run the full suite and watch the warning column, not just failed/error; update any test that feeds the degenerate input to expect the new diagnostic rather than suppress it silently. 4. @exported compute + warning() is backward-safe; stop() is not. A warning leaves the return contract intact (all callers unaffected); a stop is a behavior change. For these audit correctness items prefer surfacing-not-aborting unless the user asks otherwise. 5. Scope held / NOT done (deliberate): I did NOT add cycle rejection to qcStudbook/checkParentAge (a larger, separate change ‚Äî the warning at findGeneration is the proportionate NEW-40 fix), and did NOT touch the pre-existing modPyramid max()-on-empty warnings. Both are candidate follow-ups. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase, asked permission before RED‚ÜíGREEN and GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR, wrote tests that fail for the right reason (missing diagnostic, values already correct), then the minimal choke-point fix. (+) Verified reachability with a 3-lens workflow before writing the test, exactly as the predecessor‚Äôs gotcha demanded, and the adversarial critic‚Äôs concession (cycle reachable) made the fix decision evidence-backed rather than assumed. (+) Caught and strengthened (not merely silenced) the existing circular-reference test that my warning tripped ‚Äî by watching the warning column, not just failed/error. (+) Doc + lint REFACTOR kept the function to the project‚Äôs lint=0 bar; man-page regen was clean. (+) Held scope (no qcStudbook cycle-rejection, no modPyramid detour). (‚àí) The warning lists all unplaced ids via toString with no cap ‚Äî fine for a diagnostic, but a pathological all-NA pedigree would produce a very long message; didn‚Äôt bound it. (‚àí) Two user permission prompts (approach, REFACTOR scope) ‚Äî correct under the strict-TDD contract, but a leaner session might have proposed the unanimous-agent default and proceeded; I prioritised contract fidelity.

Session 3 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 4)

Score: 9/10. - What helped most: The ACTIVE TASK ‚ÄúWhat You Must Do‚Äù #1 named NEW-34 first with an accurate one-line mechanism (getPotentialParents unbound j ‚Üí crash) AND the ‚öÝ ‚ÄúSeveral are masked by upstream invariants ‚Äî verify reachability with real data BEFORE ‚Äòfixing.‚Äô‚Äù That warning shaped the whole session: I ran a read-only reachability + masking workflow before touching code and confirmed the crash via the canonical qcStudbook pipeline rather than assuming.
