Highest-ROI line in the handoff. - What helped (process): Gotcha #1 (sum failed AND error, not just failed) ‚Äî I tallied both columns. Gotcha #4 (the fast single-file pkgload::load_all + test_file invocation) ‚Äî used verbatim, saved time. Gotcha #2 (the e2e/create_test_app files are pre-existing baseline noise) primed me not to chase them. - What was slightly off: (a) The NEW-34 one-liner said ‚Äúwhen no animal has a missing parent‚Äù ‚Äî it‚Äôs actually no from-center animal (founders with NA parents but fromCenter=FALSE are correctly excluded); trivial, didn‚Äôt mislead. (b) Gotcha #2 framed the e2e files as 154 ERRORS, but that is test_local-specific ‚Äî under testthat::test_dir(...) they SKIP (159 skipped, 0 error). Both are runner artifacts, not regressions; see Learning #4. - What was missing: Nothing material for NEW-34. (removeAutoGenIds re-NA-ing U-prefixed parents ‚Äî which matters when building an empty-pUnknown fixture from real data ‚Äî is NEW-34-specific detail Session 3 couldn‚Äôt have anticipated.) - ROI: Strongly positive ‚Äî the reachability warning + accurate finding pointer + reusable test/lint gotchas scoped the session immediately.

What Session 4 Did

Deliverable: Fixed NEW-34 (getPotentialParents unbound-j crash) under strict TDD, with a regression test. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-30. Branch: add-methodology. Commit: dc695a3b (fix + test); close-out docs in a follow-up docs: commit. The bug: R/getPotentialParents.R assigned the loop counter j <- 0L only inside if (nrow(pUnknown) > 0L) (old line 49), but read it unconditionally at if (j > 0L) (line 112). When pUnknown has zero rows ‚Äî i.e.¬Ýno fromCenter==TRUE animal has a missing sire/dam ‚Äî the if body is skipped, j is never bound, and R raises Error: object 'j' not found instead of returning the intended NULL. The function is @exported (NAMESPACE:86), so this is reachable from the public API. Reachability (verified BEFORE fixing, per Session 3‚Äôs gotcha): a read-only 3-agent workflow (wf_95e0a06c-aad) confirmed it three independent ways ‚Äî (1) a crafted 6-animal data.frame, (2) the packaged examplePedigree filtered so pUnknown is truly empty (had to also drop U-prefixed parent ids, because removeAutoGenIds re-NA-s those and re-introduces unknowns), and (3) the canonical qcStudbook pipeline with a 5-row studbook whose lone from-center animal has both parents known. No upstream invariant masks it: fromCenter is optional in qcStudbook; founder stubs from addParents get fromCenter=NA/FALSE and never rescue pUnknown. The fix (minimal): moved j <- 0L from inside the if to immediately before it (R/getPotentialParents.R:48). Non-empty path is byte-for-byte unchanged; empty path now falls through to the existing else NULL. No internal R/ callers exist (only test/example uses), so blast radius is this function alone. TDD trail: RED ‚Äî added test_that("getPotentialParents returns NULL when no from-center animal has a missing parent") (tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R:69-90); failed for the right reason (object 'j' not found at the expect_null call), the other 8 assertions still passing. GREEN ‚Äî after the hoist, the file passes 9/9. REFACTOR ‚Äî none needed (one-line hoist, already idiomatic). Verification: full suite via test_dir = 0 failed / 0 error, 159 skipped, 862 tests; zero non-(app/e2e) offenders. Robustness sweep: all four empty-pUnknown triggers (mixed fromCenter, all-FALSE, all-resolved, all-births-NA) now return NULL; rhesusPedigree happy path unchanged (list len 50, [[1]]$id=BRI2MW). Lint = 0 on both changed files. Key files: - R/getPotentialParents.R:48 ‚Äî the fix (the hoisted j <- 0L); the unconditional read is at :112. - tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R:69-90 ‚Äî the regression test. Inline 3-animal fixture: founders A/B (fromCenter=FALSE, NA parents) excluded; from-center C has both parents known ‚áí empty pUnknown. - Workflow artifact ‚Ä¶/tasks/w27jywrus.output ‚Äî the reachability/masking verdicts and exact repro scripts. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. test_dir SKIPS the Shiny e2e files; test_local ERRORS on them. Same baseline noise, different column. For a clean compute-change regression read, run testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat", reporter="silent", stop_on_failure=FALSE), then sum(failed)/sum(error) and isolate true regressions with !grepl("test-app-|test-e2e-", file). This session: 0/0/0 offenders. (Refines Session 3 gotcha #1/#2 and Learning #2.) 2. removeAutoGenIds (R/removeAutoGenIds.R) re-introduces unknown parents ‚Äî it NA-s any sire/dam whose value starts with "U". So to build a real-data fixture with an empty pUnknown, resolving NA parents is not enough; you must also avoid U-prefixed parent ids. Relevant to any test that needs the ‚Äúno unknown parents‚Äù state. 3. NEW-53 lives in getPotentialParents too: line 28 does data.table::setDT(ped) with no copy(), mutating the caller‚Äôs ped by reference (class flips to data.table). Out of scope for NEW-34 (the crash) but should be fixed alongside the broader NEW-53 item. 4. Phase 1B stub honored this session (Session 3 missed it) ‚Äî the stub was written at task receipt and overwritten here at close-out. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ‚Äî declared every phase, asked permission before RED and before GREEN, and empirically confirmed reachability three ways before writing the test, exactly as the predecessor‚Äôs gotcha demanded. (+) Wrote the Phase 1B stub at receipt (fixing Session 3‚Äôs noted miss). (+) Verification went beyond the bar: full-suite tally counting both columns + isolation of non-e2e offenders, a 4-case robustness sweep, a happy-path regression check, and lint. (+) Held scope ‚Äî flagged but did NOT fix the copy/paste-slip test or the NEW-53 in-place mutation in the same file. (‚àí) Asserted the test_dir-skips-vs-test_local-errors difference is a ‚Äúrunner artifact‚Äù without nailing the exact mechanism (helper/setup loading); orthogonal to NEW-34 but unproven. (‚àí) The 3-agent reachability workflow was arguably heavier than a one-line fix strictly needed ‚Äî justified by the ‚Äúverify reachability/masking‚Äù gotcha and ultracode, but worth right-sizing for obviously-reachable items.

Session 2 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 3)

Score: 9/10. - What helped: The ACTIVE TASK block named NEW-15 as the #1 priority with the file:line (countKinshipValues.R:133), the precise mechanism (outer loop var overwrites the slot), and the key insight that the path is UNTESTED because the existing fixtures only exercise equal value-sets. That one paragraph scoped the whole session ‚Äî I knew immediately I had to construct differing value-sets to reach the buggy branch. Gotcha #2 (‚ÄúNEW-15 is UNTESTED ‚Äî write the failing test first‚Äù) was exactly right. - What helped (process): Gotcha #3 (‚Äúverify reachability before fixing latent items‚Äù) reinforced confirming the bug empirically ‚Äî I ran the buggy function against constructed inputs and observed the corrupted output before writing the test. - What was slightly off: The line drifted by one ‚Äî the wrong write is at countKinshipValues.R:132, not 133. Trivial; the file:function pointer landed me in <1 min. - What was missing: No warning that the broad suite has 154 pre-existing create_test_app ERRORS ‚Äî I had to discover and rule those out to confirm zero regressions. Now documented (ACTIVE TASK #5) so the next session won‚Äôt re-derive it. - ROI: Strongly positive ‚Äî turned a HIGH-severity bug hunt into a targeted 2-line fix.

What Session 3 Did

Deliverable: Fixed NEW-15 (the audit‚Äôs only HIGH-severity bug) under strict TDD, with a regression test. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-30. Branch: add-methodology. Commit: b05133ca. The bug: R/countKinshipValues.R accumulation branch ‚Äî countDiffs[index] <- kCounts[[index]][kValues[[index]] == value] used the OUTER row-loop variable index to subscript countDiffs (a vector sized to length(valueDiffs)). When a later simulation batch introduced kinship value(s) not already accumulated for a pair, this (a) overwrote one slot when >1 new value existed, and (b) wrote out of bounds for any row index > 1, padding with 0/NA and desynchronising the lengths of kValues and kCounts ‚Äî which then breaks summarizeKinshipValues() downstream (rep(values, counts)). The fix (minimal): for (i in seq_along(valueDiffs)) with countDiffs[i] <- kCounts[[index]][kValues[[index]] == valueDiffs[i]]. Kept the function‚Äôs explicit-loop idiom. TDD trail: RED ‚Äî added test_that("countKinshipValues merges new kinship values into correct slots"); it failed against the original code with the exact bug signatures (count c(6,1,0) vs c(6,1,1); length desync 3‚âÝ2; observation sum 7‚âÝ8), empirically confirmed first. GREEN ‚Äî after the fix the file passes 22/22. REFACTOR ‚Äî none needed (already minimal/idiomatic, lint-clean). Verification: full suite = 0 expectation failures, 1941 passing assertions / 866 tests; lint = 0 on both changed files. The only suite errors (154) are PRE-EXISTING, all in the 22 test-app-*/test-e2e-* files from the undefined create_test_app() (confirmed undefined at HEAD before my change; shinytest2+chromote installed so they don‚Äôt skip) ‚Äî structurally independent of countKinshipValues. Blast radius: only the two changed files; kinship/simulation cluster (countKinshipValues, summarizeKinshipValues, createSimKinships, cumulateSimKinships, geneDrop, meanKinship) all green. Key files: - R/countKinshipValues.R:131-135 ‚Äî the fix. - tests/testthat/test_countKinshipValues.R:96-149 ‚Äî the regression test. Deterministic: builds two data.table batches with dyadic kinship values (1/4, 1/8, 1/16) that round-trip exactly through table(); exercises both the index-1 overwrite and index-2 out-of-bounds modes. GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. testthat::test_local() records errors in a separate error column, NOT failed. Summing only df$failed hides the 154 create_test_app errors (I hit this ‚Äî a first tally showed ‚Äú0 fail‚Äù and looked clean). Always sum failed AND error. 2. The 154 create_test_app errors are pre-existing infra debt, not regressions ‚Äî don‚Äôt chase them when verifying a compute fix. The real fix is ACTIVE TASK #5. 3. HEAD git worktrees don‚Äôt load here: the worktree lacks renv/activate.R, so its .Rprofile errors and pkgload::load_all won‚Äôt run. For a baseline, revert the one file in place or use a structural argument (e.g.¬Ýgit grep proving a symbol is undefined at HEAD) instead of a worktree suite run. 4. Fast single-file test: Rscript -e 'suppressMessages(pkgload::load_all(".", quiet=TRUE)); testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test_X.R", reporter="summary")'. The ‚Äúout-of-sync renv‚Äù warning is benign. Self-assessment: 9/10. (+) Strict TDD honored ‚Äî empirically confirmed the buggy output, wrote a test that fails for the RIGHT reason, then the minimal fix; declared every phase. (+) Did not trust a green-looking ‚Äú0 fail‚Äù tally ‚Äî chased the run-to-run discrepancy until I found the error-vs-failed column issue, and structurally proved the e2e errors pre-existing rather than asserting it. (+) Stopped before commit per the user‚Äôs explicit request; kept the review diff to the two relevant files. (+) One deliverable; flagged (did not fix) the create_test_app debt. (‚àí) Burned several turns fighting the renv-broken worktree before pivoting to git grep-on-HEAD ‚Äî should have reached for the structural proof sooner. (‚àí) Skipped the Phase 1B session stub at task receipt (claimed the session only at close-out); low risk since I handed back cleanly, but a protocol miss to avoid next time.

Session 1 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 2)

Score: 9/10. - What helped: The ACTIVE TASK block named the exact next task (re-run PED/GV) with the reason (agent failures = unaudited, not ‚Äúclean‚Äù), pointed to Appendix C ‚ÄúKnown coverage gaps‚Äù, and listed the specific unaudited core files. Gotcha #5 (dashboard hangs as a background task; run with </dev/null) and the phantom-filename warning (#1) saved real time. The KIN-2 ‚Äúdo not naively unify the descendants lines‚Äù trap was accurate and was preserved. - What was missing: No note that this environment‚Äôs Bash can rewrite/block grep/cat (caused brief early confusion) ‚Äî but that is environmental, not Session 1‚Äôs fault. - What was wrong: Nothing material. Gotcha #2 (PED/GV = not audited, re-run) was exactly right and was the whole basis of this session. - ROI: Strongly positive ‚Äî reading the handoff directly scoped the session.

What Session 2 Did

Deliverable: PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md ‚Äî re-audit of the PED and GV clusters that returned 0 findings in Session 1 due to sub-agent failures. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-30. Branch: add-methodology. What was produced: PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md ‚Äî the full verified finding set (61 confirmed, 2 refuted of 63 candidates), deduped to ~24 distinct issues, with a correctness/dead-code section, a refuted-findings appendix, coverage + test-gap lists, and updated PED/GV cluster-overview rows. No source code modified. How: multi-agent workflow wf_8077a831-96f ‚Äî 4 parallel auditors (2 GV lenses, 1 PED cross-check, 1 deep-dive critic) ‚Üí adversarial per-finding verification (63 ‚Üí 61 confirmed / 2 refuted; 67 agents). PLUS the author independently read all 24 GV files end-to-end and corroborated every spot-checked finding. Key results: PED is largely clean (0 high; themes: founders idiom PED-1, sex codes PED-2, error/return PED-5/6, getPotentialParents PED-4). GV is well-factored but carries the audit‚Äôs only HIGH-severity bug ‚Äî NEW-15 (countKinshipValues.R:133 wrong loop index, untested path). Other notable correctness items: NEW-34 (getPotentialParents crash), NEW-40 (findGeneration NA), NEW-37 (correctParentSex H/U overwrite), NEW-45 (geneDrop period-in-id), NEW-48 (calcFEFG NA), NEW-53 (makeSimPed in-place mutation), NEW-52 (sd n=1), NEW-25 (getProportionLow empty). Plus NEW-20 dead file (makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R) and NEW-13/23 calcFE/FG/FEFG triplication. Four confirmed findings have no test: NEW-20, NEW-41, NEW-44, NEW-58. Key files: - PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md ‚Äî the deliverable (full finding tables + refuted appendix). - Workflow artifact ‚Ä¶/18efd281-‚Ä¶/tasks/w9oz3tkdf.output ‚Äî per-finding verdict JSON (incl.¬Ýthe 2 refuted, NEW-49 / NEW-60, with rationales). - Workflow script ‚Ä¶/workflows/scripts/ped-gv-audit-rerun-wf_8077a831-96f.js (resumable). GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. Tool-output rendering lagged badly in this autonomous session ‚Äî Bash/Read returned empty for many consecutive turns, flushing in batches only on external events (user messages, background-task notifications). Write/Edit and subagents worked normally. Countermeasure: delegate reads/parsing to a subagent (its final message returns reliably), or trigger a flush with a run_in_background command. 2. NEW-15 is the one HIGH bug and is UNTESTED ‚Äî its buggy branch is never hit by the current test (only equal value-sets). Write the failing test first. 3. Verify reachability before ‚Äúfixing‚Äù latent items ‚Äî several medium correctness items (NEW-45/48/53) are masked by upstream invariants/non-default inputs; confirm with real data first. 4. The 2 refuted findings (NEW-49, NEW-60) are in the report‚Äôs appendix and the artifact ‚Äî refuted because their causal mechanism didn‚Äôt hold (not a tooling failure). Self-assessment: 8.5/10. (+) Avoided Session 1‚Äôs failure mode by independently reading every GV file rather than trusting agents; adversarial verification (61/63) kept the floor high and caught 2 plausible-but-wrong candidates. (+) Surfaced one HIGH bug + ~10 latent correctness items beyond pure debt. (‚àí) Wrote a first version of the report before the full verification data had rendered (harness output lag), then had to expand and re-commit it. (‚àí) Spent excessive turns fighting the output lag before pivoting to subagent delegation.

What Session 1 Did

Deliverable: Read-only Senior-Architect technical-debt & refactoring-viability audit of nprcgenekeepr. (COMPLETE) Date: 2026-05-30 Branch: add-methodology (unchanged)

What was produced: - TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md (959 lines) ‚Äî the audit report. Sections: Executive Summary, Cluster Overview, (1) Cognitive Complexity, (2) Duplication, (3) Extensibility, (4) Prioritized Refactoring Targets (Quick Wins vs Architectural Overhauls), Appendix A Coverage, Appendix B Rejected Findings, Appendix C Method & Caveats + Known coverage gaps. - /Users/rmsharp/.claude/plans/reflective-bouncing-cat.md ‚Äî the approved audit plan. - No source code was modified (user instruction: ‚ÄúDo not modify any code‚Äù). The only repo file created is the report; SESSION_NOTES.md updated for handoff.

How it was done: Multi-agent read-only workflow (ultracode). 81 sub-agents: 11 parallel per-cluster auditors (QC, PED, LOOP, KIN, GV, GRP, GENO, APP, MISC, XDRY, XARCH) + adversarial per-finding verifiers + a coverage agent. Only verifier-CONFIRMED findings are in the main report; severity/category are verifier-ADJUSTED.

Results: 60 confirmed findings (13 complexity, 19 duplication, 28 extensibility; 44 quick-wins, 16 overhauls). 29 findings rejected by verification. Dominant themes: (a) two coexisting/diverging Shiny apps; (b) dead-code/duplicate-variant accumulation; (c) hardcoded domain constants (sex codes M/F/U/H, minParentAge=2, column-name lists) with no central schema/species profile; (d) Shiny leaking into core compute; (e) inconsistent error/return conventions.

Key report locations (TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md): - Cluster Overview table: ~line 21 - ¬ß1 Complexity ~line 35; ¬ß2 Duplication ~line 192; ¬ß3 Extensibility ~line 415 - ¬ß4 Prioritized (Quick Wins / Overhauls): ~line 741 - Appendix A Coverage (74-file gap list): ~line 811 - Appendix B Rejected findings table: ~line 899 - Appendix C Caveats + Known coverage gaps: ~line 935

GOTCHAS for the next session: 1. The audit BRIEF contained phantom filenames (e.g.¬ÝcorrectParentSexErrors.R, addPedigreeYears.R, getDateErrorMessage.R) that do NOT exist in R/. The QC and several XDRY findings against them were correctly rejected ‚Äî do not chase those files. 2. PED and GV clusters = 0 findings = NOT audited (agent failures), not ‚Äúclean.‚Äù Re-run. 3. Some Appendix-B rejections were transient tool failures, not real refutations (verifier literally got empty tool output). APP-11 was rejected this way but is the SAME issue as confirmed XARCH-1. GENO-2, APP-2/4/5, GRP-1/2/6 are also re-verify candidates. 4. Auditor line numbers drifted in some findings; the verifier‚Äôs correctedLineRange is authoritative (e.g.¬ÝKIN-1 is calcFEFG.R:36-82, not the auditor‚Äôs 44-93). 5. python3 methodology_dashboard.py hangs as a background task (it tries to open a browser). Run it in the foreground with stdin closed: python3 methodology_dashboard.py </dev/null. It prints a terminal summary and writes dashboard.html. Last health score: 78/100. 6. Uncommitted, NOT mine: working tree also has .DS_Store (modified), untracked ..Rcheck/ (an R CMD check artifact dir) and .claude/. I did not touch these; the audit commit includes ONLY the report + this file.

Self-assessment score: 8/10. - (+) Delivered exactly one deliverable (the audit), read-only, honoring the constraint. - (+) Adversarial verification caught hallucinated/phantom-file findings before they reached the report ‚Äî the report‚Äôs confirmed findings are a high-confidence floor. - (+) Honestly flagged the PED/GV coverage gap rather than presenting 0-findings as clean. - (‚àí) My cluster brief seeded phantom filenames (from training-prior guesses about R file names) that wasted auditor/verifier effort. Lesson: resolve the real file list with Glob FIRST, then build cluster briefs from actual paths ‚Äî don‚Äôt hand agents guessed filenames. - (‚àí) Two cluster agents (PED, GV) returned empty and I did not re-dispatch them within the session before finalizing; flagged for re-run instead.
